description: Indicates whether this fertilization event was executed using Variable Rate Application (VRA) technology, applying different rates across management zones within the field. True = VRA prescription followed; False = flat-rate uniform application.

description: Date and time when this fertilization event record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental loads, and audit trail compliance.

description: Actual pounds of elemental nitrogen applied per acre during this event, derived from actual_rate and nitrogen_pct. Stored as a raw field value from the source system for EPA nutrient management plan compliance and environmental reporting. Critical KPI for nitrogen use efficiency (NUE) analysis.
reference: EPA nutrient management reporting; USDA NRCS Practice Standard 590; FSMA Produce Safety Rule environmental monitoring

description: Percentage of nitrogen (N) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for nutrient budget calculations, soil nitrogen loading analysis, and EPA nutrient management plan compliance.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA nutrient management; NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium) formulation standard

description: Free-text field for operator or agronomist notes related to this fertilization event. May include observations about field conditions, equipment issues, deviations from prescription, or agronomic rationale. Supports GAP documentation requirements.
reference: GAP (Good Agricultural Practices) field record documentation; GLOBALG.A.P. activity log requirements

description: Reference number or identifier of the USDA NRCS-approved Nutrient Management Plan (NMP) that governs this fertilization event. Required for USDA conservation program compliance (e.g., EQIP) and EPA nutrient management reporting.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA Clean Water Act; USDA EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program)

description: Percentage of phosphorus (P2O5) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for phosphorus loading calculations, water quality compliance, and soil nutrient balance reporting.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA Clean Water Act nutrient runoff compliance; NPK formulation standard

description: The target or prescribed fertilizer application rate as specified in the agronomic plan or VRA prescription, expressed in the unit defined by rate_unit. Represents the intended nutrient loading before actual application.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA prescription; USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590

description: Percentage of potassium (K2O) in the NPK formulation of the fertilizer product applied. Used for potassium budget calculations and soil fertility management reporting.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium) formulation standard

description: Unit of measure for both planned_rate and actual_rate fields. Common units include lbs/acre (CWT-based), gallons/acre for liquid fertilizers, and metric equivalents for international operations.
reference: USDA NRCS measurement standards; CWT (Hundredweight) and BU (Bushel) unit conventions; ISO 80000 measurement standards

description: Date and time when the Restricted Entry Interval (REI) expires following the fertilizer application, after which workers may safely re-enter the treated field. Calculated from application_end_timestamp plus the product-specific REI duration. Critical for OSHA and EPA worker safety compliance.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS); OSHA 29 CFR 1928.110 agricultural worker safety; REI (Restricted Entry Interval) product label requirement

description: Soil temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at the time of fertilizer application. Critical for nitrogen application timing decisions (e.g., nitrification inhibitor effectiveness, anhydrous ammonia fall application restrictions) and nutrient uptake efficiency.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; Climate FieldView soil temperature data; agronomic best practices for nitrogen timing

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this fertilization event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer ingestion audit for the Databricks Lakehouse pipeline.

description: The native identifier of this fertilization event record in the originating operational system (e.g., John Deere Operations Center task ID, Trimble Ag field activity ID). Enables deduplication and reconciliation between the Silver layer and source systems.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Trimble Ag Software; Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer data lineage standards

description: Total quantity of fertilizer product applied across the entire treated area during this event, expressed in the unit defined by rate_unit. description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Yield records carry an mrl_compliant boolean flag but lack traceability to the specific MRL threshold checked. FSMA compliance, export documentation, and food safety audits require the yield record to
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Organic yield records must be traceable to the specific NOP certification for organic handler sales, premium pricing documentation, and NOP audit compliance. The yield record is the point of transfer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Yield records are the basis for crop revenue recognition. Revenue from crop sales is attributed to profit centers for segment P&L reporting. Farm financial reporting requires yield records to be linke
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the specific crop variety or hybrid planted and harvested in this record. Enables variety-level yield benchmarking and seed performance analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Climate FieldView; Trimble Ag Software

description: Reference to the grain bin, elevator, or storage facility where this harvested lot was delivered. Links to the Inventory domain for grain inventory management and WMS integration with Manhattan Associates.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM; Granular Farm Management

business_glossary_term: Actual Production History (APH) Yield (Bushels per Acre)
description: Actual Production History (APH) yield in bushels per acre as reported to USDA RMA for crop insurance purposes. May differ from yield_per_acre due to RMA-specific calculation rules (e.g., prevented planting adjustments, yield substitutions).
reference: USDA RMA; USDA FCIC

description: Timestamp when this yield record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 22000; FSMA; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Percentage of heat-damaged, mold-damaged, or otherwise damaged kernels in the harvested grain sample. USDA grading factor impacting grade and market value. Elevated damage may indicate storage or disease issues requiring agronomic investigation.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS; SAP S/4HANA QM; HACCP

description: Percentage of foreign material (weed seeds, chaff, dirt, other crop) in the harvested grain sample. USDA grading factor that affects grade assignment and may result in elevator dockage charges.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: GMO identity status of the harvested crop. Determines segregation requirements, storage bin assignment, and market channel eligibility. Critical for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling), export documentation, and identity-preserved grain premiums.
reference: USDA NOP; APHIS; FDA; Codex Alimentarius; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Calendar date on which the harvest operation was performed. Principal business event date for this transaction. Used for PHI compliance verification, crop insurance documentation, and agronomic reporting.
reference: USDA RMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; FDA FSMA; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Precise date and time when the harvest pass was completed on the field. Combined with harvest_start_timestamp to compute harvest duration and equipment OEE metrics.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Estimated percentage of harvestable crop lost during the harvest operation due to combine settings, lodging, or field conditions. Sourced from combine loss monitor data via John Deere Operations Center. Used for equipment calibration and GAP compliance reporting.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; GLOBALG.A.P.; GAP

description: Method or header type used for the harvest operation. Affects harvest efficiency, grain loss estimates, and equipment OEE reporting. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center equipment configuration data.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP S/4HANA PM; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Precise date and time when the harvest pass began on the field. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center GPS telematics. Used for OEE calculation and equipment utilization reporting.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 22000

description: Current lifecycle status of the yield record. 'draft' indicates data is being captured; 'in_progress' indicates active harvest pass; 'completed' indicates finalized and reconciled; 'cancelled' indicates voided record; 'pending_review' indicates awaiting quality or agronomic sign-off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Granular Farm Management; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: GPS-tracked actual harvested area in acres for this harvest pass. May differ from planted area due to field boundaries, obstacles, or partial harvests. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center precision agriculture data. Used as denominator for yield per acre (YPM) calculations.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; USDA RMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; Esri ArcGIS

description: Total production adjusted to standard moisture basis in bushels. Calculated as: total_production_bu × ((100 - moisture_pct_at_harvest) / (100 - standard_moisture_pct)). Used for grain settlement, sales contract fulfillment, and financial crop enterprise accounting.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS; SAP S/4HANA MM; Granular Farm Management

description: Grain moisture content percentage measured at the time of harvest (as-harvested moisture). Critical for drying cost calculation, storage decisions, and moisture-adjusted yield computation. Sourced from combine moisture sensor data via John Deere Operations Center.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS (Federal Grain Inspection Service); SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Indicates whether the harvested crop has been assessed as compliant with applicable Maximum Residue Limits (MRL) for pesticide residues per EPA, Codex Alimentarius, and destination market requirements. Required for export documentation and food safety compliance.
reference: EPA; Codex Alimentarius; FDA; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) at Harvest
description: NDVI value recorded for the field at or near the harvest date. Ranges from -1 to +1; values above 0.6 indicate healthy dense canopy. Used to correlate remote sensing data with actual yield outcomes for predictive yield modeling and AI/ML applications.
reference: Esri ArcGIS; Climate FieldView; NASA; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether this harvest was produced under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified conditions. Drives premium pricing, segregation requirements, and organic certificate of analysis (COA) documentation.
reference: USDA NOP; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000; FDA FSMA

description: Indicates whether the Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for all applied crop protection products has been satisfied prior to this harvest date. Mandatory compliance check under EPA pesticide registration requirements and FSMA produce safety rules.
reference: EPA; FDA FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Operational system of record from which this yield record was sourced. Enables data lineage tracking and reconciliation across John Deere Operations Center, Climate FieldView, Trimble Ag Software, and Granular Farm Management integrations.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Climate FieldView; Trimble Ag Software; Granular Farm Management

description: Industry-standard moisture basis used for moisture-adjusted yield calculation (e.g., 15.5% for corn, 13% for soybeans). Defined by USDA grain grading standards and used as the reference point for dry bushel equivalents.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS; Codex Alimentarius

description: Grain test weight measured in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu) at harvest. Key grain quality indicator used for USDA grade determination. Below-standard test weight may result in grade discounts at grain elevator. Sourced from combine sensors or grain elevator COA.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; FGIS; SAP S/4HANA QM

description: Total gross grain production in bushels (BU) recorded for this harvest pass at as-harvested moisture. Primary production quantity used for grain receipt (GRN) creation, sales contract fulfillment, and crop insurance documentation.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection; SAP S/4HANA MM; USDA RMA; Granular Farm Management

description: Total gross grain production converted to metric tons (MT) for international trade, export documentation, and financial reporting under IFRS. Derived from total_production_bu using commodity-specific conversion factors.
reference: Codex Alimentarius; IFRS; FAO; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Timestamp when this yield record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: ISO 22000; FSMA; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Actual harvested yield expressed as bushels per acre (BU/acre) for this harvest pass. Core agronomic performance metric (YPM) used for benchmarking against field crop plan targets, historical averages, and regional USDA NASS yield data.
reference: USDA NASS; USDA RMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; Granular Farm Management

description: Planned yield target in bushels per acre (BU/acre) from the associated crop plan for this field-season-variety combination. Used to compute yield variance and performance against plan for financial crop enterprise accounting.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA PP; USDA RMA

description: Difference between actual yield_per_acre and yield_target_bu_per_acre. Positive value indicates above-plan performance; negative value indicates shortfall. Used for financial crop enterprise accounting, crop insurance (USDA RMA APH), and agronomic benchmarking.
reference: USDA RMA; Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the cover crop planting record. Primary key for the cover_crop entity.
reference: FMIS internal identifier standard

description: Reference to the planting or seeding equipment used to establish the cover crop. Supports equipment utilization tracking, calibration records, and field operation cost allocation.
reference: John Deere Operations Center fleet telematics; SAP S/4HANA PM module

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the agricultural field on which this cover crop was planted. Links to the land and soil domain field master record.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard; USDA NRCS Field Identification

description: Foreign key linking to equipment.field_operation. Business justification: Cover crop planting and termination are executed as field operations. Linking enables equipment utilization tracking for cover crop activities, NRCS practice implementation documentation, and CSP paym
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the growing season during which this cover crop planting is associated. Typically the off-season or inter-season period between primary cash crop seasons.
reference: FMIS Season Management; Granular Farm Management

description: Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Cover crop biomass and soil health lab samples (soil organic matter, nitrogen credits, pathogen screening) are collected post-termination to validate NRCS practice compliance and nitrogen credit claim
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: Cover crops in certified organic systems must use organically produced seed and approved practices. organic_certification_id links the cover crop record to the NOP certification covering the field ope
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Self-referencing FK on cover_crop (parent_cover_crop_id)
Reference to the agronomist or crop advisor responsible for prescribing and overseeing this cover crop planting program.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; GAP (Good Agricultural Practices)

description: Reference to the crop rotation plan that prescribes this cover crop planting as part of a multi-season soil health or conservation strategy.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 340; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Reference to the seed lot used for this cover crop planting. Enables traceability from field planting back to seed origin, certification class, germination test results, and supplier.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA traceability requirements

description: Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Cover crop records reference seed_lot but variety selection for cover crops is made at planning stage before a specific lot is assigned. Linking to seed_variety supports cover crop performance analysi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to equipment.work_order. Business justification: Cover crop planting and termination operations require work orders for equipment scheduling and cost tracking. NRCS/CSP practice payment documentation requires linking cover crop field activities to a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Estimated above-ground dry biomass produced by the cover crop at termination, expressed in pounds per acre. Key indicator of soil organic matter contribution, nitrogen cycling potential, and weed suppression effectiveness.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; Sustainable Agriculture Research & Education (SARE)

description: Functional classification of the cover crop by plant family or agronomic category. Determines nitrogen fixation potential, biomass contribution, and soil health benefit. 'mix' indicates a multi-species blend.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 340; Sustainable Agriculture Research & Education (SARE)

description: Timestamp when this cover crop planting record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record provenance tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audit trail requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping

description: Number of calendar days between the cover crop planting date and the actual termination date. Indicates the length of the cover crop growing window and is used to assess biomass accumulation potential and program compliance.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; SARE cover crop management guidelines

description: Field-assessed qualitative rating of cover crop stand establishment success. Evaluated by agronomist or field scout based on germination uniformity, plant density, and canopy coverage. Informs replanting decisions.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the cover crop termination timing and method comply with FSMA Produce Safety Rule requirements for application of biological soil amendments. Particularly relevant for fields transitioning to fresh produce crops.
reference: FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule (21 CFR Part 112); GLOBALG.A.P. IFA

description: GeoJSON polygon representing the GPS-mapped boundary of the actual cover crop planted area within the field. Supports precision agriculture spatial analysis, as-applied mapping, and NRCS program acreage verification.
reference: Esri ArcGIS; John Deere Operations Center; Trimble Ag Software; USDA FSA CLU

description: Indicates whether this cover crop planting meets applicable Best Management Practices (BMP) for water quality, nutrient management, and erosion control as defined by state or federal environmental agencies.
reference: EPA BMP (Best Management Practices); USDA NRCS; State environmental agencies

business_glossary_term: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) at Termination
description: NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) value measured at or near the cover crop termination date. Provides an objective, remote-sensing-based indicator of cover crop biomass and canopy health at termination.
reference: Climate FieldView; John Deere Operations Center; NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) standard

description: Estimated plant-available nitrogen credit in pounds per acre attributed to legume cover crop nitrogen fixation or biomass decomposition. Used to adjust NPK fertilizer application rates for the subsequent cash crop season.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA Nutrient Management

description: Free-text field for agronomist or operator observations regarding this cover crop planting, including establishment challenges, weather impacts, pest pressure, or special management actions taken.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA record-keeping requirements; GAP (Good Agricultural Practices)

business_glossary_term: NRCS (Natural Resources Conservation Service) Practice Code
description: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service conservation practice standard code associated with this cover crop planting (e.g., '340' for Cover Crop). Required for EQIP, CSP, and other USDA conservation program payment documentation.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 340; EQIP; CSP

description: Indicates whether this cover crop planting is conducted under a certified organic production system in compliance with USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. Affects allowable termination methods and input restrictions.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P. Organic

description: Originally scheduled planting date for the cover crop as defined in the rotation plan or agronomic prescription. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual planting timing.
reference: Granular Farm Management; Trimble Ag Software field planning

description: Total area in acres on which the cover crop was successfully planted. Used for seed cost accounting, NRCS program payment calculations, and soil health program reporting.
reference: USDA NRCS CSP; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; Granular Farm Management

description: Actual calendar date on which the cover crop seed was planted or broadcast in the field. Used for growing degree day (GDD) accumulation tracking, establishment assessment, and program compliance verification.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; Climate FieldView planting records; USDA NRCS

description: Method used to establish the cover crop. Affects seed-to-soil contact, establishment success rate, and seeding rate requirements. 'interseeding' refers to planting into a standing cash crop canopy.
reference: USDA NRCS Plant Materials Program; Trimble Ag Software; Climate FieldView

description: Current lifecycle status of the cover crop planting record. Tracks progression from planning through establishment and termination. 'failed' indicates the cover crop did not establish adequately.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340

description: Agronomist-prescribed target seeding rate in pounds per acre as specified in the cover crop prescription or rotation plan. Enables variance analysis against actual seeding rate applied.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA prescriptions; USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340

description: Primary agronomic or conservation objective for planting this cover crop. Drives species selection, seeding rate, and termination timing decisions. Used in NRCS program reporting and CSP payment calculations.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Stewardship Program (CSP); BMP (Best Management Practices)

description: Human-readable, externally referenceable business identifier for this cover crop planting record. Used in field documentation, NRCS program submissions, and CSP contract reporting. Format: CC-{YEAR}-{SEQUENCE}.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Stewardship Program (CSP); FMIS record numbering

description: Total seed cost in US dollars for this cover crop planting, calculated from seed lot unit cost and quantity used. Used in enterprise budget tracking, NRCS program cost-share calculations, and crop enterprise profitability analysis.
reference: Granular Farm Management financial planning; SAP S/4HANA CO module; USDA EQIP cost-share

description: Actual seeding rate applied for the cover crop, expressed in pounds of seed per acre. Used to verify compliance with NRCS practice standards, calculate seed costs, and assess establishment adequacy.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA

description: Estimated percentage of soil surface covered by the cover crop canopy or residue at peak growth or at termination. Key metric for erosion control effectiveness and NRCS practice standard compliance verification.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA

description: Soil temperature in degrees Celsius measured at the time of cover crop planting. Critical for predicting germination success and establishment timing, particularly for cool-season cover crop species.
reference: Climate FieldView; USDA NRCS agronomy technical notes

description: Operational system of record from which this cover crop planting record originated. Supports data lineage, integration audit trails, and conflict resolution in the FMIS data lakehouse.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; SAP S/4HANA; Climate FieldView; Trimble Ag Software; Granular Farm Management

description: Common name or blend designation of the cover crop species or species mix planted (e.g., 'Winter Rye', 'Crimson Clover', 'Cereal Rye + Hairy Vetch Mix'). Supports soil health program documentation and seed procurement.
reference: USDA NRCS Plant Materials Program; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA

description: Actual date on which the cover crop was terminated (killed or incorporated). Critical for calculating days of cover, biomass accumulation, and ensuring adequate decomposition time before primary crop planting.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; FSMA Produce Safety Rule

description: Method used to terminate (kill) the cover crop prior to primary crop planting. Affects residue management, soil disturbance, and organic certification compliance. 'roller_crimper' is preferred in no-till and organic systems.
reference: USDA NRCS Practice Standard 340; National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Timestamp when this cover crop planting record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) audit trail requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping

description: Primary key for crop
Self-referencing FK on crop (parent_crop_id)
Historical average yield for this crop expressed in the yield_unit, used for crop planning and forecasting.
Timestamp when this crop master record was first created in the system.
High-level classification of the crop type based on agricultural use and market segment.
Standardized alphanumeric code used for crop identification in Farm Management Information Systems (FMIS) and regulatory reporting. Externally-known unique identifier.
Common name of the crop (e.g., Corn, Wheat, Soybeans, Cotton). Human-readable identifier used across all agronomic systems.
Current lifecycle status of the crop in the master reference system. Active crops are available for planting schedules and field assignments.
Classification based on the crop's lifecycle duration: annual (single season), perennial (multi-year), or biennial (two-year cycle).
General susceptibility of the crop to disease infections, used in IPM-based disease management strategies.
Crop's ability to withstand water stress and drought conditions, critical for irrigation planning.
Crop's ability to withstand frost conditions during growing season.
Indicates whether genetically modified varieties of this crop are available and approved for cultivation.
Primary season during which the crop is typically cultivated, used for crop planning and rotation scheduling.
Primary method used for harvesting the crop: manual labor, mechanical equipment, or combination.
Current market price per yield unit (e.g., price per bushel, price per kilogram), used for revenue forecasting and crop selection decisions.
Maximum number of days from planting to harvest maturity for late-maturing varieties of this crop.
Minimum number of days from planting to harvest maturity for early-maturing varieties of this crop.
Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) threshold value indicating healthy crop vigor, used for remote sensing and field health monitoring.
Recommended nitrogen fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield, used in Variable Rate Application (VRA) prescriptions.
Free-text field for additional agronomic notes, special cultivation requirements, or regional considerations for this crop.
Indicates whether this crop can be grown under organic certification standards.
General susceptibility of the crop to pest infestations, used in Integrated Pest Management (IPM) planning.
Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) - minimum number of days required between last pesticide application and harvest to ensure compliance with food safety regulations.
Recommended phosphorus fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield.
Recommended seed planting depth in centimeters for optimal germination and establishment.
Recommended potassium fertilizer application rate in kilograms per hectare for optimal yield.
Restricted Entry Interval (REI) - minimum number of hours workers must wait before entering treated fields after pesticide application, ensuring worker safety compliance.
Indicates whether this crop is suitable for inclusion in crop rotation schedules to maintain soil health and reduce pest pressure.
Recommended distance between crop rows in centimeters for optimal growth and equipment access.
Scientific botanical name of the crop using binomial nomenclature (e.g., Zea mays for corn, Triticum aestivum for wheat).
Recommended seeding rate in kilograms per hectare for standard planting density.
Maximum soil pH level tolerated by the crop for healthy growth.
Minimum soil pH level required for optimal crop growth and nutrient availability.
Maximum temperature in Celsius tolerated by the crop before heat stress occurs.
Minimum temperature in Celsius required for crop germination and growth.
Timestamp when this crop master record was last modified.
Total seasonal water requirement in millimeters, used for Evapotranspiration (ET)-driven irrigation planning.
Standard unit of measure for crop yield reporting (e.g., bushels per acre for corn, kilograms per hectare for wheat). Used in Yield Per Minute (YPM) calculations.
Unique surrogate identifier for the field crop plan record. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.

business_glossary_term: GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) Trait Used Flag
description: Indicates whether the selected crop variety contains one or more GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) traits. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling), and export documentation to GMO-restricted markets.
reference: USDA APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) Disclosure; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling); Codex Alimentarius

description: Type of irrigation infrastructure deployed on this field. Determines ET (Evapotranspiration)-based scheduling methodology, water application efficiency, and water allocation compliance reporting.
reference: USDA NRCS Irrigation Water Management; ET (Evapotranspiration) Scheduling Standards; EPA Water Quality Guidelines

description: Planned total irrigation water application depth in acre-inches for the season, derived from ET-deficit modeling and soil moisture trigger thresholds. Supports water allocation compliance and USDA NRCS irrigation water management standards.
reference: USDA NRCS Irrigation Water Management Practice Standard 449; ET (Evapotranspiration) Deficit Modeling

description: Pre-season NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) baseline value for the field, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Used to establish field health benchmarks for in-season monitoring and VRA prescription calibration. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS or Climate FieldView satellite imagery.
reference: NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index); Esri ArcGIS Remote Sensing; Climate FieldView Analytics

description: Minimum buffer distance in feet from water bodies, drainage ditches, and sensitive areas within which nutrient applications are restricted or prohibited. Defined per EPA nutrient management regulations and state BMP requirements. Captured in Esri ArcGIS environmental layer.
reference: EPA Nutrient Management Regulations; USDA NRCS 590 Practice Standard; BMP (Best Management Practices); Clean Water Act Section 319

business_glossary_term: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) Certified Flag
description: Indicates whether this crop plan is executed under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification, restricting inputs to approved organic materials and requiring organic system plan documentation.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; FSMA Produce Safety Rule

description: Minimum number of days that must elapse between the last pesticide application and harvest, as mandated by EPA pesticide label requirements. Critical for MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) compliance and food safety certification under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.
reference: EPA Pesticide Label Requirements; PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval); MRL (Maximum Residue Limit); FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 7.6

description: The business date on which this crop plan was formally created and entered into the Farm Management Information System (FMIS). Distinct from system audit timestamps.
reference: Granular Farm Management; FMIS (Farm Management Information System) Standards

description: Externally-known business identifier for the crop plan, used in Trimble Ag Software, Climate FieldView, and SAP S/4HANA PP module for cross-system reference and traceability. Format: FCP-{YEAR}-{ALPHANUMERIC}.
reference: Trimble Ag Software; SAP S/4HANA PP; GLOBALG.A.P. Record-Keeping Requirements

description: Current lifecycle state of the field crop plan. Draft: under preparation; Approved: signed off by agronomist; Active: in-season execution underway; Completed: season closed; Cancelled: plan voided; Superseded: replaced by a revised plan version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP Production Order Status; GLOBALG.A.P. AF 1.1

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for the crop plan. Incremented each time the plan is revised and re-approved, supporting audit trail and change management requirements under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.
reference: FSMA Preventive Controls Rule; GLOBALG.A.P. Record-Keeping

description: Total field area in acres designated for crop production under this plan. Used for input quantity calculations, yield projections, and USDA FSA acreage reporting. Sourced from Esri ArcGIS field boundary data.
reference: USDA FSA Acreage Reporting; Esri ArcGIS Field Boundary; GLOBALG.A.P. AF 1.2

description: Target calendar date for harvest operations to commence on this field. Drives downstream supply chain scheduling, storage capacity planning, and logistics coordination via SAP S/4HANA SD and TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP; Climate FieldView Harvest Analytics; TMS (Transportation Management System)

description: Target calendar date for planting operations to commence on this field, as defined in the crop plan. Used for scheduling labor, equipment, and input procurement in SAP S/4HANA PP and Trimble Ag Software.
reference: Trimble Ag Software Field Planning; SAP S/4HANA PP; Climate FieldView

description: Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated fields after pesticide application, as specified on EPA-registered pesticide labels. Required for OSHA worker protection standard compliance and GLOBALG.A.P. worker safety documentation.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS); REI (Restricted Entry Interval); OSHA 29 CFR 1928.110; GLOBALG.A.P. AF 4.1

description: Planned distance between crop rows in inches, as specified in the planting prescription. Affects canopy closure, weed suppression, and machinery configuration. Integrated with Trimble Ag Software VRA prescriptions.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA (Variable Rate Application) Prescription; Climate FieldView Planting Data

description: Date on which soil samples were collected for the baseline soil test underpinning this crop plan's nutrient management recommendations. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and EPA nutrient management compliance documentation.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 1.3; EPA Nutrient Management

business_glossary_term: Target Potassium (K) Application Rate (lbs per Acre)
description: Planned potassium (K) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, based on soil test K levels and crop removal rates. Part of the NPK nutrient management plan. Influences crop quality, disease resistance, and water use efficiency.
reference: EPA Nutrient Management Regulations; USDA NRCS 590 Nutrient Management Practice Standard; NPK

business_glossary_term: Target Nitrogen (N) Application Rate (lbs per Acre)
description: Planned nitrogen (N) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, derived from soil test results and yield goal. Core component of the NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium) nutrient management plan. Must comply with EPA nutrient management regulations and state BMP requirements.
reference: EPA Nutrient Management Regulations; USDA NRCS 590 Nutrient Management Practice Standard; NPK (Nitrogen Phosphorus Potassium); BMP

business_glossary_term: Target Phosphorus (P) Application Rate (lbs per Acre)
description: Planned phosphorus (P) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre, based on soil test P levels and crop removal rates. Part of the NPK nutrient management plan. Subject to EPA and state environmental setback zone restrictions.
reference: EPA Nutrient Management Regulations; USDA NRCS 590 Nutrient Management Practice Standard; NPK

description: Planned seeding rate expressed as seeds per acre. Drives seed quantity procurement, VRA prescription generation, and yield modeling. Sourced from Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView planting prescriptions.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA Prescription; Climate FieldView Planting Analytics; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 1.1

description: Planned soil tillage approach for land preparation prior to planting. Influences soil health, erosion risk, fuel consumption, and BMP compliance. Captured in Trimble Ag Software field planning module.
reference: USDA NRCS BMP (Best Management Practices) Standards; EPA Soil Erosion Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 1.2

description: System timestamp recording when this field crop plan record was most recently modified. Supports change management, version control, and audit trail requirements under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P.
reference: FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. Record-Keeping; SOX Internal Controls

description: Maximum permitted water volume in acre-feet that may be applied to this field during the season, as defined by water rights permits or irrigation district allocations. Enforced for regulatory compliance with state water authorities.
reference: State Water Rights Regulations; USDA NRCS Water Allocation Standards; EPA Clean Water Act

description: Expected crop yield expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre for the planned field and season. Serves as the primary agronomic performance benchmark for YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) reporting and WASDE comparison. Null if crop is measured in MT or CWT.
reference: USDA NASS Yield Benchmarks; WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates); YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric)

description: Expected crop yield expressed in MT (Metric Tons) per hectare, used for international reporting and crops not measured in bushels. Complements BU/acre target for global supply chain and export documentation.
reference: FAO Crop Production Statistics; Codex Alimentarius Commission; ISO 22000

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each growing season record. Serves as the primary key for the growing_season data product and is referenced by all crop production activity records (planting, cultivation, harvest, loss events) as the temporal anchor.
reference: FMIS internal identifier standard; Granular Farm Management season record

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: USDA FSA farm registration; Granular Farm Management land management; Esri ArcGIS farm boundary mapping

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual date on which the growing season was formally closed, representing completion of the final harvest event and post-harvest handling. Captured from Climate FieldView harvest data or SAP S/4HANA PP production order closure. Used for season-over-season performance benchmarking.
reference: Climate FieldView harvest analytics; SAP S/4HANA PP; Granular Farm Management

description: The actual date on which field operations for the growing season commenced (first planting event recorded). Captured from John Deere Operations Center telematics or Climate FieldView planting data. Used to measure planting timeliness against planned window and assess weather-related delays.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Climate FieldView planting data; USDA NASS planting progress reports

description: Realized yield for this growing season expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre, captured from harvest data in Climate FieldView or John Deere Operations Center yield monitors. Compared against target_yield_bu_acre for performance analysis. Used in USDA NASS yield surveys, crop insurance settlement, and FMIS season benchmarking.
reference: USDA NASS yield surveys; USDA RMA crop insurance; Climate FieldView harvest analytics; John Deere Operations Center

description: Timestamp when this growing season record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Establishes the audit trail for record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) financial reporting controls and GDPR data lineage requirements.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); ISO 22000 record-keeping

description: Type of USDA RMA crop insurance or farm program coverage elected for this growing season. APH = Actual Production History, RP = Revenue Protection, RP_HPE = Revenue Protection with Harvest Price Exclusion, YP = Yield Protection, ARC = Agriculture Risk Coverage, PLC = Price Loss Coverage. Determines indemnity calculation methodology and premium rates.
reference: USDA RMA (Risk Management Agency); USDA FSA ARC/PLC program; Federal Crop Insurance Act

description: The marketing or production year associated with this growing season, expressed as a four-digit calendar year (e.g., 2024). Used for WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates) alignment, USDA FSA program reporting, and financial crop enterprise budget year-over-year comparisons.
reference: USDA WASDE; USDA FSA; USDA NASS crop year definition

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Compliance Flag
description: Indicates whether this growing season's production activities are documented and compliant with FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Produce Safety Rule requirements, including water testing, worker hygiene, and equipment sanitation records. Mandatory for covered produce operations under FDA jurisdiction.
reference: FDA FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Produce Safety Rule; FDA 21 CFR Part 112

description: Current GAP (Good Agricultural Practices) certification status for this growing season. Indicates whether the production practices meet USDA GAP audit standards, GLOBALG.A.P. certification requirements, or buyer-mandated food safety standards (SQF, BRC, IFS). Required for retail and food manufacturer customer contracts.
reference: USDA GAP (Good Agricultural Practices) audit program; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF (Safe Quality Food); BRC (British Retail Consortium)

description: Target cumulative GDD (Growing Degree Days) required for the primary crop variety to reach maturity during this growing season. Used to project harvest timing, align PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance windows, and calibrate ET (Evapotranspiration)-based irrigation scheduling. Sourced from seed company variety data and NOAA climate records.
reference: NOAA climate data; USDA crop maturity GDD tables; Climate FieldView GDD analytics

description: Reference code identifying the IPM (Integrated Pest Management) program or protocol applied during this growing season. Links to the crop protection plan governing pesticide applications, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) monitoring, and EPA pesticide registration requirements. Aligns with Trimble Ag Software scouting protocols.
reference: EPA IPM (Integrated Pest Management) guidelines; USDA APHIS; GLOBALG.A.P. crop protection module; MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) compliance

description: Primary irrigation system used during this growing season. Determines ET (Evapotranspiration)-based water scheduling approach, water use efficiency reporting, and compliance with state water rights regulations. 'rainfed' indicates no supplemental irrigation. Integrated with IoT (Internet of Things) soil moisture sensors and FMIS irrigation scheduling modules.
reference: EPA water use regulations; USDA NRCS irrigation water management; ET (Evapotranspiration) scheduling standards

description: Indicates whether the target crop variety for this season is a GMO (Genetically Modified Organism). Critical for USDA APHIS (Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service) regulatory compliance, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) requirements, export documentation, and customer segregation requirements for non-GMO supply chains.
reference: USDA APHIS GMO regulations; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling); GLOBALG.A.P. GMO traceability

description: Indicates whether this growing season is conducted under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified organic production standards. Triggers organic-specific input restrictions, record-keeping requirements, and supply chain segregation protocols. Linked to organic certificate management in compliance systems.
reference: USDA NOP (National Organic Program); USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; GLOBALG.A.P. organic module

business_glossary_term: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) Baseline Score
description: NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) baseline score established at the start of the growing season, ranging from -1.0 to 1.0. Derived from satellite or drone imagery processed through Esri ArcGIS or Climate FieldView. Used as the reference benchmark for in-season crop health monitoring, stress detection, and VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription adjustments.
reference: Esri ArcGIS remote sensing; Climate FieldView NDVI analytics; USDA NASS crop condition monitoring

description: Free-text field for agronomic observations, management decisions, weather events, or operational notes relevant to this growing season. Captures qualitative context not represented in structured fields, such as drought stress events, pest outbreak narratives, or regulatory inspection outcomes. Supports FMIS audit trail documentation.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping requirements; FSMA documentation standards; Granular Farm Management notes

description: Minimum number of days required between the last pesticide application and harvest for this growing season's primary crop, as mandated by EPA pesticide label requirements (PHI — Pre-Harvest Interval). Critical for food safety compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) adherence, and FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) produce safety rule compliance.
reference: EPA pesticide label PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) requirements; FSMA Produce Safety Rule; FDA MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) standards

description: The agronomically planned end date for the growing season, representing the expected completion of harvest and post-harvest handling operations. Derived from expected days-to-maturity of the target crop variety and planned planting date. Used for financial crop enterprise budget planning in Granular Farm Management.
reference: Granular Farm Management financial planning; Climate FieldView harvest analytics; USDA crop maturity data

description: The agronomically planned start date for the growing season, typically aligned with the target planting window based on GDD (Growing Degree Days), soil temperature thresholds, and historical climate data. Used in Trimble Ag Software field planning and Climate FieldView planting analytics.
reference: Climate FieldView planting analytics; Trimble Ag Software field planning; USDA crop calendar

description: Tillage and planting system employed during this growing season. Drives equipment configuration in John Deere Operations Center, soil disturbance impact on OM (Organic Matter) and EC (Electrical Conductivity) measurements, and BMP (Best Management Practices) compliance reporting for USDA EQIP (Environmental Quality Incentives Program) conservation programs.
reference: USDA EQIP BMP standards; GLOBALG.A.P. soil management; Trimble Ag Software field planning

description: Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated fields after pesticide application during this growing season, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (REI — Restricted Entry Interval). Drives workforce scheduling in SAP SuccessFactors and OSHA compliance documentation for field worker safety.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard REI (Restricted Entry Interval); OSHA agricultural worker safety; FSMA

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the growing season, used across FMIS systems such as Climate FieldView and Granular Farm Management for cross-system reference. Example: 'SPRING-2024-CORN', 'FALL-2024-SOY'. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA PP production order season coding conventions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP module; Climate FieldView season management; Granular Farm Management

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the growing season, combining season type, year, and primary crop for operational clarity. Example: 'Spring 2024 Corn', 'Fall 2024 Soybean', 'Double-Crop 2024 Winter Wheat'. Used in reporting dashboards and FMIS displays.
reference: Granular Farm Management; Climate FieldView; USDA NASS crop season terminology

description: Current lifecycle state of the growing season. 'planned' indicates agronomic planning is underway but field operations have not commenced. 'active' indicates planting through harvest operations are in progress. 'completed' indicates harvest and post-harvest handling are finalized. 'cancelled' indicates the season was abandoned (e.g., due to extreme weather or market conditions). 'suspended' indicates temporary halt due to regulatory or operational constraints.
reference: Granular Farm Management season lifecycle; SAP S/4HANA PP production order status

description: Classification of the growing season by its agronomic timing and production strategy. 'double_crop' supports sequential planting common in southern US and tropical operations. 'cover_crop' denotes non-cash-crop seasons for soil health management per BMP (Best Management Practices). Drives planting window scheduling and FMIS season templates.
reference: USDA NASS; GLOBALG.A.P. crop calendar standards; GAP (Good Agricultural Practices)

description: Specific variety or hybrid designation of the primary crop planned for this season (e.g., 'DKC62-08RIB', 'AG36X6'). Drives seed procurement, VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescriptions, and yield benchmarking by variety. Aligns with seed catalog codes in SAP S/4HANA MM and Trimble Ag Software variety trials.
reference: Trimble Ag Software variety management; Climate FieldView seed performance analytics; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Agronomically planned yield target for this growing season expressed in BU (Bushels) per acre, representing the YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) goal. Established during crop planning based on historical yield data, soil productivity ratings, and variety performance benchmarks. Used as the baseline for financial crop enterprise budgets and crop insurance APH (Actual Production History).
reference: USDA RMA APH (Actual Production History); Granular Farm Management financial planning; WASDE yield benchmarks

description: Total land area actually planted during this growing season, expressed in acres. May differ from planned area due to weather delays, equipment failures, or agronomic decisions. Captured from John Deere Operations Center planting telematics and Climate FieldView as-planted maps. Used for yield calculation (YPM) and insurance reporting.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Climate FieldView as-planted data; USDA RMA crop insurance

description: Total land area planned for production under this growing season, expressed in acres. Aggregated from associated field zone assignments. Used for input planning (seed, fertilizer, crop protection), equipment scheduling, and financial crop enterprise budget calculations in Granular Farm Management. Aligns with FSA farm records.
reference: USDA FSA farm records; Granular Farm Management land management; Esri ArcGIS field mapping

description: Total harvested production volume for the growing season expressed in MT (Metric Tons), converted from bushel-based yield for international trade, export documentation, and supply chain logistics. Used in BOL (Bill of Lading) preparation, CIF/FOB export contracts, and WASDE supply estimates.
reference: WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates); Codex Alimentarius; USDA FAS export reporting

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this growing season record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline, optimistic concurrency control, and audit trail maintenance per SOX and GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping requirements.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); GDPR Article 5; GLOBALG.A.P. record-keeping; ISO 22000
