description: Current lifecycle state of the rotation plan. 'draft' = under development; 'active' = currently being executed; 'suspended' = temporarily paused; 'completed' = all rotation cycles finished; 'cancelled' = plan abandoned.
reference: Granular Farm Management; USDA NRCS CSP Compliance Framework

description: Version number of the rotation plan, incremented each time the plan is revised. Supports audit trail and change management for NRCS and GAP compliance documentation.
reference: USDA NRCS CSP; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Total field area in acres covered by this rotation plan. Used for input planning (seed, fertilizer, chemical), yield target setting, and NRCS CSP payment calculation.
reference: USDA FSA; USDA NRCS CSP; Granular Farm Management

description: The principal agronomic objective driving this rotation plan design (e.g., 'Soil organic matter improvement', 'Soybean cyst nematode suppression', 'Nitrogen fixation via legume phase', 'Weed resistance management'). Supports NRCS CSP documentation and GAP rationale.
reference: USDA NRCS CSP; GAP Standards; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Total number of years in one complete rotation cycle (e.g., 2 for corn-soybean, 3 for corn-soybean-wheat). Defines the repeating sequence length for soil health and pest cycle management.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 328 (Conservation Crop Rotation); GAP Standards

description: Classification of the rotation plan type. 'simple' = two-crop alternation; 'complex' = three or more crops; 'cover_crop' = includes dedicated cover crop phases; 'organic' = certified organic rotation; 'conventional' = standard agrochemical-supported rotation.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 328; National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Baseline composite soil health score for the field at the time of plan creation, derived from soil organic matter (OM), biological activity, and physical structure assessments. Supports NRCS CSP soil health practice documentation.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Health Assessment; GLOBALG.A.P.; GAP Standards

description: Baseline soil organic matter (OM) percentage at plan initiation, expressed as a percentage of dry soil weight. Key indicator for rotation plan design and NRCS CSP soil health improvement targets.
reference: USDA NRCS; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

business_glossary_term: Target Soil Organic Matter Percentage (Target OM %)
description: Target soil organic matter (OM) percentage to be achieved by the end of the rotation plan period. Defines the agronomic improvement goal and supports NRCS CSP outcome measurement.
reference: USDA NRCS CSP; GLOBALG.A.P.; GAP Standards

description: Tillage system applied across this rotation plan, governing soil disturbance practices. Influences soil organic matter (OM) accumulation, erosion risk, and NRCS conservation compliance scoring.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standard 345 (Residue and Tillage Management); GAP; BMP Standards

description: Timestamp when this rotation plan record was last modified. Supports change management, version control, and NRCS CSP audit trail requirements.
reference: ISO 22000; GLOBALG.A.P.; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each seed lot master record. Primary key for the seed_lot data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) Seed Regulatory Program; AOSCA (Association of Official Seed Certifying Agencies)

description: Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Seed lots require COAs documenting germination rate, purity, seed treatment active ingredients, and organic certification. seed_lot.coa_number is a denormalized reference. Normalizing to FK supports s
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: A seed lot is a physical batch of seed for a specific crop species. The crop_species column (STRING) is a denormalized free-text field that duplicates the crop master's crop_name and scientific_name.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.delivery_schedule. Business justification: Seed lots arrive per specific delivery schedule lines. Linking enables procurement-to-planting chain of custody traceability, on-time delivery performance measurement, and confirmation that the correc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: Seed lots have is_gmo and gmo_trait_stack attributes but no FK to the formal GMO declaration. Identity preservation programs, USDA BE disclosure, and export market approvals (EU, Japan, China) require
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Seed lot germination, purity, and pathogen screening tests generate lab_sample records that feed into the COA. seed_lot links to certificate_of_analysis but not to the originating lab_sample; direct l
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Seed lots must be reconciled against material master records for ERP inventory valuation, reorder planning, and procurement cost accounting. Ag ERP systems (SAP) require seed lot → material master lin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: NOP regulations require organic operations to use organically produced seed when commercially available. Linking seed_lot to organic_certification enables NOP compliance verification that certified or
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Seed lot traceability to the originating PO is required for GlobalG.A.P. and FSMA compliance. Replaces denormalized purchase_order_number plain text attribute with a proper FK enabling full procuremen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the crop variety master record associated with this seed lot, linking the lot to its specific genetic variety, species, and crop type classification.
reference: USDA Plant Variety Protection (PVP) Act; AOSCA Variety Certification Standards

description: Reference to the physical storage location (warehouse bin, cold storage unit, or seed storage facility) where this seed lot is currently stored. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM storage location (LGORT) and Manhattan Associates WMS location master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Warehouse Management; Manhattan Associates WMS; USDA Seed Storage Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Seed lots are procured under vendor contracts specifying GMO status, organic certification, pricing, and quality terms. Linking seed_lot to vendor_contract enables contract compliance verification at
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the seed supplier or vendor who provided this seed lot. Links to the supplier master for procurement traceability and vendor performance management. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM vendor master (LFA1).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; FSMA Traceability Rule; USDA AMS Seed Regulatory Program

description: AOSCA-defined seed certification class indicating the generation and purity level of the seed lot. 'breeder' = source seed from plant breeder; 'foundation' = first generation from breeder seed; 'registered' = second generation; 'certified' = third generation approved for commercial use; 'uncertified' = not certified.
reference: AOSCA (Association of Official Seed Certifying Agencies) Seed Certification Standards; USDA AMS Seed Regulatory Program

description: Timestamp when this seed lot record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 8601

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit_cost of this seed lot (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'BRL'). Required for multi-currency financial reporting under IFRS and GAAP, and for SAP S/4HANA FI currency management.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 (Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates); SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Management

description: Date after which the germination rate certification is no longer valid and retesting is required per USDA Federal Seed Act regulations. Drives lot status transitions to 'expired' and triggers procurement or retesting workflows.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); AOSA Rules for Testing Seeds; AOSCA Seed Certification Standards

description: Percentage of seeds in this lot that germinated under standard test conditions, as reported on the seed tag or Certificate of Analysis (COA). Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Critical for seeding rate calculations, yield forecasting (YPM), and USDA Federal Seed Act minimum germination standards compliance.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); AOSA (Association of Official Seed Analysts) Rules for Testing Seeds; AOSCA Seed Certification Standards

description: Date on which the germination test was conducted for this seed lot, as reported on the seed tag or COA (Certificate of Analysis). Used to determine germination validity period and compliance with USDA Federal Seed Act labeling requirements.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); AOSA Rules for Testing Seeds; AOSCA Seed Certification Standards

description: Identifier or description of the GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) trait stack present in this seed lot (e.g., 'RR2Y/YGCB', 'Roundup Ready 2 Yield + YieldGard Corn Borer'). NULL if non-GMO. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and customer GMO disclosure requirements.
reference: USDA APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services (BRS); USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS); EPA Biopesticide Registration

description: Indicates whether this seed lot contains genetically modified organisms (GMO). True = GMO seed; False = non-GMO seed. Required for USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard compliance, customer labeling, and export documentation.
reference: USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS); USDA APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; Codex Alimentarius

description: Indicates whether this seed lot is certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = NOP-certified organic seed; False = conventional seed. Required for organic crop production compliance and NOP audit trails.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; IFOAM Organic Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric lot number (LOT) assigned by the seed supplier or internal seed processing facility. Used for seed traceability from procurement through planting to harvest, and for FSMA seed traceability compliance. Maps to SAP S/4HANA MM batch number (CHARG) and Granular Farm Management lot tracking.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); USDA AOSCA Seed Certification Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the seed lot. 'available' = in storage and ready for use; 'reserved' = allocated to a planting plan; 'planted' = fully consumed in field operations; 'depleted' = quantity exhausted; 'quarantined' = held pending quality or regulatory review; 'expired' = past germination validity date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Status Management; USDA AOSCA Seed Certification Standards

description: Moisture content of the seed lot expressed as a percentage at time of testing or receipt. Critical for safe storage, preventing mold and mycotoxin development, and maintaining germination viability. Monitored per USDA grain storage standards.
reference: USDA Federal Grain Inspection Service (FGIS); USDA AMS Grain Grading Standards; ISO 6540 (Maize moisture content determination)

description: Indicates whether this seed lot has been certified as free of noxious weed seeds as defined by USDA Federal Seed Act and applicable state seed laws. True = certified noxious weed free; False = noxious weed seeds detected or certification not obtained. Required for interstate commerce compliance.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); USDA APHIS Noxious Weed Program; State Seed Laws

description: Percentage of pure seed (by weight) in this lot as determined by seed analysis, excluding inert matter, other crop seed, and weed seed. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Required for USDA Federal Seed Act labeling compliance and AOSCA certification standards.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); AOSA Rules for Testing Seeds; AOSCA Seed Certification Standards

description: Current remaining quantity of seed in this lot available for planting, expressed in the unit_of_measure. Updated as seed is allocated to planting operations. Supports inventory planning, seeding rate calculations, and procurement replenishment decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Management; Manhattan Associates WMS; Granular Farm Management Inventory

description: Total quantity of seed in this lot expressed in the applicable unit of measure (seeds, bags, CWT, or BU as defined in unit_of_measure). Tracks available inventory for planting planning, procurement, and WMS (Warehouse Management System) inventory control in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Management; Manhattan Associates WMS; USDA AMS Seed Regulatory Program

description: Date on which this seed lot was physically received at the storage facility. Marks the start of the lot's custody chain for FSMA traceability, FIFO inventory management, and storage duration tracking.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt; USDA GAP Standards

description: Relative maturity rating of the crop variety in this seed lot, expressed in days or heat units (e.g., 95-day corn, 3.8 maturity group soybean). Critical for crop planning, harvest scheduling, and matching variety to growing season length in the FMIS.
reference: USDA NRCS Crop Maturity Guidelines; Climate FieldView Planting Analytics; Granular Farm Management Crop Planning

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this seed lot was produced or conditioned. Required for USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, import/export documentation, and APHIS phytosanitary certification.
reference: USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) 7 CFR Part 60; USDA APHIS Phytosanitary Certification; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3

description: Supplier-recommended seeding rate for this seed lot expressed in seeds or units per acre. Used in VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView, and for planting plan calculations in FMIS.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA Prescriptions; Climate FieldView Planting Analytics; USDA GAP Standards

description: Commercial name of the seed treatment product applied to this lot (e.g., 'Cruiser Maxx', 'Poncho/VOTiVO', 'Apron XL'). Required for EPA FIFRA compliance, SDS (Safety Data Sheet) reference, and agronomic record-keeping under GAP standards.
reference: EPA Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA); EPA Pesticide Registration; USDA GAP (Good Agricultural Practices)

description: Type of chemical or biological coating applied to the seed lot prior to packaging. 'fungicide' = fungicide-only coating; 'insecticide' = insecticide-only coating; 'fungicide_insecticide' = combined coating; 'biological' = biological agent coating; 'untreated' = no treatment applied. Required for EPA pesticide registration compliance, REI (Restricted Entry Interval) tracking, and PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) management.
reference: EPA Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA); EPA Pesticide Registration; USDA AMS Seed Regulatory Program

description: Test weight of the seed lot expressed in pounds per bushel (BU), indicating seed density and quality. Used for quality grading, storage planning, and yield estimation. Aligns with USDA grain grading standards.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) Grain Grading Standards; USDA Federal Grain Inspection Service (FGIS)

description: Weight of 1,000 seeds in grams (TSW), used to calculate seeding rates by weight, estimate seed counts per bag, and assess seed size uniformity. Standard agronomic quality metric used in FMIS and precision planting systems.
reference: ISTA (International Seed Testing Association) Rules for Seed Testing; AOSA Rules for Testing Seeds; ISO 520 (Cereals and pulses - determination of the mass of 1000 grains)

description: Active Ingredient (AI) of the seed treatment applied to this lot (e.g., 'thiamethoxam', 'fludioxonil', 'metalaxyl'). Required for EPA FIFRA compliance, MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) tracking, and food safety regulatory reporting.
reference: EPA Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA); Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards; USDA AMS Pesticide Data Program

description: Date on which the seed treatment was applied to this lot. Used to calculate treatment age, validate treatment efficacy windows, and support EPA FIFRA compliance and PHI/REI tracking.
reference: EPA Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA); USDA GAP Standards; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Procurement cost per unit of measure for this seed lot (in USD), as recorded at time of purchase. Used for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, crop enterprise budgeting, CAPEX/OPEX reporting, and financial planning in SAP S/4HANA CO and Granular Farm Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/CO Material Valuation; Granular Farm Management Financial Planning; GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles); IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories)

description: Unit of measure for the seed lot quantity (e.g., 'seeds' for precision count, 'bags' for packaged units, 'CWT' for hundredweight, 'BU' for bushels, 'kg' or 'lbs' for weight-based). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM base unit of measure (MEINS) and USDA commodity reporting standards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; USDA AMS Commodity Reporting; NIST Handbook 44

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this seed lot record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance for FSMA and SOX requirements.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 8601

description: Seed vigor test score indicating the potential for rapid, uniform emergence under field stress conditions, expressed as a percentage or index (0.00–100.00). Complements germination rate for predicting field performance. Tested per AOSA/ISTA vigor testing protocols.
reference: AOSA (Association of Official Seed Analysts) Seed Vigor Testing Handbook; ISTA Rules for Seed Testing; USDA Federal Seed Act

description: Percentage of weed seed (by weight) found in this seed lot during purity analysis. Must meet USDA Federal Seed Act and state seed law maximum thresholds. High weed seed content may trigger quarantine or rejection.
reference: USDA Federal Seed Act (7 U.S.C. 1551-1611); AOSA Rules for Testing Seeds; State Seed Laws

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each agricultural equipment or fleet asset record in the master registry. Primary key for the asset data product.
reference: ISO 55000:2014 Asset Management

description: Foreign key reference to the asset class/category reference product defining the equipment classification hierarchy (e.g., tractor, combine harvester, planter, sprayer, irrigation system, precision agriculture hardware).
reference: ISO 55000:2014 Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.capital_project. Business justification: Equipment acquisitions in agriculture are funded through approved CAPEX projects (e.g., combine fleet renewal, precision ag hardware rollout). Linking asset to its funding capital_project enables CAPE
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In multi-entity agriculture operations (holding company with subsidiary farms), each asset must be assigned to a legal entity (company code) for statutory reporting, insurance, and intercompany asset
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to finance.cost_center

Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Assets such as grain augers, conveyors, and forklifts are permanently stationed at supply facilities (grain elevators, packing houses). Asset management and maintenance routing requires knowing the ho
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to land.farm_operation. Business justification: Farm operation-level fleet inventory, depreciation scheduling, and capital asset reporting are standard agricultural accounting requirements. The farm_operation is the legal entity that owns equipment
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the farm or agricultural operation to which this asset is primarily assigned for operational and cost allocation purposes.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA CO Module

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Farm equipment (tractors, combines, irrigation systems) are capitalized on the fixed asset register. This link enables asset register reconciliation, depreciation tracking (MACRS/Section 179), and GAA
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.loan. Business justification: Farm equipment is routinely financed via FSA or commercial loans. Linking asset to the financing loan supports collateral tracking, loan covenant compliance monitoring, and asset disposal decisions th
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: Fixed assets (irrigation pivots, grain bins, buildings) are legally attached to a specific land parcel for property tax assessment, title records, and insurance purposes. Fixed asset registers in agri
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Agriculture assets (tractors, combines, irrigation systems) are assigned to profit centers (grain, livestock, horticulture) for segment profitability reporting and IFRS 8 segment disclosure. A domain
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Original purchase price or total capitalized cost of the asset at acquisition, representing the Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) investment. Includes purchase price, freight, installation, and commissioning costs. Denominated in USD. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/AM.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX compliance

description: Date on which the asset was acquired (purchased, leased, or transferred into the fleet). Marks the start of the asset lifecycle for depreciation, warranty, and compliance tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/AM module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16

description: Human-readable descriptive name or designation for the asset (e.g., 'John Deere 8R 410 Tractor - North Field Unit 3'). Used for operational identification and reporting.
reference: ISO 55000:2014 Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Date on which the asset was formally placed into service and became operational. May differ from acquisition date if installation, calibration, or certification was required prior to first use. Triggers start of depreciation in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014; GAAP ASC 360

description: Current net book value of the asset after accumulated depreciation, calculated as acquisition cost minus total accumulated depreciation to date. Used for financial reporting, insurance valuation, and disposal decisions. Denominated in USD.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI/AM Module; SOX compliance

description: Date on which the asset was formally retired from service and decommissioned. Marks the end of the operational lifecycle for depreciation cessation, disposal processing, and regulatory reporting. Null if asset is still active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014; GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16

description: Accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation of the asset. Straight-line spreads cost evenly; declining balance accelerates early depreciation; units of production ties depreciation to actual usage hours. Configured in SAP S/4HANA FI/AM.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Cumulative total engine or operating hours recorded on the asset since manufacture or last major overhaul. Primary usage metric for maintenance scheduling (e.g., 250-hour, 500-hour service intervals), depreciation under units-of-production method, and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014; OEE standard

description: Rated engine power output of the asset in kilowatts (kW) as specified by the manufacturer. Used for equipment capability matching to field operations, fuel consumption benchmarking, and EPA emissions classification.
reference: EPA Tier 4 Final Emissions Standards; SAE J1349 engine power standard; John Deere Operations Center

description: Type of fuel or energy source used by the asset. Relevant for fuel cost allocation, emissions reporting (EPA Tier 4 Final compliance), and sustainability tracking under ISCC and ISO 14001 environmental management standards.
reference: EPA Tier 4 Final Emissions Standards; ISO 14001; ISCC sustainability certification

description: Indicates whether the asset is equipped with a Global Positioning System (GPS) / Real-Time Kinematic (RTK) positioning receiver for precision agriculture operations including Variable Rate Application (VRA), auto-steer, and field mapping. True = RTK-capable; False = no precision positioning hardware.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Trimble Ag Software; ISO 17894 (GPS for agriculture)

description: Date on which the current insurance policy for the asset expires. Triggers renewal workflow to ensure continuous coverage and compliance with financing covenants and regulatory requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; USDA Risk Management Agency (RMA)

description: Reference number of the insurance policy covering this asset against loss, damage, or liability. Used for claims processing, risk management reporting, and compliance with lender insurance requirements for financed equipment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; USDA Risk Management Agency (RMA)

description: Date of the most recent formal safety or regulatory inspection performed on the asset. Used to track compliance with OSHA equipment safety standards, GLOBALG.A.P. equipment hygiene requirements, and internal audit schedules.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1928 (Agricultural Operations); GLOBALG.A.P.; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Current lifecycle stage of the asset per ISO 55000 asset management framework. Drives maintenance scheduling, capital planning, and disposal workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acquisition|commissioning|operational|standby|maintenance|decommissioned|disposed — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 55000:2014 Asset Management Standard

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) of the asset (e.g., John Deere, Case IH, AGCO, Kubota, Trimble). Used for vendor management, parts sourcing, and warranty claims.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; John Deere Operations Center

description: Manufacturer model designation of the asset (e.g., '8R 410', 'X9 1100', 'R4045'). Combined with make and year to uniquely identify the equipment specification for parts compatibility and performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; John Deere Operations Center

description: Manufacturer model year of the asset (e.g., 2022). Used for depreciation scheduling, technology generation classification, and compliance with emissions standards (EPA Tier 4 Final).
reference: EPA Tier 4 Final Emissions Standards; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Date on which the next preventive maintenance (PM) service is scheduled for the asset based on the Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) schedule. Derived from last service date plus service interval. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA PM module maintenance plans.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014; John Deere Operations Center maintenance alerts

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special configuration details, known defects, or other relevant information about the asset that does not fit structured fields. Used by fleet managers and maintenance technicians for contextual documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014

description: Cumulative distance traveled by the asset in kilometers, applicable to self-propelled vehicles (tractors, combines, sprayers, transport vehicles). Used for road-transport maintenance scheduling and fleet management. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center or vehicle ECU. Null for stationary or non-motorized equipment.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Current operational readiness status of the asset indicating whether it is available for field deployment. Active = deployed and operational; Standby = available but not deployed; Under Repair = in maintenance; Out of Service = unavailable; Decommissioned = retired from service.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014; John Deere Operations Center

description: Classification of the asset's ownership or financing arrangement. Owned = outright purchase (CAPEX); Leased = finance or operating lease (OPEX/IFRS 16); Rented = short-term hire; Contract Hire = long-term managed service. Drives financial treatment under GAAP/IFRS 16.
reference: IFRS 16 Leases; GAAP ASC 842; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Timestamp when the asset master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and SOX financial reporting controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); ISO 55000:2014

description: Timestamp when the asset master record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and Silver Layer incremental load processing in the Databricks Lakehouse. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; GDPR Article 5; ISO 55000:2014; Databricks Delta Lake

description: Date on which the current government or regulatory registration for the asset expires. Triggers renewal workflow to maintain legal compliance for road use and regulatory inspections.
reference: USDA APHIS; State DMV; OSHA

description: Government or regulatory authority registration number for the asset where required (e.g., state DMV registration for road-going vehicles, USDA/APHIS equipment registration). Null for non-registered implements.
reference: USDA APHIS; State Department of Motor Vehicles; OSHA equipment registration requirements

description: Estimated residual or salvage value of the asset at the end of its useful life. Used as the basis for depreciation calculations under straight-line and declining balance methods. Denominated in USD.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI/AM Module

description: Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number for the equipment unit. Used for warranty tracking, recall management, and manufacturer service records. Key identifier in John Deere Operations Center.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; John Deere Operations Center; USDA APHIS equipment registration

description: Internally assigned physical asset tag or barcode label affixed to the equipment for inventory tracking and identification during audits and maintenance. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA PM asset tag field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; ISO 55000:2014

business_glossary_term: Telematics Device ID (John Deere Operations Center)
description: Unique identifier of the telematics device or machine connectivity unit installed on the asset and registered in the John Deere Operations Center platform. Enables real-time GPS tracking, remote diagnostics, fuel consumption monitoring, and field data synchronization.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; ISO 15143-3 (AEMP Telematics Data Standard)

description: Estimated useful economic life of the asset in years, used as the basis for straight-line depreciation calculations and capital replacement planning. Defined at acquisition per GAAP/IFRS guidelines.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI/AM Module

description: 17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned to motorized agricultural vehicles (tractors, combines, self-propelled sprayers) per ISO 3779 standard. Null for non-motorized implements.
reference: ISO 3779:2009 Vehicle Identification Number; USDA APHIS

business_glossary_term: Variable Rate Application (VRA) Controller Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the asset is equipped with a Variable Rate Application (VRA) controller enabling prescription-based variable rate seeding, fertilization, or chemical application. Supports precision agriculture workflows in Trimble Ag Software and Climate FieldView.
reference: Trimble Ag Software; Climate FieldView; EPA VRA compliance; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Date on which the manufacturer or dealer warranty for the asset expires. Used to trigger preventive maintenance scheduling under warranty terms and to determine whether repair costs are chargeable to the vendor or internal Operational Expenditure (OPEX) budget.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; John Deere Operations Center warranty tracking

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment asset assignment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the asset_assignment data product.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Reference to the specific agricultural equipment or fleet asset being assigned (e.g., tractor, combine, planter, sprayer, irrigation system). Links to the equipment master record.
reference: ISO 55000 Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: ISO 22400 OEE Standard; John Deere Operations Center Fleet Analytics; SEMI E10

description: Indicates whether operating assets in this category requires a specific operator license, certification, or training qualification (e.g., CDL for transport vehicles, pesticide applicator license for sprayers, OSHA-mandated forklift certification for material handling equipment). description: Indicates whether assets in this category support precision agriculture hardware integration, including RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) GPS receivers, VRA (Variable Rate Application) controllers, NDVI sensors, or FMIS (Farm Management Information System) data connectivity. reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Classification of the inspector who conducted the inspection. reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; DOT FMCSA; EPA 40 CFR

description: Number of individual checklist items that received a fail evaluation during the inspection. A non-zero value triggers deficiency documentation and corrective action requirements.
reference: ISO 55000; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Number of individual checklist items that received a pass evaluation during the inspection. Used in conjunction with checklist_item_count and items_failed_count to assess inspection completeness and asset condition.
reference: ISO 55000; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: The calculated or manually assigned date for the next scheduled inspection of this asset, based on regulatory intervals, OEM recommendations, or the preventive maintenance plan. Drives the forward maintenance calendar.
reference: ISO 55000; SAP S/4HANA PM Module; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

business_glossary_term: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) Compliance Verified Flag
description: Indicates whether OSHA occupational safety requirements were specifically verified as part of this inspection. Supports OSHA compliance reporting and audit readiness under 29 CFR 1910 and 29 CFR 1928 (agricultural operations).
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; OSHA 29 CFR 1928

description: The aggregate pass/fail determination for the entire inspection. Pass indicates all checklist items met required standards; fail indicates one or more critical items did not meet standards; conditional_pass indicates minor deficiencies noted but asset is operationally cleared with corrective actions required; incomplete indicates the inspection was not fully executed.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 55000; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether a regulatory compliance certificate (e.g., OSHA safety certificate, DOT roadworthiness certificate, EPA emissions certificate) was issued as a result of this inspection passing.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; DOT FMCSA; EPA 40 CFR

description: The date by which a follow-up re-inspection must be completed to verify that corrective actions have been implemented and deficiencies resolved. Mandatory when overall_result is fail or conditional_pass.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 55000; DOT FMCSA

description: The planned calendar date on which the inspection was scheduled to occur, as defined in the preventive maintenance plan or regulatory compliance calendar.

description: The date and time when the inspection record was most recently modified. Supports data lineage tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Unit of measure for the maintenance cycle interval. 'hours' refers to engine/operating hours from John Deere Operations Center telematics; 'acres'/'hectares' refer to field coverage metrics; calendar units support time-based strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours|days|weeks|months|years|acres|hectares|kilometers — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

reference: SAP PM Module; OEM Service Manuals; ISO 55000

description: Calculated date when the next maintenance cycle is due based on last completion and the maintenance cycle interval. Drives scheduling dashboards and work order auto-generation in SAP PM.
reference: SAP PM Module; ISO 55000

description: Engine/operating hour threshold at which the next maintenance cycle is due. Calculated as last_completed_hours plus the usage-based cycle interval. Sourced from John Deere Operations Center telematics data.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; SAP PM Module; ISO 55000

description: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) service bulletin or technical service document number that this maintenance plan is based on or references (e.g., John Deere Technical Manual TM-XXXX). Ensures alignment with manufacturer specifications.
reference: OEM Technical Manuals; ISO 55000

description: Indicates whether this maintenance plan includes tasks subject to OSHA regulatory requirements (e.g., lockout/tagout procedures, confined space entry, hazardous energy control). Triggers mandatory safety documentation on generated work orders.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (Lockout/Tagout); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the maintenance plan (e.g., 'Annual Combine Harvester PM', '250-Hour Tractor Engine Service'). Used in operator dashboards and scheduling reports.
reference: SAP PM Module; ISO 55000

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the maintenance plan, used in CMMS work order generation, SAP PM module references, and cross-system communication. Follows the PM-XXXXXX naming convention.
reference: SAP PM Module; ISO 55000

description: Current lifecycle state of the maintenance plan. 'draft' indicates plan is under development; 'active' means plan is in use and generating work orders; 'suspended' means temporarily paused; 'completed' means all cycles fulfilled; 'cancelled' means plan is no longer applicable.
reference: SAP PM Module; ISO 55000

description: Version number of the maintenance plan document (e.g., '1.0', '2.3'). Incremented when plan tasks, intervals, or compliance requirements are revised. Supports audit trail and change management per ISO 55000.
reference: ISO 55000; SAP PM Module

description: Indicates whether this maintenance plan includes calibration or servicing tasks for precision agriculture hardware such as GPS/RTK receivers, VRA controllers, NDVI sensors, or auto-steer systems. Triggers specialized calibration task workflows.
reference: John Deere Operations Center; Trimble Ag Software; SAP PM Module

description: Restricted Entry Interval in hours applicable to maintenance tasks performed on sprayer or pesticide application equipment. Specifies the minimum time after pesticide application before maintenance personnel may safely enter treated areas. Mandated by EPA/FIFRA.
reference: EPA FIFRA; EPA 40 CFR Part 170 (Worker Protection Standard); APHIS

description: Minimum skill level required to execute the maintenance plan tasks. 'operator' indicates basic tasks performable by equipment operators; 'certified_mechanic' requires OEM-certified technicians. Drives workforce assignment in SAP SuccessFactors.
reference: SAP PM Module; SAP SuccessFactors; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Comma-separated list or description of specialized tools and equipment required to execute the maintenance plan tasks (e.g., torque wrench, hydraulic press, RTK calibration kit, diagnostic scanner). Supports tool reservation and kitting.
reference: SAP PM Module; OEM Service Manuals

description: Free-text description of mandatory safety precautions required before and during execution of maintenance tasks in this plan (e.g., lockout/tagout procedures, PPE requirements, confined space protocols, chemical handling). Referenced in generated work orders.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910; EPA Worker Protection Standard; ISO 45001

description: Agricultural season associated with this maintenance plan when seasonal_flag is True. Drives scheduling alignment with the crop calendar to ensure equipment is ready for critical operational windows.

description: Indicates whether this maintenance plan is tied to a specific agricultural season (e.g., pre-planting inspection, post-harvest winterization). When True, scheduling is aligned with the crop calendar to ensure equipment readiness.
reference: SAP PM Module; Granular Farm Management; ISO 55000

description: Classification of the maintenance strategy driving this plan. 'time_based' triggers on calendar intervals (e.g., annually); 'usage_based' triggers on engine hours or acreage; 'condition_based' triggers on sensor thresholds (e.g., oil pressure); 'predictive' uses IoT/telematics analytics from John Deere Operations Center.
reference: ISO 55000; SAP PM Module; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Total number of individual maintenance task line items defined within this plan (e.g., lubrication, filter replacement, calibration, inspection). Provides a quick summary of plan complexity for scheduling and labor estimation.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this maintenance plan record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

Drives safety stock levels and emergency procurement protocols.

Governs whether the part can be issued against work orders.

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this commodity was produced or harvested. Required for USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, export documentation (BOL, AWB), and customs declarations.

description: Timestamp when this commodity lot record was first created in the system, typically upon GRN posting in SAP S/4HANA MM or inbound receipt confirmation in Manhattan Associates WMS. Serves as the audit trail anchor for record lineage and FSMA traceability documentation.

description: Number of days remaining until the lot's expiration_date as of the last inventory snapshot date. Used for FEFO prioritization alerts, customer minimum shelf-life requirement checks, and proactive disposition planning. Negative values indicate the lot is past expiration.

description: Prescribed disposition action for this commodity lot, particularly for lots in quarantine, hold, or expired status. Determined by quality management review in SAP QM. Drives downstream WMS and logistics workflows for lot resolution.

description: Hard expiration date for this commodity lot, after which the product must not be sold or used for food/feed purposes. Triggers automatic lot status change to 'expired' in WMS and SAP MM. Used as the primary FEFO sort key for lot selection in Manhattan Associates WMS pick logic.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 food labeling; FSMA Preventive Controls for Human Food 21 CFR Part 117; ISO 22000:2018

description: GMO status of the commodity lot indicating whether the crop is genetically modified, non-GMO, or identity-preserved (IP) non-GMO with documented segregation. reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service Non-GMO Program; EU Regulation 1829/2003 on GM food and feed; GLOBALG.A.P. GMO traceability; ISO 21569 GMO detection methods

description: Official USDA grade designation assigned to this commodity lot based on moisture, test weight, damaged kernels, foreign material, and other quality factors per FGIS Official Grain Standards. Grade determines market price, storage eligibility, and end-use suitability.
reference: USDA GIPSA Official Grain Standards of the United States; 7 CFR Part 810; FGIS grading procedures

description: Goods Receipt Note reference number generated in SAP S/4HANA MM upon physical receipt of the commodity lot at a storage facility. Links the lot to the inbound delivery and purchase order for procurement traceability and inventory valuation.

description: 18-digit GS1 Serial Shipping Container Code assigned to the pallet or container carrying this commodity lot, enabling RFID and barcode scanning for pallet-level traceability from farm to table. Supports EDI and WMS integration with Manhattan Associates.
reference: GS1 General Specifications; FDA FSMA Section 204 Key Data Elements; EU General Food Law Regulation 178/2002

description: Free-text description of the reason this commodity lot has been placed on hold or quarantine status. Examples include: failed mycotoxin test, suspected pesticide contamination, pending COA, supplier dispute, or regulatory investigation. Required when lot_status is 'hold' or 'quarantine'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Usage Decision; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls corrective actions; HACCP CCP deviation documentation

description: Indicates whether this commodity lot is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP) or equivalent international organic certification. Organic-certified lots require strict segregation from conventional lots and carry premium pricing. Must be supported by a valid Certificate of Analysis (COA) from an accredited certifier.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; EU Organic Regulation 2018/848; IFOAM Organic Standards

description: Date of the most recent WMS-driven cycle count or physical inventory verification for this commodity lot. Used to assess inventory accuracy, trigger recount workflows, and support FSMA traceability audit readiness. Managed through Manhattan Associates WMS cycle count module.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Cycle Count; SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory; USDA Warehouse Act inventory verification requirements

description: Externally-known alphanumeric lot identifier assigned at receipt or production, used for traceability across the agricultural supply chain. Corresponds to SAP MM batch number and Manhattan WMS lot number. Serves as the primary business-facing identifier for FIFO/FEFO rotation, recall management, and FSMA Section 204 traceability.
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GLOBALG.A.P. traceability standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the commodity lot within the inventory management system. Drives FIFO/FEFO rotation logic, WMS pick eligibility, and disposition workflows. 'quarantine' and 'hold' statuses block lot from fulfillment until quality release. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|quarantine|hold|released|consumed|expired|disposed — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Status Management; Manhattan Associates WMS lot status; FDA FSMA inventory controls

description: Date on which this commodity lot was produced, processed, or packaged. For harvested commodities, this is the harvest date. For processed products (feed, flour, oil), this is the processing date. Used as the anchor date for shelf-life and expiration calculations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101 food labeling; FSMA Preventive Controls Rule; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Moisture content of the commodity lot expressed as a percentage of total weight, measured at receipt or post-drying. Critical quality parameter for grain grading, shrinkage calculation, storage eligibility, and price discounting. USDA standard moisture for corn is 15.5%, wheat 13.5%, soybeans 13%.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection Packers and Stockyards Administration (GIPSA) grain standards; FGIS Official Grain Standards of the United States

description: Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit of measure. Used for grain elevator accounting, CBOT futures hedging, and WASDE supply reporting. One bushel equals 56 lbs for corn/sorghum, 60 lbs for wheat/soybeans.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service bushel weight standards; CBOT grain contract specifications; DTN market data

description: Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in hundredweight (CWT = 100 lbs), commonly used for livestock feed, cotton, and specialty crop pricing and billing. Supports SAP MM inventory valuation in CWT-denominated contracts.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service CWT pricing standards; SAP S/4HANA MM unit of measure configuration

description: Current on-hand quantity of the commodity lot expressed in metric tons (MT), the standard international trade unit. Used for export documentation, CIF/FOB contracts, and Codex Alimentarius reporting. Derived from BU quantity using commodity-specific conversion factors but stored for operational efficiency.
reference: Codex Alimentarius international commodity standards; FAO trade reporting; USDA FAS export data

description: Date on which this commodity lot was physically received at the storage facility and the GRN was posted in SAP S/4HANA MM. Serves as the FIFO anchor date for first-in-first-out rotation logic and the start of the storage tenure calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (MIGO); Manhattan Associates WMS inbound receipt; USDA Warehouse Act

description: Original quantity of this commodity lot at the time of GRN receipt, expressed in bushels. Preserved as the immutable receipt quantity for shrinkage calculation, inventory reconciliation, and audit trail. Distinct from quantity_bu which reflects current on-hand quantity after consumption and adjustments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt quantity; USDA Warehouse Act scale ticket requirements; Manhattan Associates WMS receipt confirmation

description: Inventory rotation method applied to this commodity lot for pick sequencing in Manhattan Associates WMS. FIFO (First In First Out) is standard for grain; FEFO (First Expired First Out) is required for perishable produce and feed with expiration dates. FEFO_FIFO applies FEFO when expiration dates exist, otherwise FIFO.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS lot rotation configuration; FDA FSMA inventory management best practices; GFSI stock rotation requirements

description: Percentage of quantity lost from this lot due to moisture evaporation, handling losses, or storage shrink since receipt. Calculated as (received_quantity - current_quantity) / received_quantity * 100. Used for inventory valuation adjustments, storage contract billing, and loss reporting.
reference: USDA GIPSA grain shrinkage standards; SAP S/4HANA MM inventory difference posting; Granular Farm Management shrink tracking

description: Type of storage facility where this commodity lot is currently held. Determines applicable storage conditions, monitoring requirements, and handling procedures. Cold storage lots require temperature and humidity monitoring per FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule.
reference: USDA Warehouse Act storage type classifications; FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112; ISO 22000 storage requirements

description: Test weight of the commodity lot measured in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu), a key grain quality indicator reflecting kernel density and market grade. Minimum test weights per USDA GIPSA: corn 56 lbs/bu (No.1), wheat 60 lbs/bu (No.1), soybeans 56 lbs/bu (No.1). Affects price premiums and discounts.
reference: USDA GIPSA Official Grain Standards; FGIS grain grading procedures; CBOT delivery specifications

description: The primary unit of measure used for inventory management and reporting of this commodity lot (e.g., BU for grain, MT for export commodities, CWT for feed). description: Human-readable short description or name of the material (e.g., 'Urea 46-0-0 Fertilizer', 'Glyphosate 41% Herbicide', 'Yellow Corn #2'). Corresponds to SAP MAKTX (material description). Used in inventory reports, purchase orders, and warehouse labels.

reference: AAPFCO Fertilizer Labeling Standards; EPA 40 CFR Part 455; USDA NRCS

description: Potassium (K2O) content percentage in fertilizer materials, expressed as percentage by weight. Part of the NPK nutrient analysis required for fertilizer labeling and soil management planning. Null for non-fertilizer materials.
reference: AAPFCO Fertilizer Labeling Standards; EPA 40 CFR Part 455; USDA NRCS

description: Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of a crop protection chemical and harvest, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Critical for FSMA compliance and food safety scheduling. Null for non-chemical materials.
reference: EPA WPS 40 CFR Part 170; FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: IFPS Price Look-Up (PLU) code assigned to fresh produce and bulk agricultural commodities for retail checkout identification. Distinguishes conventional, organic, and GMO produce at point of sale.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard price (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'BRL'). Required for multi-currency operations in international agricultural trade and IFRS financial reporting.

description: SAP procurement type (BESKZ) indicating whether the material is externally procured, produced in-house, or both. Drives MRP (Material Requirements Planning) logic in SAP PP and purchasing strategy in SAP MM.

description: Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours — the minimum time workers must wait before re-entering a treated field after pesticide application, per EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Required for OSHA and EPA compliance, field scheduling, and worker safety documentation.

description: Standard shelf life of the material in days from the date of manufacture or harvest. Used to calculate expiration dates for FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation, WMS lot management, and food safety compliance. Drives PHI scheduling for crop protection chemicals.

description: USDA NRCS conservation practice standard code applied to this management zone (e.g., 590 - Nutrient Management, 329 - Residue and Tillage Management, 340 - Cover Crop, 412 - Grassed Waterway). description: Method used to spatially delineate the management zone boundary. description: Apparent soil electrical conductivity (EC) reading at deep depth (typically 0-90 cm) expressed in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m). Measured via Veris 3100 or EM38 sensor. description: Date on which the soil electrical conductivity (EC) scanning survey was conducted for this management zone. Used to assess data currency and schedule re-scanning intervals (typically every 3-5 years per BMP guidelines).

description: Apparent soil electrical conductivity (EC) reading at shallow depth (typically 0-30 cm) expressed in deciSiemens per meter (dS/m). Measured via Veris 3100 or EM38 sensor during EC scanning. Correlates with soil texture, clay content, and salinity. Used as primary input for EC-based zone delineation and VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation.
reference: Trimble Ag Software - EC Zone Delineation; USDA NRCS Soil EC Standards; Veris Technologies Measurement Protocol

description: Native identifier of this management zone in the originating source system (e.g., Trimble Ag zone GUID, John Deere Operations Center zone ID, Esri ArcGIS feature OID). reference: Trimble Ag Software - Zone API; John Deere Operations Center - Field Boundary API; Esri ArcGIS REST API

description: Spatial boundary of the management zone stored as Well-Known Text (WKT) polygon geometry in WGS84 coordinate reference system (EPSG:4326). reference: Esri ArcGIS - Feature Class Standards; OGC Simple Features Standard; ISO 19125

reference: Climate FieldView - NDVI Analytics; NASA Landsat / Sentinel-2 Remote Sensing Standards; USDA NASS Crop Monitoring

description: Mean available nitrogen (N) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA Nutrient Management Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 4.2

description: Mean soil organic matter (OM) content expressed as a percentage of dry soil weight for the management zone. Key indicator for carbon sequestration reporting under ISCC and sustainability programs.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Health Framework; ISO 14235 Soil Quality - Organic Carbon; ISCC Carbon Certification

description: Mean available phosphorus (P) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. Component of NPK nutrient profile. reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; EPA Clean Water Act - Nutrient Runoff; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 4.2

description: Mean available potassium (K) level in the management zone soil expressed in pounds per acre (lbs/acre), derived from composite soil sampling. Component of NPK nutrient profile. Used to calculate potassium fertilizer application rates in VRA prescriptions.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; GLOBALG.A.P. CB 4.2

description: Agronomic productivity classification of the management zone based on historical yield performance and soil capability. description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this soil profile or management zone within the farm management information system (FMIS). Used for cross-referencing with Granular Farm Management and Trimble Ag Software records.

description: Horizontal positional accuracy of the GPS coordinates in meters at time of sample collection. RTK-corrected readings typically achieve sub-0.05m accuracy. Used to assess spatial data quality for precision agriculture applications.
reference: John Deere Operations Center RTK GPS Standards; Esri ArcGIS Data Quality; ISO 19157 Geographic Data Quality

description: Official accreditation or certification number of the laboratory that analyzed the sample, issued by a recognized accreditation body (e.g., A2LA, NELAP). Supports regulatory compliance and audit traceability.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; USDA AMS Laboratory Accreditation Program; NELAP Standards

description: Date on which the laboratory issued the official analytical results report for this soil sample. Used to calculate lab turnaround time and determine data currency for fertilizer prescriptions.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Laboratory Competence; USDA AMS Laboratory Accreditation Program

description: Date on which the collected soil sample was submitted to the analytical laboratory for processing. Used to track turnaround time and chain-of-custody compliance.
reference: USDA AMS Laboratory Accreditation Program; ISO/IEC 17025 Laboratory Competence

description: GPS latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84 datum) of the soil sample collection point. Captured via RTK-accurate GPS equipment for precise spatial referencing in GIS systems and VRA prescription mapping.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Spatial Reference Standards; John Deere Operations Center RTK GPS; WGS84 Datum

description: Laboratory-recommended agricultural lime application rate in pounds per acre, calculated from soil pH and buffer pH results to raise soil pH to the target level for the planned crop. Directly used in fertilizer prescription and VRA lime application planning.
reference: USDA NRCS Lime Requirement Calculation Methods; Trimble Ag Software VRA Prescriptions

description: GPS longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84 datum) of the soil sample collection point. Captured via RTK-accurate GPS equipment for precise spatial referencing in GIS systems and VRA prescription mapping.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Spatial Reference Standards; John Deere Operations Center RTK GPS; WGS84 Datum

description: Plant-available magnesium (Mg) concentration in parts per million (ppm). Magnesium is a secondary macronutrient and central component of chlorophyll. Used in CEC base saturation calculations and dolomitic lime application decisions.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Fertility Guidelines; Mehlich-3 Extraction Method; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Nutrient Standards

description: Plant-available nitrate nitrogen (NO3-N) concentration in parts per million (ppm) as measured in the soil sample. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Directly informs nitrogen fertilizer application rates and VRA prescriptions.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Planning; EPA Nitrate Leaching Standards; GLOBALG.A.P. Nutrient Management

description: Laboratory or agronomist-recommended nitrogen (N) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil nitrate-N test results, organic matter nitrogen credit, and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Planning; EPA Nitrogen Management Best Practices; Trimble Ag VRA

description: Percentage of organic matter (OM) in the soil sample, measured by loss-on-ignition or Walkley-Black method. Organic matter is a key indicator of soil health, water-holding capacity, and biological activity. Drives nitrogen credit calculations and carbon sequestration assessments.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Health Assessment; ISO 14001 Soil Carbon Monitoring; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Organic Matter Standards

description: Soil pH measured using the 1:1 soil-to-water suspension method, representing the Potential of Hydrogen (pH) — the primary indicator of soil acidity or alkalinity. Values range from 0-14; optimal crop production typically falls between 6.0-7.0. Drives lime application recommendations.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Survey Laboratory Methods Manual; Mehlich-3 / 1:1 Water pH Method; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Health

description: Laboratory extraction method used to determine plant-available phosphorus concentration. Bray-1 is standard for acidic soils; Mehlich-3 is a universal extractant; Olsen is used for calcareous/alkaline soils. Method must be recorded as results are not directly comparable across methods.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Testing Methods Manual; North Central Regional Publication 221

description: Plant-available phosphorus (P) concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Bray-1 or Mehlich-3 extraction method. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Drives phosphate fertilizer recommendations and VRA prescription maps.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management; Bray-1 / Mehlich-3 Extraction Methods; EPA Phosphorus Loss Risk Assessment

description: Laboratory or agronomist-recommended phosphate (P2O5) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil phosphorus test results and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Planning; EPA Phosphorus Index; Trimble Ag VRA Prescriptions

description: Plant-available potassium (K) concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Mehlich-3 or ammonium acetate extraction. A key component of the Nitrogen-Phosphorus-Potassium (NPK) nutrient profile. Drives potash fertilizer recommendations and VRA prescription planning.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Planning; Mehlich-3 Extraction Method; GLOBALG.A.P. Nutrient Management

description: Laboratory or agronomist-recommended potash (K2O) fertilizer application rate in pounds per acre based on soil potassium test results and planned crop yield goal. Core input for NPK fertilizer prescription and VRA planning.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Planning; Trimble Ag Software VRA Prescriptions

description: Calendar date on which the soil sample was physically collected from the field. Critical for seasonal trend analysis and determining the agronomic context (pre-plant, post-harvest, etc.).
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Protocols; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Health Standards

description: Depth in inches at which the soil sample was collected from the surface. Standard agronomic sampling depths are 0-6 inches (topsoil) or 6-12 inches (subsoil). Critical for interpreting nutrient availability relative to root zone.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Depth Standards; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Management; Trimble Ag Software

description: Method used to collect the soil sample. Grid sampling uses a systematic spatial grid; zone sampling collects within defined management zones; composite combines multiple cores; benchmark uses fixed reference points for longitudinal tracking.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Protocols; Trimble Ag Software VRA Sampling; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Externally-known, human-readable sample identifier assigned at time of collection, used for lab submission tracking and field cross-referencing. Typically formatted as a lab accession or farm-assigned code (e.g., FARM-2024-F01-Z3).
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the soil sample record, tracking progression from field collection through laboratory analysis to agronomic approval for use in fertilizer prescriptions.
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Sampling Workflow; ISO 14001 Environmental Records Management

description: Name or identifier of the agronomist, certified crop advisor (CCA), or field technician who physically collected the soil sample. Used for chain-of-custody documentation and quality assurance traceability.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Chain of Custody Requirements; USDA NRCS Soil Sampling SOPs; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: USDA soil texture classification based on the relative proportions of sand, silt, and clay particles. Texture class influences water-holding capacity, drainage, tillage requirements, and nutrient management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sand|loamy_sand|sandy_loam|loam|silt_loam|silt|sandy_clay_loam|clay_loam|silty_clay_loam|sandy_clay|silty_clay|clay — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA NRCS Soil Texture Classification System; USDA Soil Survey Manual

description: Plant-available sulfur (S) concentration in parts per million (ppm). Sulfur is a secondary macronutrient critical for protein synthesis and enzyme function. Deficiencies are increasingly common in high-yield cropping systems and drive sulfur fertilizer recommendations.
reference: USDA NRCS Nutrient Management; GLOBALG.A.P. Soil Nutrient Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this soil sample record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Standards; SOX Audit Trail Requirements; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Plant-available zinc (Zn) micronutrient concentration in parts per million (ppm), measured by Mehlich-3 or DTPA extraction. Zinc deficiency is common in high-pH soils and drives micronutrient fertilizer recommendations for corn and other sensitive crops.
reference: USDA NRCS Micronutrient Management; Mehlich-3 / DTPA Extraction Methods; GLOBALG.A.P.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: USDA FSA Common Land Unit (CLU) identifier linking this farm unit to the official USDA geospatial land unit registry. CLU is the smallest unit of land with a permanent, contiguous boundary used for FSA program administration and acreage reporting.

description: Name or code of USDA conservation programs in which this farm unit is enrolled (e.g., CRP, EQIP, CSP, RCPP). Supports BMP compliance tracking, conservation payment eligibility, and ISO 14001 environmental management reporting.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which this farm unit operates (e.g., USA, CAN, BRA). Supports international operations, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, and multi-country enterprise reporting.

description: Name of the county in which the majority of this farm unit's land is located. Required for FSA county office assignment, crop insurance county-level rate determination, and USDA program administration.
reference: USDA FSA County Office Records; USDA NASS

description: Timestamp when this farm unit record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX; ISO 22000; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Risk Management Agency (RMA) assigned crop insurance unit number linking this farm unit to its crop insurance policy structure. Used for indemnity calculations, acreage reporting, and production history tracking under USDA RMA programs.
reference: USDA RMA Crop Insurance Regulations; 7 CFR Part 457

description: Date on which this farm unit ceased operations or was retired from active management. Null for currently active farm units. Used for historical reporting, FSA record archival, and enterprise lifecycle management.
reference: USDA FSA Farm Records; Granular Farm Management

description: Date on which this farm unit became operationally active and eligible for FSA program enrollment, crop insurance, and enterprise management. Marks the beginning of the farm unit's operational lifecycle.
reference: USDA FSA Farm Records; Granular Farm Management

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Cost Center Code
description: SAP S/4HANA CO module cost center code assigned to this farm unit for enterprise financial management, OPEX/CAPEX allocation, and profitability analysis. Links farm unit operations to the general ledger for enterprise budgeting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

business_glossary_term: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) Profit Center Code
description: SAP S/4HANA CO module profit center code for this farm unit, enabling enterprise-level profitability analysis, EBITDA reporting, and ROI tracking at the farm unit level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Classification of the farm unit by primary production system. Drives enterprise budgeting templates, crop insurance unit structuring, and FSA program eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|livestock|mixed|specialty_crop|orchard|vegetable|aquaculture|dairy|poultry — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: USDA NASS Farm Typology; USDA FSA

description: Human-readable name assigned to the farm unit, typically reflecting the operating entity or geographic identity of the farm (e.g., 'Sunrise Grain Farm', 'Heartland Livestock Unit'). Used in reporting, FSA program enrollment, and enterprise budgeting.
reference: USDA FSA Farm Records; Granular Farm Management

business_glossary_term: Farm Management Information System (FMIS) Reference ID
description: External reference identifier for this farm unit in the Farm Management Information System (FMIS), such as Granular Farm Management or Climate FieldView. Enables cross-system data integration and operational data lineage tracking.
reference: Granular Farm Management; Climate FieldView; John Deere Operations Center

description: USDA FSA numeric county code for the county office administering this farm unit. Combined with FSA state code and farm number to form the complete FSA farm identifier for program enrollment and payment tracking.
reference: USDA FSA State/County Codes; USDA 7 CFR Part 718

description: USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) assigned farm number uniquely identifying this farm unit within the FSA county office system. Required for FSA program enrollment, crop insurance unit structuring, and USDA program payment tracking.
reference: USDA FSA 7 CFR Part 718; USDA Farm Records

description: USDA FSA numeric state code assigned to the state where this farm unit is registered. Used in conjunction with FSA county code and farm number for unique FSA record identification and program payment routing.
reference: USDA FSA State/County Codes; USDA 7 CFR Part 718

description: USDA FSA tract number associated with this farm unit, identifying the contiguous block of land under common ownership or control within the FSA farm. Used for FSA program eligibility, acreage reporting, and USDA payment calculations.
reference: USDA FSA 7 CFR Part 718; USDA Farm Records

description: Reference identifier or URL to the GIS polygon boundary layer for this farm unit in Esri ArcGIS or John Deere Operations Center. Enables RTK-accurate field mapping, GPS positioning, and spatial analytics for precision agriculture applications.
reference: Esri ArcGIS; John Deere Operations Center; USDA CLU (Common Land Unit)

description: GLOBALG.A.P. certification number assigned to this farm unit, confirming compliance with global Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) standards. Required for export market access and retail supply chain qualification.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) Standard

description: Primary irrigation method employed on this farm unit. Informs water resource management planning, evapotranspiration (ET) calculations, water use efficiency reporting, and USDA conservation program eligibility.
reference: USDA NRCS Irrigation Water Management; EPA Water Efficiency

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the farm unit's centroid or primary reference point. Supports GIS mapping, GPS navigation, precision agriculture applications, and spatial analytics in Esri ArcGIS.
reference: WGS84 Coordinate Reference System; Esri ArcGIS; John Deere Operations Center

description: Total acres of land under cash rent or crop share lease agreements within this farm unit. Supports OPEX planning, lease liability reporting under IFRS 16/ASC 842, and FSA program eligibility determination.
reference: IFRS 16; ASC 842; USDA FSA; Granular Farm Management

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) of the farm unit's centroid or primary reference point. Supports GIS mapping, GPS navigation, precision agriculture applications, and spatial analytics in Esri ArcGIS.
reference: WGS84 Coordinate Reference System; Esri ArcGIS; John Deere Operations Center

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special conditions, or contextual information about this farm unit not captured in structured fields. Used by farm managers and data stewards for supplemental documentation.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA

description: Current lifecycle status of the farm unit indicating whether it is actively managed, temporarily inactive, under transition (e.g., organic conversion), or permanently retired from operation.
reference: Granular Farm Management; USDA FSA Farm Records

description: Current USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification status of the farm unit. Drives premium pricing, market channel eligibility, input restrictions, and compliance reporting requirements under USDA NOP regulations.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); 7 CFR Part 205

description: Total acres of land owned outright by the operating entity within this farm unit. Supports land asset valuation, CAPEX planning, and differentiation from leased land in enterprise financial reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 360; IFRS IAS 16; Granular Farm Management

description: Name or HUC (Hydrologic Unit Code) of the primary watershed in which this farm unit is located. Used for EPA water quality compliance, BMP planning, conservation program eligibility, and environmental monitoring under ISO 14001.
reference: EPA Watershed Boundary Dataset; ISO 14001; USDA NRCS

description: Two-letter US state abbreviation (USPS standard) for the state in which this farm unit is primarily located. Used for FSA state office routing, state-level regulatory compliance, and geographic reporting.
reference: USPS State Abbreviations; USDA FSA State Office Records

description: Predominant tillage practice employed on this farm unit. Supports soil health management, carbon sequestration reporting, BMP compliance, and USDA conservation program eligibility assessments.
reference: USDA NRCS Conservation Practice Standards; ISO 14001

description: Total land area operated by this farm unit in acres, encompassing both owned and leased parcels. Used for FSA acreage reporting, crop insurance unit sizing, enterprise budgeting, and USDA program payment calculations.
reference: USDA FSA 7 CFR Part 718; USDA Acreage Reporting

description: Timestamp when this farm unit record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements for FSA program compliance and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX; ISO 22000; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the agricultural land lease agreement record. Primary key for the lease data product.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA RE-FX (Real Estate) module

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
