description: Reference to the operational farm unit to which the leased land is assigned. Provides operational context for crop planning and field operations.

description: Indicates whether the lease agreement mandates adherence to specific conservation practices (e.g., cover cropping, no-till, buffer strips) as a condition of tenancy. Supports BMP and ISO 14001 environmental compliance tracking.

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the lease record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 8601; SOX audit trail requirements; Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer conventions

description: The Assessor Parcel Number (APN) assigned by the county assessor's office for property tax assessment purposes. Also known as a parcel identification number (PIN) or tax parcel number. Used for cross-referencing with county tax records.
reference: County Assessor Standards; USDA FSA Farm Records

description: Sequential integer indicating the position of this ownership record in the chain of title for the parcel. A value of 1 represents the earliest recorded ownership; higher values represent more recent ownership records. Supports chain-of-title history and ownership lineage analysis.
reference: ISO 19152 Land Administration Domain Model (LADM); USDA FSA Land Records

description: Indicates whether this parcel is subject to a conservation easement that restricts certain land uses in perpetuity or for a defined term. Conservation easements are commonly held by land trusts or government agencies under USDA NRCS programs.
reference: USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service (NRCS) Easement Programs; Internal Revenue Code Section 170(h)

description: The date on which the ownership interest was conveyed (transferred) from the prior owner to the current owner, as stated in the deed instrument. Distinct from recording_date (when filed) and effective_date (when legally binding).
reference: County Recorder Standards; USDA FSA Land Records

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country in which the parcel is located. Supports international operations and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) Regulations

description: Name of the county in which the parcel is located and where the deed is recorded. Used for jurisdictional routing of legal documents and regulatory reporting to USDA FSA county offices.
reference: USDA FSA County Office Structure; FIPS County Codes

description: The timestamp when this ownership record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with FSMA record-keeping requirements.
reference: FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; ISO 22000; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates the data quality and verification status of this ownership record. 'Verified' means the record has been validated against official county recorder or title company sources; 'unverified' means the record has not yet been validated; 'under_review' means validation is in progress; 'rejected' means the record failed validation.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GLOBALG.A.P. Record-Keeping Requirements

description: The deed book (volume) number in the county recorder's office where the deed instrument is officially recorded. Used in conjunction with deed_page for precise legal document retrieval.
reference: County Recorder Standards; Uniform Real Property Transfer Act

description: Classification of the legal deed instrument used to convey ownership. Warranty deeds provide full title guarantees; quitclaim deeds convey only the grantor's existing interest; grant deeds include implied warranties; trustee and sheriff deeds arise from trust or court proceedings.
reference: Uniform Real Property Transfer on Death Act; County Recorder Standards

description: The page number within the deed book at the county recorder's office where the deed instrument is officially recorded. Used in conjunction with deed_book for precise legal document retrieval.
reference: County Recorder Standards; Uniform Real Property Transfer Act

description: Unique reference number assigned to the deed instrument conveying ownership of this parcel. Used to cross-reference with county recorder and title company records for chain-of-title verification.
reference: County Recorder Standards; USDA FSA Land Records

description: Free-text notes describing the nature of any ownership dispute, legal challenge, or title defect associated with this ownership record. Populated when ownership_status is 'disputed'. Supports legal team review and resolution tracking.
reference: Internal Legal Governance Standards; American Land Title Association (ALTA)

description: The date on which the ownership interest legally became effective, which may differ from the recording date. For example, a deed may be executed and effective on one date but recorded later. This is the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM field for this ownership record.
reference: USDA FSA Land Tenure Standards; ISO 19152 Land Administration Domain Model (LADM)

description: Narrative description of any encumbrances, liens, easements, mortgages, conservation easements, or other title restrictions affecting this ownership record. Populated when encumbrance_flag is True.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA) Standards; USDA NRCS Conservation Easement Program

description: Indicates whether this ownership record is subject to any encumbrances, liens, easements, mortgages, or other claims against the title that may affect the owner's full use and enjoyment of the parcel.
reference: American Land Title Association (ALTA) Standards; USDA FSA Land Records

description: The date on which the ownership interest expires or terminates, applicable for term interests such as life estates or time-limited ownership arrangements. Null for perpetual fee simple ownership. This is the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL field.

description: The USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) tract number assigned to this parcel for farm program eligibility, commodity program enrollment, and conservation program participation tracking.

description: Full legal description of the parcel as recorded on the deed, typically expressed in metes and bounds, Public Land Survey System (PLSS) section-township-range notation, or lot-block-subdivision format. This is the authoritative legal boundary description for the parcel.

description: General free-text notes or remarks related to this ownership record, including special conditions, legal caveats, or administrative observations not captured in structured fields.

description: Classification of the legal entity type of the owner. description: Current lifecycle state of this ownership record. 'Current' indicates active legal ownership; 'historical' indicates a past ownership interest that has been transferred or extinguished; 'disputed' flags records under legal challenge; 'pending' indicates a transfer in progress; 'transferred' indicates completed conveyance to a new owner.

description: Legal classification of the ownership interest held by the owner in this parcel. Fee simple is the most complete form of ownership; joint tenancy includes right of survivorship; tenancy in common allows fractional interests without survivorship; life estate is limited to the owner's lifetime; trust and LLC membership reflect entity-held interests.
reference: Uniform Real Property Transfer on Death Act; USDA FSA Land Ownership Classification

description: Fractional ownership interest held by this owner in the parcel, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 50.0000 for a 50% undivided interest). description: Two-letter USPS state abbreviation for the state in which the parcel is located. Used for jurisdictional compliance, USDA FSA state office routing, and regulatory reporting.

description: Name of the title insurance underwriter or company that issued the title insurance policy for this ownership record. Used for claims processing and title defect resolution.

description: The reason or mechanism by which ownership was transferred to the current owner. reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Expected Progeny Difference (EPD) value for milk production for the sire, expressed in pounds. Reflects the milking ability transmitted through daughters. Used in maternal breed selection for calf nutrition and growth.

description: Current lifecycle status of the breeding event or inventory record. 'planned' indicates a scheduled breeding activity; 'completed' indicates the event occurred; 'cancelled' indicates the event was voided; 'pending_confirmation' indicates awaiting conception check result.

COLOSTRUM: first-milking colostrum; ANTIBIOTIC_WITHDRAWAL: animal within withdrawal period (PHI — Pre-Harvest Interval); MASTITIS: clinical mastitis; BLOOD_TINGED: visible blood contamination; DRUG_TREATMENT: other veterinary drug treatment; NONE: not diverted. Supports antibiotic residue compliance and FSIS regulatory reporting.
reference: FDA PMO; FSIS antibiotic residue testing program; USDA AMS Grade A dairy standards; PHI (Pre-Harvest Interval) compliance

description: Percentage of butterfat content in the milk sample, measured via DHIA test-day analysis or inline sensor. Used for component-based milk pricing (fat premium/deduction), breed performance benchmarking, and nutritional ration adjustment. Regulatory threshold compliance per FDA Grade A Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO).
reference: DHIA; FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO); ICAR milk recording standards

description: Absolute quantity of butterfat produced in this milking event, calculated as milk_yield_kg × fat_pct / 100. Stored as a business field for DHIA component reporting, Federal Milk Marketing Order (FMMO) component pricing, and dairy enterprise financial reconciliation.
reference: USDA AMS Federal Milk Marketing Order (FMMO); DHIA component reporting

description: Indicates whether the milk from this event was classified as abnormal (e.g., colostrum, mastitic milk, antibiotic-treated milk, blood-tinged milk) and therefore diverted from the bulk tank. True = abnormal/diverted; False = normal saleable milk. Critical for milk quality compliance and antibiotic residue tracking under FSIS and FDA PMO.
reference: FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO); FSIS (Food Safety and Inspection Service) antibiotic residue program; USDA AMS

description: Sequential count of the animal's completed and current lactations (parity). First-calf heifers are parity 1. Used for stratified performance benchmarking, culling decision support, and genetic evaluation. Aligns with parity_number in the animal master record.
reference: DHIA; ICAR; USDA AIPL genetic evaluation standards

description: Percentage of lactose in the milk sample. Elevated lactose deviation can indicate early-stage mastitis or metabolic disorders. Used in conjunction with SCC and MUN for udder health monitoring and milk quality compliance.
reference: DHIA; ICAR milk recording standards; FDA PMO

description: Logarithmic transformation of somatic cell count expressed on a 0–9 linear scale, as defined by DHIA. Provides a normalized, statistically stable measure of udder health for genetic evaluation, herd benchmarking, and DHIA enrollment reporting.
reference: DHIA linear score methodology; USDA Animal Improvement Programs Laboratory (AIPL)

description: Temperature of the milk at the point of collection, expressed in degrees Celsius. Used for milk quality compliance (FDA PMO requires rapid cooling to ≤10°C within 4 hours of milking), cold chain monitoring, and detection of fever-related health events in the animal.
reference: FDA Pasteurized Milk Ordinance (PMO) Grade A cooling requirements; USDA AMS dairy quality standards

description: Volume of milk produced by the animal during this milking event, expressed in kilograms. Primary quantitative outcome of the milking transaction. Used for lactation curve modeling, DHIA test-day reporting, and dairy enterprise revenue estimation.
reference: DHIA; ICAR (International Committee for Animal Recording) milk recording standards; ISO 8196

description: Volume of milk produced during this milking event expressed in pounds (lbs). Retained alongside kg for compatibility with USDA NASS reporting, DHIA test-day records, and domestic dairy market pricing conventions (hundredweight/CWT basis).
reference: USDA NASS dairy reporting; DHIA; CWT (Hundredweight) pricing conventions

description: Calendar date on which the milking event or DHIA test-day observation occurred. Principal business event date used for daily, monthly, and lactation-period reporting. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd per model conventions.

description: Total elapsed time in seconds for the milking event from unit attachment to detachment. Used for parlor throughput optimization, teat condition monitoring, and robotic milking system performance analysis (OEE — Overall Equipment Effectiveness).

description: Externally-known business identifier for this milking event or test-day record, typically assigned by the parlor management system or DHIA testing service (e.g., DHIA test-day report number, parlor event ID). reference: ISO 8601; DHIA milking event standards

description: Concentration of urea nitrogen in milk expressed in milligrams per deciliter (mg/dL). MUN (Milk Urea Nitrogen) is an indicator of dietary protein-energy balance and rumen fermentation efficiency. Used for ration formulation adjustments and environmental nitrogen management compliance.
reference: DHIA; USDA dairy nutrition guidelines; EPA nitrogen management BMPs (Best Management Practices)

description: Maximum milk flow rate recorded during the milking event, expressed in kilograms per minute. Indicator of teat canal health, milking unit vacuum performance, and potential mastitis risk. Captured by parlor management systems and robotic milking units.
reference: ISO 6690; USDA dairy equipment standards; NMC (National Mastitis Council) guidelines

description: Percentage of true protein content in the milk sample. Used for component-based milk pricing (protein premium/deduction), cheese yield estimation, and feed ration optimization. Key performance indicator for dairy enterprise profitability.

description: Lifecycle status of this milk production record. description: Unique surrogate identifier for each feed ration record (formulation or delivery event). Primary key for the feed_ration data product in the livestock domain.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

description: Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Feed rations are mixed using specific TMR mixer assets. Linking feed_ration to the mixing asset supports calibration tracking (accurate ingredient inclusion rates), maintenance scheduling based on mix
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.facility. Business justification: Feed rations are approved and managed at the facility level for FDA VFD medicated feed compliance and organic certification scope. A facility_id FK enables facility-specific ration approval workflows
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Medicated and antibiotic-containing feed rations (antibiotic_flag, withdrawal_period_days) fall under HACCP/FSMA Preventive Controls for Animal Food. Linking feed_ration to its governing haccp_plan do
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to livestock.herd. Business justification: Feed rations are formulated for specific herds or production classes within a herd. Linking feed_ration to herd enables herd-level nutritional management, cost-per-head analysis by herd, and supports
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Medicated feed rations (antibiotic_flag=true) are subject to FDA VFD requirements and MRL thresholds for drug residues in food animals. Linking feed_ration to product.mrl_threshold enables automated w
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the pen or animal group to which this ration is assigned (formulation) or to which feed was delivered (delivery event). Links ration records to specific animal housing units for FCR tracking and feed cost allocation per animal group.
reference: Granular Farm Management; APHIS Animal Identification

description: Reference to the licensed animal nutritionist or feed specialist responsible for formulating or approving this ration. Supports accountability, NRC compliance documentation, and audit trail for regulatory inspections.
reference: APHIS; NRC Nutrient Requirements; USDA

description: Indicates whether this ration contains any antibiotic or medicated feed additive subject to VFD (Veterinary Feed Directive) or withdrawal period requirements. True = medicated; False = non-medicated. Critical for FSIS antibiotic residue compliance and withdrawal period tracking.
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558; FSIS; APHIS

description: Current workflow status of the ration formulation in the approval lifecycle: draft (in preparation), pending_review (submitted to nutritionist), approved (cleared for use), rejected (not approved), superseded (replaced by newer version). Applicable to formulation records.
reference: Granular Farm Management; ISO 22000; GFSI

description: Standardized numeric score (typically 0–4) assessing the feed bunk condition at the time of delivery: 0=empty/slick, 1=light cover, 2=normal, 3=heavy cover, 4=full refusal. Applicable to delivery records. Guides feeding rate adjustments and animal health monitoring.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements; BMP

description: Calculated calcium (Ca) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Critical macro-mineral for bone development, milk production, and metabolic health. NRC compliance metric for all species.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; AOAC International

business_glossary_term: Feed Cost per Head per Day (USD)
description: Calculated feed cost in USD per animal per day based on ingredient prices and inclusion rates in the formulation. Applicable to formulation records. Key input for COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) allocation, OPEX tracking, and ROI analysis per production class.
reference: Granular Farm Management; GAAP; IFRS

description: Timestamp when this feed ration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA traceability requirements.
reference: FSMA; ISO 22000; GDPR Article 5

description: Calculated crude protein content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Key NRC nutritional compliance metric for protein adequacy across species and production classes. Applicable to formulation records.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; AOAC International; USDA

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost_per_head_per_day field (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international agriculture enterprises.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; GAAP

description: Method used to deliver feed to the animal group: TMR (Total Mixed Ration) mixer wagon, bunk feeder, automated feeding system, hand feed, or conveyor. Applicable to delivery records. Impacts feed uniformity and labor cost allocation.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: Date and time at which feed was physically delivered to the pen or bunk. Principal business event timestamp for delivery records. Supports feeding schedule compliance, FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) tracking, and daily intake analysis.
reference: Granular Farm Management; FSMA Preventive Controls

description: Dry matter content of the ration expressed as a percentage of as-fed weight. Used to convert as-fed quantities to DM basis for NRC nutrient requirement compliance and ration balancing.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; AOAC International Feed Analysis Standards

description: Date from which this ration formulation becomes active and approved for use in feed delivery operations. Supports ration plan management and ensures animals receive the correct approved formulation.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: Date after which this ration formulation is no longer valid for use. Triggers review or replacement of the formulation. Applicable to formulation records.
reference: Granular Farm Management; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Date on which the ration formulation was created or last revised by the nutritionist. Applicable to formulation records. Used for version control and audit trail of recipe changes.
reference: Granular Farm Management; ISO 22000

description: Indicates whether the ration formulation contains any GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) ingredients. True = contains GMO ingredients; False = non-GMO. Supports USDA National Organic Program (NOP) compliance, COOL labeling, and customer transparency requirements.
reference: USDA NOP; COOL; GLOBALG.A.P.; Non-GMO Project

description: Operational instructions for preparing the TMR or batch mix, including ingredient loading sequence, mixer run time, and any special handling notes. Applicable to formulation records. Supports SOP compliance and feed uniformity.
reference: Granular Farm Management; GMP; SOP

description: Net Energy for Gain (NEg) value of the ration expressed in megacalories per hundredweight (CWT) of dry matter. Used for NRC-compliant growth performance prediction and ration optimization in beef cattle finishing programs.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements of Beef Cattle; USDA

description: Net Energy for Maintenance (NEm) value of the ration expressed in megacalories per hundredweight (CWT) of dry matter. Used for NRC-compliant energy adequacy assessment in beef and dairy cattle rations.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements of Beef Cattle; USDA

description: Free-text field for additional nutritionist comments, special handling instructions, or operational observations related to the ration formulation or delivery event. Supports audit trail and knowledge transfer.
reference: Granular Farm Management; ISO 22000

description: Indicates whether the ration formulation meets all NRC (National Research Council) nutrient requirement thresholds for the target species and production class. True = compliant; False = one or more NRC thresholds not met. Applicable to formulation records.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; USDA

description: Calculated phosphorus (P) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Key macro-mineral for energy metabolism and bone structure. NRC compliance metric; also tracked for environmental nutrient management under EPA guidelines.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; EPA Nutrient Management; AOAC International

description: Specific production class or physiological stage of the target animal group (e.g., 'Finishing Steers', 'Lactating Dairy Cows', 'Nursery Pigs', 'Broiler Grower'). Used for NRC-compliant nutrient requirement matching and ration cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; Granular Farm Management

description: Total weight of feed delivered expressed on a dry matter (DM) basis in pounds. Calculated from as-fed quantity and dry matter percentage. Used for NRC-compliant nutrient intake calculations and ration balancing.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; Granular Farm Management

description: Total weight of feed delivered to the pen on an as-fed basis, measured in pounds (lbs). Applicable to delivery records. Used for feed cost allocation per animal, FCR tracking, and daily intake monitoring.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: Human-readable name assigned to the feed ration formulation (e.g., 'Grower Phase 2 TMR', 'Lactating Cow High-Energy'). Used for identification in feed management systems and nutritionist communications.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: Externally-known business identifier for the ration formulation or delivery event, used for cross-system reference and traceability (e.g., RAT-2024-00123). Sourced from Granular Farm Management or SAP PP.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA PP Module

description: Discriminator field separating the two concerns captured in this product: 'formulation' for master ration recipes and 'delivery' for transactional feed delivery events. Drives downstream processing logic and reporting segmentation.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements of Beef Cattle

description: Weight of feed remaining uneaten in the bunk at the time of the next feeding, measured in pounds. Applicable to delivery records. Used to calculate actual intake, adjust ration quantities, and monitor animal health and appetite.
reference: Granular Farm Management; NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: Planned daily feed intake per head per day on an as-fed basis, in pounds. Applicable to formulation records. Derived from NRC nutrient requirements for the target species and production class. Drives delivery quantity planning.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; Granular Farm Management

description: Animal species for which this ration is formulated or delivered (e.g., cattle, swine, poultry). Drives NRC nutritional compliance checks and species-specific regulatory requirements under APHIS and FSIS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cattle|swine|poultry|sheep|goat|equine|other — promote to reference product]
reference: APHIS; NRC Nutrient Requirements; USDA FSIS

description: Total Digestible Nutrients (TDN) content of the ration as a percentage of dry matter. Primary energy metric for ruminant rations used in NRC compliance and ration balancing. Applicable to formulation records.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements; AOAC International

description: Timestamp when this feed ration record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: FSMA; ISO 22000; GDPR Article 5

description: Sequential version number of the ration formulation, incremented each time the recipe is revised. Enables tracking of formulation changes over time and ensures delivery events reference the correct recipe version.
reference: Granular Farm Management; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Number of days animals must not receive this medicated ration prior to slaughter to ensure antibiotic residue levels are within FDA/FSIS MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) tolerances. Applicable when antibiotic_flag is True. Critical for FSIS compliance and food safety.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 558; FSIS; MRL; APHIS

description: Primary key for the ration_ingredient association
reference: Internal - M:N Association PK

description: Foreign key linking to the feed ration formulation that contains this ingredient
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.input_catalog. Business justification: Feed ingredients must be in the approved procurement input catalog with GMO status, organic certification, and regulatory compliance data. Linking ration_ingredient to input_catalog enforces approved
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.lab_sample. Business justification: Individual feed ingredients (corn, soybean meal, distillers grains) are sampled and tested for nutritional content, mycotoxins, and contaminants before inclusion in rations. FDA FSMA Preventive Contro
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to the material master record representing the feed ingredient
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor_contract. Business justification: Each feed ration ingredient is sourced under a vendor supply contract with price and volume commitments. Linking ration_ingredient to vendor_contract enables contract-based ration cost modeling, suppl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The cost of this ingredient per unit of measure at the time this ration formulation was created or last revised. Captured at the ration-ingredient level to support feed cost allocation and historical cost tracking independent of current material_master standard_price.
reference: Feed Cost Accounting Standard

description: Indicates whether the inclusion_rate_pct for this ingredient in this ration is expressed on a dry matter (DM) basis (true) or as-fed basis (false). The same ingredient may be expressed differently across rations depending on nutritionist preference.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements

description: The percentage of this ingredient in the ration, expressed as a proportion of the total ration weight. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination — the same ingredient may have different inclusion rates across different rations.
reference: NRC Nutrient Requirements of Beef Cattle

description: Structured text or JSON-serialized list of feed ingredients included in the ration formulation, with inclusion rates on both as-fed and dry matter basis (e.g., 'corn silage 45%, distillers grains 15%, hay 20%'). Applicable to formulation records. Supports feed cost analysis and ingredient traceability. [Moved from feed_ration: The ingredient_list STRING/JSON blob on feed_ration is a denormalized encoding of the ration-ingredient M:N relationship. Once the ration_ingredient association product is in place, this attribute becomes redundant and should be deprecated. The structured ration_ingredient records replace it with properly typed, queryable, and auditable individual ingredient rows.]
reference: Granular Farm Management; FSMA Preventive Controls; APHIS

description: The order in which this ingredient is added to the TMR mixer or batch during ration preparation. Critical for mixing quality and nutritional homogeneity. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination, not to the ingredient or ration alone.
reference: TMR Mixing Protocol

description: The physical weight in kilograms of this ingredient required per batch mix of the ration. Derived from inclusion rate and batch size but stored explicitly for feed mill operations. Belongs to the ration-ingredient combination.
reference: Feed Mill Operations Standard

description: Indicates whether this ingredient in this specific ration may be substituted with an alternative ingredient when the primary ingredient is unavailable. A ration-ingredient-level flag — the same ingredient may be substitutable in one ration but not in another.
reference: Feed Formulation Policy

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each individual animal record. Serves as the primary key and universal reference across all livestock management systems.
reference: USDA NAIS (National Animal Identification System); APHIS Animal Traceability Framework

description: Reference identifier linking to the breed master record for this animal's registered or recognized breed (e.g., Angus, Hereford, Duroc, Merino). Supports genetic performance benchmarking, EPD (Expected Progeny Difference) analysis, and breed association reporting.
reference: USDA AMS Breed Standards; Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual animal acquisition costs and capitalized breeding animal depreciation require cost center assignment for livestock enterprise budgeting and USDA farm financial reporting. A livestock accoun
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: USDA-assigned Premises Identification Number (PIN) for the physical location where the animal currently resides. 10-character alphanumeric code (3-letter state abbreviation + 7 digits). Required for USDA NAIS/ADT interstate movement documentation and disease traceability.
reference: USDA APHIS Premises Registration; USDA NAIS; 9 CFR Part 86

description: Reference identifier for the herd or flock to which this animal is currently assigned. Drives management group decisions, vaccination scheduling, and regulatory reporting at the herd level. Sourced from Granular Farm Management herd master.
reference: USDA APHIS Herd Identification Standards; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Individual animals undergo USDA ante-mortem, state veterinary, and welfare inspections tracked per animal for regulatory compliance (FSIS, APHIS). A domain expert expects each animal record to referen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: animal.organic_certified is a boolean denormalization of an organic certification record. USDA NOP requires traceability from individual animals to the specific organic certification under which they
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference identifier for the current physical pen, pasture, or paddock where the animal is housed or grazing. Supports daily management, feeding operations, and biosecurity zone tracking. Linked to the facility location master.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module; USDA APHIS Premises Registration

description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the mother (dam) animal record within this same table. Enables maternal lineage tracing for genetic performance analysis, EPD calculations, and pedigree documentation. Null for purchased animals with unknown dam.
reference: Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines; USDA AMS Breed Association Standards

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Each purchased animal must reference the procurement PO for cost basis establishment, financial reconciliation, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) documentation. animal.source_vendor_id already lin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference identifier for the external vendor, auction house, or producer from whom the animal was purchased. Null for born-on-farm animals. Supports supplier traceability, biosecurity source tracking, and procurement analytics in SAP S/4HANA MM module.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule; USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Purchase price or capitalized cost basis of the animal at time of acquisition, expressed in USD. For born-on-farm animals, represents the capitalized cost of raising to weaning. Used for inventory valuation, COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation, and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP ASC 905 (Agriculture); IFRS IAS 41 (Agriculture); FASB Agricultural Accounting

description: Date the animal was acquired by the operation — either the birth date for born-on-farm animals or the purchase/transfer-in date for externally sourced animals. Used for inventory aging, cost basis calculation, and COOL documentation.
reference: USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; FSMA Traceability Rule; GAAP Inventory Accounting

description: Indicates whether the animal has never received antibiotic treatments and qualifies for 'Never Ever 3' (NE3) or antibiotic-free marketing programs. Drives premium market channel eligibility and label claim compliance.
reference: USDA FSIS Labeling Policy; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock; FDA Guidance for Industry #213

description: Actual or estimated calendar date the animal was born. Used to calculate age, determine production class eligibility, schedule vaccinations, and compute days-on-feed (DOF). Required for USDA age verification programs and COOL documentation.
reference: USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; USDA Age and Source Verification Program; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Body weight of the animal recorded at or shortly after birth, expressed in kilograms. Baseline measurement for growth performance tracking, EPD (Expected Progeny Difference) calculations, and dam/sire genetic evaluation.
reference: Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module

description: Description of the hot iron or freeze brand applied to the animal for permanent ownership identification. Includes brand symbol, location on body, and registration state. Required for cattle ownership verification in brand-law states and theft prevention.
reference: State Brand Laws (varies by state); USDA APHIS; Western States Livestock Brand Registries

description: Percentage of the animal's genetics attributable to the primary breed identified in breed_id. Expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 75.00 for 75% Angus). Used for crossbreeding program management, breed association registration eligibility, and genetic improvement analytics.
reference: Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines; USDA AMS Breed Association Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the animal was born, raised, or slaughtered as required by USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) regulations. Mandatory for retail muscle cut labeling compliance.
reference: USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; USDA AMS COOL Final Rule

description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the animal record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and FSMA traceability documentation. Populated automatically at record creation and never updated.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S; ISO 8601; SOX Internal Controls

description: Most recently recorded live body weight of the animal in kilograms. Updated at each weigh event. Used for feed ration calculation, marketing weight assessment, and growth performance monitoring. Distinct from birth weight and harvest weight.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module; Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines

description: Date the animal was removed from active inventory through harvest, sale, death, condemnation, or transfer out. Used for inventory reconciliation, mortality reporting, and lifecycle cost analysis. Null while animal is in ACTIVE disposition status.
reference: USDA FSIS 9 CFR Part 309; GAAP ASC 905; APHIS 9 CFR Part 86

description: Current disposition status representing the animal's standing in the operation. ACTIVE=currently on inventory; HARVESTED=sent to harvest/slaughter; SOLD=sold to external party; DIED=natural or accidental death; CONDEMNED=condemned at ante/post-mortem inspection; TRANSFERRED_OUT=moved to another facility. Core lifecycle status field for inventory reconciliation and FSIS compliance.
reference: USDA FSIS 9 CFR Part 309; APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Visual alphanumeric identifier printed on the physical ear tag applied to the animal. Used for visual identification in the field and pen. May be a farm-assigned number or official USDA 840 tag number. Supports COOL country-of-origin labeling traceability.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 USC 1638

description: Electronic Identification number as printed on the visual ear tag and encoded in the RFID transponder. May differ from the raw RFID tag number in formatted display. Used in John Deere Operations Center and Granular Farm Management for animal-level record linkage.
reference: ISO 11784/11785; USDA APHIS Animal Traceability Rule (9 CFR Part 86)

description: Indicates whether the animal is derived from genetically modified genetics (GMO), confirmed non-GMO, or status is unknown. Required for non-GMO certification programs, organic certification under USDA NOP, and export documentation to GMO-sensitive markets.
reference: USDA NOP (National Organic Program) 7 CFR Part 205; Non-GMO Project Standard; EU Regulation 1829/2003

description: Current veterinary health status of the animal. HEALTHY=no active health concerns; SICK=active illness under observation; QUARANTINE=isolated for biosecurity or regulatory reasons; TREATED=currently under veterinary treatment with active withdrawal period; RECOVERED=returned to healthy status post-treatment; CONDEMNED=condemned by veterinary inspection.
reference: USDA FSIS 9 CFR Part 309; APHIS Veterinary Accreditation; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Health Module

description: Calendar date of the most recent live weight measurement. Used to assess recency of weight data, calculate average daily gain (ADG) since last weigh, and trigger re-weigh alerts when interval exceeds operational thresholds.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module; Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines

description: Current biological and production lifecycle stage of the animal. Drives nutritional requirements, veterinary protocols, and management interventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NEONATAL|WEANED|GROWING|FINISHING|MATURE|GESTATING|LACTATING|DRY — promote to reference product]
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Base Module; NRC Nutrient Requirements of Domestic Animals

description: Official USDA 840-prefix 15-digit national uniform ear tag number assigned under the USDA Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) framework. Required for interstate movement of cattle, bison, and other covered species. Begins with country code 840 (USA).
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; USDA Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) Rule

description: Indicates whether the animal was born on the operation, purchased from an external source, or transferred in from another company-owned facility. Critical for COOL country-of-origin labeling, FSMA traceability, and biosecurity risk classification.
reference: USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; FSMA Produce Safety Rule; APHIS 9 CFR Part 86

description: Number of times a female animal has given birth (parturitions). Parity 0 = never calved/farrowed/lambed; Parity 1 = first calf/litter; etc. Used for reproductive performance analysis, culling decisions, and production benchmarking for dairy and cow-calf operations.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Reproductive Module; Dairy Herd Improvement Association (DHIA) Standards

description: Operational production class that defines the animal's role in the production system. Determines applicable management protocols, feed rations, performance benchmarks, and marketing channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: COW_CALF|STOCKER|FEEDLOT|DAIRY|BREEDING|SHOW|SEEDSTOCK|REPLACEMENT_HEIFER — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA NASS Livestock Survey; Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Official registration number issued by a recognized breed association (e.g., American Angus Association, National Swine Registry) for purebred or registered animals. Required for seedstock marketing, EPD publication, and breed association reporting.
reference: American Angus Association; National Swine Registry; American Hereford Association; USDA AMS

description: 15-digit ISO 11784/11785 compliant RFID electronic tag number embedded in the animal's ear tag or bolus. Primary electronic identifier used for automated reading at chutes, scales, and gates. Required for USDA NAIS traceability and APHIS interstate movement compliance.
reference: ISO 11784/11785; USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; USDA NAIS

description: Biological sex and reproductive status of the animal. M=Male (intact bull/boar/ram), F=Female (cow/sow/ewe), C=Castrated male (steer/barrow/wether), S=Spayed female, B=Barrow. Drives production class assignment, feed ration, and harvest yield expectations.
reference: USDA NASS Livestock Survey Codes; Beef Improvement Federation (BIF) Guidelines

description: Standardized species classification of the animal. Drives applicable regulatory frameworks (FSIS, APHIS), withdrawal period rules, and feed ration formulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BOVINE|SWINE|OVINE|CAPRINE|EQUINE|POULTRY|CERVID|BISON|OTHER — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA APHIS; FSIS 9 CFR; Codex Alimentarius

description: Permanent alphanumeric tattoo applied inside the ear or on the body for permanent identification, particularly for swine, sheep, and registered cattle. Used as a backup identifier when ear tags are lost and for breed association registration.
reference: USDA APHIS Swine Identification Standards; National Swine Registry; American Angus Association

description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent modification to the animal record. Used for change data capture (CDC), data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and Granular Farm Management, and audit trail compliance under SOX and FSMA.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S; SOX Section 404; ISO 8601

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Average live weight per individual animal in pounds at the time of transfer. Derived operationally at point of transaction (scale ticket) and stored as a business fact. Supports performance benchmarking, feed efficiency analysis, and grading decisions.
reference: USDA AMS Livestock Reporting; Granular Farm Management

description: Certificate number issued by the state brand inspection authority verifying ownership of branded livestock at time of transfer. Required in brand law states (e.g., TX, CO, MT, WY) for cattle and horse transactions to establish legal title.
reference: State Brand Laws (e.g., Texas Agriculture Code Ch. 142; Colorado CRS 35-41); USDA APHIS

description: Breed designation of the animals in the transaction (e.g., Angus, Hereford, Duroc, Hampshire). Relevant for cost basis accounting, genetic program tracking, and premium pricing in sales contracts.
reference: USDA APHIS; Granular Farm Management; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Timestamp when this animal transaction record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for data ingestion pipelines from source systems (SAP S/4HANA, Granular Farm Management).
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction value (e.g., USD for US Dollar, CAD for Canadian Dollar, MXN for Mexican Peso). Supports multi-currency operations for cross-border livestock imports and exports.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The pricing and grading basis applied to outbound sale transactions. Indicates whether the sale price is based on live weight, dressed weight, USDA yield grade, USDA quality grade, or a formula-based pricing arrangement. Populated only for sale transactions.
reference: USDA AMS Livestock Grading 7 CFR Part 54; USDA Quality and Yield Grades; FSIS

description: Number of individual animals included in this transaction. Used for inventory reconciliation, cost-per-head calculations, and APHIS movement reporting. Combined with weight and price fields to derive per-unit economics.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; SAP S/4HANA MM; Granular Farm Management

description: Official USDA APHIS Veterinary Services (VS) health certificate number issued by an accredited veterinarian certifying the health status of animals at time of movement. Required for interstate and international livestock shipments per 9 CFR Part 86.
reference: USDA APHIS VS 9 CFR Part 86; APHIS Form VS 1-27; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Batch or lot tracking identifier assigned to the group of animals in this transaction for traceability purposes. Supports APHIS animal movement traceability, FSIS recall readiness, and COOL chain-of-custody documentation from farm to harvest.
reference: USDA APHIS; FSIS 9 CFR Part 320; FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special conditions, or remarks associated with the livestock transaction. May include auction lot descriptions, special handling instructions, or negotiated contract terms not captured in structured fields.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD; Granular Farm Management

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country of origin for the animals. Required for COOL compliance, import permit processing, and APHIS international traceability. Populated for all transactions; defaults to USA for domestic transfers.
reference: COOL 7 CFR Part 60; USDA APHIS VS; CBP Import Regulations; ISO 3166-1

description: Two-letter US state code of the origin premises from which the animals were shipped. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) chain-of-custody documentation and APHIS interstate movement reporting.
reference: COOL 7 CFR Part 60; USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; FSMA

description: Purchase or sale price expressed per hundredweight (CWT = 100 lbs) in USD. Standard pricing basis for cattle and hog transactions in North American livestock markets. Populated when the transaction is priced on a weight basis.
reference: USDA AMS Livestock Market News; SAP S/4HANA SD/MM; DTN Market Data

description: Purchase or sale price expressed on a per-head basis in USD. Populated when the transaction is priced per individual animal rather than per CWT. Mutually exclusive with price_per_cwt for a given transaction; one or both may be populated depending on transaction channel.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/MM; USDA AMS Livestock Market News; Granular Farm Management

description: SAP S/4HANA MM purchase order number associated with this livestock purchase transaction. Populated for inbound (purchase) transactions only. Enables three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) in accounts payable processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; GAAP; Granular Farm Management

description: Number of days the purchased animals must remain in quarantine isolation before integration into the main herd or flock. Populated only for purchase transactions where quarantine_required = True. Supports antibiotic withdrawal period tracking and biosecurity compliance.
reference: USDA APHIS VS 9 CFR Part 93; State Veterinarian Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether the purchased animals are subject to a mandatory quarantine period upon arrival at the receiving premises. Applicable to purchase transactions only. Drives receiving pen assignment and health monitoring workflows.
reference: USDA APHIS VS 9 CFR Part 93; State Veterinarian Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Range or list of RFID electronic identification tag numbers (e.g., 840 USDA official ear tags) assigned to the animals in this transaction. Supports APHIS electronic animal identification traceability requirements and individual animal tracking.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 86; USDA 840 RFID Tag Standard; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Biological species classification of the animals involved in the transaction (e.g., bovine for cattle, swine for hogs, ovine for sheep). Drives regulatory reporting requirements, withdrawal period tracking, and FSIS/APHIS compliance rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|swine|ovine|caprine|equine|poultry|other — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA APHIS; FSIS 9 CFR; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Gross total monetary value of the livestock transfer in USD, representing the full purchase cost (for inbound) or gross sale revenue (for outbound). Used for cost basis accounting, revenue recognition, and enterprise profitability analysis in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; GAAP ASC 330 (Inventory); IFRS IAS 41 (Agriculture)

description: Aggregate live weight in pounds of all animals in the transaction at the time of transfer (purchase weight for inbound; sale weight for outbound). Used for CWT (hundredweight) pricing calculations, cost basis accounting, and yield analysis.
reference: USDA AMS Livestock Reporting; SAP S/4HANA MM/SD; Granular Farm Management

description: The commercial channel through which the livestock transfer was executed. Direct = farm-to-farm; Auction = physical livestock auction market; Video Auction = remote video-based auction (e.g., Superior Livestock); Formula = price tied to a market index or formula; Private Treaty = negotiated private sale.
reference: USDA AMS Livestock Market News; Packers and Stockyards Act 7 USC 181

description: The principal real-world business event date on which the livestock transfer (purchase or sale) was executed — i.e., the date of ownership transfer or market transaction. Distinct from transport or arrival dates.
reference: USDA APHIS Traceability; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 CFR Part 60; FSMA

description: Indicates whether this record represents an inbound livestock purchase (animals acquired from an external party) or an outbound livestock sale (animals transferred to an external buyer). Drives which conditional attributes are populated (e.g., quarantine period for purchases; grade/yield basis for sales).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Module; APHIS Premises Identification

description: Externally-known business identifier for the livestock transfer event, typically sourced from SAP S/4HANA (purchase order number for inbound or sales order number for outbound) or the auction/market reference number. Used for cross-system reconciliation and counterparty communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Module; APHIS Traceability Standards

description: Current workflow state of the livestock transfer event. Tracks the lifecycle from initial draft through confirmation, transit, completion, or cancellation. Drives downstream accounting and traceability processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/MM Module; APHIS Traceability Standards

description: Name of the licensed livestock transport carrier responsible for moving the animals between premises. Required for APHIS traceability, FMCSA compliance, and freight cost allocation.
reference: USDA APHIS; FMCSA 49 CFR; Animal Welfare Act 7 USC 2131

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this animal transaction record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: Primary key for breed
Self-referencing FK on breed (parent_breed_id)
Typical birth weight in kilograms for offspring of this breed, used for calving/farrowing management and health monitoring.
Expected daily weight gain in kilograms during the growth phase, used for performance benchmarking and feed efficiency calculations.
Expected number of offspring per birth event for the breed, used for production planning and facility capacity management.
Typical adult body weight in kilograms for the breed at maturity, used for feed planning and facility design.
Primary production purpose classification of the breed, indicating its intended agricultural use and economic function.
Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the breed across systems and external registries. Used for regulatory reporting and industry data exchange.
Official common name of the breed as recognized by breed associations and industry standards.
Current lifecycle status of the breed indicating its availability and conservation state within breeding programs.
Natural or managed breeding cycle pattern for the breed, indicating optimal reproductive timing for breeding program management.

Characteristic coat color and pattern description for the breed, used for visual identification and breed verification.
Conservation status classification indicating the urgency of genetic preservation efforts for the breed.

Notes on breeds commonly crossed with this breed and expected hybrid vigor outcomes, used for breeding program design.
Summary of known genetic resistance or susceptibility to common diseases, used for health management and breeding selection.
Date from which this breed record became active and available for use in breeding programs and animal registration.

Date on which this breed record was retired or became inactive. Null for currently active breeds.

Summary of key genetic markers or DNA characteristics used for breed verification and parentage validation in breeding programs.
Average length of pregnancy in days for the breed, used for breeding program scheduling and calving/farrowing date estimation.
Indicates whether the breed meets eligibility criteria for certified organic production programs.
Indicates whether the breed is classified as a heritage or traditional breed eligible for conservation programs and specialty marketing.
Date and time when this breed record was most recently updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.
Typical age in days at which animals of this breed reach market readiness, used for production cycle planning and facility turnover scheduling.
Optimal live weight in kilograms at which animals of this breed are typically marketed or harvested, used for production scheduling and economic planning.
Average annual milk yield in kilograms for dairy breeds, used for production forecasting and economic analysis. Null for non-dairy breeds.
Additional free-text notes or comments about the breed, including historical context, special considerations, or operational guidance.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic origin or development location of the breed.
Detailed description of distinguishing physical traits and conformation standards for the breed, used for breed verification and selection.
Name of the official breed registry or association that maintains pedigree records and breed standards for this breed.
Any import/export restrictions, quarantine requirements, or regulatory limitations applicable to this breed, used for compliance and trade planning.
Taxonomic scientific name of the breed, including genus and species classification.
Any unique care, housing, or management requirements specific to this breed, used for facility design and staff training.
Primary species classification of the breed, categorizing the animal type for operational and regulatory purposes.
Typical behavioral disposition of the breed, used for handler safety planning and animal welfare management.
Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a managed herd or flock grouping within the livestock management system. Primary key for all animal-level record associations.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary breed or genetic line of the herd (e.g., Angus, Holstein, Duroc, Cornish Cross, Merino). Influences production performance benchmarks, feed ration planning, and genetic improvement programs tracked in breeding records.
reference: USDA National Animal Germplasm Program; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Earliest expiration date across all active certifications (organic, GAP, GLOBALG.A.P.) held by this herd. Used to trigger recertification workflows and prevent lapsed-certification sales into premium market channels.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Operational classification of the herd's production stage or management purpose (e.g., breeding, finishing, nursery, cow-calf, stocker, replacement). Drives feed ration formulation, housing allocation, and performance KPI benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: breeding|finishing|nursery|grow-out|cow-calf|stocker|replacement|farrowing|lactating|dry|other — promote to reference product]
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard; USDA APHIS Herd Management Guidelines

description: System timestamp recording when this herd master record was first created in the FMIS or ERP system. Supports data lineage, FSMA traceability audit trails, and SOX financial reporting controls for livestock asset records.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); SOX Section 404; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Actual number of live animals currently in the herd as of the last inventory reconciliation. Drives feed ration calculations, housing capacity utilization, and USDA NASS livestock inventory reporting. Updated upon births, deaths, purchases, and transfers.
reference: USDA NASS Livestock Inventory; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard; Granular Farm Management

description: Current disease surveillance status of the herd as determined by USDA APHIS or state veterinarian. 'confirmed' status triggers mandatory reporting, movement restrictions, and potential depopulation under the National Animal Health Emergency Management System (NAHEMS).
reference: USDA APHIS Animal Disease Traceability Rule (9 CFR Part 86); NAHEMS; OIE Terrestrial Animal Health Code

description: Calendar date on which the herd was fully dispersed through sale, harvest, or depopulation. Null for active herds. Used to close the herd lifecycle record and trigger final regulatory reporting to USDA APHIS.
reference: USDA APHIS ADT Rule; FSIS Ante-Mortem Inspection Records

description: Projected date when the herd or a cohort of animals is expected to reach market weight and be ready for sale or harvest. Used for sales order planning, slaughter scheduling, and logistics coordination with TMS and WMS systems.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; FSIS Ante-Mortem Inspection Scheduling; Granular Farm Management

description: Calendar date on which this herd grouping was formally established or first registered in the FMIS. Used for herd age tracking, performance period calculations, and audit trail compliance under FSMA traceability requirements.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S); USDA APHIS ADT Rule

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the herd, used in operational systems such as SAP S/4HANA livestock modules, FMIS records, and regulatory submissions to USDA APHIS. Serves as the cross-system reference key.
reference: USDA APHIS National Animal Identification System (NAIS); GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Human-readable descriptive name assigned to the herd or flock grouping (e.g., 'North Pasture Angus Cow-Calf Herd', 'Broiler House 4 Flock'). Used in operational reports, Granular Farm Management, and John Deere Operations Center displays.
reference: Granular Farm Management — Livestock Module; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the herd record. 'active' indicates operational management; 'quarantined' triggers APHIS disease containment protocols; 'dispersed' indicates the herd has been sold or depopulated; 'pending' indicates formation in progress.
reference: USDA APHIS Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) Rule; FSIS Ante-Mortem Inspection

description: Primary housing or production system type for the herd (e.g., pasture, confinement, feedlot, free-range). Determines applicable animal welfare standards, GAP certification requirements, and environmental compliance obligations under EPA regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pasture|confinement|semi-confinement|feedlot|free-range|barn|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard; EPA CAFO Regulations (40 CFR Part 122); Animal Welfare Act

description: Indicates whether this herd is managed under a no-antibiotics-ever (NAE) or raised-without-antibiotics (RWA) program. Drives treatment protocol restrictions, veterinary prescription controls, and premium market channel eligibility.
reference: FDA Guidance for Industry #213 — Judicious Use of Antimicrobials; USDA FSIS Labeling; GFSI

description: Indicates whether this herd operation holds a current Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) certification. Enables premium market access and satisfies retailer food safety audit requirements under GFSI-recognized schemes.
reference: USDA GAP/GHP Audit Program; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Indicates whether this herd holds a current GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) certification for livestock. Required for export market access to EU and other international buyers demanding GFSI-recognized certification.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) Standard — Livestock Module; GFSI Benchmarking

description: Indicates whether this herd is managed under a non-GMO feed program, qualifying for Non-GMO Project Verified or equivalent labeling claims on derived products. Affects feed procurement specifications and supply chain segregation.
reference: USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (7 CFR Part 66); Non-GMO Project Standard

description: Indicates whether this herd is certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). When True, enforces organic feed sourcing, prohibits synthetic antibiotic use, and triggers organic premium pricing in sales contracts.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) — 7 CFR Part 205; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service

description: Date of the most recent veterinary health inspection or herd health assessment. Used to ensure compliance with VCPR requirements, schedule next health review, and support APHIS accredited veterinarian documentation.
reference: USDA APHIS Veterinary Accreditation Program; FDA Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD); GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Rolling percentage of animals in the herd that have been treated for illness during the current production period. Tracks herd health status, antibiotic use patterns, and biosecurity effectiveness. Supports FSIS residue avoidance program reporting.
reference: FSIS Residue Avoidance Program; USDA APHIS Veterinary Services; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Rolling mortality rate expressed as a percentage of the herd, calculated over the current production period. Key animal welfare and production efficiency KPI; triggers veterinary investigation when thresholds are exceeded. Reported to USDA NASS.
reference: USDA NASS Livestock Loss Survey; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard; APHIS Veterinary Biologics

description: Indicates whether this herd is currently subject to a USDA APHIS or state-imposed movement restriction order (e.g., due to disease quarantine, regulatory hold, or embargo). When True, blocks interstate movement documentation and sale order processing.
reference: USDA APHIS Animal Disease Traceability Rule (9 CFR Part 86); FSIS Ante-Mortem Inspection; State Veterinarian Orders

description: Total pasture or grazing area allocated to this herd in hectares. Used for stocking density calculations, EPA CAFO nutrient management planning, and USDA NRCS conservation program compliance. Integrated with Esri ArcGIS land parcel data.
reference: EPA CAFO Regulations (40 CFR Part 122); USDA NRCS Nutrient Management Practice Standard 590; GLOBALG.A.P. Livestock Standard

description: Primary agricultural production purpose of the herd (e.g., beef, dairy, swine, poultry, sheep). description: Free-text description of the observable clinical signs or symptoms presented by the animal or group at the time of treatment, as recorded by the attending veterinarian or farm staff. Supports HACCP documentation and veterinary case history.
reference: HACCP 21 CFR Part 120; USDA APHIS Disease Surveillance; ISO 22000

description: System-generated timestamp recording when this health treatment record was first created in the FMIS or SAP QM system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping compliance.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Controls; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements 21 CFR Part 1

description: Standardized veterinary diagnosis code identifying the disease or condition being treated, aligned with USDA APHIS disease reporting codes or ICD-10-CM veterinary adaptations. Used for APHIS disease surveillance reporting and herd health analytics.
reference: USDA APHIS Disease Surveillance; ICD-10-CM Veterinary Adaptation; NAHMS (National Animal Health Monitoring System)

description: Numeric quantity of the drug or vaccine product administered per animal or per unit of body weight, as prescribed. Used in conjunction with dosage_unit for complete dosage documentation required by FDA VFD and FSIS withdrawal compliance.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance

description: Unit of measure for the administered dosage amount (e.g., mL, mg, mg/kg body weight). Required for accurate withdrawal period calculation and FDA VFD documentation.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance

description: Physical ear tag identifier of the treated animal, denormalized from the animal master for rapid field lookup and cross-referencing with paper-based treatment logs. Supports USDA NAIS (National Animal Identification System) traceability.
reference: USDA APHIS National Animal Identification System (NAIS); COOL 7 CFR Part 60

description: Calculated date on which the animal first becomes eligible for harvest or product collection after completing the mandatory withdrawal period. Derived from treatment_date plus withdrawal_days; stored as a business-critical compliance field to prevent premature slaughter and FSIS violations.
reference: FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; USDA Residue Avoidance Program; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health

description: Free-text field for additional observations, special instructions, or contextual information recorded by the veterinarian or farm staff at the time of treatment. Supports HACCP documentation and veterinary case history completeness.
reference: HACCP 21 CFR Part 120; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health Standard; ISO 22000

description: Count of individual animals included in this treatment event. Value is 1 for individual animal treatments; greater than 1 for group-level treatments such as metaphylaxis or arrival processing. Used for drug inventory reconciliation and treatment cost allocation.
reference: FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; SAP S/4HANA MM Drug Inventory; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health

description: Unit of measure for the total_product_used quantity (e.g., mL, mg, doses). Required for drug inventory reconciliation and cost reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; FDA CVM Drug Label Requirements

description: Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an actual treatment event applied to an animal or group (treatment_instance) or a standardized health management protocol template used as a reference for future treatments (protocol_template). Drives downstream processing logic and reporting.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health and Welfare Standard; USDA APHIS Veterinary Biologics

description: Indicates whether this treatment record represents a re-treatment of an animal that did not respond adequately to a prior treatment for the same condition. True when this is a follow-up treatment; False for initial treatments. Supports antibiotic stewardship and treatment efficacy analytics.
reference: USDA APHIS Disease Surveillance; Antibiotic Stewardship Program Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health

description: Electronic RFID tag identifier associated with the treated animal, used for automated identification in chute-side treatment systems and integration with John Deere Operations Center livestock modules. Supports ISO 11784/11785 RFID standards.
reference: ISO 11784/11785 RFID Animal Identification; USDA APHIS NAIS

description: The method by which the drug or vaccine was delivered to the animal (e.g., subcutaneous injection, intramuscular injection, oral drench, topical pour-on, intranasal spray). Affects withdrawal period applicability and FSIS residue compliance.
reference: FDA CVM Drug Approval; FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health

description: Total quantity of the drug or vaccine product consumed for this treatment event, calculated as dosage_amount multiplied by number_of_animals_treated. Stored for drug inventory depletion recording in SAP MM and cost-of-treatment reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module Drug Inventory; FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance

description: Total cost of the treatment event in the farm's operating currency, including drug product cost and veterinary service fees where applicable. Used for herd health cost-of-production analysis, OPEX reporting, and Granular Farm Management financial planning integration.
reference: Granular Farm Management Financial Planning; SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: The calendar date on which the veterinary treatment, vaccination, or health intervention was administered to the animal or group. This is the principal real-world event date used for withdrawal period calculations, FSIS compliance, and HACCP records.
reference: FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; USDA APHIS Disease Surveillance; HACCP 21 CFR Part 120

description: Externally-known business identifier for the treatment event or protocol template, used for cross-referencing with veterinary records, VFD documentation, and SAP QM notifications. Format typically follows farm-assigned numbering conventions (e.g., TRT-2024-00123).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; FDA Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) Regulations 21 CFR Part 558

description: Clinical outcome of the treatment as assessed at the end of the treatment course or at follow-up evaluation. Used for herd health analytics, treatment efficacy reporting, and antibiotic stewardship program monitoring.
reference: USDA APHIS Disease Surveillance; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health; Antibiotic Stewardship Program Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle state of the treatment record. 'draft' indicates a record in preparation; 'active' indicates an ongoing treatment; 'completed' indicates the treatment course is finished; 'cancelled' indicates the treatment was voided; 'pending_approval' indicates awaiting veterinarian sign-off for VFD-regulated products.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Precise date and time at which the treatment was administered, captured for IoT-integrated barn management systems and time-sensitive drug administration protocols requiring exact timing records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Classification of the treatment purpose. 'therapeutic' treats an existing disease; 'prophylactic' prevents disease in healthy animals; 'vaccination' administers a biological vaccine; 'metaphylaxis' treats an entire group when a portion shows disease; 'arrival_processing' covers standard processing at receiving; 'parasite_control' targets internal or external parasites.
reference: FDA Center for Veterinary Medicine (CVM); USDA APHIS Veterinary Biologics; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health

description: For protocol_template records, describes the specific conditions that trigger execution of this protocol (e.g., 'rectal temperature > 104°F', 'morbidity rate > 10% in pen within 7 days', 'BRD pull rate exceeds threshold'). Null for treatment_instance records.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health Standard; USDA APHIS Herd Health Protocol Management

description: System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to this health treatment record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory compliance.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Controls; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements 21 CFR Part 1

description: The date on which the supervising veterinarian reviewed and approved this treatment record or protocol template. Required for VFD-regulated antibiotic treatments and GLOBALG.A.P. animal health certification audits.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; GLOBALG.A.P. Animal Health Standard; HACCP 21 CFR Part 120

description: State-issued veterinary license number of the attending or supervising veterinarian responsible for the treatment authorization. Required for VFD documentation and VCPR compliance. Null when treatment is administered by non-veterinary farm staff under a standing order.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; VCPR Requirements; State Veterinary Practice Acts

description: The official Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) document reference number issued by the licensed veterinarian authorizing the use of a VFD antibiotic in animal feed. Mandatory for medically important antimicrobials used in feed per FDA VFD regulations. Null for non-VFD treatments.
reference: FDA VFD Regulations 21 CFR Part 558; FDA Guidance for Industry #213 Antimicrobial Resistance

description: The mandatory number of days that must elapse after the last treatment before the animal or its products (meat, milk, eggs) may enter the food supply, as specified on the drug label. Also referred to as Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for meat animals. Critical for FSIS residue avoidance compliance.
reference: FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; FDA CVM Drug Label Requirements; USDA Residue Avoidance Program

description: Current compliance status of the animal with respect to drug withdrawal period. 'in_withdrawal' indicates the animal cannot yet be harvested; 'cleared' indicates the withdrawal period has elapsed; 'not_applicable' applies to non-withdrawal products or protocol templates.
reference: FSIS Antibiotic Withdrawal Compliance; USDA Residue Avoidance Program

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the veterinary prescription or Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) authorizing use of the vaccine, particularly for prescription-only biologics. Null for over-the-counter vaccines.
reference: FDA Veterinary Feed Directive 21 CFR Part 558; APHIS 9 CFR Part 160

description: The calendar date on which the vaccine was administered to the animal or group. Principal business event date for regulatory reporting, withdrawal period calculation, and booster scheduling.
reference: APHIS Animal Disease Traceability; FSIS HACCP 9 CFR Part 417; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Name or identifier of the farm, barn, pen, or facility location where the vaccination was administered. Supports geographic disease surveillance, APHIS premises identification, and GLOBALG.A.P. site-level audit trails.
reference: APHIS Premises Identification; USDA NAIS; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 AF.1

description: The anatomical route by which the vaccine was administered (e.g., subcutaneous, intramuscular, intranasal). Must match the label-approved route of administration per APHIS biologics regulations.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3; AVMA Guidelines

description: Precise date and time the vaccine was administered, captured from the farm management system or mobile device. Supports time-sensitive withdrawal period calculations and audit trail integrity.
reference: FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112; ISO 22000:2018

description: Free-text description of any adverse reaction observed following vaccination (e.g., anaphylaxis, injection site swelling, fever). Populated only when adverse_reaction_observed is True. Supports APHIS CVB adverse event reporting.
