description: Indicates whether an adverse reaction was observed in the animal(s) following vaccination. A value of True triggers creation of an adverse event report to USDA APHIS Center for Veterinary Biologics (CVB) and initiates veterinary follow-up.
reference: USDA APHIS CVB Adverse Event Reporting; 9 CFR Part 113; FDA 21 CFR Part 600

description: Count of animals vaccinated in a group/herd-level vaccination event. Populated when herd_group_id is set and individual animal records are not created. Used for herd immunity coverage calculations.
reference: APHIS Herd Vaccination Reporting; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Calculated or label-specified date by which the next booster dose must be administered to maintain protective immunity. Drives automated alerts in the herd vaccination schedule and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance monitoring.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3; APHIS Vaccination Program Standards

description: Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (COA) issued by the vaccine manufacturer for the specific lot, confirming potency, purity, and safety testing results. Corresponds to COA in common agricultural jargon.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; FDA 21 CFR Part 600.12

description: Indicates whether the required cold chain storage conditions were maintained for the vaccine from receipt through administration. A value of False triggers a non-compliance flag and may invalidate the vaccination record for regulatory purposes.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; WHO Cold Chain Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Current compliance state of this vaccination record against the herd vaccination schedule and regulatory requirements. Drives APHIS disease surveillance alerts and GLOBALG.A.P. certification audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliant|non_compliant|pending|overdue|waived|under_review — promote to reference product]
reference: APHIS Animal Disease Traceability; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3; FSIS 9 CFR Part 417

description: System timestamp when this vaccination record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2); ISO 22000:2018 Clause 8

description: The disease, pathogen, or condition the vaccine is intended to prevent or control (e.g., 'Bovine Respiratory Disease', 'Porcine Reproductive and Respiratory Syndrome', 'Newcastle Disease'). Supports disease surveillance analytics and APHIS reportable disease tracking.
reference: APHIS Animal Disease Traceability; OIE Terrestrial Animal Health Code; USDA NAHMS

description: Volume of vaccine administered in milliliters (mL) per animal or per dose. Must conform to label-approved dosage. Deviations from label dosage require veterinary authorization and are flagged for compliance review.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; FDA 21 CFR Part 600; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Sequential dose number within a multi-dose vaccination series (e.g., 1 = primary, 2 = booster). Enables tracking of vaccination series completion and booster compliance.
reference: APHIS Vaccination Program Standards; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Official ear tag or visual identification number of the individual animal at time of vaccination. Denormalized for rapid lookup and regulatory reporting without joining to the animal master. Supports APHIS Animal Disease Traceability (ADT) requirements.
reference: USDA APHIS Animal Disease Traceability 9 CFR Part 86; COOL 7 CFR Part 60

description: The earliest date on which the animal may be harvested for human consumption, calculated as administration_date plus withdrawal_period_days. Critical for FSIS pre-harvest food safety compliance and scheduling harvest operations.
reference: FSIS 9 CFR Part 309; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Specific anatomical location on the animal where the vaccine was injected (e.g., 'neck', 'behind ear', 'rump'). Supports Beef Quality Assurance (BQA) compliance and carcass quality tracking to avoid injection site lesions.
reference: USDA Beef Quality Assurance (BQA) Program; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Free-text field for additional observations, deviations from standard protocol, animal behavior notes, or other contextual information recorded by the administering technician or supervising veterinarian.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3; ISO 22000:2018 Clause 8.9

description: Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this vaccination event, used in veterinary documentation, APHIS reporting, and GLOBALG.A.P. audit trails. Format: VAC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
reference: APHIS Animal Disease Traceability; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 AF.11

description: RFID electronic identification number of the animal at time of vaccination, conforming to ISO 11784/11785 standards. Enables automated identification during chute-side vaccination and integrates with John Deere Operations Center livestock tracking.
reference: USDA APHIS ADT 9 CFR Part 86; ISO 11784/11785 RFID Animal Identification

description: USDA-assigned serial number for the specific biologics product lot, distinct from the manufacturer lot number. Required for APHIS Center for Veterinary Biologics (CVB) compliance and product release verification.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113.5; CVB Biologics Regulations

description: Species of the animal(s) vaccinated. Determines applicable regulatory framework (APHIS, FSIS), withdrawal period tables, and label compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bovine|porcine|poultry|ovine|caprine|equine|aquaculture|other — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR; FSIS 9 CFR Part 381; OIE Terrestrial Animal Health Code

description: Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the vaccine was stored prior to administration. Verified against label-required cold chain specifications (typically 2–8°C for refrigerated biologics). Out-of-range values trigger cold chain breach alerts.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; WHO Cold Chain Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to this vaccination record. Supports change detection, data quality monitoring, and regulatory audit requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2); FSMA 21 CFR Part 117

description: Expiration date printed on the vaccine vial or packaging. Used to verify that the vaccine was not expired at time of administration and to flag non-compliant records for regulatory review.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; FDA 21 CFR Part 600.12

description: Biological classification of the vaccine formulation. Determines handling requirements, storage conditions, and regulatory classification under APHIS biologics licensing.
reference: USDA APHIS 9 CFR Part 113; CVB Veterinary Biologics Regulations

description: Number of days that must elapse after vaccination before the animal or its products (meat, milk, eggs) may enter the food supply. Derived from the vaccine label and used to calculate the earliest eligible harvest date for FSIS pre-harvest food safety compliance.
reference: FSIS 9 CFR Part 309; FDA 21 CFR Part 530; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v5.4 LB.3

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each veterinary prescription or Veterinary Feed Directive (VFD) record. Serves as the primary key for the veterinary_prescription data product.

description: Reference to the specific animal or group of animals for which the prescription was issued. Links to the livestock animal master record.

description: Reference to the licensed veterinarian (DVM) who issued the prescription. Required for VCPR validation and FDA VFD compliance.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the herd or flock for which the prescription applies when issued at the group level rather than individual animal level.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The average or representative body weight in kilograms of the animals at the time of prescription. Used for weight-based dosage calculations and to verify appropriate dosing.
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; FSIS Antibiotic Stewardship

description: The animal species for which the drug is approved and prescribed (e.g., cattle, swine, poultry, sheep, goats). Must align with FDA label approval or AMDUCA extra-label use authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cattle|swine|poultry|sheep|goats|equine|aquaculture — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; AMDUCA Extra-Label Drug Use; APHIS

description: The date and time when this veterinary prescription record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FSIS record retention compliance (VFD records must be retained for 2 years).
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558.6(c) Record Retention; FSMA Recordkeeping Requirements; SOX Internal Controls

description: The numeric quantity of drug to be administered per dose or per unit of feed (e.g., grams per ton of feed, mg per kg body weight). The principal quantitative fact for this prescription.
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558.6(b)(6)

description: The frequency or schedule at which the prescribed drug is to be administered (e.g., once daily, continuous feed, single dose). Required on the prescription label per FDA regulations.
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558.6(b)(6); FDA Animal Drug Regulations

description: The unit of measure for the prescribed dosage amount (e.g., g/ton for medicated feed, mg/kg for injectable drugs). Required for accurate compounding and dispensing. reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; USP Veterinary Drug Standards

description: Antimicrobial or pharmacological class of the prescribed drug (e.g., tetracycline, macrolide, ionophore, beta-lactam). Used for antibiotic stewardship analytics and WHO critically important antimicrobial (CIA) classification tracking.

description: The US state in which the prescribing veterinarian holds their active license. Required for cross-state regulatory compliance verification.

description: The number of days eggs must be withheld from sale following drug administration in laying poultry. Applicable only for poultry operations; null for non-poultry species.

description: Indicates whether this prescription involves extra-label drug use (use of an FDA-approved drug in a manner not specified on the approved label). Extra-label use requires a valid VCPR and is regulated under AMDUCA (Animal Medicinal Drug Use Clarification Act).

description: The disease, condition, or clinical indication for which the drug is being prescribed (e.g., bovine respiratory disease, necrotic enteritis, mastitis prevention). Required for VCPR documentation and FSIS antibiotic use justification.

description: The calendar date on which the veterinarian issued the prescription or VFD. This is the principal real-world event date for the prescription transaction and is required on all VFD documents per FDA regulation.

description: The number of days milk must be withheld from sale following drug administration in dairy animals. Separate from meat withdrawal period as milk and meat withdrawal periods often differ for the same drug.
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; FSIS Residue Avoidance; Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards

description: The total count of animals covered under this prescription or VFD. Required on VFD documents per FDA regulation and used for dosage calculation and antibiotic use density metrics.
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558.6(b)(7); FSIS Antibiotic Stewardship

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the prescription or VFD at issuance. Used for regulatory filing, pharmacy dispensing, and audit trail purposes.
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558.6; FSIS Antibiotic Stewardship Documentation

description: Current lifecycle state of the veterinary prescription. Active indicates the prescription is valid and may be dispensed; expired indicates the expiration date has passed; cancelled indicates the prescribing veterinarian voided the order; filled indicates the full authorized quantity has been dispensed.
reference: FDA VFD Rule 21 CFR Part 558; FSIS Antibiotic Stewardship

description: Classification of the prescription by regulatory category. VFD (Veterinary Feed Directive) applies to medicated feed products; prescription_drug covers legend drugs requiring a valid VCPR; OTC_extra_label covers over-the-counter drugs used in an extra-label manner; compounded covers compounded preparations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VFD|prescription_drug|OTC_extra_label|compounded — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Parts 514, 558; AMDUCA Extra-Label Drug Use

description: The production class or use category of the animals covered by the prescription (e.g., beef cattle, dairy cattle, broiler, layer). Determines applicable withdrawal periods and regulatory requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: beef_cattle|dairy_cattle|swine_market|swine_breeding|broiler|layer|turkey|sheep|goat|other — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Animal Drug Regulations 21 CFR Part 514; FSIS Residue Avoidance; USDA NASS

business_glossary_term: Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) Scheme Code
description: Identifies the GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme under which this sample was collected or is being used to demonstrate compliance. Supports certification audit readiness for SQF, BRC, IFS, and GLOBALG.A.P. schemes. Aligns with GFSI, SQF, BRC, IFS business jargons.
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements v2020; SQF Code Edition 9; BRC Global Standard Issue 9; IFS Food Version 7; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6

description: Indicates whether this sample was collected as part of HACCP (Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points) critical control point monitoring or verification activities. True requires enhanced documentation and regulatory retention per FSMA Preventive Controls rule. Aligns with HACCP business jargon.
reference: HACCP Principle 4 - Monitoring CCPs; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 Subpart C; Codex Alimentarius HACCP Guidelines

description: Indicates whether any test parameter in this sample exceeded the applicable Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) as defined by EPA, Codex Alimentarius, or customer specification. True triggers non-conformance workflow and regulatory notification procedures. Aligns with MRL business jargon.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180 - Pesticide Tolerances; Codex Alimentarius MRL Database; EU Regulation 396/2005

description: Consolidated pass/fail determination for the sample based on all test parameters in the requested panel against defined acceptance criteria. Pass: all parameters within specification; Fail: one or more parameters exceeded MRL or acceptance threshold; Inconclusive: results require further review; Pending: testing not yet complete.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - Usage Decision; HACCP CCP Acceptance Criteria; MRL (Maximum Residue Limit) Compliance

description: Indicates whether the sample collection date was verified to be after the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) for any applied crop protection products. True confirms PHI compliance at time of sampling. Critical for pesticide residue MRL compliance. Aligns with PHI business jargon.
reference: EPA FIFRA PHI Requirements; USDA AMS Pesticide Data Program; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6 CB 7.6.4

description: Date and time when the sample was officially received and logged at the testing laboratory. Used to calculate sample transit time, assess sample integrity, and enforce holding time limits per analytical method requirements.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.4 - Sample Handling; FDA Laboratory Accreditation

description: Target date by which laboratory testing and result reporting must be completed. Drives laboratory scheduling, SLA monitoring, and release-hold decisions for production lots awaiting quality clearance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - Inspection Deadline; HACCP Verification Turnaround Requirements

description: Date and time when the laboratory officially released the test results and COA. Marks the completion of the testing lifecycle and triggers downstream quality disposition workflows in SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - Usage Decision Timestamp; ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.8

description: Assessment of the physical condition of the sample upon receipt at the laboratory. Acceptable: sample integrity maintained; Compromised: minor integrity issues noted but testing may proceed with caveats; Rejected: sample integrity failed, testing cannot proceed. Drives chain-of-custody decisions.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.4.2 - Sample Condition Assessment; FSMA Chain of Custody

description: Free-text field for additional observations, anomalies, or contextual information recorded at the time of sample collection or receipt. Captures field conditions, equipment calibration notes, or deviations from standard sampling protocol that may affect result interpretation.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.4.2; HACCP Monitoring Records; FSMA Preventive Controls Documentation

description: Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the sample at collection or submission. Used for chain-of-custody documentation, COA traceability, and communication with external laboratories. Follows enterprise sample numbering convention (e.g., LAB-2024-000123).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Inspection Lot Number; FSMA Subpart O Sampling Plan

description: Measured quantity of material collected for the sample (e.g., weight in grams, volume in milliliters). Used to verify adequacy of sample size per analytical method requirements and sampling plan specifications.
reference: AOAC Sampling Procedures; SAP S/4HANA QM - Sample Quantity; ISO/IEC 17025

description: Unit of measure for the sample quantity field. Standard units include grams (g), kilograms (kg), milliliters (mL), liters (L), milligrams (mg), and ounces (oz). Must align with the unit specified in the analytical method.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - Unit of Measure; ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.4

description: Current lifecycle status of the laboratory sample from submission through disposition. Drives workflow routing in SAP QM and laboratory tracking systems. Values: submitted (collected and logged), received (confirmed at lab), in_testing (analysis underway), completed (results available), rejected (sample failed receipt criteria), disposed (sample destroyed or returned per protocol).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module - Usage Decision; ISO/IEC 17025

description: Classification of the physical material collected for laboratory analysis. Determines applicable analytical methods, acceptance criteria, and regulatory reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|soil|water|tissue|feed|environmental_swab|air|product — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA AMS Sampling Procedures; FDA FSMA Environmental Monitoring; HACCP CCP Classification

description: Classification of why this sample was collected relative to the sampling plan schedule. Routine: scheduled periodic sampling; Triggered: collected in response to an event or non-conformance; Regulatory: mandated by USDA/FDA/EPA inspection; Verification: HACCP verification sampling; Validation: process or method validation sampling.
reference: HACCP Principle 6 - Verification; FSMA Preventive Controls Verification; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6

description: Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the sample was stored during transit and prior to analysis. Critical for maintaining sample integrity for microbiological and chemical analyses. Deviations may invalidate results per ISO/IEC 17025 requirements.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Section 7.4.2; FDA BAM Chapter 1 - Sample Handling; HACCP Cold Chain

description: System timestamp of the most recent modification to the lab sample record. Supports incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Data Integrity Controls; ISO 22000 Record Management

description: Primary key for laboratory
Self-referencing FK on laboratory (parent_laboratory_id)
Name of the accreditation body that granted laboratory certification (e.g., A2LA, ANAB, UKAS).
Date when the laboratory accreditation became effective.
Date when the current laboratory accreditation expires and requires renewal.
Unique accreditation certificate number issued by the accreditation body.
Current accreditation status of the laboratory under ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent standards.
Primary street address of the laboratory facility.
Secondary address information such as suite, building, or floor number.
Biosafety level classification for laboratories handling biological agents.
City where the laboratory is located.
CLIA certificate number if laboratory performs clinical testing on agricultural workers or food safety personnel.
Three-letter ISO country code where the laboratory is located.
Timestamp when this laboratory record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the laboratory meets data integrity requirements under ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available).
Standard frequency in days for calibration of laboratory testing equipment.
Date when the laboratory was established and began operations.
FDA registration number for laboratories conducting food safety testing under FSMA requirements.

GFSI-recognized certification scheme the laboratory operates under (e.g., SQF, BRC, IFS, FSSC 22000). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SQF|BRC|IFS|FSSC_22000|GLOBALG.A.P.|PrimusGFS|CanadaGAP — promote to reference product]
Externally-known unique code for the laboratory used in regulatory filings and certifications.
Official registered name of the laboratory facility.
Classification of laboratory based on operational model and ownership structure.
Date of the most recent external audit or assessment of the laboratory.
Timestamp when this laboratory record was last updated.
Name of the Laboratory Information Management System used for sample tracking and data management.
Scheduled date for the next external audit or surveillance assessment.
Current operational status of the laboratory facility.
Postal or ZIP code for the laboratory location.
Email address of the primary laboratory contact for quality and compliance communications.
Full name of the primary contact person or laboratory director.
Primary phone number for the laboratory contact.
Indicates whether the laboratory participates in proficiency testing programs to validate testing competency.
Quality management system framework the laboratory operates under.
Primary area of laboratory specialization (e.g., microbiology, chemistry, pesticide residue, mycotoxin, allergen, GMO, nutritional analysis).
State or province where the laboratory is located.
Maximum number of samples the laboratory can process per day under normal operating conditions.
Comprehensive description of testing capabilities and scope of accreditation including test methods and matrices.
Standard turnaround time in hours for routine test results from sample receipt to report delivery.
USDA establishment number for laboratories testing meat, poultry, and egg products.
Unique system-generated identifier for each individual analytical test result record. Primary key for the test_result data product in the quality domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.agrochemical_product. Business justification: Pesticide residue test results must trace to the specific EPA-registered agrochemical product for MRL compliance reporting, regulatory submission, and resistance management tracking. Agriculture labs
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the laboratory instrument or equipment used to perform the analysis (e.g., HPLC, GC-MS, PCR thermocycler). Supports instrument calibration traceability and ISO/IEC 17025 audit requirements.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: test_result has coa_eligible (BOOLEAN) indicating the result qualifies for inclusion in a Certificate of Analysis. Adding coa_id FK links the individual test result to the specific COA it was included
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the specific commodity lot (batch) from which the sample was drawn. Enables lot-level traceability for COA generation, regulatory reporting, and potential recall management under FSMA.
reference: FDA FSMA Traceability Rule; USDA; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Reference to the laboratory analyst who performed the test and is responsible for sign-off on the result. Required for COA generation and regulatory audit trails.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Reference to the laboratory (internal or third-party accredited) that performed the analytical test. Supports COA generation and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Laboratory Accreditation; FDA FSMA

description: Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Test results for moisture, protein, test weight, and defects are evaluated against specific grading standards. Linking test_result to grading_standard enables automated pass/fail against official USDA
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent laboratory sample from which this test result was derived. Links the individual test result to its originating sample submission record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: test_result stores mrl_threshold as a denormalized numeric column. Linking to mrl_threshold normalizes the regulatory tolerance reference, enabling pass/fail determination against jurisdiction-specifi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the non-conformance record raised as a result of a failed test result. Populated when pass_fail_status is 'fail', linking the test result to the corrective action workflow in SAP QM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 22000; GFSI; SQF; BRC Global Standards; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt. Business justification: Incoming quality test results (MRL, mycotoxin, germination) must be linked to the specific GRN for lot acceptance/rejection decisions, return claim evidence, and FSMA supplier verification records. te
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Test results drive stock release decisions in SAP QM — a pass result releases blocked stock, a fail triggers quarantine. Linking test_result to stock_position enables automated QM usage decisions, sto
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Internal or regulatory action threshold below the MRL that triggers corrective action or investigation before the regulatory limit is breached. Supports HACCP CCP monitoring and FSMA preventive controls.
reference: FDA FSMA Preventive Controls; HACCP Codex Alimentarius; GFSI; SQF

description: The date and time when the laboratory analyst formally reviewed and signed off on the test result. Required for COA issuance and ISO/IEC 17025 quality system compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; FDA FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF; BRC Global Standards

description: The date and time when this test result record was first created in the quality management system. Supports audit trail requirements under FSMA, ISO 22000, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: FDA FSMA; ISO 22000; SOX; GDPR Article 5

description: The lowest concentration or quantity of the analyte that can be reliably detected by the test method. Critical for interpreting non-detect results and MRL compliance reporting to FDA and USDA.
reference: AOAC International; EPA Method Guidelines; Codex Alimentarius; FDA

description: The dilution factor applied to the sample prior to analysis. Used to back-calculate the actual concentration in the original sample matrix and ensure accurate MRL compliance determination.
reference: AOAC International; ISO/IEC 17025; EPA Method Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) Critical Control Point (CCP) Flag
description: Indicates whether this test result is associated with a HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) monitoring activity. CCP-flagged results require immediate corrective action upon failure and are subject to FSIS and FDA regulatory review.
reference: HACCP Codex Alimentarius; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls; FSIS; ISO 22000

description: The ISO/IEC 17025 or equivalent accreditation number of the laboratory that performed the test. Printed on COAs and required for regulatory submissions to USDA, FDA, and FSIS to validate laboratory competency.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; USDA; FDA; FSIS; A2LA; PJLA

description: The quantitative result of the analytical test as reported by the laboratory instrument or analyst. For qualitative tests (e.g., Salmonella presence/absence), this field is null and qualitative_result is used instead.
reference: AOAC International; ISO 22000; Codex Alimentarius

description: Broad classification of the test parameter type. Supports regulatory reporting segmentation and HACCP critical control point (CCP) categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mycotoxin|pesticide_residue|microbiological|physical|chemical|nutritional|heavy_metal|sensory — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]
reference: HACCP Codex Alimentarius; FDA FSMA; GFSI; SQF; BRC Global Standards

description: Name of the analytical parameter being tested (e.g., aflatoxin B1, glyphosate residue, Salmonella spp., moisture content, pH, Electrical Conductivity (EC), nitrate). Drives COA line-item labeling and MRL threshold comparison.
reference: AOAC International; ISO 22000; Codex Alimentarius; EPA; USDA; FDA

description: The compliance determination for this individual test result based on comparison of the measured value against the MRL threshold and action limit. Drives COA issuance, lot release decisions, and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; FSIS; ISO 22000; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF; BRC Global Standards

description: Qualitative outcome for presence/absence or positive/negative tests (e.g., Salmonella not detected, GMO absent). Used when a numeric measured_value is not applicable. Supports FSIS microbiological reporting.
reference: FSIS; FDA FSMA; USDA APHIS; ISO 22000

description: The lowest concentration at which the analyte can be quantitatively measured with acceptable precision and accuracy. Distinct from LOD; used for MRL compliance determination and COA reporting.
reference: AOAC International; EPA Method Guidelines; Codex Alimentarius; FDA

description: The percentage recovery of a spiked control sample used to validate the analytical method performance for this test run. Acceptable recovery ranges are defined by AOAC and Codex Alimentarius guidelines (typically 70–120%).
reference: AOAC International; Codex Alimentarius; ISO/IEC 17025; EPA

description: Code identifying the regulatory monitoring or surveillance program under which this test was conducted (e.g., USDA Pesticide Data Program (PDP), FDA Pesticide Residue Monitoring Program, FSIS National Residue Program (NRP), USDA AMS Microbiological Data Program).
reference: USDA PDP; FDA PRMM; FSIS NRP; EPA; APHIS

description: Indicates whether this test result must be reported to a regulatory authority (USDA, FDA, FSIS, EPA, or APHIS). Triggered by failed MRL results, CCP deviations, or mandatory surveillance program requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; FSIS; EPA; APHIS; Codex Alimentarius

description: The date and time when the test result was formally reported and made available for COA generation and lot release decisions. Used to calculate laboratory turnaround time KPIs.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; FDA FSMA; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Current workflow status of the test result record within the quality management system. Controls whether the result is eligible for COA inclusion and lot release authorization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 22000; GFSI

description: Indicates whether this result is an original first-run test, a repeat test, a confirmatory test following a suspect result, or a retest after corrective action. Supports regulatory investigation workflows.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA; ISO 22000; HACCP Codex Alimentarius

description: The physical material or commodity type being tested (e.g., grain, soil, water, feed, fresh produce, processed food, animal tissue). Determines applicable MRL thresholds and regulatory reporting pathways under USDA, FDA, or FSIS jurisdiction.
reference: Codex Alimentarius; USDA; FDA; FSIS; EPA

description: The standardized analytical method reference code used to perform the test (e.g., AOAC 2007.01, ISO 6579, EPA Method 8270D, USDA FSIS MLG 4.10). Required for COA documentation and regulatory method validation compliance.
reference: AOAC International; ISO 22000; EPA; USDA FSIS; FDA BAM

description: Descriptive name of the analytical method used (e.g., 'AOAC QuEChERS Multi-Residue Pesticide Method', 'ISO Horizontal Method for Salmonella Detection'). Complements the method code for COA readability.
reference: AOAC International; ISO 22000; EPA; USDA FSIS

description: Free-text field for laboratory analyst observations, anomalies, interferences, or qualifications associated with this test result. Supports audit trail documentation and COA footnotes.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: The date and time when the analytical test was physically performed in the laboratory. Distinct from sample receipt and result sign-off timestamps. Critical for FSMA traceability and COA dating.
reference: FDA FSMA Traceability Rule; ISO/IEC 17025; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Externally-known business identifier for this test result, used on Certificates of Analysis (COA) and regulatory submissions. Typically sourced from SAP QM or the laboratory information management system (LIMS).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; USDA; FDA FSMA

description: The expanded measurement uncertainty associated with the test result, expressed in the same unit as the measured value. Required for ISO/IEC 17025 accredited laboratory reporting and MRL compliance boundary decisions.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; EURACHEM/CITAC Guide CG4; Codex Alimentarius

description: Unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., ppb, ppm, CFU/g, %, mg/kg, pH units, mS/cm for EC). Aligns with Codex Alimentarius and AOAC reporting conventions for regulatory submissions.
reference: Codex Alimentarius; AOAC International; ISO 22000; USDA; FDA

description: The date and time when this test result record was last modified in the quality management system. Required for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and regulatory audit trail compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA; ISO 22000; SOX; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) Plan ID
description: Unique system-generated identifier for the HACCP plan record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running regulatory compliance plan binding a facility/process to documented food safety controls.
reference: FDA FSMA Preventive Controls Rule (21 CFR Part 117); Codex Alimentarius HACCP Guidelines (CAC/RCP 1-1969)

description: Reference to the agricultural commodity or food product category covered by this HACCP plan (e.g., fresh produce, poultry, dairy). Aligns with USDA/FDA commodity classification for regulatory reporting.
reference: USDA AMS Commodity Classification; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126

description: Reference to the production facility, processing plant, or packing house where this HACCP plan is applied. Links to the facility master record for location, registration, and regulatory context.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; FSIS Directive 5000.1

description: Foreign key linking to land.farm_unit. Business justification: FSMA Produce Safety Rule requires on-farm food safety/HACCP plans scoped to specific farm operations. haccp_plan has fsma_preventive_controls_applicable and plan_scope. Farm unit provides the operatio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: HACCP plans for chemical CCPs (pesticide residue control points) define critical limits based on MRL thresholds. A food safety manager designing a HACCP plan would reference the applicable MRL thresho
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the qualified individual designated as the HACCP team leader responsible for plan development, maintenance, and regulatory compliance. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Step 1 (assemble HACCP team).
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Step 1; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126(a)(2); FSIS 9 CFR Part 417.7

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: HACCP plans in grain storage and cold chain agriculture govern specific storage locations (CCPs for temperature, fumigation, moisture). haccp_plan has facility_id but not storage_location granularity.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date on which the current version of the HACCP plan was formally approved by authorized management. Distinct from effective_date; approval may precede implementation.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126(b); FSIS 9 CFR Part 417.2(c); BRC Global Standard Issue 9

description: Indicates whether biological hazards (e.g., Salmonella, E. coli O157:H7, Listeria monocytogenes) were identified as significant hazards requiring a Critical Control Point (CCP) in this HACCP plan.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130(a)(2)(i); FSIS HACCP Regulations 9 CFR Part 417

description: Total number of Critical Control Points (CCPs) identified and documented within this HACCP plan. Used for plan completeness validation and audit readiness checks.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 2; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.135

description: Narrative description of the CCP process step where control is applied (e.g., 'Pasteurization heat treatment', 'Metal detection at packaging line exit', 'Chlorine wash for fresh produce').
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 2; SQF Code Edition 9 Module 11.2

description: Unique identifier for a specific Critical Control Point within the HACCP plan (e.g., CCP-1B for biological, CCP-2C for chemical, CCP-3P for physical). Used to cross-reference monitoring logs and corrective action records.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 2; FSIS HACCP 9 CFR Part 417.2

description: Indicates whether chemical hazards (e.g., pesticide residues exceeding MRL, allergens, mycotoxins, heavy metals, cleaning chemical residues) were identified as significant hazards in this HACCP plan.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130(a)(2)(ii); EPA MRL Regulations; Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards

description: Documented procedure to be followed when a deviation from the critical limit is detected at the CCP, including product disposition, process correction, and root cause investigation steps. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 5.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 5; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.150; FSIS 9 CFR Part 417.3

description: Timestamp when this HACCP plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.190; SOX Section 404; ISO 22000:2018 Section 7.5

description: Documented critical limit(s) for each CCP that distinguish safe from unsafe product (e.g., 'Internal temperature ≥ 72°C for ≥ 15 seconds', 'Chlorine concentration 50–200 ppm', 'Metal detector sensitivity: 1.5mm ferrous'). Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 3.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 3; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.135(c)(1)

description: Indicates whether any active deviation from a critical limit has been recorded against this HACCP plan in the current monitoring period. True signals that a corrective action is open or pending closure.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.150; Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 5; FSIS 9 CFR Part 417.3

description: Date on which this version of the HACCP plan became operationally effective and was implemented in production. Marks the start of the plan's binding period for compliance purposes.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.5; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6

description: Date on which this HACCP plan version expires or is scheduled for mandatory reassessment review. Nullable for open-ended plans; populated when a defined review cycle is mandated by the regulatory framework or certification body.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.165(b); BRC Global Standard Issue 9 Section 2.7.1; SQF Code Edition 9

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Preventive Controls Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether this HACCP plan is subject to the FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Preventive Controls for Human Food rule (21 CFR Part 117). False may apply to exempt facilities or animal food operations governed by a separate rule.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Public Law 111-353

description: GFSI-benchmarked certification scheme under which this HACCP plan is audited (Safe Quality Food (SQF), British Retail Consortium (BRC), International Featured Standards (IFS), FSSC 22000, or GLOBALG.A.P.). Drives audit scope and documentation requirements.
reference: GFSI Benchmarking Requirements v2020.1; SQF Code Edition 9; BRC Global Standard Issue 9; IFS Food Version 7

description: Narrative summary of the hazard analysis conducted for this plan, documenting the methodology used to identify and evaluate biological, chemical, and physical hazards at each process step. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 1.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 1; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.5.2

description: Documented intended use and consumer population for the product covered by this plan, including whether the product is intended for vulnerable populations (e.g., infants, immunocompromised). Informs hazard severity assessment.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Rev. 4-2003 Step 3; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130(a)(1)

description: Date of the most recent formal reassessment or revalidation of the HACCP plan, triggered by process changes, new hazard information, regulatory updates, or scheduled periodic review. Required by FDA FSMA and GFSI schemes.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.165(b)(1); Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 6; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.8.2

description: Frequency at which the monitoring parameter is measured at the CCP (e.g., continuous, every batch, hourly, per shift). Defined in the HACCP plan and enforced during production operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continuous|every_batch|hourly|every_2_hours|daily|per_shift|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 4; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.145(b)(2)

description: Description of the procedure, instrument, or technique used to monitor the CCP parameter (e.g., 'Calibrated digital thermometer', 'Inline pH meter', 'Metal detector test piece challenge'). Supports equipment calibration traceability.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 4; BRC Global Standard Issue 9 Section 2.7

description: The physical or chemical parameter measured at the CCP to verify the critical limit is being met (e.g., temperature in °C, pH, chlorine concentration in ppm, metal detector sensitivity in mm). Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 4. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temperature|pH|chlorine_concentration|metal_detector_sensitivity|water_activity|time|pressure|flow_rate|turbidity|conductivity|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 4; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.145

description: Date by which the next formal reassessment of the HACCP plan must be completed. Used for compliance calendar management and audit readiness tracking.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.165(b); BRC Global Standard Issue 9 Section 2.7; SQF Code Edition 9 Section 2.4

description: Count of corrective action records currently open and unresolved against this HACCP plan. Used for compliance dashboard monitoring and audit readiness assessment. Zero indicates full compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.150; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; ISO 22000:2018 Section 10.1

description: Indicates whether physical hazards (e.g., metal fragments, glass, bone, hard plastic, stones) were identified as significant hazards requiring a CCP such as metal detection or X-ray inspection in this HACCP plan.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130(a)(2)(iii); BRC Global Standard Issue 9 Section 4.9

description: Descriptive human-readable name of the HACCP plan, typically referencing the commodity, process line, or facility scope (e.g., 'Fresh-Cut Leafy Greens Processing — Facility A').
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Rev. 4-2003; SQF Code Edition 9

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier for the HACCP plan, used in regulatory submissions, audit documentation, and cross-system references (e.g., SAP QM inspection lot linkage). Format: HACCP-{FACILITY_CODE}-{YEAR}-{SEQ}.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements v2020.1

description: Defines the operational scope of the HACCP plan — whether it covers an entire facility, a specific process line, a commodity category, or a specific finished product. Determines applicability during audits.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Step 1; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126

description: Current lifecycle state of the HACCP plan. 'active' indicates the plan is in operational use; 'under_review' indicates a scheduled reassessment is in progress; 'suspended' indicates temporary halt pending corrective action; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.126; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6 CB Annex; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.5

description: Version number of the HACCP plan document, incremented upon each formal review or revision cycle. Supports document control and audit trail requirements (e.g., '1.0', '2.3').
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.165; BRC Global Standard for Food Safety Issue 9

description: List of prerequisite programs (PRPs) supporting this HACCP plan, such as Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), sanitation standard operating procedures (SSOPs), pest control, and allergen management programs. PRPs are foundational controls that underpin the HACCP system.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117 Subpart B; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.2; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA v6

description: Narrative description of the production or processing scope covered by this HACCP plan, including process flow steps from raw material receipt through finished product dispatch. Required by Codex Alimentarius Step 4 (process flow diagram description).
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Rev. 4-2003 Step 4; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.130

description: Description of the records that must be maintained to document HACCP plan implementation, including monitoring logs, corrective action records, verification records, and retention periods. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 7.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 7; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.190; FSIS 9 CFR Part 417.5

description: Primary regulatory or certification framework under which this HACCP plan was developed and is maintained (e.g., FDA FSMA Preventive Controls, FSIS HACCP, GFSI scheme such as SQF or BRC). Determines audit requirements and record retention obligations. reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; FSIS 9 CFR Part 417; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements v2020.1

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this HACCP plan record in the system. Used for change tracking, version control, and data synchronization with SAP QM module.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.190; SOX Section 404; SAP QM Module Audit Log Standards

description: Documented activities used to confirm the HACCP plan is functioning effectively, including calibration schedules, record review, periodic product testing, and internal audits. Corresponds to Codex Alimentarius Principle 6.
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/RCP 1-1969 Principle 6; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117.165; ISO 22000:2018 Section 8.8

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The national or international accreditation body that accredits the certification body (e.g., ANAB - ANSI National Accreditation Board, UKAS - United Kingdom Accreditation Service, DAkkS). Validates the legitimacy of the certification body and is required for GFSI scheme recognition.
reference: GFSI, ISO/IEC 17065, IAF - International Accreditation Forum

description: The type of audit that resulted in or is associated with this certification record: initial (first-time certification), recertification (renewal at end of cycle), surveillance (periodic interim audit), unannounced (surprise audit required by some GFSI schemes), or extension (scope extension audit).
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: The name of the accredited third-party certification body or registrar that issued the certificate (e.g., NSF International, SGS, Bureau Veritas, DNV, Intertek). Required for regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS and for GFSI scheme compliance.
reference: GFSI, ISO/IEC 17065, USDA NOP, FDA FSMA

description: Reference path or document management system identifier pointing to the scanned or digital copy of the issued certificate (e.g., SharePoint URL, SAP DMS document number). Enables direct access to the certificate document for audits and customer requests.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., FDA FSMA

description: The externally-issued, unique certificate number assigned by the certification body. Used for regulatory submissions, audit trails, and customer-facing documentation (e.g., COA, supplier declarations). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA QM module certificate tracking.
reference: GFSI, SQF, BRC Global Standards, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., USDA NOP

description: Current lifecycle status of the certification. Active: certificate is valid and in good standing. Suspended: temporarily halted by the certification body pending corrective action. Withdrawn: permanently revoked. Expired: past the expiry date without renewal. Pending: initial application or renewal in progress. Under_review: surveillance audit or non-conformance review in progress. Drives certification readiness dashboards and renewal workflows.
reference: GFSI, SQF Institute, BRC Global Standards, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: High-level classification of the certification by its primary purpose: food_safety (SQF, BRC, IFS, HACCP-based), environmental (ISO 14001, ISCC), organic (USDA NOP), sustainability (ISCC, Fair Trade), social (Fair Trade), or quality_management (ISO 22000). Supports portfolio-level reporting and gap analysis.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, ISO 14001, USDA NOP, ISCC, Fair Trade USA

description: The deadline by which all corrective actions arising from audit non-conformances must be submitted to the certification body. Null if no open non-conformances exist. Drives corrective action workflow and escalation alerts.
reference: GFSI, BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute, ISO 22000, FDA FSMA

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the certified facility or operation is located (e.g., USA, BRA, DEU). Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance and international regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1, USDA COOL, Codex Alimentarius, GDPR

description: The timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, SOX, GDPR Article 5

description: The date on which the certificate expires and must be renewed to maintain certified status. Null for open-ended certifications. Triggers renewal workflow alerts and drives certification readiness dashboards.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, USDA NOP, FDA FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P.

business_glossary_term: Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) Scheme Flag
description: Indicates whether this certification is recognized under the Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) benchmarking framework (True) or is a non-GFSI scheme (False). GFSI-recognized schemes include SQF, BRC, IFS, FSSC 22000, and GLOBALG.A.P. Critical for customer and retailer compliance requirements.
reference: GFSI, SQF Institute, BRC Global Standards, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: The unique GLOBALG.A.P. producer or certificate number assigned by the GLOBALG.A.P. secretariat and published in the GLOBALG.A.P. database. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. certification verification by buyers and retailers.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices)

description: The grade, level, or rating awarded by the certification body upon successful audit (e.g., SQF Level 2, SQF Level 3, BRC Grade AA/A/B/C/D, IFS Higher Level/Foundation Level). Null for schemes that do not use graded outcomes. Used for benchmarking and customer qualification.
reference: SQF Institute, BRC Global Standards, IFS

description: The date on which the facility or operation first achieved certification under this scheme, regardless of subsequent renewals. Used to calculate certification tenure and demonstrate long-standing compliance to customers and regulators.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., USDA NOP

business_glossary_term: International Sustainability and Carbon Certification (ISCC) Certificate Number
description: The unique certificate number issued under the International Sustainability and Carbon Certification (ISCC) scheme for bioenergy and biomass sustainability compliance. Null for non-ISCC certifications.
reference: ISCC - International Sustainability and Carbon Certification

description: The date on which the certification body formally issued the certificate. Marks the start of the certification validity period. Used for compliance tracking and regulatory reporting to USDA, FDA, and FSIS.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, USDA NOP, FDA FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether this certificate covers multiple sites or facilities under a single certificate number (True) or a single site (False). Multi-site certifications are common for GLOBALG.A.P. Option 2 and BRC multi-site schemes.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P., BRC Global Standards, ISO 22000

description: The planned date for the next certification or surveillance audit as agreed with the certification body. Drives the surveillance audit schedule and renewal workflow alerts in the certification readiness dashboard.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute

description: The number of major non-conformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Major non-conformances require corrective action within a defined timeframe and may result in certification suspension if unresolved. Tracked per SAP S/4HANA QM module non-conformance records.
reference: GFSI, BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute, IFS, ISO 22000

description: The number of minor non-conformances identified during the most recent certification audit. Minor non-conformances require corrective action but do not immediately threaten certification status. Tracked per SAP S/4HANA QM module.
reference: GFSI, BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute, IFS, ISO 22000

description: Free-text field for additional remarks, conditions, or qualifications associated with the certification (e.g., scope limitations, special conditions imposed by the certification body, internal observations). Supports operational context for quality managers.
reference: ISO 22000, GFSI, Internal SOP

description: The product category or commodity type covered by the certification scope as defined by the scheme (e.g., GLOBALG.A.P. crop category: Fruit and Vegetables; BRC product category: Fresh Produce; SQF food sector category). Supports product-level compliance mapping.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P., BRC Global Standards, SQF Institute, GFSI

description: The number of calendar days before the expiry date at which automated renewal reminder notifications should be triggered. Configurable per scheme to align with certification body lead times and internal preparation requirements.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., Internal SOP

business_glossary_term: SAP Quality Management (QM) Inspection Lot Number
description: The SAP S/4HANA QM module inspection lot number associated with the certification audit or quality inspection event. Enables traceability between the certification record and the source system inspection record for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module, ISO 22000, GFSI

description: The name of the food safety or quality certification scheme under which the certificate was issued (e.g., Safe Quality Food (SQF), British Retail Consortium (BRC), International Featured Standards (IFS), GLOBALG.A.P., ISO 22000, USDA Organic/National Organic Program (NOP), International Sustainability and Carbon Certification (ISCC), Fair Trade). Drives certification readiness dashboards and renewal workflows.
reference: GFSI, SQF Institute, BRC Global Standards, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P., ISO 22000, USDA NOP, ISCC, Fair Trade USA

description: The edition or version number of the certification scheme standard under which the audit was conducted (e.g., SQF Edition 9, BRC Issue 9, IFS Food Version 8). Critical for tracking standard upgrades and ensuring compliance with the current scheme revision.
reference: SQF Institute, BRC Global Standards, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Narrative description of the products, processes, activities, or operations covered by the certificate as defined by the certification body (e.g., 'Production and packing of fresh vegetables', 'Manufacture of animal feed'). Directly reflects the scope statement printed on the certificate.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, GLOBALG.A.P., USDA NOP, BRC Global Standards

description: The date on which the certification body formally suspended the certificate, if applicable. Null for active or non-suspended certifications. Required for regulatory reporting and customer notification obligations.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, USDA NOP, FDA FSMA

description: Narrative description of the reason for certification suspension as communicated by the certification body (e.g., unresolved major non-conformances, product recall, failure to submit corrective actions). Null if not suspended.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, FDA FSMA, USDA NOP

description: The timestamp when this certification record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and FSMA record-keeping requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA, ISO 22000, SOX, GDPR Article 5

description: The USDA National Organic Program (NOP) operation number assigned to the certified organic operation. Required for USDA organic labeling compliance and traceability under the National Organic Program regulations.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP), 7 CFR Part 205

description: The date on which the certificate was permanently withdrawn or voluntarily surrendered. Null for active certifications. Required for regulatory reporting and audit trail completeness.
reference: GFSI, ISO 22000, USDA NOP, FDA FSMA

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the freight carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider record within the agricultural supply chain.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Vendor master record number assigned to this carrier in SAP S/4HANA MM module. Used for cross-referencing freight invoices, purchase orders (PO), and goods receipt notes (GRN) in the ERP system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master (LFA1/LFB1 tables)

description: Date on which the carrier was formally approved to transport trial materials to or from this specific trial site. Belongs to the approval relationship, not to the carrier or site master records.

description: Comma-delimited list of agricultural commodity categories this carrier is approved to transport (e.g., 'grain', 'fresh_produce', 'livestock', 'fertilizer', 'pesticides'). Derived from carrier certifications and equipment capabilities.

description: The specific date on which audit activities were conducted for this carrier. Relevant when a multi-carrier audit spans multiple site visits on different dates.

description: The audit outcome determined specifically for this carrier within the audit engagement. A multi-carrier audit may yield different outcomes per carrier.

description: Indicates whether this carrier is required to submit or implement corrective actions as a result of findings identified during this audit. Drives follow-up workflow in the compliance domain.

reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS; John Deere Operations Center Fleet Telematics

description: Indicates whether the carrier holds USDOT hazardous materials (HAZMAT) certification for transporting regulated agricultural inputs such as pesticides, fertilizers, and fumigants with Safety Data Sheet (SDS) requirements.
reference: USDOT 49 CFR Parts 171-180 HAZMAT Regulations; EPA; OSHA

description: Expiration date of the carrier's current insurance certificate (cargo liability, general liability, and auto liability). Carrier is ineligible for load assignment if this date has passed. Monitored for proactive renewal alerts.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR Part 387; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation

description: Total cargo liability insurance coverage amount in USD as stated on the carrier's current insurance certificate. Used to validate coverage adequacy for high-value agricultural commodity shipments.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR Part 387; USDA Agricultural Commodity Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the carrier master record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Full legal registered business name of the freight carrier or 3PL provider as recorded with the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FMCSA, DOT). Used for contracts, BOL documentation, and compliance filings.
reference: FMCSA Carrier Registration; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the carrier's temperature-controlled units can maintain. Defines the upper bound of the carrier's cold chain capability for fresh produce and ambient-chilled agricultural shipments.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Operating authority number (MC number) issued by FMCSA to carriers transporting regulated commodities for hire in interstate commerce. Validates carrier authority to haul agricultural products.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR Part 365; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule

description: Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the carrier's refrigerated units can maintain. Used for TMS routing of frozen or deep-chilled agricultural products requiring specific temperature ranges.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Rolling percentage of shipments delivered within the committed delivery window. Primary KPI for carrier performance evaluation, tier assignment, and preferred carrier status in TMS routing for agricultural logistics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation; GLOBALG.A.P. Supply Chain Standard

description: Rolling percentage of shipments where the carrier completed pickup within the agreed pickup window. Key performance indicator (KPI) for carrier scorecard evaluation and performance tier assignment in the agricultural supply chain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation; GLOBALG.A.P. Supply Chain Standard

description: The fiscal quarter and year (e.g., '2024-Q3') for which the current rolling performance metrics (on-time rates, damage claim rate, compliance score, performance tier) were last evaluated and updated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation

description: Performance rating (0.00–5.00) assigned to the carrier based on observed performance specifically at this trial site. Distinct from the carrier's aggregate rolling performance metrics, which span all sites and shipments. Enables site managers to flag carriers that perform well globally but poorly at their specific location.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Current performance tier classification assigned based on the carrier's rolling KPI scores (on-time rates, damage claims, compliance score). Drives TMS primary/secondary/tertiary carrier selection logic for agricultural shipment routing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Evaluation; GLOBALG.A.P. Supply Chain Standard

description: Comma-delimited list of preferred origin-destination lane codes (e.g., 'MIDWEST-SOUTHEAST', 'CA-TX') where this carrier has committed capacity or preferred routing agreements. Used by TMS for primary carrier selection in route optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS Route Determination; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Primary email address for the carrier's operational contact used for load tender notifications, BOL transmissions, and compliance document exchange.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Full name of the primary business contact at the carrier organization responsible for load tendering, dispatch coordination, and issue resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Primary telephone number for the carrier's operational contact used for dispatch coordination, load tendering, and exception management in the agricultural supply chain.
reference: GDPR Article 4; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: Count of loads rejected or refused by the carrier after tender acceptance within the current evaluation period. High rejection rates indicate capacity reliability issues and impact carrier tier scoring.

description: Indicates whether the carrier supports RFID-based shipment and lot (LOT) tracking for end-to-end traceability of agricultural commodities from farm to food manufacturer. Integrates with Manhattan Associates WMS RFID infrastructure.

description: Unique 2-4 character Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) to identify the carrier in EDI transactions, BOL documents, and TMS routing.

description: Comma-delimited list of transportation service modes offered by the carrier (e.g., FTL, LTL, refrigerated, flatbed, bulk, tanker, intermodal). Used for TMS lane matching and load tendering in agricultural logistics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Rolling ratio of cold chain shipments where a temperature excursion (deviation outside the specified range) was recorded to total cold chain shipments. Critical metric for FSMA compliance and food safety risk management.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: Cumulative count of shipments executed by this carrier within the current evaluation period. Provides volume context for interpreting rate-based KPIs (on-time, damage, temperature excursion) in carrier performance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Operating or 'doing business as' (DBA) trade name of the carrier, if different from the legal name. Used in day-to-day communications and TMS lane assignments.
reference: FMCSA Carrier Registration

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each supply chain network node (grain elevator, cold storage warehouse, processing plant, distribution center, port terminal, cross-dock facility, fumigation station). Primary key for the facility master record.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
