description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the facility is located. Used for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, import/export documentation, and international logistics routing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling); Codex Alimentarius

description: Timestamp when the facility master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance and FSMA traceability requirements.
reference: ISO 8601; FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S (recordkeeping); SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley) audit trail

description: Number of dock doors designated for cross-docking operations where inbound shipments are transferred directly to outbound without storage. Relevant for distribution centers and cross-dock facilities in the agricultural supply chain.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Cross-Dock Module; TMS Route Optimization

description: Number of dock doors designated for inbound receiving operations (GRN processing). Used for inbound logistics scheduling, carrier appointment planning, and WMS receiving capacity management.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Receipt (GRN)

description: Number of dock doors designated for outbound shipping operations (SO fulfillment). Used for outbound logistics scheduling, carrier appointment planning, and WMS shipping capacity management.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Issue

description: Total number of loading and unloading dock doors at the facility. Used for throughput capacity planning, dock scheduling in TMS/WMS, and carrier appointment management.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Dock Management; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the facility used across TMS, WMS, and ERP systems (e.g., SAP plant code, Manhattan WMS location code). Unique across the logistics network and referenced on BOL, shipment, and transport order documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Official business name of the supply chain facility as registered with USDA/FDA and used in logistics documentation, carrier communications, and BOL/AWB records.
reference: USDA APHIS Facility Registration; FDA Food Facility Registration (FSMA)

description: Current lifecycle state of the facility within the supply chain network. Drives eligibility for shipment routing, carrier assignment, and logistics planning in TMS and WMS.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master

description: Classification of the supply chain network node by its primary operational function. Drives logistics routing rules, compliance requirements, and capacity planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain_elevator|cold_storage|processing_plant|distribution_center|port_terminal|cross_dock|fumigation_station — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O

business_glossary_term: FDA (Food and Drug Administration) Food Facility Registration Number
description: FDA-issued food facility registration number required under FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold food for human or animal consumption. Must be renewed biennially.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart H; FDA Food Facility Registration; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Registration Expiry Date
description: Date on which the FDA FSMA food facility registration expires. FSMA requires biennial renewal in even-numbered years (October 1 – December 31). Used for compliance monitoring and renewal workflow triggers.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart H; FDA Biennial Registration Renewal

business_glossary_term: FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Facility Registration Status
description: Current FDA FSMA food facility registration status. Facilities with suspended or cancelled registration cannot legally ship food products. Drives compliance gating in shipment and transport order processing.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart H; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (2011)

business_glossary_term: RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) Reader Infrastructure Indicator
description: Indicates whether the facility is equipped with RFID reader infrastructure for automated lot (LOT) tracking, pallet identification, and real-time inventory visibility. Enables FSMA traceability lot code (TLC) scanning at facility entry/exit points.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S (Food Traceability); GS1 RFID Standards; EPC Global

description: Indicates whether the facility has a certified weighbridge (truck scale) for gross and tare weight measurement of inbound and outbound agricultural commodity shipments. Required for accurate net weight determination on BOL and GRN documents.
reference: USDA Federal Grain Inspection Service (FGIS); NIST Handbook 44 (Weighing Devices); 7 CFR Part 800

description: GPS latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), used for geospatial route optimization in TMS, GIS network mapping in Esri ArcGIS, and RTK-based precision logistics.
reference: Esri ArcGIS; GPS/RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) positioning standards; WGS84

description: GPS longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), used for geospatial route optimization in TMS, GIS network mapping in Esri ArcGIS, and RTK-based precision logistics.
reference: Esri ArcGIS; GPS/RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) positioning standards; WGS84

description: Indicates whether the facility operates on a 24-hour, continuous basis. When true, overrides operating_hours_start and operating_hours_end for scheduling purposes. Common for major port terminals and large grain elevators during harvest season.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; TMS Carrier Appointment Management

description: Standard daily closing time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for carrier appointment scheduling, TMS delivery window planning, and dock availability management.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Appointment Scheduling; TMS Carrier Management

description: Standard daily opening time of the facility in HH:MM (24-hour) format. Used for carrier appointment scheduling, TMS delivery window planning, and dock availability management.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Appointment Scheduling; TMS Carrier Management

description: Postal or ZIP code of the facility location, used for carrier rate zones, geographic clustering in GIS analysis, and regulatory reporting.
reference: USDA Facility Registration; Esri ArcGIS geospatial standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this facility master record originates (e.g., SAP S/4HANA MM Plant Master, Manhattan Associates WMS Location Master, Granular Farm Management). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Manhattan Associates WMS; Granular Farm Management; Oracle NetSuite; Data Governance — DAMA DMBOK

description: State or province where the facility is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping (USDA, FDA, EPA, OSHA), tax determination, and logistics lane planning.
reference: USDA APHIS; FDA FSMA; EPA; OSHA

description: Maximum rated storage capacity of the facility expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit of measure. Particularly relevant for grain elevators and on-farm storage. Complements MT capacity for dual-unit reporting.
reference: USDA WASDE (World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates); USDA Grain Stocks Report; 7 CFR Part 868

description: Maximum rated storage capacity of the facility expressed in Metric Tons (MT). Used for inventory planning, shipment routing, and capacity utilization analytics across the agricultural supply chain network.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; Granular Farm Management; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the facility's primary temperature zone. Used to validate cold chain compliance for inbound and outbound shipments and to configure IoT temperature monitoring alerts.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA Cold Chain Guidance; ISO 22000

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the facility's primary temperature zone. Used to validate cold chain compliance for inbound and outbound shipments and to configure IoT temperature monitoring alerts.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA Cold Chain Guidance; ISO 22000

description: Temperature zone classification of the facility's primary storage environment. Drives cold chain management requirements, carrier equipment type selection, and FSMA Sanitary Transportation compliance for temperature-sensitive agricultural commodities.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O (Sanitary Transportation); FDA Cold Chain Guidance; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., America/Chicago, America/Los_Angeles). Used for accurate scheduling of carrier appointments, operating hours, and timestamp normalization across multi-timezone logistics networks.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; TMS Scheduling Standards

description: Timestamp when the facility master record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in Databricks lakehouse Silver layer processing and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S (recordkeeping); SOX audit trail; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

business_glossary_term: USDA (United States Department of Agriculture) Facility Registration Number
description: Official USDA-issued facility registration number assigned by APHIS or AMS for grain elevators, processing plants, and other regulated agricultural facilities. Required for USDA-inspected operations and federal market reporting.
reference: USDA APHIS; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS); 7 CFR Part 868

description: Unique system-generated identifier for a freight rate record. Primary key for the freight_rate data product within the supply domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Condition Record

description: Reference to the carrier or third-party logistics (3PL) provider with whom this freight rate is contracted. Links to the carrier master record for carrier selection optimization and freight audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Vendor Master; TMS Carrier Management

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural freight rates are commodity-specific — grain, produce, and livestock rates differ significantly based on handling requirements, temperature needs, and regulatory compliance costs. Linking
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the destination facility, food manufacturer, retailer distribution center, or port for the freight lane covered by this rate. Defines the endpoint of the origin-destination (O-D) lane.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant/Storage Location; TMS Lane Management

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Freight rates are used to pre-accrue freight costs to GL accounts during rate-based accrual processes. Agriculture logistics finance teams use rate cards to generate freight expense accruals by GL acc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the origin facility, farm, elevator, or distribution center for the freight lane covered by this rate. Defines the starting point of the origin-destination (O-D) lane for carrier selection and route optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant/Storage Location; TMS Lane Management

description: Foreign key linking to supply.route. Business justification: Freight rates in agricultural logistics are negotiated per lane/route. Adding route_id to freight_rate normalizes the lane definition, linking the rate schedule directly to the route's origin/destinat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the parent freight contract or rate agreement under which this rate schedule is established. Supports contract lifecycle management and volume commitment tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Outline Agreement; TMS Contract Management

description: The contracted base freight rate amount, expressed in the unit defined by rate_type (e.g., USD per mile, USD per CWT, USD per MT, USD per BU, or flat USD amount). This is the principal monetary fact for the rate record, excluding fuel surcharge and accessorial charges. Used as the foundation for freight cost estimation and carrier invoice audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Amount; GAAP; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Percentage discount applied to the base rate when the contracted volume commitment tier is met or exceeded. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.05 = 5% discount). Incentivizes volume commitment with carriers and 3PLs for agricultural commodity lanes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Discount; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Timestamp when this freight rate record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) - Internal Controls; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the freight rate is denominated (e.g., USD for US Dollar, CAD for Canadian Dollar, EUR for Euro). Required for multi-currency freight cost estimation and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP; IFRS

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination of the freight lane. Required for export documentation, customs compliance, and international freight rate applicability.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; USDA COOL Regulations; Codex Alimentarius

description: ZIP code of the destination point for the freight lane. Used for lane-level rate matching in TMS when a specific facility ID is not available. Supports zone-based and mileage-based rate calculations.
reference: USPS ZIP Code Standard; TMS Lane Rate Management

description: Accessorial charge rate applied per hour when a carrier's equipment is detained beyond the contracted free time at origin or destination. A key component of the accessorial rate schedule for agricultural commodity shipments where loading/unloading delays are common.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Accessorial Condition; Carrier Tariff - Detention/Demurrage Rules

description: Date from which this freight rate becomes binding and applicable for freight cost estimation and carrier selection. Aligns with contract start date in carrier agreements. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Record Validity Start; GAAP Contract Accounting

description: Type of transportation equipment required for this rate. Refrigerated (reefer) equipment is required for cold chain management of perishable agricultural commodities. Hopper trailers are used for bulk grain. Tankers for liquid commodities. Critical for cold chain compliance under FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O); SAP S/4HANA TM Equipment Type

description: Date after which this freight rate is no longer valid for new shipment cost estimation. Null indicates an open-ended rate with no defined expiration. Retained post-expiry for freight audit variance analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Record Validity End; GAAP Contract Accounting

description: Number of hours of free loading/unloading time included in the base rate before detention charges begin to accrue. Standard free time for agricultural commodity shipments is typically 2 hours. Critical for managing detention/demurrage cost exposure at grain elevators and processing facilities.
reference: Carrier Tariff - Detention Rules; SAP S/4HANA TM Freight Agreement

description: Contractual freight payment terms defining who bears the freight cost and when title transfers. FOB (Free On Board) origin and destination are standard agricultural commodity trade terms. Prepaid = shipper pays; Collect = consignee pays; Third Party = billed to a third party. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA SD billing and SAP MM purchasing conditions.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); SAP S/4HANA SD - Freight Terms; GAAP

description: Indicates whether this freight rate is associated with a carrier that has been verified as compliant with the FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O). True = carrier has confirmed FSMA compliance for this lane and equipment type. Required for food safety audit and carrier qualification.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) - Sanitary Transportation Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O)

description: Description or formula code defining how the fuel surcharge is calculated, typically indexed to the US Department of Energy (DOE) weekly diesel retail price index. Examples: 'DOE_TABLE_LOOKUP', 'DOE_PERCENT_OF_BASE', 'FIXED_CPM' (cents per mile). Drives dynamic fuel surcharge computation in TMS freight cost estimation.
reference: US Department of Energy (DOE) Diesel Retail Price Index; SAP S/4HANA MM Condition Supplement

description: The current or baseline fuel surcharge rate expressed as a percentage of the base rate or as a fixed amount per mile (depending on fuel_surcharge_formula). Updated periodically based on DOE index triggers. Used in freight cost estimation and carrier invoice audit.
reference: US Department of Energy (DOE) Diesel Retail Price Index; SAP S/4HANA MM Condition Rate

description: Contracted or calculated mileage for the origin-destination freight lane. Used for per-mile rate calculations, fuel surcharge computation, and route optimization analysis. Typically derived from PC*MILER or equivalent mileage engine integrated with TMS.
reference: PC*MILER Mileage Standard; SAP S/4HANA TM - Distance Calculation

description: Accessorial charge for lumper (third-party unloading labor) services at destination facilities such as food manufacturer warehouses and retail distribution centers. Included in the accessorial rate schedule when the carrier is responsible for arranging unloading labor.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Accessorial Condition; Manhattan Associates WMS - Receiving Labor

description: The minimum freight charge applicable regardless of shipment weight, volume, or distance. Ensures carrier cost recovery for small or short-haul shipments. Applied during freight cost estimation when the calculated base rate falls below this threshold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Minimum Condition Value; Carrier Tariff Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin of the freight lane. Required for cross-border agricultural commodity movements and Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) Regulations

description: ZIP code of the origin point for the freight lane. Used for lane-level rate matching in TMS when a specific facility ID is not available. Supports zone-based and mileage-based rate calculations.
reference: USPS ZIP Code Standard; TMS Lane Rate Management

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the freight rate record, used in carrier negotiations, freight audit, and SAP S/4HANA condition record references. Enables cross-system traceability between TMS, ERP, and carrier portals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Record Number; TMS Rate Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the freight rate record. Active rates are available for freight cost estimation and carrier selection. Expired rates are retained for freight audit variance analysis against historical invoices. Pending rates are awaiting carrier confirmation. Suspended rates are temporarily unavailable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Record Status; TMS Rate Management

description: Classification of how the freight rate is calculated and applied. Per mile applies to truckload movements; per CWT (Hundredweight) and per MT (Metric Ton) apply to weight-based commodity pricing; per BU (Bushel) applies to grain and oilseed movements; flat rate applies to fixed-price lane agreements. Drives freight cost estimation logic in TMS.
reference: NMFC (National Motor Freight Classification); SAP S/4HANA MM Condition Type

description: Operational system of record from which this freight rate record originated. SAP_S4HANA indicates the rate was created in SAP S/4HANA MM condition records; TMS indicates origin from the Transportation Management System; MANUAL indicates direct entry. Supports data lineage and freight audit traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Condition Record; Oracle NetSuite Procurement

description: Accessorial charge applied for each additional stop beyond the primary origin-destination pair on a multi-stop shipment. Common in agricultural distribution routes serving multiple farm locations or delivery points. Part of the accessorial rate schedule.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Accessorial Condition; NMFTA Tariff Rules

description: Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered by this rate. Applicable to refrigerated equipment rates for perishable agricultural commodities. Enforces FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule cold chain requirements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O); FDA Food Safety Regulations; ISO 22000

description: Minimum required temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered by this rate. Applicable to refrigerated equipment rates for perishable agricultural commodities (produce, dairy, meat). Enforces FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule cold chain requirements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O); FDA Food Safety Regulations; ISO 22000

description: Contracted standard transit time in calendar days for the origin-destination lane at this rate. Used for delivery scheduling, cold chain management planning, and carrier service level evaluation. Critical for perishable agricultural commodity shipments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Transit Time; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule

description: Mode of transportation for which this freight rate applies. Truckload (TL) and less-than-truckload (LTL) are primary modes for domestic agricultural commodity movements; rail and intermodal for bulk grain; ocean for export; air for high-value perishables. Aligns with BOL and AWB documentation requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM - Freight Order; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O)

description: Timestamp when this freight rate record was last modified. Supports change tracking, freight audit variance analysis, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) - Internal Controls; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Maximum contracted volume (in the unit defined by rate_type) applicable for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper bound. Defines the upper boundary of the volume commitment tier for tiered discount rate structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scale Quantity; Carrier Contract Volume Commitment

description: Minimum contracted volume commitment (in the unit defined by rate_type, e.g., CWT, MT, BU, or number of loads) required to qualify for this rate tier. Supports volume-based discount structures in carrier contracts. Used for commitment tracking and carrier performance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Scale Quantity; Carrier Contract Volume Commitment

description: Unit of measure for the volume tier commitment quantities (volume_tier_min, volume_tier_max). Aligns with the rate_type unit. CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), and BU (Bushel) are standard agricultural commodity units. Ensures consistent volume commitment tracking across carrier contracts.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service - Commodity Units; SAP S/4HANA MM - Unit of Measure

description: Primary key for plant

Foreign key linking to finance.capital_project. Business justification: Plants are built or expanded via capital projects; linking plant to capital_project enables tracking of construction/expansion spend against the plant asset. Agriculture processing plant construction
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to finance.company_code
FK to finance.cost_center

Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Agricultural processing plants operate under specific USDA grading standards (grain grading, produce grading). This FK supports quality control operations, USDA inspection compliance reporting, and gr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.haccp_plan. Business justification: Plants (grain elevators, processing facilities) operate under specific HACCP plans as required by FSMA Preventive Controls rules. supply.plant is distinct from supply.facility and has no existing qual
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to workforce.employee

Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: NOP-certified organic handler facilities must maintain organic certification. This FK links plants to their NOP handler certification for organic integrity audits, USDA organic compliance reporting, a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: In agriculture ERP (SAP-style), a plant is assigned to a sales organization for order fulfillment determination and revenue attribution. This organizational link drives order routing, pricing procedur
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to land.parcel. Business justification: Processing plant siting requires linking the plant to its land parcel for property tax assessment, deed records, zoning compliance, and USDA/FSA registration. supply.facility already has parcel_id; su
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Self-referencing FK on plant (parent_plant_id)

Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural plants (grain elevators, packing houses, cotton gins) are specialized for primary commodity types. This FK enables commodity-specific capacity planning, USDA/FSMA registration by commodit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Plants (processing/storage facilities) are attributed to a profit center for plant-level P&L reporting. Agriculture processing plants are standard profit center assignments in ERP systems; this link e
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Plants in agriculture must maintain quality certifications (SQF, BRC, GFSI) for customer audits and regulatory compliance. supply.plant has organic_certified_flag and fsma_compliant_flag (denormalized
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

Secondary address information such as building, suite, or unit number.
Access code or identifier used by carriers for facility entry and shipment pickup/delivery coordination.
City or municipality where the plant facility is located.
Cold chain storage capacity in cubic meters for temperature-controlled agricultural products.
Three-letter ISO country code where the plant facility is located.
Timestamp when the plant facility record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the plant facility is a customs bonded warehouse for international shipments.
Date when the plant facility ceased operations or was decommissioned. Null for active facilities.
Date when the plant facility became operational and available for supply chain activities.
Primary email address for plant facility communications and logistics coordination.
Fax number for the plant facility, used for documentation transmission.
Indicates whether the plant facility meets FSMA transportation and food safety requirements.
Indicates whether the plant facility is certified to handle hazardous materials such as pesticides or fertilizers.
Timestamp when the plant facility record was last updated in the system.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant facility for route optimization and logistics planning.
Number of loading docks available at the plant facility for inbound and outbound logistics operations.

Standard operating hours of the plant facility, used for shipment scheduling and carrier coordination.

Official business name of the plant facility.
Classification of the plant facility based on its primary operational function within the agricultural supply chain.
Postal or ZIP code for the plant facility location.
Indicates whether the plant facility is equipped with RFID technology for lot tracking and traceability.
State, province, or administrative region where the plant facility is located.
Total storage capacity of the plant facility measured in cubic meters, critical for warehouse and cold storage planning.
Indicates whether the plant facility has temperature-controlled storage capabilities for cold chain management.
Indicates whether the plant facility is operated by a third-party logistics provider rather than owned directly.
IANA time zone identifier for the plant facility location, used for scheduling and shipment coordination.
Unique system-generated identifier for a transportation route definition within the agricultural supply chain network. Primary key for the route data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM (Transportation Management); Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural routes are commodity-specific (grain corridors, cold-chain produce lanes). commodity_class is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to commodity enables commodity-specific route planning
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the destination facility node (distribution center, food manufacturer, retailer DC, port terminal) where the route ends. Used for delivery planning, customer service level management, and COOL compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Shipping Point; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 CFR Part 60; FSMA

description: Identifier of the origin facility node (farm, elevator, warehouse, processing plant) where the route begins. Used for network planning, freight cost estimation, and FSMA traceability. Links to the facility/location master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Plant/Storage Location; FSMA Food Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: Identifier of the preferred carrier or 3PL assigned to this route based on contracted rates, service performance, and commodity handling capabilities. Used by TMS for automated carrier selection and freight tendering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Carrier Master; Manhattan Associates TMS

description: Historical average delay in hours experienced on this route beyond the estimated transit time, calculated from actual shipment performance data. Used for realistic delivery promise date calculation, carrier performance benchmarking, and TMS transit time buffer planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Performance Analytics; KPI (Key Performance Indicator) Transportation; USDA Agricultural Transportation Statistics

description: Timestamp when this route record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and SOX financial reporting controls for transportation cost records.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity); SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for freight cost values on this route (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN). Required for multi-currency international route cost management and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the route destination location. Required for COOL compliance, import/export documentation, APHIS permits, and international freight cost calculation.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; COOL 7 CFR Part 60; USDA APHIS; CBP Customs

description: Total planned distance of the route in kilometers, as calculated from GPS/RTK route geometry or GIS network analysis. Used for freight cost estimation, fuel consumption planning, driver hours-of-service compliance, and carbon footprint calculation.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Network Analysis; FMCSA Hours of Service 49 CFR Part 395; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Total planned distance of the route in miles, the standard unit for US domestic trucking freight rate calculations, FMCSA compliance, and carrier contract negotiations. Maintained alongside kilometers for dual-unit reporting.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR; USDA Agricultural Transportation; SAP S/4HANA TM

description: Date after which this route definition is no longer valid for TMS planning. Null indicates an open-ended route with no planned expiry. Used for seasonal route deactivation, road closure management, and contract expiry alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Route Validity; FHWA Road Closure Management

description: Date from which this route definition becomes valid and available for TMS transportation planning. Supports seasonal route activation (e.g., harvest season routes, post-spring-thaw routes) and contract-aligned route management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Route Validity; FSMA Transportation Planning

description: Estimated total transit time in hours for the route under standard operating conditions, accounting for transport mode, distance, and typical delays. Used for delivery scheduling, customer promise dates, and cold chain temperature exposure calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Transit Time; FSMA Sanitary Transportation 21 CFR Part 1; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Standard contracted freight cost per kilometer for this route in the base currency (USD). Used by TMS for freight cost estimation, carrier rate benchmarking, and transportation spend analytics. Derived from carrier contracts negotiated in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing; TMS Freight Cost Calculation; GAAP Transportation Cost Accounting

business_glossary_term: FSMA (Food Safety Modernization Act) Compliance Indicator
description: Indicates whether this route and its designated carriers have been validated as compliant with FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rules (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O), including vehicle sanitation, temperature control, and shipper-carrier agreement requirements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: Reference identifier or URI pointing to the GPS/RTK route geometry definition stored in Esri ArcGIS or the GIS layer repository. Contains the spatial polyline representation of the route for map visualization, precision agriculture VRA delivery planning, and geofencing. Follows GeoJSON or Esri feature service reference convention.
reference: Esri ArcGIS GIS Standards; RTK (Real-Time Kinematic) Positioning; ISO 19115 Geographic Metadata

description: Indicates whether this route includes one or more international border crossings requiring customs documentation, phytosanitary certificates, APHIS import/export permits, or COOL compliance. Triggers additional documentation requirements in SAP S/4HANA GTS.
reference: USDA APHIS Import/Export; COOL 7 CFR Part 60; CBP Customs Regulations; CFIA (Canada); SAP GTS

description: Indicates whether this route experiences significant traffic congestion during harvest season due to high agricultural vehicle volumes. Used by TMS for seasonal transit time adjustments, route optimization, and harvest logistics planning.
reference: USDA Agricultural Transportation; SAP S/4HANA TM Seasonal Planning; John Deere Operations Center

description: Indicates whether this route is subject to seasonal spring weight limit restrictions (spring thaw load restrictions) that reduce allowable gross vehicle weight on roads during spring months. Affects load planning, carrier selection, and delivery scheduling during spring planting season.
reference: FHWA Spring Weight Restrictions; State DOT Seasonal Load Restrictions; USDA Farm-to-Market Road Standards

description: Indicates whether this route includes toll roads, bridges, or tunnels that incur additional freight costs. Used for accurate freight cost estimation, carrier rate negotiations, and transportation spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Freight Cost Calculation; FHWA Toll Facilities

description: Indicates whether this route is approved for transportation of hazardous agricultural materials including pesticides, fertilizers (ammonium nitrate), and fumigants under DOT/EPA hazmat regulations. Drives carrier certification requirements and routing restrictions through populated areas.
reference: DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations 49 CFR Parts 171-180; EPA RCRA; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: Indicates whether this route is designated as a cold chain corridor requiring temperature-controlled transport equipment (refrigerated trailers, insulated containers) throughout the entire route. Critical for perishable agricultural commodities, fresh produce, and dairy products under FSMA Sanitary Transportation rules.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA Food Safety; GFSI Cold Chain Standards

description: Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius that must not be exceeded throughout the cold chain corridor route. Null for non-cold-chain routes. Used for carrier equipment specification, temperature monitoring alert thresholds, and FSMA compliance documentation.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation 21 CFR Part 1; FDA Food Safety; HACCP Temperature Control

description: Maximum allowable gross vehicle weight in kilograms for this route, determined by the most restrictive bridge weight limit or road weight restriction along the route. Critical for load planning of heavy agricultural commodities (grain, fertilizer, equipment) to prevent infrastructure damage and regulatory violations.
reference: FHWA Bridge Weight Limits 23 CFR Part 658; State DOT Weight Regulations; FMCSA 49 CFR Part 658

description: Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius that must be maintained throughout the cold chain corridor route. Null for non-cold-chain routes. Used for carrier equipment specification, temperature monitoring alert thresholds, and FSMA compliance documentation.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation 21 CFR Part 1; FDA Food Safety; HACCP Temperature Control

description: Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, known road hazards, carrier-specific requirements, or regulatory advisories relevant to this route. Used by dispatchers, logistics planners, and carrier coordinators for route-specific guidance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Route Notes; Manhattan Associates WMS Operational Notes; SOP (Standard Operating Procedure)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the route origin location (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Required for COOL compliance, APHIS phytosanitary documentation, customs declarations, and international freight rate determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; COOL 7 CFR Part 60; USDA APHIS; CBP Customs

description: Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) lot tracking infrastructure is available along this route for real-time shipment visibility and LOT traceability. Supports FSMA traceability requirements and Manhattan Associates WMS RFID integration.
reference: RFID (Radio Frequency Identification); FSMA Food Traceability Rule; Manhattan Associates WMS RFID; GS1 RFID Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the transportation route, used in TMS, BOL documentation, and carrier communications. Serves as the business-facing identifier across SAP S/4HANA SD/MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Transportation; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the transportation route (e.g., 'Midwest Grain Corridor - Chicago to Memphis'). Used in TMS planning screens, carrier briefings, and operational reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Current lifecycle status of the route definition. 'active' indicates the route is available for TMS planning; 'seasonal' indicates availability limited to specific periods; 'restricted' indicates regulatory or physical constraints are in effect; 'under_review' indicates pending validation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Route Master; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule

description: Classification of the route by its supply chain function: 'inbound' for farm inputs to storage/processing, 'outbound' for finished goods to customers, 'inter_facility' for transfers between company facilities, 'last_mile' for final delivery to farm or retailer, 'backhaul' for return load optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Transportation; Manhattan Associates WMS; USDA Agricultural Supply Chain

description: Calendar month number (1-12) when seasonal restrictions end on this route. Used alongside seasonal_restriction_start_month for automated TMS seasonal planning windows and carrier capacity pre-booking.
reference: State DOT Seasonal Load Restriction Programs; USDA Agricultural Transportation Seasonal Planning

description: Calendar month number (1-12) when seasonal restrictions (spring weight limits, harvest congestion, winter road closures) begin on this route. Used for automated TMS seasonal planning and proactive carrier notification.
reference: State DOT Seasonal Load Restriction Programs; USDA Agricultural Transportation Seasonal Planning

description: Primary mode of transportation for this route (e.g., truck for over-the-road, rail for grain shuttle, intermodal for container movements, barge for river grain transport). Drives carrier selection, freight cost estimation, and transit time calculation in TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM; USDA Agricultural Transportation; FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this route record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data synchronization between TMS and SAP S/4HANA, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; ISO 8601; GDPR Article 5; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

business_glossary_term: Variable Rate Application (VRA) Delivery Eligible Indicator
description: Indicates whether this route is designated for precision agriculture VRA (Variable Rate Application) input deliveries, requiring GPS-guided last-mile delivery to specific field zones. Integrates with Trimble Ag Software and John Deere Operations Center for precision delivery coordination.
reference: VRA (Variable Rate Application) Precision Agriculture; Trimble Ag Software; John Deere Operations Center; FMIS

description: Number of intermediate waypoints (stops, transfer points, relay stations, grain elevators) along the route between origin and destination. Used for multi-stop delivery planning, driver relay scheduling, and route complexity assessment in TMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TM Multi-Stop Route; Manhattan Associates TMS

description: Primary key for service_contract

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Carrier service contracts in agriculture are commodity-specific, defining temperature requirements, FSMA obligations, and committed volumes per commodity type. A domain expert expects service contract
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Carrier service contracts are executed by a specific legal entity. Multi-entity agriculture operations require company code on contracts for contract liability reporting, intercompany freight allocati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Carrier service contracts commit freight spend to a cost center for commitment accounting and budget consumption tracking. Agriculture logistics controllers assign cost centers to contracts to monitor
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the internal employee or team responsible for managing this service contract, including SLA monitoring, renewal negotiations, and counterparty relationship management. Links to workforce/HR master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchasing Group; SAP SuccessFactors Employee Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Carrier service contracts drive freight cost accruals and committed spend that must map to GL accounts for budget vs. actual freight expense reporting. Agriculture finance teams use contract GL assign
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.sales_organization. Business justification: Logistics service contracts are negotiated by and scoped to specific sales organizations in multi-org agriculture companies. Contract performance reporting, freight spend analysis by sales org, and ca
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Self-referencing identifier linking this contract to a master framework agreement or umbrella contract of which this is a sub-agreement or addendum. Null for standalone contracts. Enables contract hierarchy navigation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement Hierarchy; GAAP ASC 606

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Carrier service contracts are attributed to a profit center for logistics contract cost tracking and segment P&L reporting. Agriculture logistics contracts are managed at profit center level to enable
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Service contracts for food-grade agricultural transport require counterparties to hold specific quality certifications (GFSI, organic, FSMA) as contract conditions. Linking service_contract to quality
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.contract. Business justification: Logistics service contracts with carriers are established to fulfill volume commitments in sales contracts. Contract performance reporting, freight cost allocation, and committed-volume tracking requi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the vendor/carrier master record in the supply domain representing the contracted service provider. Links to the carrier or 3PL party master for SLA tracking, payment processing, and performance analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master (LFA1); Manhattan Associates WMS — Carrier Master

description: Indicates whether this contract automatically renews upon expiry unless either party provides notice of termination within the notice period defined in termination_notice_days. Drives contract expiry alerting and renewal workflow in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Renewal Configuration; GAAP ASC 842; IFRS 16

description: Contracted base freight rate per unit of measure (per MT, per load, per km, or per CWT as defined by freight_rate_uom). Represents the primary commercial term of the agreement. Confidential as it reflects negotiated pricing strategy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Record (KONP); GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Minimum required cargo insurance coverage amount in USD that the carrier or 3PL must maintain. Validated against certificate of insurance (COI) during contract onboarding and annual review.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR Part 387; USDA Risk Management Agency; SOX Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether the counterparty is contractually required to maintain cargo insurance coverage for agricultural commodities transported under this agreement. True triggers insurance certificate validation during onboarding and annual renewal.
reference: FMCSA Insurance Requirements 49 CFR Part 387; USDA Risk Management Agency

description: Minimum number of loads (truck, rail car, container) committed per contract period (weekly, monthly, or annually as defined by volume_period_type). Complements committed_volume_mt for load-based capacity planning and take-or-pay enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement; TMS Load Planning

description: Minimum guaranteed volume in Metric Tons (MT) per contract period that the shipper commits to tender to the carrier or 3PL. Used for take-or-pay penalty calculations and capacity planning. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM outline agreement target quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Target Quantity; USDA AMS Commodity Transport Standards

description: Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned at execution (e.g., SC-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference in SAP S/4HANA MM, TMS, and legal document management. Aligns with SAP outline agreement number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: Current lifecycle state of the service contract. Draft indicates the contract is under negotiation; active indicates it is binding and operational; expired indicates the term has lapsed without renewal; terminated indicates early cancellation; suspended indicates temporarily paused due to dispute or regulatory hold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Release Status; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: Classification of the service contract by counterparty category. Distinguishes carrier agreements (trucking, rail, ocean, air), third-party logistics (3PL) contracts, cold storage operator agreements, freight broker arrangements, customs broker engagements, and general logistics service provider contracts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier|3pl|cold_storage|freight_broker|customs_broker|logistics_service|intermodal — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Category; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: System timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this service contract record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(e); ISO 22000

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this contract (base_freight_rate, penalty amounts, incentive amounts). Typically USD for domestic US agricultural logistics; EUR or other codes for international operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Calendar date on which the service contract becomes legally binding and operationally active. Used for SLA enforcement start, rate applicability, and FSMA compliance obligation commencement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Validity Start Date; IFRS 16 Lease Commencement

description: Calendar date on which the service contract term ends. Nullable for evergreen/open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow and rate renegotiation processes in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Validity End Date; IFRS 16

description: Unit of measure against which the base_freight_rate is applied. Agricultural logistics commonly uses per Metric Ton (MT), per Bushel (BU), per Hundredweight (CWT), per load, or per kilometer depending on commodity and mode.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Unit; USDA AMS Freight Rate Standards

description: Indicates whether the counterparty has acknowledged and agreed to comply with FSMA Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart O) requirements within this contract. Mandatory for all food-grade agricultural logistics agreements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA; FSIS

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Obligation Description
description: Narrative description of specific FSMA Sanitary Transportation obligations assigned to the carrier or 3PL under this contract, including vehicle cleaning and sanitization requirements, temperature monitoring, and written procedures for food safety.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; FDA; Codex Alimentarius

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country/state code identifying the legal jurisdiction whose laws govern this contract (e.g., US-IL for Illinois, USA for federal US law). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and dispute resolution venue.
reference: ISO 3166-1; USDA; FDA; FMCSA; GDPR (for international operations)

description: Narrative description of financial incentive or bonus terms awarded for exceeding SLA targets (e.g., OTD above 98%, zero temperature exceedances, volume over-performance). Confidential commercial term used in carrier performance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Bonus Terms; GAAP ASC 606

description: Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount in USD. Covers third-party bodily injury and property damage claims arising from logistics operations under this contract.
reference: FMCSA 49 CFR Part 387; OSHA; SOX Internal Controls

description: Contracted minimum on-time delivery (OTD) percentage the carrier must achieve over the measurement period (e.g., 95.00%). Triggers SLA penalty clauses when actual OTD falls below this threshold. Key KPI for carrier performance scorecards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS — SLA Configuration; GLOBALG.A.P. Transport Standards; GFSI

description: Narrative description of financial penalty terms triggered by SLA breaches (OTD failures, temperature exceedances, volume shortfalls, cargo damage). Includes penalty calculation methodology (per-load flat fee, percentage of freight charge, or liquidated damages). Confidential commercial term.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Penalty Terms; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Narrative description of the logistics services covered under this contract, including lane definitions (origin-destination pairs), commodity types handled (grain, produce, livestock feed, refrigerated goods), equipment types, and any special handling requirements such as HACCP-compliant cold chain or FSMA-regulated food transport.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; GLOBALG.A.P. Transport Standards

description: Operational system of record from which this service contract record originated (e.g., SAP_S4HANA for MM outline agreements, ORACLE_NETSUITE for NetSuite contract records, MANUAL for paper-based contracts digitized into the platform). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Oracle NetSuite; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage Standards

description: Contracted minimum percentage of cold chain shipments that must remain within the contracted temperature range (temp_min_celsius to temp_max_celsius) throughout transit. SLA breach triggers penalty clauses and FSMA corrective action requirements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; HACCP; GFSI; SQF Code

description: Maximum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered under this contract. Defines the upper bound of the contracted temperature compliance threshold. Exceedances trigger SLA penalties and FSMA non-compliance events.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; HACCP Cold Chain Standards; FDA Food Code

description: Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments covered under this contract. Defines the lower bound of the contracted temperature compliance threshold for refrigerated or frozen agricultural commodities (produce, dairy, meat). Null for ambient/dry contracts.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; HACCP Cold Chain Standards; FDA Food Code

description: Actual date on which the contract was terminated early (prior to expiry_date). Null for contracts that expire naturally or are still active. Used for financial accrual cutoff and carrier performance record closure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Termination; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Number of calendar days advance written notice required by either party to terminate or elect not to renew this contract. Drives contract management alerts and legal review workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Terms; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Reason code for early contract termination. Used for carrier relationship analytics, legal risk reporting, and procurement sourcing strategy. Null for active or naturally expired contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Termination Reason; SOX Internal Controls

description: Contracted maximum transit time in hours from pickup to delivery for standard lanes covered under this agreement. Core SLA metric used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement and penalty trigger evaluation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS — Transit Time; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1

description: Primary mode of transportation covered by this service contract. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (FMCSA for road, FRA for rail, FMC for ocean, FAA/IATA for air) and FSMA sanitary transportation requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TMS — Shipment Type; FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1; FMCSA

description: System timestamp (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) of the most recent modification to this service contract record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(e); ISO 22000

description: Time period granularity to which committed_volume_mt and committed_loads_per_period apply. Determines the cadence for take-or-pay calculations and volume shortfall penalty assessments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Outline Agreement Period; GAAP Revenue Recognition ASC 606

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Date and time when the shipment was physically delivered to and accepted by the consignee. Used for on-time delivery KPI measurement, proof of delivery, and triggering goods receipt in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: FSMA Section 204; SAP S/4HANA MM/SD; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Date and time when the carrier physically picked up the agricultural shipment from the origin facility. Used for transit time calculation, carrier performance KPI measurement, and FSMA traceability event recording.
reference: FSMA Section 204; SAP S/4HANA TM; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Externally-known, carrier-assigned or shipper-assigned Bill of Lading number that serves as the primary legal reference for the shipment contract of carriage. Used for FSMA food safety traceability and cross-system reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA SD and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: FSMA Section 204 Traceability Rule; SAP S/4HANA SD Module; 49 CFR Part 373

description: Current lifecycle status of the BOL document. Tracks progression from draft creation through issuance, active transit, final delivery confirmation, or cancellation. Drives workflow in TMS and SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD Module; Manhattan Associates WMS

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Country Code
description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the agricultural commodity per COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) requirements. Mandatory for covered commodities under USDA COOL regulations.
reference: COOL 7 CFR Part 60 and 65; USDA Agricultural Marketing Act; ISO 3166-1

description: Timestamp when the BOL record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and FSMA record retention compliance (minimum 2-year retention for traceability records).
reference: FSMA Section 204; SOX; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Captured electronic signature data (encoded string or reference token) from the driver or consignee acknowledging receipt of goods. Supports paperless BOL workflows and serves as legal proof of delivery for dispute resolution.
reference: E-SIGN Act 15 USC 7001; FSMA Section 204; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Commercial freight payment terms indicating who bears the cost and risk of transportation. FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) are standard Incoterms used in agricultural commodity trade. Determines title transfer point and insurance responsibility.
reference: Incoterms 2020 (ICC); UCC Article 2; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Traceability Lot Code (TLC)
description: FSMA Section 204 Traceability Lot Code (TLC) assigned to the food commodity on this BOL. Mandatory for foods on the Food Traceability List (FTL) under the FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods.
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S; Food Traceability List (FTL)

description: Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials (e.g., agricultural chemicals, pesticides, fertilizers classified as hazmat). When true, DOT hazmat regulations and EPA reporting requirements apply.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 172; EPA RCRA; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Calendar date on which the Bill of Lading was officially issued and signed, representing the principal business event date for the shipment contract. Required for FSMA traceability records and COOL compliance documentation.
reference: FSMA Section 204; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 CFR Part 60; 49 CFR Part 373

description: Internal facility or location code for the shipment origin point (farm, warehouse, processing plant). Maps to the storage location master in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS for inventory reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Manhattan Associates WMS

description: Number of pallets included in the shipment. Used for trailer space planning, dock scheduling, and warehouse receiving operations in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Indicates who is responsible for paying the freight charges: prepaid (shipper pays), collect (consignee pays), or third-party billing. Drives accounts payable and receivable processing in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: 49 CFR Part 373; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Carrier-assigned Progressive (PRO) number used to track the shipment within the carrier's system. Distinct from the BOL number; used for freight claims, shipment tracing, and carrier invoice reconciliation.
reference: 49 CFR Part 373; NMFTA Freight Bill Standards

description: The date and time when this lot traceability record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and regulatory record retention compliance.

description: The date and time of the most recent chain-of-custody transfer event for this lot. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the current custody state. Updated at each facility handoff.

description: The date on which the final disposition of this lot was recorded (sold, consumed, destroyed, etc.). Used for lot lifecycle closure reporting and regulatory record retention.

description: The ultimate disposition of this lot upon closure of the traceability record. 'sold' = delivered to customer; 'consumed' = used in internal processing; 'destroyed' = disposed due to quality failure or recall; 'donated' = donated to food bank or relief; 'returned' = returned to supplier; 'pending' = disposition not yet determined.
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA recall procedures; USDA commodity disposition reporting

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Traceability Lot Code (TLC)
description: The FSMA Section 204-compliant Traceability Lot Code (TLC) assigned to this lot at the first point of land-based receiving or transformation. Required for FDA food traceability rule compliance and one-up/one-back recall response.
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA Food Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S

business_glossary_term: FSMA Traceability Lot Code (TLC) Source Point
description: Identifies the point in the supply chain where the FSMA Traceability Lot Code (TLC) was first assigned. Required by FDA food traceability rule to establish the Critical Tracking Event (CTE) origin.
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA Food Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S

description: The calendar date on which this lot was harvested from the originating field. Critical for Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) compliance calculations and shelf-life determination. Sourced from Climate FieldView or John Deere Operations Center harvest records.
reference: FSMA Section 204 Key Data Elements; EPA PHI requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Classifies the nature of the lot creation or transformation event. 'original' = first-time lot creation at harvest; 'split' = divided from a parent lot; 'merge' = combined from multiple parent lots; 'repack' = repackaged without commodity change; 'reclassify' = grade or classification change.
reference: FSMA Section 204 Critical Tracking Events (CTE); GLOBALG.A.P. Chain of Custody

description: The externally-known, human-readable lot identifier assigned at origin (farm or first point of aggregation). Serves as the primary business identifier for traceability across the supply chain. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM batch number and FSMA Traceability Lot Code (TLC).
reference: FSMA Section 204; FDA Food Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S; GLOBALG.A.P. Chain of Custody

description: Current lifecycle status of the lot within the supply chain traceability record. Drives operational workflows, recall response, and final disposition reporting. 'on_hold' indicates quarantine or quality hold pending investigation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|in_transit|in_storage|processing|on_hold|sold|consumed|destroyed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FSMA Section 204; GLOBALG.A.P. Chain of Custody; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Status

description: The moisture content of the commodity in this lot expressed as a percentage. Critical for storage safety (mold/mycotoxin prevention), weight adjustment calculations, and grade determination. Measured at intake and updated after drying operations.
reference: USDA Grain Grading Standards 7 CFR Part 810; GLOBALG.A.P. post-harvest handling

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this lot was produced or harvested. Required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance and import/export documentation.
reference: USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 CFR Part 60; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; Codex Alimentarius

description: Indicates whether the required Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) was observed between the last pesticide application and the harvest date for this lot. True = PHI compliance confirmed; False = PHI violation detected. Mandatory for food safety and EPA compliance.
reference: EPA Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) requirements; FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112

description: The actual number of days elapsed between the last recorded pesticide application and the harvest date for this lot. Used to verify PHI compliance against the label-required interval.
reference: EPA PHI label requirements; FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112

description: The current net quantity of commodity in this lot expressed in Bushels (BU), the standard US grain trade unit. Maintained alongside MT for domestic market reporting and USDA WASDE alignment.
reference: USDA WASDE; CBOT grain contract specifications; SAP S/4HANA MM alternate unit of measure

description: The current net quantity of commodity in this lot expressed in Metric Tons (MT). Updated at each custody transfer or split/merge event. Principal quantitative measure for the lot.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Quantity; WASDE reporting units

description: Indicates whether this lot is currently subject to an active recall or withdrawal action. True = lot implicated in recall; False = no active recall. Triggers immediate operational holds and one-up/one-back notification workflows.
reference: FDA Recall Procedures 21 CFR Part 7; FSMA Section 204; USDA FSIS recall procedures

description: The official FDA or USDA recall reference number associated with this lot if recall_flag is True. Enables cross-referencing with regulatory recall databases and internal recall management records.
reference: FDA Recall Procedures 21 CFR Part 7; USDA FSIS Recall Case Number; FSMA Section 204

description: Indicates whether this lot requires continuous cold chain management throughout storage and transportation. True = cold chain mandatory; False = ambient storage acceptable. Drives carrier selection and facility assignment.
reference: FDA FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: The current active RFID tag EPC (Electronic Product Code) associated with this lot for automated identification and tracking through the supply chain. Supports real-time location tracking in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: GS1 EPC Tag Data Standard; RFID-enabled supply chain traceability; FSMA Section 204

description: The operational system of record from which this lot traceability record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.

description: The maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this lot. Exceedance triggers a cold chain exception event and potential quality hold.

description: The minimum required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this lot to maintain food safety and quality. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring and facility assignment validation.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Sanitary Transportation Rule; GLOBALG.A.P. cold chain requirements

description: The date and time when this lot traceability record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and change detection for downstream consumers.

description: Minimum allowable temperature in degrees Celsius for cold chain shipments during transport. Breach of this threshold triggers an exception event and potential food safety hold per HACCP and FSMA requirements.
reference: FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule; HACCP; FDA 21 CFR Part 110 (GMP); ISO 22000

description: Net weight of the agricultural commodity in Metric Tons (MT), excluding tare weight of containers and packaging. Used for commodity pricing, yield reconciliation, and USDA grading.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin. Required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, customs documentation, and international trade reporting.
reference: COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) - 7 CFR Part 60; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; USDA AMS

description: Timestamp when proof of delivery was captured at the destination, including electronic signature, GPS coordinates, and/or photo evidence. Triggers invoice payment release in SAP S/4HANA FI and closes the delivery lifecycle.

description: Indicates whether this shipment requires temperature-controlled cold chain management throughout transport. True for fresh produce, dairy, meat, and certain agricultural inputs requiring refrigeration per FDA and FSMA requirements.

description: Planned date on which the shipment is scheduled to depart from the origin facility. Used for transportation planning, carrier booking, and on-time departure KPI measurement.

description: Tamper-evident seal number affixed to the transport container or trailer at point of loading. Required for food safety chain-of-custody verification and documented on the BOL per 49 CFR and FSMA Sanitary Transportation Rule.

description: Identifier of the internal employee or account manager responsible for managing and maintaining this account relationship. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors workforce records and aligned with Salesforce CRM owner assignments.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the originating or initiating account party in the relationship (e.g., the cooperative, buying group, or broker initiating the affiliation). Represents side A of the lateral relationship.

description: Identifier of the receiving or counterpart account party in the relationship (e.g., the grain elevator, food manufacturer, or retailer on the receiving end). Represents side B of the lateral relationship.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master

description: Contractually committed annual commodity volume in Metric Tons (MT) that the source account agrees to supply or the target account agrees to purchase within this relationship. Supports WASDE-aligned supply planning and cooperative grain pool management.
reference: USDA WASDE; USDA AMS Grain Marketing; SAP S/4HANA SD Scheduling Agreements

description: Regulatory license number of the agricultural commodity broker when relationship_type is broker_buyer. Required for compliance with USDA Packers and Stockyards Act and grain broker licensing regulations.

description: Identifier for the buying group or purchasing alliance to which the target account belongs when relationship_type is buying_group. Supports volume-based pricing and consolidated procurement negotiations in SAP S/4HANA SD.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Buying Group Configuration; USDA Agricultural Marketing Act

description: Indicates whether the target account acts as a co-packer (True) for the source account, manufacturing or packaging agricultural products under the source accounts brand or specifications. Supports FSMA supplier verification and GFSI audit tracking.
reference: FSMA Section 103 Preventive Controls; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; BRC Global Standard Issue 9

description: Indicates whether the account relationship is underpinned by a formal written contract (True) or is an informal/operational arrangement (False). Supports legal and compliance review processes under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.
reference: FSMA Supplier Verification; GLOBALG.A.P. Contract Farming Standard; SOX Internal Controls

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Compliance Required Flag
description: Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance documentation is required for commodity transactions within this relationship. Applicable to covered commodities under USDA AMS COOL regulations.
reference: USDA AMS COOL Regulations (7 CFR Part 60 and 65); Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946

description: Formal membership identifier assigned by the agricultural cooperative to the member account. Applicable when relationship_type is cooperative_member. Used for grain marketing pool allocations and patronage dividend calculations.
reference: USDA Agricultural Cooperative Service; USDA AMS Grain Marketing Rules

description: Timestamp when this account relationship record was first created in the system. Provides full audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 22000 Record Keeping

description: Maximum aggregate credit exposure permitted across both accounts within this relationship, expressed in the base currency. Used for combined credit risk management in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Oracle NetSuite AR modules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310 Credit Risk

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to financial terms within this relationship (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Governs credit exposure limit and any contractual monetary thresholds.

description: Calendar date on which the account relationship becomes or became legally and operationally binding. Used for time-bounded network analysis and contract compliance tracking.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Management

description: Indicates whether the relationship carries an exclusivity clause (True), preventing either party from entering into equivalent relationships with competing accounts within the defined commodity and geographic scope.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Terms; USDA Agricultural Marketing Act; IFRS 15

description: Calendar date on which the account relationship expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null indicates an open-ended relationship with no defined end. Supports proactive renewal management in Salesforce CRM.
reference: IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Expiry; Salesforce CRM Opportunity Management

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Verified Supplier Flag
description: Indicates whether the target account has been verified as a qualified supplier under FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP) or Preventive Controls supplier verification requirements applicable to this relationship.
reference: FSMA Section 301 FSVP; FDA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L; FSMA Preventive Controls Rule

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or region code defining the geographic territory within which this account relationship is operative. Supports COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance and territorial sales analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; USDA COOL Regulations (7 CFR Part 60); GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether the target account holds a valid GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification relevant to this relationship. Supports buyer certification requirements and supply chain compliance audits.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Certification Standard v5.4; GFSI Benchmarking

description: GMO handling preference governing commodity transactions within this relationship. Drives identity preservation protocols, segregation requirements, and labeling compliance under USDA BE Disclosure Standard.
reference: USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (7 CFR Part 66); GLOBALG.A.P. GMO Module

description: Date on which the account relationship was most recently formally reviewed by the relationship manager or account team. Supports periodic relationship health checks and CRM activity tracking in Salesforce.

description: Planned date for the next formal review of this account relationship. Enables proactive relationship management workflows and CRM task generation in Salesforce for the assigned relationship manager.
reference: Salesforce CRM Task Management; Internal Account Management SOP

description: Indicates whether the relationship involves certified organic commodity transactions (True), requiring both parties to maintain USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification. Drives organic supply chain segregation and audit trail requirements.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; USDA AMS Organic Integrity Database

description: Designates the target account as a preferred or strategic partner (True) for the source account. Influences pricing tiers, allocation priority during commodity shortages, and CRM opportunity scoring in Salesforce.
