description: Type of pricing mechanism governing commodity transactions within this relationship. Fixed pricing locks a rate; basis pricing ties to a futures market spread; spot uses current market price; formula uses a defined calculation; negotiated is case-by-case.
reference: USDA AMS Commodity Pricing; DTN Market Data Standards; CME Group Grain Futures

description: Indicates whether the relationship is directional (source acts upon target only) or mutual (both parties share equal standing). Critical for cooperative membership networks where obligations may flow one way.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Relationship Model; GLOBALG.A.P. Group Certification

description: Free-text field for capturing qualitative context, special terms, operational nuances, or historical background relevant to this account relationship. Used by relationship managers for CRM narrative and account planning in Salesforce.
reference: Salesforce CRM Notes and Attachments; Internal Account Management SOP

description: Current lifecycle state of the account relationship. Drives CRM pipeline visibility and supply chain partner eligibility checks in Salesforce and SAP S/4HANA SD.
reference: Salesforce CRM; SAP S/4HANA SD Partner Functions

description: Categorical classification of the lateral relationship between the two account parties. Drives network analysis for agricultural commodity trading and cooperative grain marketing. reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Supply Chain Standard; USDA Agricultural Marketing Act; Salesforce CRM Relationship Type

description: External identifier for this account relationship record as stored in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for sales pipeline and partner management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Relationship Object

description: Indicates whether this relationship involves a toll processing arrangement (True), where one party processes agricultural commodities owned by the other party for a fee. Relevant for grain milling, oilseed crushing, and feed manufacturing partnerships.
reference: USDA FSIS Processing Agreements; FSMA Preventive Controls Rule; SAP S/4HANA PP Subcontracting

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this account relationship record. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture, and audit trail requirements in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 22000 Record Keeping; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Unit of measure for the annual_volume_commitment field. Standard agricultural units include Metric Ton (MT), Bushel (BU), Hundredweight (CWT), Pound (LB), and Kilogram (KG). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM unit of measure configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; USDA NASS Commodity Reporting Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each address record associated with a customer account or contact. Serves as the primary key for the address entity in the customer domain Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module – Business Partner Address Object

description: Reference to the customer account this address belongs to. Links the address record to the parent customer entity in the customer domain.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Account Object; SAP S/4HANA SD – Business Partner

description: Reference to the individual contact person this address is associated with, when the address belongs to a specific contact rather than the overall account. Nullable for account-level addresses.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Contact Object

description: Self-referencing FK on address (parent_address_id)

Identifies the operational system of record from which this address was originally sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution during master data management synchronization across Salesforce CRM, SAP S/4HANA, Oracle NetSuite, and Granular Farm Management.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance – Master Data Management (MDM); GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing)

description: Classifies the functional purpose of the address. 'billing' is the invoicing address; 'shipping' is the delivery destination; 'registered' is the legal business registration address; 'farm_gate' is a pickup point at the farm; 'cold_storage' is a refrigerated facility address; 'mailing' is for general correspondence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|shipping|registered|farm_gate|cold_storage|mailing|warehouse|processing_plant — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Address Usage Type; Salesforce CRM – Address Type Field

description: City, town, municipality, or locality name of the address. For rural agricultural addresses, may reference the nearest incorporated municipality.
reference: USPS Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-1 (Addressing)

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Country Code
description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code designating the country of origin for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance purposes. May differ from the physical address country for re-export or processing facility scenarios. Mandatory for USDA COOL compliance on covered commodities including beef, pork, lamb, chicken, fish, shellfish, perishable agricultural commodities, peanuts, pecans, ginseng, and macadamia nuts.
reference: COOL – Country of Origin Labeling (7 CFR Part 60 and 65); USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS); FSMA

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the address (e.g., 'USA', 'CAN', 'MEX', 'BRA'). Used for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, international shipping documentation (BOL, AWB), and FDA/USDA import/export regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; COOL – Country of Origin Labeling (7 CFR Part 60); FDA Import Regulations; USDA APHIS

description: County or parish name of the address. Critical for USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) county-level agricultural program eligibility, crop insurance administration, and state agricultural reporting. Also used for EPA and APHIS county-level regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: USDA Farm Service Agency (FSA) County Office Structure; USDA National Agricultural Statistics Service (NASS); EPA County-Level Reporting

description: Timestamp when this address record was first created in the system. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under FSMA, GDPR, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing Activities); SOX Section 404; FSMA Recordkeeping Requirements

description: Free-text field for special delivery instructions specific to this address. Includes gate access codes, dock numbers, receiving hours, refrigeration requirements for cold chain deliveries, and farm-specific access directions not captured in structured address fields. Sourced from Manhattan Associates WMS and TMS delivery notes.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS – Delivery Notes; SAP S/4HANA SD – Delivery Instructions

description: Indicates that shipments must not be dispatched to this address. May be set due to regulatory restrictions, customer request, failed delivery history, or compliance holds. Enforced during order fulfillment in Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP S/4HANA SD.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS – Address Restrictions; SAP S/4HANA SD – Delivery Block

description: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS (Data Universal Numbering System) nine-digit identifier for the business entity at this address. Used for supplier qualification, credit risk assessment, USDA program eligibility verification, and federal procurement compliance.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS Number Standard; USDA Supplier Qualification; SAM.gov Federal Procurement

description: Date from which this address record is valid and effective for business use. Supports time-bounded address management for seasonal farm gate locations, temporary cold storage facilities, and lease-based distribution points.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Address Validity Period; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Date after which this address record is no longer valid for business use. Nullable for indefinitely active addresses. Used to manage seasonal farm gate closures, facility relocations, and lease expirations for distribution network addresses.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Address Validity Period; ISO 8601 Date Standard

description: Classifies the type of physical facility at this address. Supports logistics planning, cold chain management, and regulatory compliance routing. 'farm' indicates a production site; 'cold_storage' indicates temperature-controlled facility relevant to food safety (FSMA, HACCP); 'cooperative' indicates an agricultural cooperative location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: farm|warehouse|distribution_center|cold_storage|processing_plant|retail_store|office|cooperative|grain_elevator|packing_house — promote to reference product]
reference: FSMA – Food Safety Modernization Act; HACCP – Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points; USDA FSIS

description: Five-digit FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) county code assigned by the U.S. Census Bureau. Used for USDA program administration, FSA county office routing, crop insurance mapping, and federal agricultural subsidy eligibility determination.
reference: FIPS 6-4 (Counties and Equivalent Entities); USDA Farm Service Agency; USDA Risk Management Agency (RMA)

description: Precision level of the latitude/longitude geocoding result from Esri ArcGIS. 'rooftop' indicates exact parcel-level match; 'range_interpolated' is street-level interpolation; 'geometric_center' is centroid of a polygon; 'approximate' is postal code or city centroid; 'unverified' means coordinates have not been validated.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Geocoding Quality Codes; ISO 19160-4 (Address Quality)

description: GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) — a 13-digit identifier uniquely identifying this physical location in the global supply chain. reference: GS1 Global Location Number (GLN) Standard; GLOBALG.A.P. Location Registration; GS1-128 Logistics Label Standard; FSMA Traceability Rule (21 CFR Part 1, Subpart S)

description: Indicates whether this address record is currently active and usable for business operations. Inactive addresses are retained for historical audit and traceability but excluded from operational address selection for orders, invoices, and shipments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD – Address Validity; Salesforce CRM – Address Status

description: Indicates whether this is the primary address for the customer account or contact. Only one address per customer per address type should be flagged as primary. Used to determine the default address for billing, shipping, or correspondence when multiple addresses exist.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Primary Address Flag; SAP S/4HANA SD – Standard Address

description: Indicates whether this is a residential address as opposed to a commercial or agricultural business address. Affects carrier surcharge calculations, delivery scheduling windows, and B2C vs B2B routing logic for direct-to-consumer agricultural product deliveries.
reference: USPS Residential Delivery Indicator; UPS/FedEx Residential Surcharge Standards

description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), sourced from Esri ArcGIS geocoding. Supports GIS-based spatial analysis, delivery route optimization via TMS, farm gate proximity calculations, and precision agriculture field mapping. Range: -90.0000000 to +90.0000000.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Geocoding Service; WGS84 Datum (EPSG:4326); ISO 6709

description: Primary street address line including street number and street name. For farm gate addresses, may include rural route or farm road designation.
reference: USPS Addressing Standards; GDPR Article 4; ISO 19160-1 (Addressing)

description: Secondary address line for suite, unit, building, floor, dock number, or additional location detail. Commonly used for distribution center dock numbers or warehouse unit identifiers.
reference: USPS Addressing Standards; ISO 19160-1 (Addressing)

description: Tertiary address line for additional location detail such as industrial park name, complex name, or rural delivery instructions. Used primarily for international addresses requiring additional lines.
reference: ISO 19160-1 (Addressing); Universal Postal Union (UPU) Standards

description: Indicates whether a loading dock is available at this address for freight deliveries. Critical for logistics planning of bulk agricultural inputs (fertilizers, seeds, pesticides) and large-volume produce shipments requiring dock-height unloading.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS – Facility Capabilities; SAP S/4HANA SD – Shipping Point Configuration

description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), sourced from Esri ArcGIS geocoding. Supports GIS-based spatial analysis, delivery route optimization, and proximity-based territory management. Range: -180.0000000 to +180.0000000.
reference: Esri ArcGIS Geocoding Service; WGS84 Datum (EPSG:4326); ISO 6709

description: ZIP code or postal code for the address. Supports USPS ZIP+4 format for domestic addresses and international postal code formats. Used for delivery routing, sales territory assignment, and COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance zone mapping.
reference: USPS ZIP Code Standards; Universal Postal Union (UPU); COOL – Country of Origin Labeling (7 CFR Part 60)

description: Indicates whether refrigerated or cold storage receiving capability is available at this address. Essential for cold chain compliance when delivering perishable agricultural commodities, fresh produce, dairy, and meat products per FSMA and HACCP requirements.
reference: FSMA – Sanitary Transportation of Human and Animal Food (21 CFR Part 1); HACCP; FDA Food Safety

description: External identifier for this address record in Salesforce CRM. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Databricks Silver layer and Salesforce for sales pipeline management, partner relationship tracking, and customer account maintenance.
reference: Salesforce CRM – Account/Contact Address Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Integration

description: External identifier for this address record in SAP S/4HANA Business Partner (BP) address management. Used for reconciliation between the enterprise ERP and the lakehouse Silver layer for billing, shipping, and procurement address data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA – Business Partner (BP) Address Management; SAP MM/SD Modules

description: State, province, territory, or administrative region of the address. Stored as ISO 3166-2 subdivision code (e.g., 'US-IA' for Iowa, 'CA-ON' for Ontario). Critical for USDA state-level agricultural reporting and regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-2 (Country Subdivision Codes); USDA State Agricultural Statistics

description: IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used for scheduling delivery windows, coordinating harvest pickup times, and aligning order cutoff times across multi-timezone distribution networks.

description: Timestamp when this address record was last modified. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and audit compliance under GDPR and SOX.
reference: GDPR Article 30; SOX Section 404; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Date on which the address was last validated against postal authority databases or geocoding services. Used to schedule re-validation cycles and assess address data freshness for logistics and compliance purposes.
reference: USPS CASS Certification Standards; ISO 19160-4 (Address Quality)

description: Current validation status of the address record against postal authority databases. 'validated' confirms the address is deliverable per USPS CASS certification or international equivalent; 'unvalidated' has not been checked; 'invalid' failed validation; 'pending_review' requires manual verification. Critical for ensuring delivery accuracy for agricultural inputs and produce shipments.
reference: USPS Coding Accuracy Support System (CASS); Universal Postal Union (UPU); ISO 19160-4

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the individual contact person record within the customer domain. Serves as the primary key for all contact-related data.

description: Reference to the parent customer account with which this contact is associated (e.g., food manufacturer, distributor, retailer, cooperative).

description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the contact's manager or superior within the same customer organization. Enables organizational hierarchy mapping for enterprise B2B accounts (e.g., a buying agent reporting to a procurement director).

description: The contact's functional role within the sales and account management process. Supports Salesforce CRM opportunity management and stakeholder mapping for complex B2B agricultural supply chain deals.

description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. 'active' indicates an engaged contact; 'inactive' indicates a former contact no longer at the organization; 'pending' indicates awaiting verification; 'do_not_contact' indicates opt-out or legal restriction.
reference: GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure); Salesforce CRM — Contact Status

description: Classification of the contact's functional role in the business relationship. 'primary' is the main account contact; 'technical' covers agronomists and food safety officers; 'billing' covers accounts payable; 'regulatory' covers FSMA/HACCP compliance officers. description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the contact is based. Used for GDPR jurisdiction determination, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) preference alignment, and regional sales territory assignment.

description: Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail anchor for record lineage, GDPR data retention calculations, and data governance compliance.

description: Organizational department or functional area of the contact within their company (e.g., Procurement, Food Safety, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Agronomy, Finance). Supports targeted communication and segmentation.

description: Primary business email address of the contact. Used for order confirmations, compliance notifications, FSMA correspondence, and marketing communications subject to GDPR consent.

description: Given (first) name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communication and CRM correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; GLOBALG.A.P. Contact Record Standard

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Lawful Processing Basis
description: The GDPR lawful basis under which this contact's personal data is processed. Required for all contacts associated with EU/international operations. 'consent' requires explicit opt-in; 'contract' applies to contacts involved in active supply agreements; 'legitimate_interest' applies to B2B prospecting.
reference: GDPR Article 6; GDPR Article 9; General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679

description: Indicates whether this contact holds purchasing or contractual decision-making authority at the customer organization. Used by sales teams to prioritize engagement and route proposals through Salesforce CRM opportunity management.

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Point of Contact Flag

reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Section 204; 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) Coordinator Flag
description: Indicates whether this contact serves as the HACCP coordinator at the customer organization. HACCP coordinators are responsible for food safety plan oversight and are key contacts for quality assurance, audit communications, and corrective action notifications.

description: Indicates whether this contact is the primary/main point of contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per customer account should be flagged as primary. Used for default routing of account communications and escalations.

description: Official job title or position of the contact at their organization (e.g., Procurement Manager, Food Safety Officer, Agronomist, Buying Agent, Quality Assurance Director). Used for routing communications and identifying decision-making authority.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Contact Object; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 region subtag) representing the contact's preferred language for communications (e.g., en-US, es-MX, fr-FR, pt-BR). Supports multilingual correspondence for global food manufacturer and distributor contacts.
reference: ISO 639-1; ISO 3166-1; GDPR Article 12 (Transparent Communication in Intelligible Language)

description: Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to identify dormant contacts, measure engagement health, and trigger re-engagement workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Last Activity Date Field; CRM KPI Best Practices

description: Family (last) name of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for reporting and correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; GLOBALG.A.P. Contact Record Standard

description: The originating channel or event through which this contact was first acquired or identified. Used for marketing attribution, ROI analysis of lead generation programs, and sales pipeline reporting in Salesforce CRM.

description: URL to the contact's LinkedIn professional profile. Used by sales and account management teams for relationship intelligence, prospecting, and partner engagement via Salesforce CRM integration.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Contact Social Fields; GDPR Article 6 (Legitimate Interest)

description: Indicates whether the contact has provided explicit consent to receive marketing communications (newsletters, product promotions, agricultural insights). Required under GDPR for international operations and CAN-SPAM for US contacts. True = opted in; False = opted out or no consent given.

description: Date on which the contact provided explicit marketing consent. Required for GDPR audit trail demonstrating lawful basis for processing personal data for marketing purposes.

description: Date on which the contact withdrew marketing consent or requested opt-out. Triggers suppression from all marketing campaigns. Required for GDPR compliance and CAN-SPAM unsubscribe processing.

description: Mobile/cell phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for SMS alerts, two-factor authentication, and field-level communications with agronomists or logistics coordinators.

description: Free-text field for account managers and sales representatives to capture qualitative context about the contact, including relationship history, communication preferences, and strategic account notes. description: Primary business phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for urgent supply chain communications, food safety alerts, and account management calls.

description: The contact's preferred channel for receiving business communications. Drives outreach strategy for sales, compliance notifications, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with GDPR consent management.
reference: GDPR Article 7; Salesforce CRM — Contact Preferences

description: External identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM, used for bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for sales pipeline and partner management.

description: Formal salutation or honorific prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal correspondence, contract documents, and regulatory communications.

description: IANA time zone identifier for the contact's location (e.g., America/Chicago, Europe/London). Used to schedule communications, meetings, and automated alerts at appropriate local times for global agricultural supply chain partners.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; Salesforce CRM — Contact Time Zone

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the contact record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer pipeline, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy Principle); SOX Section 404; Databricks Delta Lake CDC

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each authorized delivery and receiving location registered to a customer account. Primary key for the delivery_location entity.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Free-text notes entered by the credit analyst documenting the rationale for credit decisions, observations from customer financial reviews, special conditions, or escalation context. Supports audit trail and credit committee review processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; SOX; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date on which the credit hold was placed on the customer account. Populated when credit_hold_flag is True. Used to track hold duration and escalation timelines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/SD Credit Management

description: Indicates whether the customer account is currently placed on credit hold, blocking new sales order processing and shipment release. When True, all outbound order fulfillment is suspended pending credit resolution. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA credit block indicator (VBAK-CMGST / KNKK-CTLPC).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Credit Management; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Reason code explaining why the customer account is on credit hold. Populated when credit_hold_flag is True. 'compliance_hold' covers FSMA, GLOBALG.A.P., or regulatory non-compliance scenarios that trigger a commercial hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overdue_balance|credit_limit_exceeded|payment_default|legal_dispute|manual_hold|compliance_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Credit Management; FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Date on which the most recent credit hold was released and normal trading resumed. Null if the account is currently on hold or has never been held. Used for hold duration analysis and collections performance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/SD Credit Management

description: Indicates whether the customer account is covered by a trade credit insurance policy, which mitigates the company's exposure to non-payment risk. When True, the insured credit limit and policy details should be referenced in the credit analyst notes.
reference: IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310; Trade Credit Insurance industry practice

description: Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer account. Populated only when credit_insurance_flag is True. Used for claims processing and insurer reporting.
reference: Trade Credit Insurance; IFRS 9

description: Maximum approved trade credit exposure extended to the customer account, expressed in the account base currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI credit segment (FD32 / KNKK-KLIMK). Governs order release and credit hold logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR Credit Management; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date from which the current credit limit became effective. Enables historical tracking of credit limit changes and supports audit trail requirements for credit management governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9; SOX

description: Date on which the current approved credit limit expires and requires renewal or re-approval. Null indicates an open-ended credit limit with no scheduled expiry. Used to trigger credit renewal workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9

description: Scheduled or next-due date for the periodic credit review of this customer account. Credit analysts use this date to trigger reassessment of credit limits, ratings, and payment terms. Typically set annually or triggered by payment events.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Aggregated credit risk classification for the customer account, derived from internal rating, DSO, payment history, and external rating inputs. Used for portfolio-level credit risk reporting, provisioning under IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss, and prioritization of credit analyst workload.
reference: IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss; GAAP ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the credit limit, outstanding balance, and available credit are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Aligns with the customer account base currency in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; IFRS

description: Number of currently open invoice disputes raised by or against the customer. Elevated dispute counts may indicate billing issues, quality disputes (e.g., COA failures, MRL exceedances), or credit risk signals. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR dispute management or Oracle NetSuite AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR Dispute Management; Oracle NetSuite AR; FSMA

description: Days Sales Outstanding — the average number of days the customer takes to pay invoices, calculated over the trailing measurement period. A key accounts receivable efficiency and credit risk KPI. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR aging analysis or Oracle NetSuite AR reports. High DSO values may trigger credit review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9

description: The rolling lookback period in calendar days over which the DSO metric was calculated (e.g., 90, 180, 365). Provides context for interpreting the dso_days value and ensures consistent comparison across customers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date from which this credit profile record became the active governing credit profile for the customer account. Supports temporal validity tracking and historical credit profile versioning in the lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9; SOX

description: Credit rating or score sourced from an external credit bureau or agency such as Dun & Bradstreet (D&B PAYDEX score), Experian Business, or Equifax Commercial. Stored as a free-form string to accommodate varying agency rating scales. Supplements the internal rating for credit limit decisions and risk classification.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet PAYDEX; Experian Business Credit; IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Name of the third-party credit bureau or rating agency that provided the external_credit_rating. Enables traceability and comparison across multiple agency sources when multiple ratings are maintained. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Dun & Bradstreet|Experian|Equifax|Moody's|S&P|Fitch|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Dun & Bradstreet; Experian Business; IFRS 9

description: Internally assigned credit risk rating for the customer, reflecting the company's own assessment of payment reliability and financial health. Rating scale: A1 (lowest risk) through D (highest risk / default). Assigned by the credit analyst and reviewed periodically. Used for credit limit setting and risk-based pricing decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A1|A2|A3|B1|B2|B3|C1|C2|D — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss; GAAP ASC 310

description: Monetary value of the most recent payment received from the customer, in the account base currency. Provides context alongside last_payment_date for assessing payment behavior and partial payment patterns.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date of the most recent payment received from the customer against an outstanding invoice or account balance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR clearing records or Oracle NetSuite AR payment history. Used in DSO calculation and payment behavior analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed for this customer account. Used to track review frequency compliance and to calculate time elapsed since last assessment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IFRS 9

description: The internal customer ID as maintained in Oracle NetSuite AR, used for cross-system reconciliation of credit exposure data between SAP S/4HANA and NetSuite for customers managed in both systems.
reference: Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Total value of open, unshipped, or unbilled sales orders currently outstanding for the customer. Included in credit exposure calculations alongside the AR balance to determine true credit utilization. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD open order value (VBAK/VBAP).
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Credit Management; GAAP ASC 606

description: Current total open accounts receivable balance for the customer, including all unpaid invoices and debit memos. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR open item management or Oracle NetSuite AR. Used to calculate available credit and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9

description: Total value of invoices that have passed their due date and remain unpaid, in the account base currency. A key indicator of collection risk. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR aging buckets or Oracle NetSuite AR aging report.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9

description: Aging bucket classification for the customer's overdue balance, aligned with standard AR aging analysis periods: current (not yet due), 1–30 days, 31–60 days, 61–90 days, 91–120 days, over 120 days. Drives collections prioritization and IFRS 9 provisioning stage assignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/AR Aging; Oracle NetSuite AR; IFRS 9 Stage Classification; GAAP ASC 310

description: Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the customer's historical payment behavior, derived from on-time payment rate, average days late, and dispute frequency. Higher scores indicate better payment discipline. Used to calibrate internal credit ratings and credit limit decisions.

description: Standard payment terms code assigned to this customer credit profile, governing invoice due dates and early payment discount eligibility (e.g., NET30, NET60, NET90, COD, CIA, 2/10NET30). reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: SOX Section 404, GAAP ASC 606, ISO 22000

description: Minimum approved credit limit (in functional currency) that a customer must hold to be eligible for this payment term. Supports credit risk management for extended or deferred agricultural payment programs. Null if no minimum credit limit is required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management (FCV), GAAP ASC 310

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this payment term's monetary thresholds and discount calculations are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency agricultural export transactions.
reference: ISO 4217, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Maximum number of calendar days that payment may be deferred from the invoice date under a seasonal deferral program, regardless of when the trigger event occurs. Caps the deferral window to manage credit exposure. Null when is_seasonal_deferral is false.
reference: USDA Farm Service Agency, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: The business event that triggers the start of the payment window for seasonally deferred terms. For example, 'harvest_completion' starts the net due days clock after crop harvest is recorded. Null when is_seasonal_deferral is false.
reference: USDA Farm Service Agency, GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition, Granular Farm Management

description: Number of days from the baseline date within which payment must be received to qualify for the first-tier early payment discount percentage. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD1T field. Null if no early payment discount applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI ZBD1T, GAAP ASC 606 Variable Consideration

description: Number of days from the baseline date within which payment must be received to qualify for the second-tier early payment discount. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD2T field. Null if no second discount tier applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI ZBD2T, GAAP ASC 606 Variable Consideration

description: Percentage discount applied to the invoice amount if payment is received within discount_days_1. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0200 = 2%). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD1P field. Null if no early payment discount applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI ZBD1P, GAAP ASC 606 Variable Consideration, IFRS 15

description: Percentage discount applied to the invoice amount if payment is received within discount_days_2 but after discount_days_1. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA ZBD2P field. Null if no second discount tier applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI ZBD2P, GAAP ASC 606 Variable Consideration, IFRS 15

description: SAP S/4HANA dunning procedure code assigned to this payment term, controlling the escalation schedule for overdue AR notices. Seasonal agricultural terms may use specialized dunning procedures that account for harvest timing before escalating collection actions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Dunning (F150), GAAP ASC 310

description: Date from which this payment term configuration becomes valid and may be applied to new invoices, sales orders, or purchase orders. Supports seasonal term activation aligned with crop or livestock cycles.
reference: GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Native payment term key as stored in the source ERP system (SAP S/4HANA ZTERM field, Oracle NetSuite term ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI ZTERM, Oracle NetSuite Payment Terms

description: Date after which this payment term configuration is no longer valid for assignment to new transactions. Null indicates an open-ended term with no planned expiration. Supports seasonal program sunset dates.
reference: GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA FI

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether this payment term configuration has been reviewed and confirmed compliant with FSMA supplier payment provisions, particularly for produce safety rule supplier agreements and preventive controls.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA), 21 CFR Part 112

description: Total number of installment payments required under this term when is_installment is true. For example, 3 for a three-payment seasonal schedule. Null when is_installment is false.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Installment Payment Terms, GAAP ASC 606

description: Defines how installment amounts are distributed across the payment schedule. 'harvest_triggered' supports agricultural deferred programs where installment timing is tied to crop harvest completion events. Null when is_installment is false.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Installment Terms, GAAP ASC 606, Granular Farm Management

description: Indicates whether this payment term requires payment in multiple installments rather than a single lump sum. When true, installment_count and installment_schedule_type define the installment structure. Common for large agricultural equipment purchases and seasonal input financing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Installment Payment Terms, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Indicates whether this payment term includes a seasonal deferral provision, allowing payment to be deferred to a post-harvest or post-season window. Characteristic of agricultural deferred grain payment programs and spring input financing.
reference: USDA Farm Service Agency Payment Programs, GAAP ASC 606, Granular Farm Management

description: Number of calendar days beyond the due date before late payment penalties begin to accrue. Provides a grace window before penalty charges are triggered. Null if no grace period applies.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Dunning, GAAP ASC 310

description: Annual percentage rate applied to overdue invoice balances as a late payment penalty or interest charge. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0150 = 1.5% per annum). Null if no late payment penalty applies under this term.
reference: GAAP ASC 310, IFRS 9, SAP S/4HANA FI Dunning Configuration

description: Number of calendar days from the invoice date (or baseline date) by which full payment is due. For example, 30 for Net 30 terms. Core field in SAP S/4HANA payment term configuration (ZBD3T). Drives AR due date calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI ZBD3T, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Payment term identifier as defined in Oracle NetSuite GL/AR module. Populated when the term originates from or is synchronized with NetSuite. Supports dual-ERP environments where SAP S/4HANA and Oracle NetSuite coexist.
reference: Oracle NetSuite AR Payment Terms Configuration

description: Restricts the allowable payment methods under this term. For example, large grain settlement programs may require ACH or wire transfer only. 'none' indicates no restriction. Maps to SAP S/4HANA payment method configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Methods, NACHA ACH Rules

description: Indicates whether the commodity price must be fixed (priced) before the payment due date clock starts. Relevant for deferred pricing grain contracts (basis contracts, hedge-to-arrive) where the final invoice amount is not determined at delivery.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service, CFTC Commodity Exchange Act, GAAP ASC 606

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the payment term configuration, as defined in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD module (e.g., NET30, DEFGRAIN90, LSTK45). Used on invoices, sales orders, and purchase orders for term lookup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Payment Terms Configuration, GAAP ASC 606

description: Detailed narrative description of the payment term, including applicable conditions, seasonal rules, commodity restrictions, and any special settlement provisions relevant to agricultural billing scenarios.
reference: GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15, GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Human-readable name of the payment term configuration (e.g., 'Net 30 Days', 'Deferred Grain Payment 90 Days Post-Harvest', 'Livestock Settlement 14 Days'). Displayed on customer-facing documents and AR aging reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Payment Terms, GAAP ASC 606

description: Current lifecycle status of the payment term configuration. Inactive or deprecated terms are retained for historical invoice reference but cannot be assigned to new transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Configuration Lifecycle, GAAP ASC 606

description: Classification of the payment term by its structural type. Supports agricultural-specific types including deferred seasonal programs (post-harvest grain payment windows), commodity-price-based settlement, and livestock consignment settlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_net|early_payment_discount|deferred_seasonal|installment|consignment_settlement|commodity_price_based|prepayment — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this payment term configuration record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404, GAAP ASC 606, ISO 22000

description: Indicates whether payment under this term requires reconciliation against a weight ticket (scale ticket) before the invoice due date is triggered. Critical for grain elevator and commodity settlement billing scenarios where final payment amount depends on certified weight.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection Packers and Stockyards Administration (GIPSA), GAAP ASC 606

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each price adjustment record in the invoice domain. Serves as the primary key for the price_adjustment data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Module

description: Identifier of the customer (grain elevator, food manufacturer, commodity buyer) to whom the price adjustment applies. Used for AR aging, customer account reconciliation, and credit management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; Salesforce CRM

description: Foreign key linking to livestock.animal_transaction. Business justification: Livestock price adjustments (grade discounts, weight shrink corrections, quality premiums) are issued against specific sale transactions. Linking price_adjustment to animal_transaction enables traceab
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Price adjustment authorization is a named audit/regulatory process in agriculture grain operations. The approving employee (grain merchandiser, quality manager) must be traceable for dockage disputes
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the originating accounts receivable invoice against which this price adjustment is applied. Links the adjustment to the provisional invoice issued pending final commodity pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Foreign key linking to invoice.billing_account. Business justification: Price adjustments affect AR balances for agricultural customers and must be linked to the billing_account AR master for account-level adjustment tracking and AR balance reconciliation. price_adjustmen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Price adjustments in commodity grain trading are directly driven by COA test results (moisture, protein, dockage, mycotoxin). A FK from price_adjustment to coa_id enables direct quality-test-to-price-
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Price adjustments for moisture, dockage, and grade are commodity-specific operations in ag settlement. A commodity_id FK normalizes the denormalized commodity_code and commodity_description, supportin
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.commodity_lot. Business justification: Quality-based price adjustment traceability: grain price adjustments for moisture, dockage, grade, and MRL factors are tied to specific commodity lots. Direct FK enables audit trail from financial adj
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to invoice.price_basis. Business justification: price_adjustment.pricing_basis is a denormalized string reference to the price_basis reference table. Price adjustments in commodity billing are driven by specific pricing bases (futures + basis). Nor
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the underlying commodity purchase or sales contract that governs the pricing terms being adjusted. Supports basis adjustment, futures price settlement, and contract price true-up scenarios.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GLOBALG.A.P.; GAAP ASC 606

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price adjustments post to specific GL accounts (price variance, quality allowance, moisture deduction). price_adjustment.gl_account_code is a denormalized plain attribute; proper FK to finance.gl_acco
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Gl Posting Journal Entry Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Price adjustments for basis changes, moisture dockage, and grade adjustments in agriculture generate GL postings. Linking price_adjustment to its journal entry supports audit trail for commodity price
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Price adjustments in ag are frequently triggered by grade reclassification — linking to grading_standard_id provides a formal audit trail for grade-based price corrections, supports dispute resolution
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to crop.harvest_event. Business justification: Grain price adjustments (moisture discounts, test weight premiums, dockage deductions) are calculated directly from harvest event quality measurements. Linking price_adjustment to harvest_event enable
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_finding. Business justification: Inspection findings (GAP audit, field sanitation, MRL exceedance) directly trigger price adjustments in agriculture commodity contracts. price_adjustment links to nonconformance and test_result but no
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_receipt. Business justification: Quality determination at receipt triggers price adjustment: moisture, test weight, and grade are determined at goods receipt and drive price adjustments. Direct FK enables traceability from financial
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to invoice.invoice_line. Business justification: Price adjustments in agricultural billing are applied at the commodity line level (e.g., moisture adjustment, grade reclassification). price_adjustment already has ar_invoice_id -> invoice (header lev
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.lot_trace. Business justification: Ag price adjustments for moisture, dockage, grade, and quality factors are applied at the lot level. Linking price_adjustment to lot_trace enables lot-level quality deduction tracking, supports FSMA-c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.master. Business justification: Price adjustments for branded/packaged agricultural products (seeds, agrochemicals) require master product reference for product-level adjustment reporting, P&L attribution, and audit trails. Distinct
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: MRL exceedance-triggered price adjustments (dockage, rejection, price reduction) require FK to the specific MRL threshold breached for EPA/USDA regulatory audit trails and export compliance documentat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: Price adjustments triggered by nonconformances (MRL exceedance, pesticide residue, grade failure) are a standard agriculture billing process. A FK from price_adjustment to nonconformance_id enables qu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price adjustments (quality deductions, moisture adjustments, basis changes) must be attributed to profit centers for segment-level margin analysis. price_adjustment.profit_center_code is a denormalize
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.quarantine_hold. Business justification: MRL exceedances or quality holds trigger mandatory price adjustments (discounts, rejections) on commodity invoices. Linking price_adjustment to quarantine_hold enables automated price deduction workfl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Seed quality price adjustments (germination failure, purity deficiency below label claim) require variety reference for PVP compliance, breeder royalty recalculation, and seed certification audit trai
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Price adjustments in agriculture (moisture deductions, dockage, quality discounts) are applied at the shipment level. Direct link supports shipment-level price adjustment tracking, carrier claim deduc
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to quality.test_result. Business justification: Individual test results (moisture %, protein %, mycotoxin ppb) directly calculate price adjustments in commodity grain settlement. price_adjustment.quality_factor_code is a denormalized text reference
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the weight ticket record used for final weight reconciliation adjustments. Applicable when the adjustment is triggered by a moisture dockage correction or net weight discrepancy between provisional and final scale readings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; USDA Grain Inspection Standards

description: Foreign key linking to crop.yield_record. Business justification: Quality-based price adjustment traceability: when grain is re-tested post-delivery and moisture, dockage, or grade differs from invoice, a price adjustment is issued. This FK links the adjustment to t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The final settled unit price per unit of measure after applying the price adjustment. Reflects final commodity pricing, quality re-grading outcome, or contract true-up result.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; USDA WASDE

description: Net monetary value of the price adjustment (positive for increases, negative for decreases). Calculated as (adjusted_unit_price - original_unit_price) × quantity_affected. Drives AR debit or credit memo issuance and revenue recognition true-up.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the adjustment increases or decreases the original invoice amount. Drives debit memo vs. credit memo issuance and AR balance impact.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS 15

description: Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the price adjustment document. Used in customer communications, remittance advice, and audit trails. Format: PA-YYYY-NNNNNN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Module; GAAP; IFRS 15

description: Current lifecycle state of the price adjustment record. Controls workflow progression from initial creation through approval, GL posting, and potential reversal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|posted|reversed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; SOX Section 404

description: Classification of the business reason driving the price adjustment. Determines the accounting treatment and GL account mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: final_price_true_up|quality_premium|quality_discount|moisture_dockage|futures_settlement|basis_adjustment|freight_differential — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; USDA WASDE; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: Date on which the price adjustment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail, SLA monitoring, and period-end close management.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Current state of the price adjustment in the internal approval workflow. Price adjustments above a materiality threshold require multi-level authorization before GL posting per SOX internal controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP

description: The basis differential (in cents per bushel or dollars per metric ton) representing the difference between the local cash price and the relevant futures contract price. Used in basis adjustment and futures settlement price adjustment types.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; CBOT/CME Group; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: System timestamp recording when the price adjustment record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and period-end close monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Supports multi-currency operations for international commodity trading.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS 21

description: Percentage deduction applied to gross weight for foreign material, weed seeds, or other non-grain matter. Used in moisture dockage correction adjustments to reconcile provisional vs. final net weight and settlement amount.
reference: USDA GIPSA Official Grain Standards; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: The business date on which the price adjustment becomes effective for pricing and settlement purposes. For futures price settlements, this aligns with the contract delivery period or pricing date specified in the commodity contract.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; USDA WASDE

description: Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the adjustment amount from the transaction currency to the functional (reporting) currency at the time of posting. Relevant for cross-border commodity sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Adjustment amount expressed in the entity's functional (reporting) currency after applying the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SOX

description: The CBOT/CME futures contract delivery month code used as the pricing reference for basis or futures-plus-basis adjustments (e.g., Z24 for December 2024 corn). Applicable when pricing_basis is 'futures_plus_basis' or 'basis'.
reference: CBOT/CME Group commodity futures specifications; USDA WASDE; SAP S/4HANA SD Module

description: The accounting date on which the price adjustment is posted to the General Ledger. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition true-up and AR balance adjustment. May differ from effective_date for period-end accruals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SOX Section 404

description: Final measured moisture content (%) of the commodity used to calculate moisture dockage corrections. Deviations from standard moisture (e.g., 15.5% for corn) trigger dockage adjustments that reduce the net settlement weight and price.
reference: USDA GIPSA Grain Inspection Standards; FGIS Official Grain Standards; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Free-text field for additional context, business justification, or operational notes related to the price adjustment. May include market conditions, counterparty communications, or inspector remarks supporting the adjustment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD/FI Module

description: The provisional unit price per unit of measure (e.g., per bushel, per metric ton, per hundredweight) stated on the originating invoice before any adjustment. Basis for calculating the adjustment delta.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; USDA WASDE

description: Unit of measure against which the original and adjusted unit prices are expressed. Common agricultural units include BU (Bushel), CWT (Hundredweight), MT (Metric Ton), LB (Pound), KG (Kilogram), TON (Short Ton).
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ISO 80000-1

description: Customer or counterparty purchase order number associated with the original commodity transaction. Included on adjustment documents for customer reconciliation and three-way matching in accounts payable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Oracle NetSuite Procurement

description: Specific quality attribute code (e.g., test weight, heat damage, total damage, broken kernels, foreign material) that triggered a quality premium or discount adjustment. Aligns with USDA GIPSA grading factor codes.
reference: USDA GIPSA Official Grain Standards; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: The quantity of commodity (expressed in price_unit_of_measure) subject to the price adjustment. Used to calculate the total adjustment amount as (adjusted_unit_price - original_unit_price) × quantity_affected.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; USDA Grain Inspection Standards

description: Adjustment number of the reversing price adjustment document if this record has been reversed. Populated when adjustment_status is 'reversed'. Supports audit trail and double-entry accounting integrity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; SOX Section 404

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this price adjustment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability across integrated systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; Oracle NetSuite; Granular Farm Management; Climate FieldView

description: System timestamp recording the most recent modification to the price adjustment record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Primary key for price_basis

Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Price basis records (futures exchange, basis amount, delivery specs) are inherently commodity-specific in ag trading. Linking to commodity_id enables basis-by-commodity reporting, forward contract pri
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Price basis in ag commodity trading is grade-specific — No. 2 Yellow Corn commands a different basis than No. 3. A grading_standard_id FK normalizes the denormalized grade_standard field and enables g
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to sales.market_price. Business justification: A price basis record in grain trading references a specific market price source (exchange settlement, WASDE reference). Linking price_basis to market_price enables automated basis calculation, price f
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Commodity pricing configuration: price basis records define futures-based pricing for specific commodities; material_master is the authoritative commodity specification record (grade standards, moistu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price basis records in grain trading are managed by profit center (trading desk, merchandising division). Linking price_basis to profit_center enables basis trading P&L reporting by division and suppo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Commodity price basis (futures differential) is location-specific in grain trading — each elevator/storage location has its own basis. Linking price_basis to storage_location replaces denormalized del
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Comma-separated list of contract types for which this price basis is valid (e.g., 'basis_contract,hedge_to_arrive,forward_contract,spot_purchase'). Restricts the use of this basis record to appropriate contract contexts in invoice generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: basis_contract|hedge_to_arrive|forward_contract|spot_purchase|deferred_payment|minimum_price — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA AMS Grain Contract Types; SAP S/4HANA SD Contract Categories

description: Name or employee identifier of the authorized person who approved this price basis record for use in invoice calculations. Required for SOX internal controls over financial reporting and GAAP revenue recognition governance.
reference: SOX Section 302/404 Internal Controls; GAAP Revenue Recognition ASC 606

description: The date on which this price basis record was formally approved for use. Required for SOX audit trail and financial reporting controls. Null if the record is in pending or draft status.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA Approval Workflow

description: The premium or discount applied over or under the futures contract price to arrive at the cash price, expressed in the currency unit per unit of measure (e.g., -0.15 USD/bushel represents a 15-cent under basis for corn). Negative values indicate a discount below futures; positive values indicate a premium above futures.
reference: USDA AMS Grain Basis Reporting; CME Group Basis Conventions

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this price basis configuration (e.g., CORN-CBOT-DEC, SOY-CME-MAR). Used in contract references, invoice line items, and grain elevator systems to reference the applicable pricing basis.
reference: CBOT/CME Exchange Conventions; SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the basis amount is denominated (e.g., USD for US domestic grain markets, CAD for Canadian operations). Ensures correct currency handling in multi-currency agricultural operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the price basis (e.g., 'December Corn CBOT Basis - Central Illinois'). Used in reports, invoices, and contract documents for clear identification of the pricing basis applied.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) Grain Reporting

description: Current lifecycle status of the price basis record. Active records are eligible for invoice pricing; inactive/expired records are retained for historical reference and audit; superseded records have been replaced by a newer basis version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Condition Record Status; GAAP ASC 606

description: The date and time when this price basis record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record creation per SOX and GAAP financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 606; ISO 8601

description: The calendar date from which this price basis record becomes active and eligible for use in invoice calculations. Basis records are date-sensitive as commodity basis levels change with market conditions and seasonal patterns.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Pricing Condition Validity

description: The calendar date after which this price basis record is no longer valid for new invoice calculations. Null indicates an open-ended basis with no defined expiration. Aligns with futures contract expiration cycles and seasonal marketing periods.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; CME Group Contract Expiration Calendar

description: For formula-based pricing methods, the mathematical expression or rule used to calculate the final price (e.g., 'CBOT_CLOSE + BASIS + FREIGHT_ALLOWANCE - MOISTURE_DISCOUNT'). Null for non-formula pricing methods. Stored as a string expression interpreted by the pricing engine.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Procedure; GAAP Revenue Recognition ASC 606

description: The freight cost component embedded in or deducted from the basis amount, expressed per unit of measure. Represents the transportation cost from the delivery location to the terminal market or export elevator. Used in FOB vs. CIF pricing reconciliation.
reference: USDA AMS Grain Transportation Reports; Incoterms FOB/CIF

description: The specific futures contract delivery month and year used as the pricing reference (e.g., DEC-2025, MAR-2026). Critical for grain and livestock invoice pricing to identify which futures contract price is applied in basis calculations.
reference: CBOT/CME Futures Contract Month Codes; USDA WASDE

description: The futures exchange whose contract price is used as the reference price for this basis (e.g., CBOT for corn/soybeans/wheat, CME for cattle/hogs, ICE for cotton). Null for spot_market or flat_price methods.
reference: CME Group Exchange Designations; CBOT Contract Specifications

description: Indicates whether this price basis applies to GMO commodity (True) or non-GMO/identity-preserved commodity (False). Non-GMO commodities typically command a premium basis over standard GMO commodity prices, requiring separate basis records.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P. GMO Traceability; FDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure

description: The per-unit elevator or handling fee included in or deducted from the basis calculation (e.g., 0.02 USD/BU for grain elevator handling). Represents the cost of receiving, storing, and loading grain at the delivery location.
reference: USDA AMS Grain Storage and Handling Rates; SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Type

description: The maximum commodity quantity (in the specified unit of measure) for which this basis applies. Basis records may be quantity-banded for volume pricing tiers. Null indicates no maximum quantity restriction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Scale Conditions; USDA AMS Contract Terms

description: The minimum commodity quantity (in the specified unit of measure) required for this basis to apply. Basis levels may differ for small lots vs. full truckload or barge quantities. Null indicates no minimum quantity restriction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Scale Conditions; USDA AMS Contract Minimums

description: The per-unit price adjustment applied per percentage point of moisture above or below the standard moisture level (e.g., -0.0035 USD/BU per 1% moisture above 15.5% for corn). Used in weight-ticket reconciliation and invoice adjustments for grain quality discounts.
reference: USDA AMS Grain Inspection Standards; FGIS (Federal Grain Inspection Service) Moisture Tables

description: Free-text field for additional context, special conditions, or exceptions associated with this price basis record (e.g., 'Applies only to identity-preserved white corn', 'Subject to CBOT limit move provisions'). Supports operational transparency and audit documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Condition Record Text; GAAP Documentation Requirements

description: Indicates whether this price basis applies to USDA-certified organic commodity (True) or conventional commodity (False). Organic commodities command significant basis premiums and require separate pricing basis records for accurate invoice generation.

description: The maximum cash price per unit of measure above which this basis will not result in an invoice price, regardless of futures movement. Null if no ceiling applies. Used in capped-price contract billing scenarios common in livestock and specialty crop markets.

description: The minimum cash price per unit of measure below which this basis will not result in an invoice price, regardless of futures movement (e.g., minimum price guarantee in minimum-price contracts). Null if no floor applies. Used in minimum-price contract billing scenarios.

description: The method used to determine the commodity price for invoice calculations. Options include: futures_basis (price = futures + basis), spot_market (current cash price), formula (custom formula-driven), flat_price (fixed agreed price), basis_contract (basis locked, futures open), hedge_to_arrive (futures locked, basis open).
reference: USDA AMS Grain Pricing Guidelines; CME Group Contract Specifications

description: Indicates whether this price basis is seasonal in nature (True) or applies year-round (False). Seasonal bases are common in grain marketing where harvest-time basis levels differ significantly from pre-harvest or post-harvest periods.

description: The originating operational system from which this price basis record was sourced (e.g., SAP_SD for SAP S/4HANA Sales & Distribution pricing conditions, GRANULAR for Granular Farm Management, DTN for DTN market data feed). reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Granular Farm Management; DTN Market Data

description: The reference moisture percentage at which no moisture adjustment is applied (e.g., 15.5% for corn, 13.0% for soybeans). Moisture readings from weight tickets above this level trigger discounts; below may trigger premiums per the moisture_adjustment_rate.

description: The reference test weight in pounds per bushel at which no test weight adjustment is applied (e.g., 56 lbs/BU for corn, 60 lbs/BU for soybeans, 60 lbs/BU for wheat). Deviations from this standard trigger price adjustments per test_weight_adjustment_rate.

description: The per-unit price adjustment applied per pound below the standard test weight (e.g., -0.01 USD/BU per pound below 56 lbs/BU for corn). Test weight is a key quality factor in grain pricing that affects invoice amounts.

description: The unit of measure against which the basis amount is applied (e.g., BU for bushel in grain, CWT for hundredweight in livestock, MT for metric ton in export contracts).
