business_glossary_term: Broken Kernels and Foreign Material Percentage (BCFM)
description: Percentage of broken corn kernels and foreign material (BCFM) in a corn sample, as defined by USDA FGIS grading standards. A key grade factor for corn that drives price discounts and grade assignment.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810

description: Broad classification of the commodity category. Drives applicable grading standards, inspection protocols, and settlement calculation rules (e.g., grain uses bushel-based pricing; livestock uses CWT-based pricing).
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service

description: Timestamp when the weight ticket record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA MM or Granular Farm Management). Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Controls; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the settlement amount (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Required for multi-currency operations and IFRS financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Percentage of grain kernels that are heat-damaged, mold-damaged, sprouted, or otherwise damaged as determined by USDA FGIS inspection. A primary grade factor affecting commodity grade assignment and price discounts.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810

description: Percentage of the gross sample weight consisting of material other than the grain (weed seeds, chaff, straw, sand) that passes through a prescribed sieve. Deducted from net weight before pricing per USDA FGIS grading standards.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810

description: Full name of the truck driver delivering the commodity. Required for chain-of-custody documentation, FSMA traceability, and facility access records.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule; GDPR Article 4 (international operations); DOT Regulations

description: Percentage of the grain sample consisting of foreign material (non-grain matter that does not pass through the dockage sieve). A grade-determining factor per USDA FGIS standards that affects commodity grade and price discounts.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810

description: Total certified scale weight of the loaded vehicle (truck/trailer plus commodity) in kilograms as recorded on the certified scale. Foundational quantity for net weight calculation per state weights-and-measures law.
reference: State Weights-and-Measures Regulations; USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; NIST Handbook 44

description: Indicates whether the commodity delivered on this weight ticket is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO). Required for identity preservation programs, non-GMO certification, and international trade compliance (EU GMO labeling regulations).
reference: USDA APHIS Biotechnology Regulations; EU GMO Labeling Regulation 1829/2003; GLOBALG.A.P. Standard

description: Indicates whether the commodity is certified organic under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). Drives premium pricing, segregated storage requirements, and organic certificate documentation requirements.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); 7 CFR Part 205; GLOBALG.A.P. Standard

description: Indicates whether this weight ticket represents a split-load delivery where a single truck carries commodity from multiple fields, farms, or ownership lots. When True, multiple weight tickets may share the same truck/trailer ID and require proportional allocation.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Moisture content of the grain or oilseed sample expressed as a percentage of total weight, measured at time of delivery. Used to calculate moisture dockage/shrink adjustments to net weight and price per USDA FGIS grading standards. Null for livestock.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810; AACC International Method 44-15A

description: Net commodity weight converted to bushels (BU) using USDA standard test weight per bushel for the specific commodity (e.g., 56 lbs/bu for corn, 60 lbs/bu for wheat). Used for grain pricing, settlement, and WASDE reporting. Null for livestock and cotton.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; USDA WASDE; 7 CFR Part 800

description: Certified net commodity weight in kilograms, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight. This is the authoritative quantity used for invoice line reconciliation, settlement calculations, and USDA FGIS reporting. Stored as a certified value from the scale system.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; State Weights-and-Measures Regulations; NIST Handbook 44

description: Free-text field for scale operator or receiving clerk notes regarding special conditions, quality observations, load discrepancies, or dispute details associated with this weight ticket. Supports exception handling and dispute resolution.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Contracted or market price per unit of measure (per bushel for grain, per CWT for livestock, per pound for cotton) applied to this weight ticket for settlement calculation. Sourced from the applicable sales order or commodity contract.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing; DTN Market Data

description: Unit of measure to which the price per unit applies. BU = Bushel (grain); CWT = Hundredweight (livestock); MT = Metric Ton; LB = Pound (cotton); KG = Kilogram. Drives settlement amount calculation.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards

description: The certified date and time the commodity was weighed on the certified scale. This is the principal business event timestamp and the authoritative quantity determination moment per state weights-and-measures law. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: State Weights-and-Measures Regulations; USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards

description: Gross settlement amount payable to the vendor/grower for this weight ticket, calculated as adjusted net weight multiplied by price per unit before deductions. Represents the principal monetary value of this transaction for AR/AP reconciliation.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Certified scale weight of the empty vehicle (truck/trailer without commodity) in kilograms. Subtracted from gross weight to determine net commodity weight. May be captured on inbound or outbound weigh.
reference: State Weights-and-Measures Regulations; USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; NIST Handbook 44

description: Measured weight per bushel of the grain sample in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu), indicating grain density and quality. Used as a grade factor per USDA FGIS standards (e.g., minimum 56 lbs/bu for No. 2 Yellow Corn). Null for non-grain commodities.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; 7 CFR Part 810; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service

description: Externally-known, human-readable certified scale ticket number assigned by the elevator or receiving facility. Used for reconciliation against invoice lines, grain settlement documents, and USDA FGIS inspection records. Unique within a facility.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; State Weights-and-Measures Regulations; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Current lifecycle state of the weight ticket. 'draft' = captured but not yet certified; 'open' = certified and awaiting settlement; 'settled' = reconciled to an invoice line and payment; 'voided' = cancelled/corrected; 'disputed' = under quality or quantity dispute.
reference: USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the weight ticket record. Tracks corrections, grade adjustments, or settlement status changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Financial Reporting Controls; USDA FGIS Grain Inspection Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the dispute. 'open' = newly filed; 'under_review' = assigned and being investigated; 'resolved' = final determination reached; 'escalated' = elevated to senior arbitration or regulatory body; 'withdrawn' = customer retracted the claim.

description: Classification of the reason for the dispute. Common agricultural billing dispute types include: price_discrepancy, weight_variance, quality_rejection, grade_dispute, mrl_exceedance_deduction, duplicate_billing, contract_terms_disagreement, short_shipment, packaging_defect. description: The portion of the invoice amount that the customer is formally contesting. May equal the full invoice amount (full dispute) or a subset (partial dispute) such as a weight variance deduction or MRL exceedance penalty.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: The date on which the most recent escalation action was taken. Null if the dispute has never been escalated. Used for escalation aging and regulatory timeline compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, USDA GIPSA

description: Numeric indicator of how many times the dispute has been escalated to a higher authority. Level 0 = not escalated; Level 1 = escalated to senior AR manager; Level 2 = escalated to legal/compliance; Level 3 = escalated to external arbitration (e.g., USDA GIPSA commodity arbitration).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, USDA GIPSA

description: Free-text description of the reason the dispute was escalated, such as failure to reach agreement within SLA, regulatory involvement required, or high-value commodity arbitration triggered.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, USDA GIPSA

description: The total billed amount on the original invoice as issued to the customer, captured at the time of dispute initiation for reference and variance calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Indicates whether the dispute covers only a portion of the invoice amount (True) or the full invoice amount (False). Drives AR aging treatment and partial payment clearing logic in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether the dispute involves a regulatory compliance dimension (e.g., FSMA traceability violation, EPA MRL exceedance, USDA grade certification challenge, COOL labeling dispute). Regulatory disputes trigger mandatory escalation and compliance reporting workflows.
reference: FDA FSMA, EPA, USDA GIPSA, COOL (Country of Origin Labeling), GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Business priority assigned to the dispute, influencing reviewer assignment and resolution SLA targets. 'critical' is typically reserved for disputes involving regulatory violations (e.g., MRL exceedance, FSMA non-compliance) or high-value commodity contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, FSMA Compliance Framework

description: The calendar date on which the dispute was formally closed and a resolution determination was recorded. Null if the dispute is still open or under review. Used for SLA compliance measurement and AR aging reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Free-text notes recorded by the assigned reviewer documenting the investigation findings, evidence reviewed (weight tickets, COA, grade certificates), and the rationale for the resolution determination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606

description: The method by which the dispute was resolved. 'credit_issued' = credit memo applied to AR; 'price_adjusted' = invoice repriced per commodity grade or weight; 'claim_denied' = dispute rejected after review; 'partial_settlement' = negotiated partial credit; 'replacement_shipment' = goods replaced; 'contract_amended' = contract terms updated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606, USDA GIPSA

description: The final agreed monetary settlement amount upon resolution of the dispute. Populated only when dispute_status = 'resolved'. May reflect a full credit, partial credit, price adjustment, or zero (claim denied).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GAAP ASC 606, IFRS 15

business_glossary_term: Dispute Resolution SLA (Service Level Agreement) Due Date
description: The target date by which the dispute must be resolved per internal Service Level Agreement (SLA) or contractual dispute resolution terms. Calculated from dispute_date plus the applicable SLA window based on dispute_type and priority_level.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, GLOBALG.A.P. Contract Terms

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this dispute record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management, Oracle NetSuite AR, Granular Farm Management

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404, GAAP, GDPR Article 5, SAP S/4HANA FI-AR

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Specific business purpose for advance or prepayment transactions. Applicable only when payment_type is 'advance' or 'deposit'. Supports agricultural-specific advance payment programs including crop pre-purchase financing, seed and input supply advances, and grain storage deposits.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; USDA Farm Service Agency; Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Gross amount of the payment as submitted by the payer in the transaction currency. Represents the total funds received before any deductions, discounts, or adjustments are applied.
reference: GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR

description: Portion of the payment amount that has been successfully applied against one or more AR invoices. The difference between payment_amount and applied_amount yields the unapplied balance.
reference: GAAP ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Unique reference number assigned by the bank for the payment transaction (e.g., ACH trace number, wire confirmation number, check clearing reference). Used for bank reconciliation and payment tracing.
reference: NACHA ACH Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI-TR; Oracle NetSuite Bank Reconciliation

description: Interface or channel through which the payment was submitted. 'portal' = customer self-service web portal; 'edi' = EDI 820 electronic remittance; 'manual' = manually entered by AR staff; 'bank_direct' = bank-initiated lockbox or direct debit; 'mobile' = mobile payment application.
reference: EDI X12 820; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite

description: Paper check number when payment_method is 'check'. Required for check reconciliation, stop-payment processing, and audit trail in agricultural cooperative and distributor payments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP

description: Date on which the payment was cleared against the AR invoice in the accounting system. Marks the completion of the cash application process and AR balance reduction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Lot number (LOT) or batch identifier of the agricultural commodity (grain, produce, livestock) associated with this payment. Enables traceability from payment back to specific commodity lot for FSMA compliance and quality reconciliation.
reference: FSMA; USDA; SAP S/4HANA MM; Granular Farm Management

description: Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle NetSuite

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Supports multi-currency agricultural commodity sales and international trade settlements.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS 21; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Early payment discount amount deducted by the payer per agreed payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Recorded separately for cash discount accounting and terms compliance tracking.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Date by which the payment was contractually due per the invoice payment terms. Used for early/late payment analysis, discount eligibility determination, and AR aging reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the company's functional currency (USD) at the time of payment. Required for multi-currency AR reconciliation and IFRS 21 translation.
reference: IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Payment amount converted to the company's functional currency (USD) using the exchange_rate at the time of payment. Used for consolidated financial reporting and GL posting.
reference: IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI-CO

description: General Ledger (GL) account code to which this payment is posted in the financial accounting system. Supports revenue recognition, AR clearing, and advance payment liability accounting per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; Oracle NetSuite GL

description: Indicates whether this payment is an intercompany transaction between related legal entities within the agricultural enterprise group. Required for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP ASC 810; IFRS 10; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether this payment covers only a portion of the total invoice amount. True when applied_amount is less than the full invoice balance. Supports installment and partial payment tracking for agricultural seasonal payment programs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; GAAP ASC 310

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by AR staff regarding the payment, such as dispute context, special handling instructions, customer communication details, or reconciliation remarks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Masked or tokenized bank account identifier of the payer used for ACH, EFT, or wire payments. Stored in masked format for PCI compliance. Used for payment routing validation and fraud detection.
reference: PCI DSS; NACHA ACH Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI-TR; GDPR Article 4

description: ABA routing transit number (9 digits) of the payer's bank for ACH and wire payment processing. Used for payment routing validation and bank reconciliation.
reference: NACHA ACH Rules; ABA Routing Number Standard; SAP S/4HANA FI-TR

description: The business event date on which the payment was received or initiated by the payer. Used for aging analysis, cash application, and revenue recognition timing per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Financial instrument or mechanism used to submit the payment. 'ACH' = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; 'wire' = bank wire transfer; 'check' = paper check; 'EFT' = Electronic Funds Transfer; 'commodity_offset' = payment settled via commodity credit or offset against grain/livestock delivery — common in agricultural cooperative and elevator transactions.
reference: NACHA ACH Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Externally-known business identifier for the payment transaction, typically matching the payment document number in SAP S/4HANA FI or Oracle NetSuite. Used for cross-system reconciliation and customer remittance matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction. 'pending' = received but not yet bank-confirmed; 'cleared' = bank-confirmed and applied to AR; 'returned' = rejected by bank (e.g., NSF check, ACH return); 'voided' = cancelled before clearing; 'on_hold' = suspended pending investigation or dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9

description: Classification of the payment by its business purpose and timing relative to the invoice. 'standard' = payment against an existing invoice; 'advance' = prepayment before invoice issuance (crop pre-purchase, seed advance, input supply advance); 'deposit' = security or storage deposit; 'installment' = partial payment under a deferred or seasonal payment plan.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Granular Farm Management

description: Reference number from the payer's remittance advice document accompanying the payment. Used to match payment to specific invoices and deductions in the cash application process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR; EDI 820 Payment Order/Remittance Advice

description: Standardized bank return reason code when payment_status is 'returned' (e.g., NACHA ACH return codes R01=Insufficient Funds, R02=Account Closed, R03=No Account). Required for returned payment processing and customer follow-up.
reference: NACHA ACH Return Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR

description: Remaining payment balance not yet applied to any invoice. Represents funds held in suspense or advance payment accounts pending invoice matching. Common in agricultural commodity sales with prepayment programs.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payment record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage for SOX compliance and financial controls.
reference: SOX; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI; Oracle NetSuite

description: The bank value date on which funds are credited to the company's account. May differ from payment_date for wire transfers and ACH batches. Critical for accurate cash position reporting and bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR; Oracle NetSuite Bank Reconciliation; IFRS 9

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each payment allocation record linking a payment to a specific invoice or invoice line. Serves as the primary key for this association entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module - Clearing Document

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the invoice header being settled by this allocation. Supports multi-invoice settlement scenarios common in commodity contract billing and cooperative patronage distribution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Reference to a specific invoice line item being settled. Enables partial payment tracking at the line level, critical for weight-ticket reconciliation and commodity-based billing where individual line items may be settled independently.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; Oracle NetSuite AR

description: Reference to the payment record being allocated. Links the allocation to the source payment transaction in the accounts receivable (AR) ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; Oracle NetSuite AR

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The gross monetary amount of the payment applied to this invoice or invoice line in the transaction currency. Core financial field for AR settlement, revenue recognition, and cash application reporting per GAAP/IFRS standards.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing Amount; Oracle NetSuite Payment Application Amount

description: The business date on which the payment was applied to the invoice. Used for aging analysis, cash application reporting, and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation. May differ from the payment receipt date in seasonal agricultural billing cycles.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date

description: Externally visible business identifier for this payment allocation record, used in AR clearing documents, remittance advice matching, and audit trails. Corresponds to the SAP FI clearing document number or Oracle NetSuite application number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing Document; Oracle NetSuite Payment Application

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment allocation: pending (awaiting posting), posted (applied to AR ledger), reversed (allocation reversed/cancelled), reconciled (matched and confirmed in bank reconciliation), disputed (under investigation for discrepancy). Drives AR aging and cash application workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Clearing Status; Oracle NetSuite AR Application Status; GAAP ASC 310

description: Categorizes the nature of the payment application: full (complete invoice settlement), partial (portion of invoice settled), advance (prepayment applied against future invoice), credit_offset (credit memo applied), netting (offsetting payable against receivable), write_off (uncollectable balance cleared). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: full|partial|advance|credit_offset|netting|write_off — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Clearing Types; GAAP ASC 310; IFRS 9

description: The user ID or username of the finance manager or controller who approved this payment allocation, particularly for write-offs, large variance clearances, or deduction approvals. Supports segregation of duties and SOX internal control requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302 and 404; SAP S/4HANA FI Workflow Approval; Oracle NetSuite Approval Workflow

description: Allocated amount converted to the company's functional (base) currency for general ledger (GL) posting and financial reporting. Required for multi-currency operations in international agricultural commodity trading per IFRS 21.
reference: IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI Local Currency Amount

description: Reference to the agricultural commodity purchase or sales contract against which this payment is being applied. Supports commodity contract payment matching for grain, livestock, and produce contracts. Links to forward contracts, basis contracts, and hedge-to-arrive (HTA) arrangements.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS); CFTC Commodity Exchange Act; SAP S/4HANA SD Contract

description: The user ID or username of the AR specialist or system process that created this payment allocation record. Required for SOX internal controls, audit trail, and segregation of duties compliance in financial operations.
reference: SOX Section 302 and 404; SAP S/4HANA FI User; Oracle NetSuite Created By

description: The date and time when this payment allocation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting per SOX financial controls.
reference: SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI Document Creation Timestamp

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the allocated amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency agricultural commodity trading and international supply chain billing.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Monetary value of deductions taken by the payer against the invoice, such as quality dockage, moisture penalties, freight charges, or promotional allowances. Distinct from discount_taken_amount as deductions are typically disputed or contractually defined adjustments in commodity billing.
reference: USDA GIPSA Grain Standards; SAP S/4HANA SD Deduction; Oracle NetSuite Deduction Amount

description: Code identifying the reason for any deduction taken by the payer against the invoice amount (e.g., quality deduction, moisture dockage, freight deduction, promotional allowance). Common in agricultural commodity billing where buyers deduct for grade, moisture, or foreign material.
reference: USDA GIPSA Grain Standards; SAP S/4HANA SD Deduction Reason; Oracle NetSuite Deduction Management

description: The last date by which payment must be received to qualify for the early payment discount. Used to validate whether the early_payment_discount_amount was legitimately earned. Derived from invoice date and payment terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; Oracle NetSuite Discount Date; GAAP ASC 606-10-32

description: Monetary value of the trade or cash discount applied at the time of payment allocation. Represents the discount actually utilized by the payer, which may differ from the discount offered. Used in AR reconciliation and discount expense reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606-10-32; SAP S/4HANA FI Cash Discount; Oracle NetSuite Discount Applied

description: Monetary value of the early payment discount earned by the payer for settling the invoice before the discount due date. Distinct from standard trade discounts; tied to payment terms such as 2/10 Net 30 common in agricultural supply chain billing.
reference: GAAP ASC 310-10; SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Discount; Oracle NetSuite Early Payment Discount

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the base/functional currency at the time of allocation. Sourced from the ERP system's daily exchange rate table. Required for IFRS 21 and GAAP ASC 830 compliance.
reference: IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate; Oracle NetSuite Currency Exchange Rate

description: The general ledger (GL) account code to which this payment allocation is posted for revenue recognition and AR clearing. Ensures proper accounting treatment per GAAP/IFRS chart of accounts. Sourced from SAP FI GL account determination or Oracle NetSuite account mapping.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI GL Account; Oracle NetSuite Chart of Accounts

description: Indicates whether this payment allocation is between entities within the same corporate group (intercompany transaction). Required for intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements per GAAP ASC 810 and IFRS 10.
reference: GAAP ASC 810 Consolidation; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany

description: The date and time when this payment allocation record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to allocation status, amounts, or reconciliation state. Required for data lineage and SOX audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA FI Change Document; Oracle NetSuite Last Modified Date

description: Reference identifier for the intercompany or counterparty netting arrangement when allocation_type is 'netting'. Used in agricultural supply chain scenarios where a company is both buyer and seller to the same counterparty (e.g., grain merchandiser netting payables against receivables).
reference: GAAP ASC 210-20 Offsetting; IFRS 32 Financial Instruments Presentation; SAP S/4HANA FI Netting

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by the AR specialist regarding this payment allocation. May include details about partial payment agreements, dispute resolutions, weight-ticket discrepancies, or special handling instructions for seasonal payment arrangements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Text; Oracle NetSuite Memo

description: The accounting period posting date for this allocation in the general ledger (GL). May differ from the allocation date when period-end adjustments are made. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and AR aging per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date; Oracle NetSuite Posting Period

description: Status of the bank reconciliation matching for this allocation: unreconciled (not yet matched to bank statement), matched (confirmed against bank statement), partially_matched (partial bank confirmation), exception (discrepancy requiring investigation), cleared (fully reconciled and closed). Essential for month-end close and cash management in agricultural operations.
reference: GAAP ASC 310; SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation; Oracle NetSuite Bank Reconciliation

description: The date on which this payment allocation was reversed. Populated only when allocation_status is 'reversed'. Used for AR aging recalculation and audit trail documentation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Date; Oracle NetSuite Void Date; GAAP ASC 310

description: Business reason for reversing this payment allocation (e.g., returned check, bank rejection, incorrect application, duplicate payment). Populated only when allocation_status is 'reversed'. Required for audit trail and AR dispute management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Reason; Oracle NetSuite Void Reason; GAAP ASC 310

description: The bank value date on which funds were credited or debited, used for cash flow reporting and bank reconciliation. May differ from the posting date in agricultural cooperative payment cycles and commodity settlement workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Value Date; Oracle NetSuite Bank Value Date; IFRS 9

description: Difference between the invoice amount and the payment allocated, representing unresolved discrepancies such as pricing disputes, weight-ticket measurement differences, or deduction claims. Positive value indicates underpayment; negative indicates overpayment. Critical for weight-ticket settlement reconciliation in grain and commodity billing.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; SAP S/4HANA FI Residual Item; Oracle NetSuite Payment Variance

description: Monetary amount of the invoice balance written off as uncollectable or as an approved variance adjustment. Used in bad debt management, small balance write-offs, and weight-ticket variance clearance in commodity billing workflows.
reference: GAAP ASC 310-10-35; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI Write-Off; Oracle NetSuite Bad Debt Write-Off

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the qualified vendor or supplier responsible for fulfilling this delivery schedule line. Supports vendor performance tracking and USDA/EPA-compliant supplier qualification records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Vendor Master; USDA Supplier Qualification

description: The actual calendar date on which the agricultural input was physically received at the delivery location. Populated upon Goods Receipt Note (GRN) processing. Used for on-time delivery KPI calculation and vendor performance scoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MIGO Goods Receipt; GRN Processing

description: Bill of Lading (BOL) number issued by the carrier as the legal contract of carriage and receipt of goods. Required for FSMA traceability, COOL compliance, and AP invoice matching. Critical for agricultural chemical and biological shipment documentation.
reference: FSMA Section 204 Traceability; COOL Regulations; UCC Article 7

description: Name of the transportation carrier or logistics provider responsible for delivering the agricultural input. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Forwarding Agent; TMS Carrier Master

description: Carrier-assigned reference or tracking number for the shipment. Enables real-time shipment tracking and exception management for in-transit deliveries.
reference: TMS Carrier Reference; SAP S/4HANA MM - Shipment Number

description: Certificate of Analysis (COA) document number provided by the vendor confirming the quality specifications of the delivered agricultural input (e.g., NPK analysis for fertilizers, AI concentration for crop protection chemicals, nutritional profile for feed ingredients).
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - Quality Certificate; GFSI COA Requirements; ISO 22000

description: Timestamp when this delivery schedule record was first created in the system, sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle NetSuite. Supports audit trail requirements under SOX financial reporting controls and FSMA traceability.
reference: SOX Section 404; FSMA Traceability; SAP S/4HANA Audit Log

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the unit price and transaction value (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Required for multi-currency procurement operations and IFRS/GAAP financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS; GAAP

description: Reason code or description explaining why a delivery schedule line has been delayed or cancelled. Supports supply chain risk analysis, vendor performance management, and contingency planning for critical agricultural inputs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Exception Management; GLOBALG.A.P. Supplier Performance

description: Classification of the delivery destination type. Determines receiving procedures, storage requirements, and compliance checks (e.g., feed mill requires FDA feed safety compliance; chemical warehouse requires SDS and EPA storage regulations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: farm_gate|grain_elevator|warehouse|feed_mill|fuel_tank|processing_plant — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Feed Safety Regulations; EPA Chemical Storage; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.119

description: End timestamp of the estimated arrival window at the delivery location. Together with estimated_arrival_start defines the delivery window for receiving operations and carrier coordination.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Inbound Delivery; TMS Delivery Window

description: Start timestamp of the estimated arrival window at the delivery location. Supports dock scheduling, receiving staff planning, and cold-chain management for perishable biologicals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Inbound Delivery; Manhattan Associates WMS Receiving

description: Goods Receipt Note (GRN) number generated upon physical receipt and inspection of the agricultural input at the delivery location. Triggers three-way match (PO-GRN-Invoice) in SAP S/4HANA MM and Oracle NetSuite AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MIGO GRN; Oracle NetSuite AP Three-Way Match; FSMA

description: International Commercial Terms (INCOTERMS) code defining the point of risk and cost transfer between vendor and buyer. Common agricultural trade terms include FOB (Free On Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight). Governs freight cost allocation and insurance responsibility.
reference: ICC INCOTERMS 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM - Incoterms

description: Classification of the agricultural input material being delivered. Drives compliance workflows: crop protection chemicals require SDS and EPA/APHIS documentation; fertilizers require NPK specification; seeds require GMO and phytosanitary certificates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|fuel|parts|supplies — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA FIFRA; APHIS Phytosanitary; USDA NOP; FDA FSMA

description: Indicates whether the agricultural input is a perishable biological product (e.g., live beneficial insects, biostimulants, inoculants, vaccines) requiring cold-chain management and expedited just-in-time delivery. Triggers special handling and temperature monitoring requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA Preventive Controls; APHIS Biological Import Regulations

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery schedule record. Tracks schedule revisions, status changes, and quantity adjustments. Required for data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer and SOX audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA Change Log; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: Batch or Lot Number (LOT) assigned to the agricultural input shipment by the manufacturer or vendor. Enables end-to-end traceability from supplier to field application, supporting FSMA Section 204 traceability, recall management, and Certificate of Analysis (COA) matching.
reference: FSMA Section 204 Traceability; SAP S/4HANA MM - Batch Management; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether the delivered crop protection chemical requires Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) compliance tracking. When True, the receiving system must enforce PHI restrictions to ensure MRL compliance at harvest. Applicable to pesticides and herbicides with EPA-registered PHI requirements.
reference: EPA FIFRA PHI Requirements; FDA MRL Compliance; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: The principal planned calendar date on which the agricultural input is scheduled to arrive at the delivery location. Aligned with seasonal crop planting calendars and livestock feeding program schedules to ensure timely availability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - ME38 Schedule Line Date; FMIS Crop Calendar

description: Category of dispute raised against the vendor invoice. Null if no dispute.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Procurement budgets in multi-entity agriculture operations are scoped to a legal entity for consolidated financial reporting, intercompany budget allocation, and USDA program payment tracking by entit
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which this procurement budget is allocated. Enables OPEX/CAPEX tracking and spend analytics by organizational unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Foreign key linking to finance.crop_enterprise_budget. Business justification: The procurement operational budget references the finance-side crop enterprise budget to ensure input procurement spending aligns with the crop-level financial plan. Supports integrated budget managem
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the farm enterprise or business unit to which this budget is assigned. Supports spend analytics segmented by farm operation within the agricultural enterprise.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center

description: Foreign key linking to land.field. Business justification: Field-level budget allocation is a standard precision agriculture financial management process — input cost budgets, yield revenue projections, and profitability analysis are tracked per field. Curren
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Procurement budgets for agricultural inputs map to GL accounts (seed expense, fertilizer expense, pesticide expense) for budget vs. actual variance reporting. The existing gl_account_code is a denorma
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.position. Business justification: Agricultural farm budgets are built at position level for headcount and labor cost planning (H2A positions, seasonal labor). Linking budget to position enables labor budget vs. actual payroll variance
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: FK to finance.profit_center

The total monetary value of procurement spend actually incurred to date against this budget, based on posted Goods Receipt Notes (GRNs) and approved vendor invoices processed through SAP MM/FI and Oracle NetSuite AP. Represents confirmed expenditure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI; Oracle NetSuite AP; GAAP; IFRS

description: The total monetary amount formally allocated and approved for procurement spend within this budget record for the defined period and input category. Represents the authorized spending ceiling before any commitments or actuals are recorded.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP; IFRS

description: The date on which this procurement budget was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from draft to approved status and establishes the authorization date for audit and SOX compliance purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; SOX; GAAP

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this procurement budget record within the ERP and farm management systems. Used for cross-system reconciliation between SAP CO and Granular Farm Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Oracle NetSuite; Granular Farm Management

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the procurement budget record (e.g., 'FY2025 Q2 Seed & Fertilizer - North Farm'). Used in reporting dashboards and budget owner communications.
reference: Granular Farm Management; SAP CO

description: Current lifecycle state of the procurement budget record. Draft budgets are under preparation; approved budgets are authorized for spend; active budgets have open commitments; frozen budgets are temporarily suspended; closed budgets have completed their period; cancelled budgets are voided.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP

business_glossary_term: Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) vs Operational Expenditure (OPEX) Classification
description: Indicates whether this procurement budget is classified as Capital Expenditure (CAPEX) for long-term asset acquisition (e.g., irrigation infrastructure, equipment) or Operational Expenditure (OPEX) for recurring inputs (e.g., seeds, fertilizers, agrochemicals, fuel). Critical for financial reporting under GAAP and IFRS.
reference: GAAP; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

description: The total monetary value of open and confirmed Purchase Orders (POs) that have been raised against this budget but not yet invoiced or goods-receipted. Derived from open PO commitments in SAP MM. Represents encumbered budget that is no longer freely available.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/CO; Oracle NetSuite; GAAP commitment accounting

description: The timestamp when this procurement budget record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation event required for SOX financial controls and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; SOX; GAAP; ISO 22000

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary amounts on this budget record are denominated (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency agricultural operations and IFRS reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The fiscal period granularity of this budget record — annual for full-year budgets or quarterly (Q1–Q4) for sub-annual allocations. Supports both annual OPEX planning and quarterly reforecast cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP; IFRS

description: The fiscal year (e.g., 2025) to which this procurement budget allocation applies. Aligns with the agricultural enterprise's financial reporting calendar in SAP FI/CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; GAAP; IFRS

description: Classification of the agricultural input type covered by this budget record. Drives spend analytics by input category and supports NPK fertilizer, AI (Active Ingredient) agrochemical, and feed ingredient cost tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|agrochemical|feed|fuel|parts|supplies|other — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA; EPA; APHIS; SAP S/4HANA MM material group; Granular Farm Management

description: Indicates whether this procurement budget record is locked against further modifications (True) or remains open for amendment (False). Budgets are typically locked at period close or upon reaching a frozen status to preserve financial integrity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; SOX; GAAP

description: The date on which this procurement budget record was most recently revised or amended. Tracks the most recent budget adjustment event, supporting audit trails and reforecast cycle management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP

description: Free-text field for budget owner or finance controller annotations, including justification for budget revisions, seasonal adjustments, or compliance notes related to EPA/APHIS regulated input categories such as agrochemicals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management

description: The initial budget amount as approved at the start of the budget period, before any revisions or amendments. Preserved for variance analysis comparing original plan versus revised plan versus actuals — a key OPEX control metric.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP; IFRS

description: The calendar date on which this budget period closes. Defines the boundary for spend commitment and actuals accumulation within this budget record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; GAAP; IFRS

description: The calendar date on which this budget period becomes effective. For annual budgets this is typically the first day of the fiscal year; for quarterly budgets it is the first day of the quarter.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; GAAP; IFRS

description: The unencumbered budget balance remaining for new procurement commitments, calculated as allocated_amount minus committed_amount minus actual_spend_amount. Provides real-time budget availability for procurement controllers and farm managers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP

description: Sequential integer counter tracking how many times this procurement budget has been revised since initial approval. Version 0 represents the original approved budget; each subsequent amendment increments this counter. Supports budget reforecast audit trails.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; SOX

description: The percentage of the allocated budget that has been consumed by actual spend to date (actual_spend_amount / allocated_amount × 100). A key KPI for procurement controllers to monitor budget utilization rate and flag potential overspend risk.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP

description: The total monetary amount transferred into this budget record from other budget lines or cost centers during the period. Supports budget reallocation tracking between input categories or farm enterprises.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; GAAP; IFRS

description: The total monetary amount transferred out of this budget record to other budget lines or cost centers during the period. Together with transfer_in_amount, enables full reconciliation of budget movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; GAAP; IFRS

description: The timestamp when this procurement budget record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and provides the audit trail update event for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; SOX; GAAP

description: The monetary difference between the allocated budget amount and the total of committed plus actual spend. A positive value indicates underspend; a negative value indicates overspend. Used for OPEX control reporting and KPI dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP; IFRS

description: The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the allocated budget amount (variance_amount / allocated_amount × 100). Enables proportional comparison of budget performance across cost centers, input categories, and farm enterprises of different sizes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Granular Farm Management; GAAP

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each chemical compliance record in the procurement domain. Primary key for the chemical_compliance data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a regulatory compliance record for a procured crop protection chemical or biological input.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; EPA FIFRA Registration

description: Reference to the agrochemical product master record in the product domain. Links this compliance record to the specific crop protection chemical or biological input product being regulated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; product.agrochemical_product

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FSMA Preventive Controls and EPA regulations require a named responsible person for each chemical compliance record. Ag compliance officers are accountable for SDS currency, MRL limits, and registrati
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to crop.crop. Business justification: Chemical compliance records specify which crop a pesticide is registered for and its MRL. Linking to crop enables EPA label compliance verification, MRL reporting by crop, and FSMA preventive control
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Chemical compliance records track MRL values (mrl_value, mrl_commodity, mrl_market) that are denormalized from the mrl_threshold product. Normalizing via FK supports FSMA preventive controls documenta
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Percentage concentration of the active ingredient (AI) in the formulated product, expressed as weight/weight (w/w) or weight/volume (w/v) percent. Used for dose calculation, MRL compliance, and SDS documentation.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; FAO/WHO Codex Alimentarius

description: Common chemical name of the primary active ingredient (AI) responsible for the pesticidal action of the product, as listed on the EPA-approved label. Critical for MRL compliance checks and PHI determination.
reference: EPA FIFRA; Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards; ISO 1750 Pesticide Nomenclature

business_glossary_term: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) Import Permit Number
description: APHIS-issued permit number authorizing the importation of regulated biological inputs, biopesticides, or plant protection products. Required for cross-border procurement of biological agents under APHIS jurisdiction.
reference: USDA APHIS 7 CFR Part 330; APHIS PPQ

description: Current lifecycle status of the chemical compliance record. 'active' indicates the product is currently registered and compliant for use; 'suspended' indicates temporary regulatory hold; 'cancelled' indicates EPA registration cancellation; 'pending_review' indicates under regulatory review; 'expired' indicates registration or SDS has lapsed.
reference: EPA FIFRA; SAP S/4HANA QM Status Management

description: Unit of measure for the active ingredient concentration value (e.g., % w/w, % w/v, g/L). Ensures correct interpretation of concentration for compliance calculations.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; ISO 1750

description: Timestamp when this chemical compliance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for OSHA HazCom, EPA regulatory reporting, and FSMA recordkeeping. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA QM or Oracle NetSuite document creation timestamp.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200; FSMA Recordkeeping Requirements; SOX

description: Disposal methods and instructions for the chemical product and its container as specified in SDS Section 13. Includes EPA waste classification, disposal facility requirements, and container rinsing procedures. Required for EPA RCRA compliance and environmental management.
reference: EPA RCRA 40 CFR Parts 260-270; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix D Section 13

description: 24-hour emergency telephone number for chemical emergency response, as listed on SDS Section 1 (e.g., CHEMTREC 1-800-424-9300). Required by OSHA HazCom and DOT hazardous materials regulations for emergency response coordination.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix D Section 1; DOT 49 CFR Part 172.604

description: EPA-issued registration number for the pesticide product under the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA). Required for legal sale and use in the United States. Format: registrant-product[-supplemental].
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3; 40 CFR Part 152

description: Summary of first aid instructions for exposure routes (inhalation, skin contact, eye contact, ingestion) as specified in SDS Section 4. Required for OSHA HazCom compliance and emergency response planning at field and warehouse locations.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix D Section 4; GHS Rev 9

description: Standardized formulation type code for the crop protection product (e.g., EC=Emulsifiable Concentrate, WP=Wettable Powder, SC=Suspension Concentrate, WG=Water Dispersible Granule, GR=Granule, SL=Soluble Liquid). Used for application equipment selection, mixing instructions, and storage classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EC|WP|SC|WG|GR|SL|EW|CS|DP|UL|SP|ZC — promote to reference product]
reference: FAO/WHO Pesticide Specifications; CropLife International Formulation Codes

business_glossary_term: Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) Certification Applicability Flag
description: Indicates whether this chemical compliance record is subject to GAP certification requirements, including GLOBALG.A.P. IFA or equivalent scheme. When true, the chemical's use must be documented in the farm's GAP compliance records and subject to third-party audit.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard v6; USDA GAP/GHP Audit Program

description: GHS hazard classification category for the chemical product, identifying the type and severity of physical, health, and environmental hazards (e.g., 'Acute Toxicity Category 3', 'Flammable Liquid Category 2'). Drives PPE requirements, storage segregation, and emergency response planning.
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Rev 9; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200; UN GHS Purple Book

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Point (HACCP) Critical Control Point Flag
description: Indicates whether this chemical input is identified as a critical control point (CCP) in the facility's HACCP plan. When true, mandatory monitoring, corrective action, and verification procedures apply per Codex Alimentarius HACCP principles and FSMA Preventive Controls.
reference: Codex Alimentarius HACCP Guidelines CAC/RCP 1-1969; FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; FDA

description: Standardized GHS H-code hazard statements describing the nature and degree of hazards posed by the chemical (e.g., 'H301: Toxic if swallowed; H400: Very toxic to aquatic life'). Stored as a semicolon-delimited list of H-codes and descriptions. Required on SDS Section 2.
reference: GHS Rev 9 Annex 3; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix C

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Preventive Controls Applicability Flag
description: Indicates whether this chemical input is subject to FSMA preventive controls requirements, triggering mandatory hazard analysis, preventive control measures, monitoring, corrective actions, and recordkeeping under the FSMA Preventive Controls for Human Food rule.
reference: FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; FDA Food Safety Modernization Act

description: Indicates whether the biological input contains or is derived from genetically modified organisms (GMO). Required for APHIS biotechnology regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and organic certification exclusion under USDA National Organic Program (NOP).
reference: USDA APHIS 7 CFR Part 340; USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; EU GMO Regulation 1829/2003

description: Indicates whether the product is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring purchase and use only by certified applicators or persons under their direct supervision. Critical for procurement authorization and applicator certification tracking.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3(d); 40 CFR Part 152 Subpart I

description: Indicates whether the product label has been reviewed and confirmed to comply with all applicable EPA label requirements, including use directions, safety precautions, PHI, REI, and first aid statements. Required for GAP certification and HACCP compliance documentation.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 2(p); GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; HACCP Codex Alimentarius

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this chemical compliance record. Tracks changes to SDS revisions, registration status updates, MRL changes, or label amendments. Required for regulatory audit trail and change management.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200(g)(5); FSMA; SOX

description: Pre-Harvest Interval (PHI) in days — the minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of the chemical and the harvest of the crop. Mandatory for GAP compliance and FSMA preventive controls to ensure MRL compliance at harvest.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA; FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112

description: Required personal protective equipment (PPE) for handling, mixing, loading, and applying the chemical product as specified on the EPA label and SDS Section 8. Includes gloves, respirator type, eye protection, and protective clothing specifications. Mandatory for EPA Worker Protection Standard compliance.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard 40 CFR Part 170; OSHA PPE Standard 29 CFR 1910.132; SDS Section 8

description: Standardized GHS P-code precautionary statements describing recommended measures to minimize or prevent adverse effects from exposure, storage, or disposal (e.g., 'P260: Do not breathe dust/fume; P501: Dispose of contents in accordance with local regulations'). Stored as semicolon-delimited P-codes.
reference: GHS Rev 9 Annex 4; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix C

description: Classification of the crop protection product by its primary mode of action or target pest category. Drives regulatory pathway, label requirements, and IPM program classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: herbicide|insecticide|fungicide|rodenticide|biopesticide|plant_growth_regulator|adjuvant|nematicide|bactericide — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA FIFRA; USDA NASS Pesticide Use Survey; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA

description: Date on which the EPA registration for this chemical product became effective. Marks the start of the legally authorized period for sale and use of the product in the United States under FIFRA.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3; 40 CFR Part 152

description: Date on which the EPA registration for this chemical product expires or is due for renewal. Null for registrations without a fixed expiry. Triggers procurement hold and re-registration workflow when approaching expiry.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3(g); 40 CFR Part 152

description: Restricted Entry Interval (REI) in hours — the minimum time that must pass after a pesticide application before workers may re-enter the treated area without personal protective equipment (PPE). Required for OSHA HazCom and EPA Worker Protection Standard compliance.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard 40 CFR Part 170; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Unique document control number assigned to the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for this chemical product. Used for document management, OSHA HazCom compliance, and audit trail. Corresponds to the SDS document identifier in the supplier's document management system.
reference: OSHA HazCom Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Rev 9

business_glossary_term: Safety Data Sheet (SDS) Expiry / Review Date
description: Date by which the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) must be reviewed and reissued or confirmed current. Typically set 3-5 years from the revision date per industry practice. Triggers SDS renewal workflow to maintain OSHA HazCom compliance.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200; ANSI Z400.1

description: Date of the most recent revision of the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) as issued by the chemical manufacturer or supplier. OSHA requires SDS to be updated within 3 months of new hazard information. Critical for ensuring workers have access to current hazard information.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200(g)(5); GHS Rev 9 Section 1

description: GHS/EPA signal word appearing on the product label and SDS indicating the relative level of hazard severity: DANGER (most severe), WARNING (moderate), CAUTION (least severe), or none for non-hazardous products. Required on SDS Section 2 and product label.
reference: GHS Rev 9 Section 1.4; EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Two-letter US state code (USPS abbreviation) for which the state_registration_status applies. Enables state-by-state compliance tracking for pesticide products that require individual state registrations under FIFRA Section 24(c).
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 24(c); USPS State Abbreviations

description: Registration status of the pesticide product in the specific state(s) where it will be used. States may impose additional registration requirements beyond federal EPA registration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: registered|not_registered|pending|cancelled|restricted — promote to reference product if multi-state tracking is required]
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 24(c); State Lead Agency Pesticide Regulations

description: Storage conditions and requirements for the chemical product as specified in SDS Section 7, including temperature range, humidity, segregation from incompatible materials, ventilation, and container requirements. Used for warehouse storage planning and EPA/OSHA compliance.
reference: OSHA HazCom 29 CFR 1910.1200 Appendix D Section 7; EPA FIFRA; NFPA 30

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each agricultural input record in the master catalog. Primary key for the input_catalog data product in the procurement domain.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Input catalog entries for commodity-type inputs reference the specific commodity for grade standards, MRL thresholds, and storage requirements. Commodity-level catalog management drives procurement sp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
