reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Input catalog items (seeds, chemicals, fertilizers) carry default GL accounts for automatic account determination during PO creation — standard SAP MM account determination pattern. Agriculture operat
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: Input catalog entries for commodity inputs must reference the applicable grading standard to define quality acceptance criteria for procurement. Goods receipt inspection and vendor scorecard quality m
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: Input catalog entries for agrochemicals carry a denormalized mrl_ppm value. Normalizing via FK to mrl_threshold supports regulatory compliance screening at catalog entry time and ensures procurement u
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.seed_variety. Business justification: Input catalog entries for seed products reference the specific seed variety for trait package, maturity rating, and regulatory attributes (APHIS petition, PVP number). Variety-level catalog management
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Primary active ingredient(s) in crop protection chemicals or veterinary products (e.g., 'glyphosate', 'chlorpyrifos', 'azoxystrobin'). Critical for MRL compliance, PHI determination, and HACCP hazard analysis. Null for non-chemical inputs.
reference: EPA FIFRA Active Ingredient Definition; Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards; FDA FSMA

description: Concentration of the active ingredient expressed as a percentage of the total formulation (e.g., 41.0 for 41% glyphosate). Used for application rate calculations, MRL compliance verification, and SDS documentation.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; Codex Alimentarius; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

business_glossary_term: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) Permit Category
description: USDA APHIS permit classification for biological inputs including biocontrol agents, plant pests, and veterinary biologicals. Required for import, interstate movement, and field release of regulated biological organisms. Null or 'not_applicable' for non-biological inputs.
reference: USDA APHIS 7 CFR Part 330; APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; USDA APHIS VS

description: Date on which this input was approved and added to the active procurement catalog following vendor qualification, regulatory compliance verification, and internal approval workflow. Supports audit trail for GLOBALG.A.P. and GFSI certification.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Input Approval Process; GFSI Benchmarked Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Release

description: Primary unit of measure for procurement and inventory transactions (e.g., KG, MT, L, GAL, BU, CWT, EA). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA MM base UOM and drives GRN quantity validation and stock position calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure (MARA-MEINS); ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units

description: Current lifecycle status of the input in the master catalog. Controls whether the input is available for purchase requisition and PO creation in SAP S/4HANA MM. 'restricted' indicates regulatory or internal procurement hold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status (MARA-MMSTA); GLOBALG.A.P. Approved Input List

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this input was manufactured or produced. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance, import documentation, and APHIS phytosanitary permit requirements.
reference: USDA COOL 7 CFR Part 60; APHIS Import Permit Requirements; CBP 19 CFR Part 102; ISO 3166-1

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost and pricing fields (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'BRL'). Supports multi-currency procurement operations and IFRS financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; GAAP

description: Pesticide or fertilizer formulation type code (e.g., EC=Emulsifiable Concentrate, WP=Wettable Powder, SC=Suspension Concentrate, WG=Water Dispersible Granule, GR=Granule, SL=Soluble Liquid). Affects application equipment compatibility and handling procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EC|WP|SC|WG|GR|SL|CS|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FAO/WHO Pesticide Specifications; EPA FIFRA Label; CropLife International Formulation Codes

description: Indicates whether the input (primarily seeds) is genetically modified (GMO), non-GMO, conventional (no GMO claim), or not applicable for non-seed inputs. Required for USDA APHIS biotech regulatory compliance, COOL labeling, and customer supply chain transparency.
reference: USDA APHIS 7 CFR Part 340; USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: DOT hazardous materials classification code for transport and storage compliance (e.g., '3' for flammable liquids/fuels, '6.1' for toxic substances, '8' for corrosives). Null for non-hazardous inputs. Required for BOL documentation and TMS routing.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Parts 171-180; EPA RCRA; OSHA HazCom; GHS Classification

description: High-level classification of the agricultural input type. Drives procurement workflows, regulatory compliance checks, and SDS documentation requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: seed|fertilizer|crop_protection|feed_ingredient|fuel|spare_part|operational_supply|biological — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Type; USDA APHIS Input Classification; EPA Pesticide Registration

description: Human-readable commercial or trade name of the agricultural input (e.g., 'Roundup PowerMAX', 'Pioneer P1197AM', 'Urea 46-0-0'). Used for display, reporting, and procurement communications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Description; GLOBALG.A.P. Input Register

description: Granular classification within the input category (e.g., for crop_protection: herbicide, insecticide, fungicide, nematicide; for fertilizer: nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, micronutrient, organic). Supports IPM program management and NPK spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group; EPA Pesticide Product Classification; USDA NOP

description: Date of the most recent regulatory and compliance review of this catalog entry, including EPA registration status, MRL updates, SDS currency, and vendor qualification renewal. Supports periodic review cycle management per GLOBALG.A.P. and FSMA requirements.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Annual Review Requirements; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls; ISO 22000 Management Review

description: Standard procurement lead time in calendar days from PO issuance to GRN for this input from the preferred vendor. Used for reorder point calculation, planting season procurement planning, and supply chain risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Planning (MARC-PLIFZ); Granular Farm Management Procurement Planning

description: Indicates whether this input requires lot/batch number tracking throughout the supply chain for traceability, recall management, and FSMA compliance. True for seeds, crop protection chemicals, feed ingredients, and biologicals.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S Traceability; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Externally-known unique material number assigned in SAP S/4HANA MM or Oracle NetSuite for this agricultural input. Used as the primary cross-system business identifier for procurement, inventory, and AP processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master (MARA-MATNR); Oracle NetSuite Item Record

description: Minimum quantity per purchase order as required by the preferred vendor, expressed in the purchase UOM. Prevents creation of POs below vendor minimums and supports procurement cost optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record (EINE-MINBM); Oracle NetSuite Vendor Item Record

description: Biochemical mode of action classification for crop protection chemicals using HRAC (herbicides), IRAC (insecticides), or FRAC (fungicides) group codes (e.g., 'HRAC Group 9', 'IRAC Group 4A'). Critical for IPM resistance management programs.
reference: HRAC (Herbicide Resistance Action Committee); IRAC (Insecticide Resistance Action Committee); FRAC (Fungicide Resistance Action Committee); EPA IPM Guidelines

description: NPK nutrient analysis expressed as N-P-K percentage (e.g., '46-0-0' for urea, '18-46-0' for DAP, '0-0-60' for potash). Mandatory for all fertilizer inputs. Null for non-fertilizer inputs. Drives soil fertility planning and nutrient management compliance.
reference: AAPFCO (Association of American Plant Food Control Officials) Official Publication; USDA NRCS Nutrient Management

description: Indicates whether this input is certified for use in USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified operations. True only for inputs on the USDA NOP National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances. Critical for organic farm procurement compliance.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; USDA National List of Allowed Substances; GLOBALG.A.P. Organic Module

description: Name of the USDA-accredited certifying agent that issued the organic certification for this input (e.g., 'CCOF', 'Oregon Tilth', 'OMRI'). Required when organic_certified is True. Supports NOP audit trail and supplier qualification.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.501; OMRI (Organic Materials Review Institute); Fair Trade USA

description: Minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of a crop protection chemical and harvest, as specified on the EPA-registered label. Enforced during crop protection event scheduling to ensure MRL compliance.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. Crop Protection Module; FSMA Produce Safety Rule

description: Unit of measure used on purchase orders and vendor invoices, which may differ from the base UOM (e.g., purchased in MT but stocked in KG). Supports PO creation and AP invoice matching in SAP S/4HANA MM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; Oracle NetSuite Purchase UOM

description: Minimum number of hours workers must stay out of treated areas after pesticide application, as mandated by EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Critical for workforce safety compliance and OSHA reporting.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) 40 CFR Part 170; OSHA 29 CFR 1928.110; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether this crop protection chemical is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring a certified pesticide applicator license for purchase and application. Drives applicator certification verification in procurement workflow.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3(d); 40 CFR Part 152 Subpart I; USDA APHIS

description: Indicates whether a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is required for this input under OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom) and EPA regulations. True for all crop protection chemicals, fertilizers, and fuels. Drives SDS document management workflow.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 HazCom Standard; EPA FIFRA; GHS (Globally Harmonized System)

description: Indicates whether seeds are chemically treated (fungicide/insecticide coating), untreated, organically treated, or not applicable for non-seed inputs. Affects handling requirements, PHI/REI applicability, and USDA NOP organic certification eligibility.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; EPA FIFRA Seed Treatment Registration; GLOBALG.A.P. Seed Module

description: Maximum number of days this input remains effective and safe from the date of manufacture or receipt, as specified by the manufacturer. Drives FIFO/FEFO inventory management, reorder policy, and expiry-based quarantine holds in the WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management; Manhattan Associates WMS Expiry Management; GMP

description: EPA-mandated signal word on the pesticide label indicating acute toxicity level: DANGER (highest toxicity, Category I/II), WARNING (moderate toxicity, Category III), CAUTION (lowest toxicity, Category IV), or not_applicable for non-pesticide inputs. Required for SDS and worker safety training.
reference: EPA FIFRA 40 CFR Part 156.64; GHS Hazard Communication; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Vendor-assigned or internal SKU code for this input, used for order management, barcode scanning, and RFID tracking in the warehouse. Supports Manhattan Associates WMS receiving and inventory control operations.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Item Master; GS1 Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM EAN/UPC

description: Required storage condition for this input to maintain efficacy, safety, and regulatory compliance. Drives WMS bin location assignment in Manhattan Associates and warehouse task planning for proper segregation of incompatible materials.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Storage Requirements; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.106; GMP Storage Guidelines; ISO 22000

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the vendor master record in the procurement domain. Primary key for all vendor-related transactions and qualifications.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master (LFA1)

description: Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ag procurement teams assign an internal relationship manager to each vendor for scorecard management, audit coordination, and corrective action follow-up. This FK drives vendor performance reporting b
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) Permit Number
description: USDA APHIS permit number for vendors supplying regulated biological materials, plant propagation materials, or animal health products subject to APHIS import/movement controls.
reference: USDA APHIS 7 CFR Part 330; APHIS Plant Protection Act; APHIS VS (Veterinary Services) permits

description: Vendor's bank account number used for electronic funds transfer (EFT) and ACH payment processing. Classified restricted due to PCI/financial data sensitivity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI LFBK (vendor bank details); PCI DSS financial data protection; SOX financial controls

description: ABA routing transit number identifying the vendor's financial institution for ACH and wire transfer payments. Classified restricted due to financial data sensitivity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI LFBK; Federal Reserve ABA routing number standard; PCI DSS; SOX AP controls

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the vendor's primary registered business address. Used for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance, import/export documentation, and FOB/CIF contract defaults.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) regulations; Codex Alimentarius import requirements

description: Timestamp when the vendor master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1 creation date; SOX internal controls; ISO 22000 supplier record management

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the vendor's invoicing currency (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). Used for AP invoice processing, FX conversion, and spend analytics.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA MM LFB1-WAERS; IFRS/GAAP foreign currency transaction standards

description: Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number uniquely identifying the vendor's legal entity globally. Used for supplier risk assessment, credit checks, and USDA/FDA supplier verification.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet DUNS Standard; FSMA Supplier Verification (SVCP); USDA procurement requirements

description: EPA registration number for vendors supplying pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or other regulated agrochemicals. Required under FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act) for legal sale and distribution of crop protection products.
reference: EPA FIFRA registration; 40 CFR Part 152; APHIS import permit requirements for biological inputs

description: Percentage (0.00–100.00) of ordered quantity actually delivered by the vendor within the scorecard period. Measures supply reliability for critical agricultural inputs such as seeds, NPK fertilizers, and crop protection chemicals.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM vendor evaluation; procurement supply reliability KPI standards

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Supplier Verification Control Program (SVCP) Compliant
description: Indicates whether the vendor has been verified as compliant with FSMA Supplier Verification Control Program (SVCP) requirements under 21 CFR Part 1, Subpart L. Required for vendors supplying food-grade or food-contact agricultural inputs.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L; FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP)

description: Default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) agreed with this vendor for shipment of agricultural inputs. Determines risk transfer point and freight cost responsibility. Free On Board (FOB) and Cost Insurance and Freight (CIF) are most common for bulk commodity inputs.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing info record; USDA import/export logistics standards

description: Percentage (0.00–100.00) of vendor invoices that matched the corresponding PO and GRN without discrepancy within the scorecard period. Drives AP processing efficiency and three-way match success rate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI three-way match (PO-GRN-Invoice); Oracle NetSuite AP invoice matching; GAAP AP controls

description: Date of the most recently completed vendor qualification or food safety audit. Used to determine audit currency and trigger re-audit scheduling.
reference: FSMA SVCP audit frequency requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. annual audit cycle; SQF/BRC/IFS audit schedules

description: Numeric score (0.00–100.00) from the most recent vendor qualification audit. Reflects compliance with GAP, GMP, HACCP, and food safety standards. Scores below threshold trigger conditional status or disqualification.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. audit scoring methodology; SQF/BRC/IFS audit scoring; GFSI benchmarked audit criteria

description: Full legal registered name of the vendor organization as it appears on official government and tax documents. Used for contract execution, invoice matching, and regulatory filings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1-NAME1; IRS EIN registration; USDA supplier registration

description: Percentage (0.00–100.00) of purchase orders delivered by the vendor on or before the confirmed delivery date within the current scorecard period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for vendor scorecard and contract renewal decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM vendor evaluation (ME61); GLOBALG.A.P. supply chain performance metrics; procurement KPI standards

description: Count of open corrective action requests (CARs) issued to the vendor for quality, compliance, or delivery failures. Non-zero values may trigger conditional status or procurement holds pending resolution.
reference: GFSI corrective action requirements; SQF/BRC/IFS non-conformance management; SAP S/4HANA QM notification management

description: Composite vendor performance rating derived from on-time delivery, fill rate, quality acceptance, invoice accuracy, and regulatory compliance scores. Drives preferred vendor designation and contract renewal decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM vendor evaluation (ME61); GLOBALG.A.P. supplier performance standards; procurement scorecard best practices

description: SAP/NetSuite payment terms code defining the agreed payment schedule (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30). Governs AP invoice due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI LFB1-ZTERM; Oracle NetSuite AP payment terms; GAAP accounts payable standards

description: Primary business email address for the vendor's account manager or procurement liaison. Used for PO transmission, GRN confirmations, and compliance correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4 (for international vendors); SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1 contact data

description: Primary business telephone number for the vendor's procurement or account management contact. Includes country dialing code for international vendors.
reference: GDPR Article 4 (for international vendors); SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1-TELF1

description: Date on which the vendor was most recently granted approved or conditional qualification status following audit and documentation review.
reference: FSMA SVCP; GLOBALG.A.P. certification audit cycle; SQF/BRC/IFS supplier qualification standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the vendor's qualification within the approved supplier program. Controls whether purchase orders may be issued. 'Conditional' indicates approval with restrictions (e.g., limited input categories or volume caps).
reference: FSMA SVCP (Supplier Verification Control Program); GLOBALG.A.P. supplier qualification; GFSI approved supplier programs; SAP S/4HANA MM vendor blocking logic

description: Percentage (0.00–100.00) of received goods that passed quality inspection (GRN quality check) without rejection or return within the scorecard period. Reflects vendor's adherence to COA specifications and MRL compliance for agrochemical inputs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM quality inspection results; HACCP incoming material acceptance criteria; MRL compliance per EPA/Codex Alimentarius

description: Composite score (0.00–100.00) reflecting the vendor's compliance with applicable regulatory requirements including EPA registration currency, APHIS permit validity, SDS documentation completeness, FSMA SVCP status, and MRL adherence.
reference: EPA FIFRA compliance; FSMA SVCP; APHIS permit requirements; OSHA HazCom SDS; Codex Alimentarius MRL standards

description: Date by which the vendor must complete re-qualification activities (audit, documentation refresh, certification renewal) to maintain approved status. Triggers procurement alerts when approaching.
reference: FSMA SVCP annual review requirements; GLOBALG.A.P. re-certification cycle; SQF/BRC/IFS periodic audit schedules

description: SAP S/4HANA MM vendor account group code (e.g., LIEF for standard suppliers, KRED for one-time vendors) controlling field selection, number ranges, and partner functions in the vendor master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1-KTOKK vendor account group configuration

description: End date of the performance measurement period for which the current scorecard metrics (on-time delivery, fill rate, quality acceptance, invoice accuracy, regulatory compliance) were calculated.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM vendor evaluation period; procurement performance review cycle

description: Indicates whether the vendor has provided current Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for all supplied chemical inputs (fertilizers, agrochemicals, fuel) in compliance with OSHA HazCom 2012 (GHS). True = all required SDS documents on file and current.
reference: OSHA HazCom 2012 (29 CFR 1910.1200); GHS SDS requirements; EPA chemical safety documentation

description: Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number (TIN) assigned by the IRS. Required for 1099 reporting, AP processing, and regulatory compliance. Classified restricted due to sensitivity of tax identity data.

description: Doing-business-as (DBA) or trade name used in day-to-day commercial operations, which may differ from the legal entity name.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM LFA1-NAME2

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the vendor master record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and Oracle NetSuite, and audit trail maintenance.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

Draft: under negotiation; Pending Approval: awaiting authorization; Active: binding and in force; Suspended: temporarily halted; Expired: past end date without renewal; Terminated: ended early; Closed: fully fulfilled and archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Release Status; FSMA Supplier Verification

Framework agreements cover multiple POs over time; spot contracts are single-event; annual supply agreements govern a full crop year; blanket purchase orders allow call-offs; consignment contracts defer payment until consumption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: framework|spot|annual_supply|blanket_purchase|consignment|index_linked — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Contract Category (EKKO-BSART); GLOBALG.A.P. Supply Chain Standard

reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Target Quantity (EKPO-KTMNG); USDA WASDE Volume Reporting

description: System timestamp recording when the vendor contract record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act — Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 22000 Record Keeping

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (base_price, volume_tier thresholds, penalty amounts). Typically USD for domestic US agricultural contracts.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA MM — Currency (EKKO-WAERS); IFRS 21

description: International commercial terms (Incoterms) governing risk transfer and freight responsibility between the enterprise and the vendor. FOB = Free On Board; CIF = Cost Insurance and Freight; EXW = Ex Works; DAP = Delivered at Place; DDP = Delivered Duty Paid; FCA = Free Carrier; CFR = Cost and Freight. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|DAP|DDP|FCA|CFR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM — Incoterms (EKKO-INCO1)

description: Named place or port associated with the Incoterms delivery terms (e.g., 'Port of New Orleans' for FOB, 'Chicago Terminal' for DAP). Required to complete the Incoterms designation per ICC 2020.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP S/4HANA MM — Incoterms Part 2 (EKKO-INCO2)

description: Date on which the contract expires or is scheduled to terminate. Nullable for open-ended evergreen agreements. Aligns with SAP MM validity end date (EKKO-KDATE).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO-KDATE; IFRS 15

description: Indicates whether the agricultural input covered by this contract (e.g., pesticides, herbicides, fungicides) requires EPA registration under FIFRA (Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act). Mandatory compliance check for chemical input procurement.
reference: EPA FIFRA Registration; APHIS Import Regulations; SAP S/4HANA QM — Compliance

description: Indicates whether the contract includes a force majeure clause that excuses non-performance due to extraordinary events (natural disasters, crop failures, regulatory actions). Critical for agricultural supply chain risk management.
reference: USDA Agricultural Risk Management; FSMA Supply Chain Program; ICC Force Majeure Clause 2020

description: Indicates whether the vendor is contractually required to hold a valid GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification for the duration of this contract. True = certification mandatory.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Certification Standard; GFSI Benchmarking; FSMA Foreign Supplier Verification Program (FSVP)

description: Minimum quantity per individual purchase order call-off against this contract. Expressed in the same unit of measure as volume_uom. Vendor may refuse orders below this threshold.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Minimum Order Quantity (EKPO-MINBM)

description: Indicates whether the vendor must supply inputs certified under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). Applicable for organic seed, organic fertilizer, and approved organic crop protection inputs.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) — 7 CFR Part 205; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service

description: Agreed payment terms governing invoice settlement (e.g., 'Net 30', '2/10 Net 30', 'Net 60 EOM'). Drives AP payment scheduling in SAP FI and Oracle NetSuite AR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — Payment Terms (T052); Oracle NetSuite AP; GAAP Accounts Payable

description: Indicates whether the contract contains penalty clauses for non-performance, late delivery, or quality failures. True = penalty clauses exist; False = no penalty provisions.
reference: Procurement Best Practice — Contract Risk Management; GLOBALG.A.P. Supplier Compliance

description: Narrative description of penalty provisions including trigger conditions, calculation method, and maximum liability cap (e.g., '2% of PO value per week of delay, capped at 10%'). Null if penalty_clause_flag is False.
reference: Procurement Best Practice — Contract Risk Management

description: Text description or algebraic expression defining how the price is adjusted for formula-based or index-linked contracts (e.g., 'WASDE corn price + $0.15/BU basis'). Null for fixed-price contracts.
reference: USDA WASDE; DTN Market Data; SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Formula

description: Date from which the contracted pricing terms (base_price, price_type) become effective. May differ from contract start_date if pricing is renegotiated mid-term.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Validity Start (KONH-DATAB); IFRS 15

description: Date on which the contracted pricing terms expire. After this date, new pricing must be negotiated or the contract reverts to spot pricing. Aligns with SAP MM condition validity end date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Validity End (KONH-DATBI); IFRS 15

description: Mechanism by which the contract price is determined. Fixed: locked price for the contract term; Formula-based: price derived from a calculation; WASDE-indexed: price tied to USDA World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates; Spot: prevailing market price at time of order; Cost-plus: vendor cost plus agreed margin.
reference: USDA WASDE Report; SAP S/4HANA MM — Condition Type (KONP); IFRS 15

description: Cumulative monetary value of all purchase orders (POs) released against this contract to date. Enables spend tracking against contract ceiling and open commitment reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Release Order Value (EKKO-LPLNO); Oracle NetSuite Procurement

description: Number of calendar days prior to the contract end date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or terminate. Drives procurement calendar alerts.
reference: Procurement Best Practice — Contract Lifecycle Management

business_glossary_term: SAP MM Outline Agreement Document Number
description: SAP S/4HANA MM module outline agreement document number (10-digit) serving as the system-of-record identifier for this contract. Used for integration with AP processing and GRN matching.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — EKKO Table (EBELN field)

description: Date on which the contract becomes legally binding and the vendor is authorized to supply against it. Aligns with the SAP MM validity start date (EKKO-KDATB).

description: The EPA-label approved method(s) for applying the agrochemical product to crops or soil (e.g., foliar spray, soil incorporation, seed treatment, aerial application). description: Unit of measure for the maximum application rate (e.g., oz/acre, lb/acre, L/ha). description: The United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) registration number assigned to the pesticide product under FIFRA. Format: registrant-number-product-number (e.g., '524-308'). description: The EPA acute toxicity category (I through IV) assigned to the agrochemical product based on oral, dermal, inhalation, and eye/skin irritation studies. Category I = most toxic (DANGER label); Category IV = least toxic. Drives PPE requirements, worker safety training, and medical surveillance programs.
reference: EPA FIFRA 40 CFR Part 156.62; EPA Pesticide Registration Notice; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: The physical and chemical formulation classification of the agrochemical product (e.g., emulsifiable concentrate, wettable powder, suspension concentrate, granule). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emulsifiable_concentrate|wettable_powder|suspension_concentrate|granule|soluble_liquid|dry_flowable|water_dispersible_granule|microencapsulated|ultra_low_volume — promote to reference product]
reference: FAO/WHO Pesticide Specifications; CIPAC (Collaborative International Pesticides Analytical Council); EPA FIFRA

description: Indicates whether the EPA-registered label includes a groundwater advisory statement, signaling potential for leaching into groundwater under certain soil and climatic conditions. True = groundwater advisory present; False = no advisory. Drives BMP (Best Management Practices) and buffer zone requirements.

description: The US DOT hazardous materials classification for the agrochemical product as defined in 49 CFR. Determines transportation, labeling, placarding, and emergency response requirements. reference: US DOT 49 CFR Parts 171-180; IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations; GHS; EPA FIFRA; TMS (Transportation Management System)

description: The date and time when the agrochemical product record was most recently modified in the master catalog. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with source systems (SAP S/4HANA), and regulatory audit trails. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; ISO 22000; SAP S/4HANA MM Change Documents

description: The name of the company that physically manufactures or formulates the agrochemical product. May differ from the EPA registrant for generic or private-label products. Used for supplier management and procurement in SAP S/4HANA MM.

description: The maximum allowable application rate of the agrochemical product per application event as specified on the EPA-registered label. Used in conjunction with application_rate_unit to enforce label compliance and prevent over-application.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; 40 CFR Part 156; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard

description: The maximum number of times the agrochemical product may be applied per growing season per crop as specified on the EPA-registered label. Enforces resistance management and prevents MRL exceedances from cumulative applications.

description: The IRAC (Insecticide Resistance Action Committee), HRAC (Herbicide Resistance Action Committee), or FRAC (Fungicide Resistance Action Committee) mode of action classification code for the active ingredient. description: Indicates whether the agrochemical product appears on the Organic Materials Review Institute (OMRI) Products List, confirming suitability for use in certified organic operations. True = OMRI listed; False = not listed. Complements organic_approved_flag with third-party verification.
reference: OMRI (Organic Materials Review Institute) Products List; USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205

description: Indicates whether the agrochemical product is approved for use in certified organic production systems under the USDA National Organic Program (NOP). True = approved for organic use; False = not approved. Drives product selection for organic-certified fields and NOP compliance documentation.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; OMRI (Organic Materials Review Institute) Listed; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard

description: The quantity of agrochemical product contained in the standard commercial packaging unit (e.g., 2.5, 1.0, 5.0). Used in conjunction with package_unit for procurement, inventory management, and application rate calculations in SAP S/4HANA MM.

description: Unit of measure for the package size (e.g., gallon, liter, kg, lb). Combined with package_size to define the standard commercial unit for procurement, inventory, and cost tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gallon|quart|pint|liter|kg|lb|oz|fl_oz|ton — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

description: The minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of the agrochemical product and the harvest of the treated crop, as specified on the EPA-registered label. Critical for MRL compliance and food safety under FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.

description: The registered commercial or trade name of the agrochemical product as it appears on the EPA-registered label and product packaging (e.g., 'Roundup PowerMAX', 'Headline AMP Fungicide').

description: Current lifecycle status of the agrochemical product in the master catalog. 'active' = currently registered and available for use; 'discontinued' = no longer manufactured or sold; 'suspended' = EPA registration suspended pending review; 'pending_registration' = awaiting EPA approval; 'cancelled' = EPA registration voluntarily cancelled.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 6; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard

description: Classification of the agrochemical product by its primary mode of action or functional category. Drives regulatory compliance requirements, application protocols, and PHI/REI enforcement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pesticide|herbicide|fungicide|insecticide|fertilizer|adjuvant|plant_growth_regulator|rodenticide|nematicide|fumigant — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA FIFRA; USDA NASS Pesticide Use Survey; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard CB 7

description: The name of the company or individual holding the EPA registration for the agrochemical product (e.g., 'Bayer CropScience', 'Syngenta AG', 'BASF Corporation'). Used for regulatory correspondence, label verification, and supplier qualification.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3; EPA Pesticide Registration System (PRISM)

description: The date on which the EPA registration for the agrochemical product expires and must be renewed. Used to trigger re-registration workflows and prevent use of products with lapsed registrations. Critical for FSMA and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance audits.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3(g); 40 CFR Part 152; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard

description: The resistance management group designation (e.g., 'HRAC Group 9', 'IRAC Group 4A', 'FRAC Group 3') used to plan chemical rotation programs and prevent development of resistant pest populations. Supports IPM compliance and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.
reference: IRAC; HRAC; FRAC; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard CB 7.7; IPM (Integrated Pest Management) Best Practices

description: The minimum number of hours that must pass after a pesticide application before workers may re-enter the treated area without personal protective equipment (PPE), as mandated by the EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS). Required for OSHA and EPA compliance.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) 40 CFR Part 170; OSHA 29 CFR 1928.110; GLOBALG.A.P. IFA Standard

description: Indicates whether the agrochemical product is classified as a Restricted Use Pesticide (RUP) by the EPA, requiring purchase and application only by certified pesticide applicators. True = restricted use; False = general use. Drives applicator certification verification workflows.

description: Reference identifier or URL to the current Safety Data Sheet (SDS) for the agrochemical product, compliant with OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom 2012) and GHS format. reference: OSHA HazCom 2012 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS (Globally Harmonized System) Rev. 9; EPA FIFRA; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: The guaranteed shelf life of the agrochemical product in months from the date of manufacture, as specified by the manufacturer. Used for inventory rotation (FIFO), expiry date tracking, and procurement planning in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Shelf Life Management; Manhattan Associates WMS; EPA FIFRA Label; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices)

description: The EPA-mandated signal word appearing on the pesticide label indicating the acute toxicity category of the product: DANGER (Category I - most toxic), WARNING (Category II), CAUTION (Category III - least toxic), or EXEMPT. Drives PPE requirements and worker safety protocols.
reference: EPA FIFRA 40 CFR Part 156.62; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; GHS (Globally Harmonized System)

description: The maximum storage temperature in degrees Celsius permitted for safe storage of the agrochemical product, as specified in the SDS and product label. Exceeding this threshold may cause product degradation, pressure buildup, or hazardous reactions. Used for WMS storage compliance.
reference: OSHA HazCom 2012; EPA FIFRA Label; GHS SDS Section 7; Manhattan Associates WMS Storage Conditions

description: The minimum storage temperature in degrees Celsius required to maintain product integrity and prevent degradation, as specified in the SDS and product label. Used for warehouse storage condition compliance in Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: OSHA HazCom 2012; EPA FIFRA Label; GHS SDS Section 7; Manhattan Associates WMS Storage Conditions

description: The United Nations (UN) four-digit identification number assigned to hazardous materials for international transport (e.g., 'UN1993' for flammable liquids, 'UN2902' for pesticides). Required on shipping documents, BOL, and placards for DOT and IATA compliance.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods; US DOT 49 CFR; IATA DGR; GHS

description: Indicates whether this commodity is classified as a major food allergen under FDA regulations (e.g., wheat, soybeans, peanuts, tree nuts, milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, sesame). Triggers allergen labeling and cross-contact prevention requirements.

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this commodity to maintain quality and safety. Used to configure cold chain requirements in warehouse management and transportation planning.

description: Minimum acceptable test weight in pounds per bushel for grade determination of this grain commodity (e.g., US No. 2 Yellow Corn = 54 lbs/bu). Used in USDA GIPSA official grading and price dockage calculations.
reference: USDA Grain Inspection, Packers and Stockyards Administration (GIPSA) Official Grain Standards of the United States

reference: John Deere Operations Center Planter Configuration; AOSA Seed Testing Standards; Equipment Calibration SOPs
