reference: EPA Active Ingredient Registry; USDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule; Codex Alimentarius MRL Standards

description: Name or code of the seed treatment product(s) applied to the seed lot prior to sale or planting (e.g., 'Poncho 1250 + Acceleron', 'Cruiser Maxx Vibrance'). Includes fungicide, insecticide, and biological seed treatments. Required for EPA label compliance, PHI/REI tracking, and NOP organic eligibility determination.
reference: EPA Pesticide Registration (FIFRA); USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.204 (Seed Treatment Restrictions); SDS (Safety Data Sheet) Requirements

description: Manufacturer or agronomist-recommended seeding rate expressed as seeds per acre for optimal stand establishment. Used for VRA (Variable Rate Application) prescription generation in Trimble Ag Software and seed quantity planning in Granular Farm Management.
reference: Trimble Ag Software VRA Prescription Standards; Climate FieldView Planting Recommendations; USDA Extension Agronomic Guidelines

description: Number of seeds per standard commercial unit (e.g., seeds per 80,000-count bag for corn, seeds per 140,000-count bag for soybean). Used for inventory management, seeding rate calculations, and order quantity planning in SAP S/4HANA MM and Manhattan Associates WMS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure Configuration; Manhattan Associates WMS Item Master; USDA AMS Seed Labeling

description: Descriptive label of the bundled biotechnology or conventionally bred trait stack applied to this variety (e.g., 'RoundupReady2Xtend + Bollgard3', 'Enlist E3', 'LibertyLink GT27'). Captures herbicide tolerance, insect resistance, drought tolerance, and other engineered or selected traits. Used for IPM planning, agrochemical compatibility, and regulatory reporting.
reference: USDA APHIS BRS Trait Registry; EPA Pesticide Registration (for Bt traits); Integrated Pest Management (IPM) Framework

description: Internally assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the seed variety in ERP and FMIS systems (e.g., SAP S/4HANA material master number or Trimble Ag Software variety code). Used as the business key for cross-system integration and SKU linkage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module Material Master; Trimble Ag Software Variety Database

description: Commercial or proprietary name of the seed variety as registered by the breeder or licensor (e.g., 'DeKalb DKC62-08', 'Pioneer P1197AM'). This is the primary human-readable identifier used in field operations, procurement, and sales.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) Variety Registration; GLOBALG.A.P. Seed Traceability Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the seed variety in the product catalog. 'active' indicates commercially available; 'discontinued' indicates no longer sold or planted; 'under_review' indicates pending regulatory or internal evaluation; 'pending_approval' indicates awaiting APHIS or NOP certification; 'withdrawn' indicates removed due to regulatory or quality issues.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; USDA National Organic Program (NOP); Internal Product Lifecycle Policy

description: Categorical rating of the variety's yield potential relative to comparable varieties in the same crop class and maturity group, as determined by multi-environment trial (MET) data. Used for variety selection, agronomic planning, and YPM (Yield Per Acre Metric) benchmarking in Granular Farm Management.
reference: USDA NASS Variety Performance Trials; Association of Official Seed Certifying Agencies (AOSCA); Climate FieldView Variety Analytics

business_glossary_term: Crop Use Registration - Crop Use Registration Id
description: Primary key for the crop_use_registration association
reference: Internal - M:N Association PK

business_glossary_term: Crop Use Registration - Agrochemical Product Id
description: Foreign key linking to the registered agrochemical product (pesticide, herbicide, fungicide, or fertilizer) in the master agrochemical catalog.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: Foreign key linking to the agricultural commodity for which this agrochemical product use is registered.
reference: Internal - M:N Association FK

description: The date on which this agrochemical product was formally approved for use on this commodity in the specified jurisdiction. Required for regulatory audit trails and GLOBALG.A.P. compliance documentation.
reference: EPA/USDA Crop Registration Record

description: The date on which this crop-use registration expires and must be renewed with the relevant regulatory authority. Specific to the agrochemical-commodity-jurisdiction combination.
reference: EPA Registration Renewal Schedule

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the regulatory jurisdiction under which this crop-use registration is valid. The same agrochemical-commodity pair may have different approvals, rates, and PHI values across jurisdictions (e.g., USA vs. EU vs. Brazil).
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3

description: The maximum allowable application rate of the agrochemical product for this specific commodity, as specified in the crop-use registration. This value is commodity-specific and may differ from the product-level max_application_rate on agrochemical_product.
reference: EPA Crop Use Registration Label

description: Minimum number of days that must elapse between the last pesticide application and harvest for this commodity, as required by EPA label regulations. Ensures MRL compliance at point of harvest. Populated from agrochemical_product PHI data for the commodity's primary crop protection program. [Moved from commodity: phi_days on commodity represents a commodity-level PHI reference, but PHI is actually a function of the specific agrochemical-commodity combination, not the commodity alone. The authoritative PHI for compliance purposes belongs on the crop_use_registration association where it is tied to both the specific agrochemical product and the specific commodity.]
reference: EPA Federal Insecticide, Fungicide, and Rodenticide Act (FIFRA); EPA Pesticide Label Requirements (40 CFR Part 156)

description: The minimum number of days that must elapse between the last application of this agrochemical product and harvest of this specific commodity, as specified in the crop-use registration. Overrides the product-level PHI for this commodity context.
reference: EPA Label PHI by Crop

description: Current lifecycle status of this crop-use registration record. Tracks whether the registration is currently active, suspended by a regulatory authority, cancelled, or pending renewal. Belongs to the registration, not to either parent entity.
reference: EPA Registration Status Codes

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each GMO declaration record. Primary key for the gmo_declaration data product.
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS); APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services

description: Reference to the agricultural product or commodity for which this GMO declaration is recorded. Links to the product catalog master.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS); USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS)

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: gmo_declaration.organic_certified_flag and organic_certificate_number indicate that a GMO declaration may reference an associated organic certification (non-GMO products are often also organically cer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) Petition Number
description: APHIS petition number associated with the deregulation or regulatory approval of the specific bioengineered trait or transformation event. Used to verify US market approval status and cross-reference APHIS regulatory records.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services 7 CFR Part 340; USDA

description: Indicates whether this product requires Bioengineered (BE) food disclosure labeling under the USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS). Drives label generation and compliance workflows.
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS) 7 CFR Part 66; FSMA

description: Method of BE food disclosure used on product labeling as required by USDA NBFDS. Options include on-package text, USDA BE symbol, electronic/digital link (QR code), or phone number. 'not_required' when product is exempt.
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS) 7 CFR Part 66; AMS

description: Date on which the non-GMO certificate or GMO verification certificate expires and must be renewed. Drives compliance monitoring and re-certification workflows.
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard; USDA NOP; GLOBALG.A.P.; GFSI

description: Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for import or commercial use in China. Required for export documentation to Chinese buyers and compliance with MARA (Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs) biosafety certificates.
reference: China MARA Biosafety Certificate; Codex Alimentarius; USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (FAS)

description: Reference number or document identifier for the Certificate of Analysis (COA) associated with this GMO declaration. The COA contains the full laboratory test results and is provided to customers and regulatory bodies.
reference: USDA AMS; GFSI; SQF; BRC Global Standards; ISO 22000

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Country of Origin
description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of origin of the product as required for Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance. Relevant for GMO declarations as country of origin may affect applicable GMO regulations.
reference: USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) 7 CFR Part 60; AMS; ISO 3166-1

description: System timestamp recording when this GMO declaration record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX; GDPR Article 5; ISO 22000; FSMA

description: Date on which the GMO declaration was formally submitted or issued. Represents the principal business event date for this record, distinct from system audit timestamps.
reference: USDA BFDS 7 CFR Part 66; APHIS; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Externally-known unique business identifier for this GMO declaration record, used in regulatory submissions, export documentation, and audit trails. Follows internal numbering convention (e.g., GMO-2024-00123).
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS); APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Current lifecycle status of the GMO declaration record. Tracks the workflow from initial draft through regulatory review to approval, expiry, or withdrawal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_review|approved|rejected|expired|withdrawn — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS); APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; ISO 22000

description: Name or identifier of the person or organization responsible for submitting this GMO declaration. Used for accountability, audit trail, and regulatory compliance documentation.
reference: USDA BFDS; APHIS; FSMA; SOX

description: Scientific name of the organism from which the introduced genetic material was derived (e.g., 'Agrobacterium tumefaciens', 'Bacillus thuringiensis'). Required for regulatory submissions and export documentation.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 45-2003; EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003

description: Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for commercial use or import in the European Union. Critical for export compliance and trade documentation.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003; EU Regulation (EC) No 1830/2003; European Food Safety Authority (EFSA)

description: Declared GMO classification of the product. 'bioengineered' aligns with USDA BE disclosure requirements. 'non_gmo' indicates verified absence of bioengineered material. 'conventional' indicates no GMO testing performed. 'under_assessment' indicates testing in progress.
reference: USDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (BFDS) 7 CFR Part 66; APHIS; EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003

description: Maximum allowable percentage of GMO material for the product to be classified as non-GMO or to meet the applicable regulatory threshold for the target market (e.g., 0.9% for EU, 5.0% for Japan). Drives compliance determination logic.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003; Japan MHLW; Non-GMO Project Standard (0.9%); USDA BFDS

description: Name of the gene or genetic construct introduced into the organism (e.g., 'cp4 epsps', 'cry1Ab', 'bar'). Used for scientific documentation, regulatory submissions, and export market approval cross-referencing.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 45-2003; EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003

description: Reference code or document identifier for the Identity Preservation (IP) protocol applied to maintain the segregation and traceability of non-GMO or specific GMO product streams throughout the supply chain. Supports USDA BE disclosure and export market requirements.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) IP Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000; GFSI

description: Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for import or commercial use in Japan. Required for export documentation to Japanese buyers under Japan's Food Safety Commission GMO approval framework.
reference: Japan Food Safety Commission; Japan Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW); Codex Alimentarius

description: System timestamp recording when this GMO declaration record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data freshness monitoring.
reference: SOX; GDPR Article 5; ISO 22000; FSMA

description: Certificate or verification number issued by the non-GMO verification body. Used for audit trail, customer documentation, and regulatory compliance. Null when no third-party verification exists.
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard; USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GFSI

description: Name of the third-party organization that verified and certified the non-GMO status of the product (e.g., Non-GMO Project, USDA Organic NOP). Applicable only when gmo_status is 'non_gmo'. 'none' indicates no third-party verification.
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard; USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Indicates whether the product holds USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certification. Organic certification inherently prohibits GMO use; this flag supports cross-validation of GMO status against organic certification status.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; IFOAM; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Free-text field for additional notes, qualifications, or explanations related to the GMO declaration. May include details on testing anomalies, conditional approvals, or market-specific exceptions.
reference: USDA BFDS; GFSI; ISO 22000

description: Date on which the GMO declaration was reviewed and approved or rejected by the responsible quality or regulatory team. Supports workflow tracking and audit compliance.
reference: USDA BFDS; GFSI; SQF; BRC Global Standards; ISO 22000

description: Name of the quality assurance or regulatory compliance officer who reviewed and approved or rejected this GMO declaration. Required for audit trail and SOX compliance.
reference: USDA BFDS; GFSI; ISO 22000; SOX; FSMA

description: Lot or batch number of the product sample submitted for GMO testing. Enables traceability from the GMO declaration back to the specific production lot for recall management and audit purposes.
reference: FSMA Traceability Rule 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S; USDA AMS; HACCP; ISO 22000

description: Quantitative result of GMO testing expressed as a percentage of GMO material detected in the sample (e.g., 0.0500 = 0.05%). Used to assess compliance against regulatory thresholds (e.g., EU 0.9% labeling threshold, Japan 5% threshold).
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003 (0.9% threshold); Japan MHLW (5% threshold); ISO 21570; Codex Alimentarius

description: Name of the accredited laboratory that performed GMO testing on the product. Must be an ISO 17025-accredited laboratory for regulatory acceptance of results.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025; USDA AMS; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 74-2010

description: Laboratory method used to test for the presence or absence of GMO material in the product (e.g., PCR - Polymerase Chain Reaction, ELISA - Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay). 'not_tested' when no laboratory testing was performed.
reference: ISO 21569; ISO 21570; ISO 21571; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 74-2010; USDA AMS

description: Jurisdiction or market for which the gmo_threshold_percent applies. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or 'global' for internationally harmonized thresholds. Enables multi-market compliance management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|EU|CHN|JPN|BRA|CAN|AUS|global — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 3166-1; Codex Alimentarius; USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (FAS)

description: Descriptive name and functional summary of the introduced genetic trait (e.g., 'Herbicide tolerance - glyphosate', 'Insect resistance - Bt Cry1Ab protein', 'Drought tolerance - DREB transcription factor'). Supports product labeling, COA documentation, and export market compliance.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 45-2003; USDA BFDS

description: Indicates whether this GMO transformation event has received regulatory approval for commercial use in the United States market. Verified against APHIS deregulation status and FDA/EPA approvals.
reference: APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; FDA; EPA; USDA BFDS 7 CFR Part 66

description: Date on which GMO status was verified through testing, certification, or third-party audit. Used to determine currency of the declaration and trigger re-verification workflows.
reference: USDA BFDS; Non-GMO Project Standard; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each grading standard record in the agricultural product catalog. Primary key for the grading_standard reference entity.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grade Standards

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: grading_standard is defined for specific commodity types (e.g., USDA Grade No. 1 Hard Red Winter Wheat). grading_standard.commodity_name is a denormalized string reference to the commodity catalog. Ad
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: grading_standard currently stores mrl_threshold_ppm as a denormalized DECIMAL scalar. The mrl_threshold table is the authoritative SSOT for Maximum Residue Limits. A grading standard for an agricultur
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Federal agency responsible for administering and enforcing this grading standard (e.g., AMS - Agricultural Marketing Service, GIPSA - Grain Inspection Packers and Stockyards Administration, FSIS - Food Safety and Inspection Service). Determines inspection authority and regulatory citation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AMS|GIPSA|FSIS|APHIS|FDA|EPA|NOP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; FSIS; APHIS; FDA; EPA; NOP

description: Market class or sub-class of the commodity to which this grading standard applies (e.g., 'Hard Red Winter', 'Soft White', 'Yellow Dent Corn', 'Angus Beef'). Provides finer commodity segmentation within the commodity type for accurate grade assignment.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grain Standards; FSIS

description: Reference identifier for the COA document template associated with this grading standard. Used by quality inspection workflows in SAP QM to auto-populate COA documents upon grade assignment.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 22000

description: Descriptive color specification or color score range required for this grade designation (e.g., 'minimum 50% red color for US Extra Fancy apples', 'bright yellow for US No. 1 corn'). Applies primarily to produce and livestock grading per USDA AMS standards.
reference: USDA AMS Produce Grading Standards; FSIS

description: Broad commodity category to which this grading standard applies (e.g., grain, oilseed, produce, fiber, livestock, dairy, poultry, egg, processed). Aligns with USDA commodity classification for routing to the correct inspection workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grain|oilseed|produce|fiber|livestock|dairy|poultry|egg|processed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: USDA AMS Commodity Classification; GIPSA

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) is required for products graded under this standard. When True, COA and product labeling must include country of origin per USDA AMS COOL regulations.
reference: USDA AMS COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) Regulations; 7 CFR Part 60

description: Timestamp when this grading standard record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per SOX and FSMA compliance frameworks.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FSMA; ISO 22000; GDPR Article 5

description: Date on which this grading standard became or becomes officially effective and enforceable. Used to determine applicability during quality inspection and COA generation workflows.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; Federal Register

description: Date on which this grading standard expires or is superseded. Null indicates the standard is open-ended with no scheduled expiration. Used to enforce version control in quality inspection workflows.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; Federal Register

description: Maximum allowable percentage of foreign material (non-commodity matter) permitted for this grade designation. A standard GIPSA grading factor for grains, oilseeds, and other bulk commodities.
reference: GIPSA Official Grain Standards; USDA AMS

business_glossary_term: Global Food Safety Initiative (GFSI) Scheme Alignment
description: Identifies the GFSI-recognized food safety scheme (e.g., SQF, BRC, IFS, GLOBALG.A.P., FSSC 22000) with which this grading standard is aligned or compatible. Supports customer audit and certification requirements.
reference: GFSI (Global Food Safety Initiative); SQF; BRC Global Standards; IFS; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Indicates whether this grading standard applies to GMO products, non-GMO products, or both. Supports product line segregation for non-GMO and conventional product lines per USDA and GLOBALG.A.P. requirements.
reference: USDA AMS; GLOBALG.A.P.; Non-GMO Project Standard

description: Official grade label assigned by the administering agency (e.g., 'US No. 1', 'US No. 2', 'US Extra Fancy', 'USDA Prime', 'USDA Choice', 'US Grade A'). Represents the quality tier within the standard. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US No. 1|US No. 2|US No. 3|US Extra Fancy|USDA Prime|USDA Choice|USDA Select|US Grade A|US Grade B|US Grade C — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grade Standards; FSIS Grading Standards

description: Primary method used to assess and assign this grade (e.g., visual inspection, mechanical sizing, laboratory analysis, electronic/NIR sensing, or a combination). Determines inspection workflow and equipment requirements in SAP QM and field operations.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; ISO 22000; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Required frequency of grading inspections for commodities subject to this standard (e.g., per lot, per shipment, per batch, periodic, continuous). Drives quality inspection scheduling in SAP QM and Manhattan WMS workflows.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; FSMA; ISO 22000; HACCP

description: Timestamp when this grading standard record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with SAP QM, and regulatory audit trail maintenance.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); FSMA; ISO 22000; GDPR Article 5

description: Maximum allowable moisture content (%) for a commodity to qualify for this grade designation. A critical grading criterion for grains and oilseeds per GIPSA standards. Exceeding this threshold results in grade reduction or rejection.
reference: GIPSA Official Grain Standards; USDA AMS

description: Free-text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or clarifications related to this grading standard (e.g., seasonal applicability, regional exceptions, special handling requirements). Used by quality and compliance teams.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA

description: Indicates whether this grading standard is applicable to USDA National Organic Program (NOP) certified organic products. When True, the standard supports organic product line quality certification and COA generation.
reference: USDA NOP (National Organic Program); 7 CFR Part 205

description: Date on which this grading standard was officially published in the Federal Register or by the administering agency. Distinct from the effective date; used for regulatory audit trails and compliance documentation.
reference: Federal Register; USDA AMS; GIPSA

description: Official regulatory citation for the grading standard, including CFR title, part, and section (e.g., '7 CFR Part 26', '7 CFR Part 868', '9 CFR Part 319'). Required for COA generation and FSMA compliance documentation.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; FSIS; Code of Federal Regulations (CFR); FSMA

description: Maximum allowable percentage of serious defects (e.g., heat-damaged kernels, mold, insect damage) permitted for this grade. Serious defects are a distinct sub-category of total defects with stricter tolerance limits per GIPSA standards.
reference: GIPSA Official Grain Standards; USDA AMS

description: Size specification or size range required for this grade designation (e.g., minimum diameter in mm, weight per unit, count per carton). Applies to produce, eggs, and livestock products per USDA AMS grading standards.
reference: USDA AMS Produce Grading Standards; FSIS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the grading standard (e.g., 'USDA-GR1-CORN', 'AMS-EF-APPLE'). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and COA generation.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grade Standards; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: URL link to the official published grading standard document on the administering agency's website (e.g., USDA AMS, GIPSA). Provides direct access to the authoritative source document for quality inspectors and compliance teams.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; Federal Register

description: Full descriptive name of the grading standard as published by the administering agency (e.g., 'United States Standards for Grades of Corn', 'USDA Extra Fancy Grade for Apples'). Used in COA documentation and quality inspection workflows.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grade Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the grading standard. 'active' indicates the standard is currently in force; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version; 'withdrawn' indicates no longer applicable; 'draft' or 'pending' indicates under review or awaiting official publication.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA Official Grade Standards

description: Standard code of the newer grading standard that replaces this record when status is 'superseded'. Enables forward navigation in the version chain for compliance and historical reporting.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA

description: Minimum test weight in pounds per bushel (lbs/bu) required for a grain or oilseed commodity to qualify for this grade. Test weight is a key GIPSA grading factor reflecting grain density and quality.
reference: GIPSA Official Grain Standards; USDA AMS; BU (Bushel) unit of measure

description: Maximum allowable percentage of total defects (damaged kernels, heat damage, broken kernels, etc.) permitted for this grade designation. Applies to grain, oilseed, and produce grading per GIPSA and AMS standards.
reference: GIPSA Official Grain Standards; USDA AMS

business_glossary_term: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Commodity Code
description: Official USDA commodity code associated with this grading standard, enabling alignment with USDA AMS, GIPSA, and WASDE reporting systems. Used for regulatory reporting and supply chain traceability.
reference: USDA AMS Commodity Codes; GIPSA; WASDE

description: Version or revision number of the grading standard as published by the administering agency (e.g., '1.0', '2023', '3'). Supports audit trails and historical compliance verification.
reference: USDA AMS; GIPSA; Federal Register

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each label record in the agricultural product labeling catalog. Primary key for the label data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.gmo_declaration. Business justification: label currently has a non_gmo_claim_flag (BOOLEAN) that indicates a non-GMO claim on the label but provides no traceability to the supporting declaration. Under USDA Bioengineered (BE) Disclosure regu
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.grading_standard. Business justification: label.usda_grade_designation is a denormalized string reference to the applicable USDA grade standard displayed on the label. Adding grading_standard_id FK normalizes this to the authoritative grading
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the agricultural product or commodity this label is associated with in the product catalog (crop commodity, livestock product, processed good, seed/input, or value-added product).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.mrl_threshold. Business justification: label.mrl_compliance_flag indicates whether the label is MRL-compliant, but there is no reference to the specific MRL threshold being complied with. Adding mrl_threshold_id FK links the label to the a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: label.organic_certification_code is a denormalized string reference to the NOP organic certification that authorizes the organic seal on the label. Adding organic_certification_id FK normalizes this t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Assessed risk level for unintentional allergen cross-contact during production, handling, or packaging. Drives 'may contain' advisory statement decisions and HACCP allergen control plan requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA Preventive Controls — 21 CFR Part 117; HACCP; GFSI; SQF; BRC Global Standards

description: Indicates whether milk (FDA Big 9 allergen) is intentionally included as an ingredient in this product. When True, milk must be declared in the ingredient list and allergen statement per FALCPA.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); FDA FSMA Preventive Controls — 21 CFR Part 117

description: Indicates whether soybeans (FDA Big 9 allergen) are intentionally included as an ingredient. Particularly relevant in agricultural products given soy's prevalence in processed and feed products.
reference: FDA FALCPA; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls — 21 CFR Part 117

description: Indicates whether wheat (FDA Big 9 allergen) is intentionally included as an ingredient. When True, wheat must be declared in the ingredient list and allergen statement per FALCPA.
reference: FDA FALCPA; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls — 21 CFR Part 117

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or market identifiers for which this label version is approved and valid (e.g., 'USA,CAN,MEX'). Supports multi-market label management and export compliance.
reference: COOL; Codex Alimentarius; GLOBALG.A.P.; FDA; USDA

description: File path, document management system reference, or URI pointing to the approved label artwork file (e.g., PDF, AI, EPS). Supports label artwork version control and print production workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; GS1 Label Standards

description: Total caloric value per serving as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel, expressed in kilocalories (kcal). Calculated from macronutrient composition per FDA rounding rules.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 2-1985

description: Country of origin declaration required under COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) regulations. Specifies the country or countries where the product was produced, grown, or processed. Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., 'USA', 'MEX', 'CAN').
reference: USDA COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) — 7 CFR Part 60 and 65; AMS

description: Timestamp when this label record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory record-keeping requirements.
reference: FDA FSMA; SOX; ISO 22000

description: Date on which this label version becomes commercially active and legally binding for product packaging and distribution. Aligns with regulatory approval and market launch timelines.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA AMS; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling)

description: Date on which this label version is no longer valid for use on new production runs. Null for open-ended approvals. Triggers label review and renewal workflows.
reference: USDA AMS Label Approval; FDA FSMA

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) Allergen Control Plan Reference
description: Reference identifier for the HACCP allergen control plan document governing allergen management for this product label. Links label allergen declarations to the facility's preventive controls documentation.
reference: FDA FSMA Preventive Controls — 21 CFR Part 117; HACCP; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO 22000

description: Full ingredient list as it appears on the label, listed in descending order of predominance by weight. Must comply with FDA 21 CFR Part 101.4 ingredient declaration requirements.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.4; Codex Alimentarius STAN 1-1985

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to this label version, used for cross-referencing with SAP QM label management and regulatory submission tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; USDA AMS Label Approval

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the label, typically reflecting the product name, market, and version (e.g., 'Organic Whole Wheat Flour — US Retail v3').
reference: USDA AMS; FDA FSMA

description: Current lifecycle state of the label record. Tracks progression from draft through regulatory approval to active use, supersession by a newer version, or withdrawal from market.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; USDA AMS Label Approval; FDA FSMA

description: Classification of the label by its intended commercial use channel: retail consumer packaging, foodservice bulk, export market, private label, or ingredient/intermediate product.
reference: FDA FSMA; USDA AMS

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this label record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and downstream data synchronization.
reference: FDA FSMA; SOX; SAP S/4HANA QM Module

description: Indicates whether this label's associated product has been verified to comply with applicable Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) thresholds for pesticide and agrochemical residues as set by EPA, Codex Alimentarius, or destination market regulators.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius MRL Database; FDA Pesticide Residue Monitoring Program

description: Mandatory net weight or net quantity statement as it appears on the label (e.g., '5 LB (2.27 kg)', '16 FL OZ (473 mL)'). Must comply with NIST Handbook 133 and FDA net quantity of contents regulations.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.105; NIST Handbook 133; Fair Packaging and Labeling Act

description: Method used to determine the nutrition facts panel values: laboratory analysis (wet chemistry), nutrient database calculation (USDA FoodData Central), recipe calculation, or a combination. Affects accuracy and regulatory defensibility.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; USDA FoodData Central; AOAC International

description: Indicates whether this label is authorized to display the USDA Organic seal under the National Organic Program (NOP). True only when the product and operation hold valid NOP certification.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) — 7 CFR Part 205; ISO 22000

description: 4 or 5-digit Price Look-Up (PLU) code used for fresh produce identification at retail point-of-sale. Assigned by the International Federation for Produce Standards (IFPS). Applicable to fresh fruit, vegetable, and herb products.
reference: IFPS PLU Code Standard; USDA AMS

description: Protein content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Mandatory declaration on FDA Nutrition Facts label.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9

description: Official reference number or tracking code issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., USDA AMS label approval number, FDA submission reference) confirming this label has been reviewed and authorized.
reference: USDA AMS; FDA FSMA; FSIS

description: Serving size as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel (e.g., '1 cup (240g)', '2 tablespoons (32g)'). Must align with FDA Reference Amounts Customarily Consumed (RACC) for the product category.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; FDA RACC Table

description: Number of servings per container as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. May be a whole number or decimal (e.g., 2.5 servings). Required for FDA-compliant Nutrition Facts labeling.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9

description: Sodium content per serving in milligrams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Key nutrient for dietary guidance and FDA mandatory declaration.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9

description: Total carbohydrate content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel, including dietary fiber and total sugars.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9

description: Total fat content per serving in grams as declared on the Nutrition Facts panel. Includes saturated fat, trans fat, polyunsaturated fat, and monounsaturated fat.
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 101.9; Codex Alimentarius

description: 12-digit Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode number printed on the label for retail point-of-sale scanning and supply chain identification. Assigned by GS1 and linked to the product SKU.
reference: GS1 US; FDA FSMA; USDA AMS

business_glossary_term: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) Label Approval Status
description: Status of the label submission and approval with the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), required for meat, poultry, and egg products. Tracks submission through final approval or rejection.
reference: USDA AMS Label Approval Program; FSIS

description: Indicates whether this label is authorized to display a USDA grade shield (e.g., USDA Grade A, USDA Choice). Requires grading certificate from USDA AMS grading service.
reference: USDA AMS Grading and Certification Programs

description: Version identifier for this label iteration (e.g., '1.0', '2.3'). Incremented whenever label content, claims, or regulatory data changes. Supports label version history and change control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM Module; ISO 22000; FDA FSMA Preventive Controls

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each MRL threshold record in the regulatory reference catalog. Primary key for this table.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius CAC/MRL

business_glossary_term: Active Ingredient Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) Registry Number
description: CAS Registry Number uniquely identifying the pesticide active ingredient chemical substance. Enables unambiguous cross-referencing across regulatory databases and SDS documentation.
reference: Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS); EPA Pesticide Chemical Search

description: Common or IUPAC chemical name of the pesticide active ingredient (AI) for which the MRL is established. Used to cross-reference against agrochemical product labels and COA verification.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius CAC/MRL; ISO 1750 Pesticide Common Names

description: Reference to the validated analytical method (e.g., AOAC, EN, EPA method number) used for laboratory detection and quantification of the residue to verify compliance with this MRL. Supports COA and laboratory accreditation alignment.
reference: AOAC International Official Methods; EPA Pesticide Analytical Methods; EU SANTE/11312/2021 Analytical Quality Control

description: Method of pesticide application for which this MRL tolerance is established (e.g., foliar spray, soil application, seed treatment, post-harvest treatment, fumigation). Affects residue behavior and PHI calculation.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) Standard

description: Official Codex Alimentarius MRL reference number (CXL number) for this tolerance, enabling direct cross-reference to the Codex MRL database. Used for international trade documentation and export COA verification.
reference: Codex Alimentarius Commission CAC/MRL Database; Codex Alimentarius Procedural Manual

description: Broader commodity group classification under which the specific commodity falls (e.g., cereal grains, fruiting vegetables, root and tuber vegetables, meat and poultry). Supports group tolerance application per EPA and Codex rules.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180 Subpart D; Codex Alimentarius CAC/MISC 4 Commodity Classification

description: Specific part of the commodity to which the MRL applies (e.g., whole commodity, edible portion only, peel, grain, muscle tissue, fat, liver, milk, eggs). Relevant for processed commodity tolerances and animal product MRLs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_commodity|edible_portion|peel|seed|grain|muscle|fat|liver|kidney|milk|eggs — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Codex Alimentarius CAC/MISC 4 Commodity Classification; EPA 40 CFR Part 180; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 Annex I

description: Timestamp when this MRL threshold record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking per Databricks Lakehouse governance standards.
reference: ISO 22000:2018 Food Safety Management; FSMA Records Requirements 21 CFR Part 112; SOX Section 404

description: Originating regulatory database or system from which this MRL record was sourced (e.g., EPA Office of Pesticide Programs tolerance database, Codex MRL database, EU Pesticides database). Supports data lineage and refresh cycle management.
reference: EPA OPP Tolerance Database; Codex Alimentarius MRL Database; EU Pesticides Database (ec.europa.eu/food/plant/pesticides)

description: Indicates whether this MRL is a default/general tolerance applied in the absence of a specific established tolerance (e.g., Codex default of 0.01 ppm, EU default of 0.01 ppm under Regulation EC 396/2005). True = default MRL applies; False = specific established tolerance.
reference: EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 Article 18(1)(b); Codex Alimentarius CAC/MRL Default

description: Date on which this MRL tolerance became legally effective and enforceable by the regulatory jurisdiction. Used to determine applicability for current crop season compliance and PHI scheduling.
reference: EPA Federal Register Final Rule; Codex Alimentarius Step 8 Adoption; EU Official Journal Publication

description: EPA pesticide registration number associated with the active ingredient or product formulation. Links MRL tolerance to the registered pesticide product for PHI compliance and export documentation.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 3; 40 CFR Part 152

description: Regulatory justification for a tolerance exemption, applicable when tolerance_type is 'exemption'. Describes why the active ingredient is exempt from MRL requirements (e.g., naturally occurring substance, minimal risk, biochemical pesticide). Null for non-exemption tolerance types.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180.910-180.960 Exemptions; FIFRA Section 25(b)

description: Date on which this MRL tolerance expires or is scheduled for revocation. Null for indefinite tolerances. Critical for time-limited emergency exemptions (Section 18) and temporary import tolerances.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 18 Emergency Exemption; 40 CFR Part 180.470; Codex Alimentarius Procedural Manual

description: U.S. Federal Register citation (volume, page number, and date) for the EPA final rule establishing or amending this tolerance. Provides regulatory traceability for audit and legal compliance purposes.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; U.S. Federal Register; Administrative Procedure Act

description: Indicates whether this MRL tolerance specifically applies to or was established in the context of GMO crop varieties (e.g., herbicide-tolerant GMO crops with higher herbicide residue tolerances). True = GMO-specific tolerance; False = applies to conventional and GMO equally.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; USDA APHIS Biotechnology Regulatory Services; EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003

description: Indicates whether this MRL was established specifically as an import tolerance to facilitate trade for commodities treated with a pesticide not registered domestically. True = import tolerance; False = domestic tolerance. Relevant for FOB/CIF export documentation.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 408(e); 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius Import Tolerance Procedures

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the importing or regulating country associated with this MRL. Used for export eligibility checks and Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance. Null for multi-country jurisdictions (e.g., EU, Codex).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; USDA Foreign Agricultural Service (FAS) Export Requirements; COOL 7 USC 1638

description: Date on which this MRL record was last reviewed and confirmed against the authoritative regulatory source database. Supports data quality management and ensures currency of compliance reference data.
reference: FSMA Preventive Controls Rule 21 CFR Part 117; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements; ISO 22000:2018 Food Safety Management

description: Timestamp when this MRL threshold record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data refresh, change detection, and regulatory data currency validation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: ISO 22000:2018 Food Safety Management; FSMA Records Requirements 21 CFR Part 112; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: Limit of Quantification (LOQ) in Parts Per Million (ppm)
description: Lowest concentration of the active ingredient residue that can be reliably quantified by the validated analytical method. Used to determine whether a non-detect result constitutes MRL compliance, particularly for default MRL tolerances.
reference: EU SANTE/11312/2021 Analytical Quality Control Guidelines; AOAC International; EPA Pesticide Analytical Methods

description: IRAC (Insecticide Resistance Action Committee), FRAC (Fungicide Resistance Action Committee), or HRAC (Herbicide Resistance Action Committee) mode of action classification code for the active ingredient. Supports IPM resistance management planning.
reference: IRAC Mode of Action Classification; FRAC Code List; HRAC Classification; EPA Pesticide Registration

business_glossary_term: Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) Value in Parts Per Million (ppm)
description: The established maximum residue limit expressed in parts per million (ppm, equivalent to mg/kg) for the active ingredient on the specified commodity. Core compliance threshold used for COA verification, pre-harvest interval enforcement, and export eligibility determination.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius MRL Database (CXL); EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005

description: Free-text field for additional regulatory notes, conditions, or qualifications associated with this MRL tolerance (e.g., specific use restrictions, footnotes from regulatory publications, pending review status, or special conditions for application).
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius CAC/MRL; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005

description: Indicates whether the active ingredient is approved for use in certified organic production under USDA National Organic Program (NOP) standards. True = approved for organic use; False = not approved. Supports organic product line compliance and NOP certification maintenance.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205; OMRI Listed Materials

description: Minimum number of days required between the last pesticide application and harvest to ensure residues do not exceed the MRL at time of harvest. Critical for crop protection scheduling and FSMA compliance. Sourced from EPA label requirements.
reference: EPA FIFRA Label Requirements; FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR Part 112; GLOBALG.A.P. Crop Protection Module

description: Ratio of residue concentration in the processed commodity to residue concentration in the raw agricultural commodity (RAC). Used to calculate MRL compliance for processed food products derived from the raw commodity. Values >1 indicate concentration; values <1 indicate reduction.
reference: Codex Alimentarius Guidelines on Pesticide Residues in Processed Foods (CAC/GL 79); EPA 40 CFR Part 180

description: The regulatory authority or jurisdiction that established this MRL (e.g., US_EPA for United States Environmental Protection Agency, EU for European Union Regulation EC 396/2005, CODEX for Codex Alimentarius Commission). Critical for export documentation and multi-market compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: US_EPA|EU|CODEX|CANADA|JAPAN|AUSTRALIA|CHINA|BRAZIL|MEXICO|KOREA|INDIA|TAIWAN — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005; Codex Alimentarius CAC/MRL; GLOBALG.A.P. MRL Monitoring

description: Regulatory definition of what constitutes the measurable residue for enforcement purposes, including parent compound and relevant metabolites or degradation products. Critical for laboratory COA analysis method alignment.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180 Tolerance Definitions; Codex Alimentarius Residue Definition Guidelines; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 Annex I

description: Date on which a previously established MRL tolerance was formally revoked or cancelled by the regulatory authority. Null for active tolerances. Supports historical compliance audit trails and COA re-verification.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180 Revocation Notices; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 Amendments

description: Native record identifier from the originating regulatory database (e.g., EPA tolerance petition number, Codex CXL reference, EU database entry ID). Enables traceability back to the authoritative source system for audit and refresh purposes.
reference: EPA OPP Tolerance Database; Codex Alimentarius MRL Database; EU Pesticides Database

description: Current lifecycle status of the MRL tolerance record: 'active' for currently enforceable tolerances, 'revoked' for cancelled tolerances, 'expired' for time-limited tolerances past their expiry date, 'pending' for proposed tolerances under regulatory review, 'suspended' for temporarily suspended tolerances.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 180; Codex Alimentarius Procedural Manual; EU Regulation (EC) No 396/2005

description: Classification of the MRL tolerance type: 'established' for standard EPA/Codex tolerances, 'exemption' for active ingredients exempt from tolerance requirements, 'import_tolerance' for tolerances set specifically for imported commodities, 'emergency_exemption' for Section 18 emergency use tolerances, 'group_tolerance' for tolerances covering a commodity group.
reference: EPA FIFRA Section 18; 40 CFR Part 180.1; Codex Alimentarius Procedural Manual

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each organic certification record. Primary key for the organic_certification data product.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP) 7 CFR Part 205

description: Reference to the agricultural product or commodity in the product catalog that is covered under this organic certification. Links the certification record to the specific sellable or transferable item for organic labeling and premium pricing eligibility.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.303; USDA Commodity Codes

description: Document reference or identifier for the approved materials list associated with this certification. Lists all inputs (fertilizers, pesticides, processing aids) approved for use under the certified organic system plan, cross-referenced against the USDA National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.601-205.606; OMRI (Organic Materials Review Institute) Listed Products

description: Tracks the current annual renewal status of the organic certification. Renewed: successfully renewed for the current certification year. Pending_renewal: renewal application submitted and under review. Not_renewed: operation chose not to renew. Overdue: renewal deadline passed without submission.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406(b); USDA NOP Annual Update Requirements

description: File path, document management system reference, or URL pointing to the scanned or digital copy of the official organic certificate issued by the certifying agent. Supports audit trail, labeling compliance verification, and customer COA (Certificate of Analysis) requests.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; ISO 22000 Document Control

description: Externally-issued certificate number assigned by the accredited certifying agent. This is the official document reference used in regulatory filings, labeling claims, and audit trails under USDA NOP requirements.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; GLOBALG.A.P. Organic Certification

description: The operational scope covered by this organic certificate as defined by USDA NOP. Valid values: crops (field and greenhouse crop production), livestock (animal husbandry), handling_processing (post-harvest handling and processing operations), wild_crops (wild-harvested plant materials), aquatic_animals (aquatic animal production). Determines which products and operations are covered under this certificate.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.201; USDA NOP Accreditation Program

description: Current lifecycle status of the organic certification. Active: certificate is valid and in good standing. Suspended: temporarily halted pending investigation or corrective action. Revoked: permanently cancelled due to violation. Pending: application submitted, awaiting certifying agent approval. Expired: past expiration date without renewal. Withdrawn: voluntarily relinquished by the operation.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662; USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.665

description: Total land area in acres covered under this organic certification. Represents the certified organic production footprint and is used for yield planning, premium pricing calculations, and regulatory reporting to USDA.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.201; USDA NASS Organic Survey

description: The official USDA-assigned accreditation code or identifier for the certifying agent. Used for regulatory cross-referencing with USDA AMS accreditation records and electronic filing systems.
reference: USDA AMS National Organic Program Accreditation; USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.500

description: The name of the USDA-accredited certifying agent (ACA) that issued and administers this organic certificate. Certifying agents are third-party organizations accredited by the USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) to certify organic operations.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.501; USDA AMS Accredited Certifying Agents List

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the organic certification record supports Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) compliance requirements. COOL mandates disclosure of the country of origin for covered commodities at retail, and organic products must meet both NOP and COOL labeling requirements.
reference: USDA COOL 7 CFR Part 60 and 65; Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the certified organic product was produced or primarily grown. Required for COOL compliance and international organic equivalency agreements.
reference: USDA COOL 7 CFR Part 60; ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 2-1985

description: The timestamp when this organic certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance record-keeping in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 22000 Record Control; GDPR Article 5(e)

description: The date on which the organic certification becomes legally valid and binding. Marks the start of the certified period during which organic labeling claims and premium pricing are authorized.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406(a)

description: Identifies the specific international organic standard or equivalency arrangement recognized by this certification (e.g., EU Organic, Canada Organic, Japan JAS Organic, IFOAM). Populated only when international_equivalency_flag is True.
reference: USDA NOP International Organic Equivalency Arrangements; IFOAM Organic Standards

description: The date on which the organic certification expires and must be renewed to maintain organic status. Organic certificates are typically issued on an annual basis. Expiration triggers renewal workflow and may affect organic premium pricing eligibility.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406(b); USDA NOP Annual Renewal Requirements

business_glossary_term: Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the certified organic operation is also compliant with applicable Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) rules, including the Produce Safety Rule and Preventive Controls for Human Food. Organic certification does not automatically confer FSMA compliance.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 112; FDA FSMA Produce Safety Rule

description: Indicates whether the operation holds concurrent GLOBALG.A.P. (Global Good Agricultural Practices) certification in addition to USDA NOP organic certification. GLOBALG.A.P. certification is often required by major retail buyers and export markets.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) Standard; GFSI Benchmarking

description: Indicates whether the certified operation and its products are verified as free from Genetically Modified Organisms (GMOs). USDA NOP prohibits the use of excluded methods including genetic engineering; this flag confirms GMO-free status as part of organic compliance.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.105(e); Non-GMO Project Standard

description: Indicates whether this certification is recognized under an international organic equivalency arrangement (e.g., USDA-EU Organic Equivalency Agreement, USDA-Canada Organic Equivalency Arrangement). Enables certified organic products to be sold as organic in partner countries without additional certification.
reference: USDA NOP International Trade; EU Organic Regulation 2018/848; Canada Organic Regime

description: Date of the most recent on-site inspection conducted by the certifying agent at the certified operation. Annual on-site inspections are required under USDA NOP to verify compliance with organic standards.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.403; USDA NOP On-Site Inspection Requirements

description: The timestamp when this organic certification record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to certification status, renewal events, suspension/revocation actions, and document updates for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: SOX Section 404; ISO 22000 Record Control; GDPR Article 5(e)

description: Scheduled date for the next on-site inspection by the certifying agent. Used for compliance planning and operational readiness to ensure the operation is prepared for the annual NOP inspection.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.403

business_glossary_term: National Organic Program (NOP) Organic System Plan Reference
description: Document reference number or identifier for the Organic System Plan (OSP) submitted to and approved by the certifying agent. The OSP is the foundational compliance document describing all practices, materials, and monitoring activities for the certified operation.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.201; USDA NOP Organic System Plan Requirements

description: Free-text field for additional remarks, conditions, or special provisions associated with this organic certification record. May include certifying agent conditions, corrective action requirements, or operational notes relevant to compliance management.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; ISO 22000 Food Safety Management

description: The legal name of the certified organic operation (farm, ranch, or handling facility) as it appears on the organic certificate. This is the entity name recognized by the certifying agent and USDA NOP for compliance purposes.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.401; USDA NOP Organic System Plan

description: Classification of the certified organic operation type. Producer: farms and ranches growing organic crops or raising organic livestock. Handler: facilities processing or packaging organic products. Broker: entities buying and selling organic products. Importer: entities importing certified organic products. Distributor: entities distributing certified organic products.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.2; USDA NOP Definitions

description: The USDA NOP-defined organic labeling category authorized for products under this certification. 100_percent_organic: all ingredients are certified organic. Organic: at least 95% certified organic ingredients. Made_with_organic: 70-95% certified organic ingredients. Organic_ingredients: less than 70% organic, may list specific organic ingredients. Determines permissible label claims and USDA Organic Seal usage.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.301-205.310; USDA Organic Seal Usage Guidelines

description: Indicates whether products covered by this certification are currently eligible for organic premium pricing in sales and distribution channels. Eligibility requires active certification status and valid organic label type authorization.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.300; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service Organic Price Reporting

description: The deadline by which the annual renewal application and supporting documentation must be submitted to the certifying agent to maintain uninterrupted organic certification status.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406(b)

description: The date on which the organic certification was permanently revoked by the certifying agent. Populated only when certification_status is 'revoked'. Revocation is the most severe enforcement action and prohibits the operation from making organic claims.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.665

description: Narrative description of the reason for permanent revocation of the organic certification. Documents the specific violation or fraudulent activity that resulted in the certifying agent revoking the certificate.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.665; USDA NOP Adverse Action Procedures

description: The date on which the organic certification was suspended by the certifying agent. Populated only when certification_status is 'suspended'. Suspension may result from non-compliance findings, use of prohibited substances, or failure to submit required documentation.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662

description: Narrative description of the reason for suspension of the organic certification. Documents the specific non-compliance finding or violation that triggered the suspension action by the certifying agent.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662; USDA NOP Adverse Action Procedures

description: Indicates whether the mandatory 36-month organic transition period has been completed for the land or operation covered by this certification. True indicates the operation has fulfilled the transition requirement and is eligible for full organic certification.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.202(b)

description: The date on which the operation began the mandatory 36-month transition period required before land can be certified organic under USDA NOP. During transition, no prohibited substances may be applied to the land.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.202(b); USDA NOP Transition Requirements

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each product certification record in the agricultural certification catalog.
reference: Internal Data Standard

description: APHIS petition number or unique transformation event identifier assigned to a specific genetically modified organism event (e.g., MON810, GTS 40-3-2). Required for USDA Bioengineered (BE) food disclosure and export market approval documentation.
reference: USDA APHIS Petition Process 7 CFR Part 340; USDA BE Disclosure Standard 7 CFR Part 66; Codex Alimentarius CAC/GL 45-2003

description: Reference to the agricultural product or commodity in the product master catalog to which this certification applies.
reference: Internal Data Standard

description: Foreign key linking to product.organic_certification. Business justification: certification is the consolidated SSOT for all third-party and regulatory certifications. When a certification record represents an NOP organic certification, it should reference the detailed organic_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference identifier or document URL for the USDA NOP National List of Allowed and Prohibited Substances applicable to this organic certification scope. Ensures that only approved inputs are used in certified organic production.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.600-205.606; OMRI (Organic Materials Review Institute) Listed Products

description: Comma-separated list of ISO 3-letter country codes representing jurisdictions where this GMO transformation event or certification has received regulatory approval for commercial use or import (e.g., USA, CAN, BRA, CHN, EU member states). Critical for export documentation and market access decisions.
reference: USDA APHIS; EU Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003; Codex Alimentarius; COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) 7 USC 1638

description: Frequency of the required audit or inspection cycle expressed in months (e.g., 12 for annual, 6 for semi-annual). Determines the recurrence interval for compliance audits under the certification program.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.403; GLOBALG.A.P. General Regulations; SQF Code; BRC Global Standard

description: Indicates whether the product is subject to USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard (NBFDS) labeling requirements. True if the product contains detectable modified genetic material from the USDA BE disclosure list.
reference: USDA BE Disclosure Standard 7 CFR Part 66; National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Law (NBFDL) Public Law 114-216

description: BRC Global Standard audit grade awarded at the most recent certification audit (AA, A, B, C, or D), where AA is the highest achievable grade for unannounced audits. Null if certification_type is not BRC.
reference: BRC Global Standard for Food Safety Issue 9; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: File path, URL, or document management system reference to the scanned or digital copy of the official certificate document. Supports audit readiness, export documentation, and retail label claim substantiation.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; FDA FSMA Records Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.; BRC Global Standard

description: Externally-issued unique certificate number assigned by the certifying body (e.g., USDA NOP organic certificate number, GLOBALG.A.P. GGN, SQF certificate number). Used for audit trails, export documentation, and retail label claims.
reference: USDA National Organic Program (NOP); GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF Institute; BRC Global Standards

description: Operational scope covered by the certification as defined by the certifying body. For USDA NOP organic, scope is one of: crops, livestock, handling (processing), or wild crop. For food safety certifications, scope may reference specific product categories or processing operations.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.201; GLOBALG.A.P. Scope Documents

description: Current lifecycle state of the certification. Active indicates the certificate is valid and in force; suspended indicates a temporary hold pending investigation; revoked indicates permanent cancellation by the certifying body; expired indicates the certificate has passed its expiration date without renewal.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662; GLOBALG.A.P. General Regulations; FDA FSMA

description: Category of certification held by the product. Covers USDA NOP organic, USDA Bioengineered (BE) food declaration, Non-GMO Project Verified, GLOBALG.A.P., SQF, BRC, and others. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic_nop|gmo_be_declaration|non_gmo_verified|globalg_ap|sqf|brc|ifs|fair_trade|rainforest_alliance|kosher|halal|iscc — promote to reference product]
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205; USDA BE Disclosure Standard 7 CFR Part 66; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF Institute; BRC Global Standards; IFS; Fair Trade USA; Rainforest Alliance; ISCC

description: Accreditation number assigned to the certifying body by the relevant oversight authority (e.g., USDA NOP accreditation number for organic certifying agents, GFSI accreditation reference). Validates the legitimacy of the certifying organization.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.501; ISO/IEC 17011

description: Name of the accredited third-party organization or regulatory agency that issued the certification (e.g., Oregon Tilth, CCOF, NSF International, SGS, Bureau Veritas, Non-GMO Project, SQF Institute). Required for audit and export documentation.
reference: USDA NOP Accredited Certifying Agents List; GFSI; GLOBALG.A.P.; ISO/IEC 17065

description: Reference identifier linking to the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document associated with this certification, containing laboratory test results for residues, contaminants, or identity preservation verification.
reference: FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 117; USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.670; Codex Alimentarius General Standard for Contaminants

business_glossary_term: Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Country Code
description: ISO 3-letter country code indicating the country of origin of the certified product as required under USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) regulations. Used for retail label compliance and export documentation.
reference: USDA COOL 7 USC 1638; AMS COOL Regulations 7 CFR Part 60 and 65

description: Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ISO 8601; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Date from which the certification is operationally effective and the product may be marketed under the certified claim. May differ from issue_date if there is a transition or grace period.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.400; FDA FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Date on which the certification expires and must be renewed to maintain certified status. Critical for organic premium pricing, non-GMO market access, and retail label claims. Triggers renewal workflow when approaching.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; GLOBALG.A.P. General Regulations; SQF Code

description: Unique producer or cooperative code assigned by Fair Trade USA or Fairtrade International to identify the certified producer organization associated with this product's fair trade certification.
reference: Fair Trade USA Certification Standards; Fairtrade International Standard

description: 13-digit GLOBALG.A.P. Number (GGN) assigned to the certified producer or production site under the GLOBALG.A.P. certification scheme. Used for supply chain traceability and retail buyer verification via the GLOBALG.A.P. database.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. General Regulations Part I; GLOBALG.A.P. Integrated Farm Assurance (IFA) Standard

description: Name of the recognized halal certifying organization (e.g., IFANCA, ISWA, HFA) that issued the halal certification for this product. Null if certification_type is not halal.
reference: IFANCA Halal Standards; OIC/SMIIC 1:2019 General Guidelines for Halal Food

description: Reference code or document identifier for the Identity Preservation (IP) protocol applied to maintain non-GMO or specialty crop segregation integrity throughout the supply chain. Required for Non-GMO Project Verified and identity-preserved commodity programs.
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS) IP Guidelines; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Date on which the certifying body formally issued the certificate. Marks the start of the certification's legal validity and is printed on the certificate document.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; GLOBALG.A.P. General Regulations

description: Name of the recognized kosher certifying agency (e.g., OU, OK, KOF-K, Star-K) that issued the kosher certification for this product. Null if certification_type is not kosher.
reference: Orthodox Union (OU) Kosher Standards; Star-K Kosher Certification

description: Approved marketing claim text authorized by this certification for use on product packaging and retail labels (e.g., 'USDA Organic', 'Non-GMO Project Verified', 'Certified Kosher', 'Fair Trade Certified'). Must align with certifying body approved language.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.303; USDA BE Disclosure Standard; FTC Green Guides 16 CFR Part 260; FDA Labeling Regulations 21 CFR Part 101

description: Date of the most recent on-site inspection or audit conducted by the certifying body or accredited auditor. Used to track audit cycle compliance and readiness for renewal.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.403; GLOBALG.A.P. Inspection Protocol; SQF Code Edition 9; BRC Global Standard Issue 9

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record. Used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: ISO 8601; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Date of the most recent laboratory testing, residue verification, or document review performed to confirm ongoing compliance with certification requirements (e.g., MRL residue testing for organic, identity preservation testing for non-GMO).
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.670; EPA MRL Regulations; GLOBALG.A.P. Residue Monitoring

description: Date on which the next scheduled inspection or audit by the certifying body is planned. Supports audit cycle planning and operational readiness.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.403; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF Code; BRC Global Standard

description: Indicates whether the product has received Non-GMO Project Verified status, entitling it to display the Non-GMO Project butterfly seal on retail packaging. Supports non-GMO market access and premium pricing.
reference: Non-GMO Project Standard Version 15; USDA BE Disclosure Standard

description: Indicates whether the product is in the USDA NOP organic transition period (36-month land transition requirement) rather than fully certified organic. Transition products cannot be labeled or sold as certified organic.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.202; National Organic Program

description: Date on which the annual or periodic renewal of the certification was last completed or is next due. Supports proactive renewal management and continuity of certified product claims.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.406; GLOBALG.A.P. Annual Renewal Requirements

description: Date on which the certifying body formally revoked the certification. Populated only when certification_status is 'revoked'. Required for regulatory reporting and product recall risk management.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662; FDA FSMA 21 CFR Part 1; GLOBALG.A.P. Sanctioning Procedure

description: SQF certification level achieved: Level 1 (food safety fundamentals), Level 2 (HACCP-based food safety plan), or Level 3 (comprehensive food safety and quality management system). Null if certification_type is not SQF.
reference: SQF Code Edition 9; GFSI Benchmarking Requirements

description: Textual description of the reason for suspension of the certification by the certifying body (e.g., prohibited substance detected, recordkeeping violation, failure to allow inspection). Populated only when certification_status is 'suspended'.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.662; GLOBALG.A.P. Sanctioning Procedure

description: Date on which the 36-month USDA NOP organic transition period began for the associated land or product. Null if organic_transition_flag is false. Used to calculate transition completion eligibility.
reference: USDA NOP 7 CFR Part 205.202

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
