description: Physical quantity associated with this line item posting (e.g., bushels of grain, metric tons of fertilizer, hundredweight of livestock). Supports quantity-based COGS allocation and commodity volume reconciliation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE role LINE_QUANTITY category requirement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Module; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; GAAP ASC 330

description: Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., BU=Bushel, MT=Metric Ton, CWT=Hundredweight, KG=Kilogram). Aligns with USDA standard agricultural units and SAP base unit of measure configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BU|MT|CWT|KG|LB|EA|L|GAL|TON — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; USDA Agricultural Marketing Service; ISO 80000-1

description: External reference document number associated with this line item (e.g., vendor invoice number, purchase order number, GRN number, sales order number). Enables cross-system traceability between SAP FI and source operational documents.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; SOX Section 302/404; FSMA Traceability Rule

description: Document number of the reversing journal entry that cancelled this line item. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Provides the audit chain link required for SOX Section 302/404 compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 250

description: Flag indicating whether this line item has been reversed by a subsequent correcting journal entry. True if reversed; False if original and active. Supports SOX audit trail integrity and financial restatement tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 250

description: Reporting segment identifier for this posting, enabling disaggregated financial disclosures by operating segment (e.g., Crop Production, Livestock, Agri-Supply Chain). Required for IFRS 8 and GAAP ASC 280 segment reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IFRS 8; GAAP ASC 280; SOX Section 302/404

description: Identifier of the originating system that generated this journal entry line (e.g., SAP_FI=SAP S/4HANA FI, NETSUITE=Oracle NetSuite, GRANULAR=Granular Farm Management, MANUAL=manual entry, INTERFACE=automated interface). Supports data lineage and SOX IT general controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite; Granular Farm Management; GAAP ASC 230

description: SAP Special GL indicator for non-standard postings such as down payments, guarantees, or bill of exchange transactions. Used in agricultural advance payment scenarios (e.g., prepaid seed contracts, crop advance payments).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 37

description: Calculated tax amount in company code currency for this line item based on the assigned tax code. Used for VAT/GST reporting and tax liability reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740

description: Tax code assigned to this line item, determining the applicable tax rate and tax account for VAT, GST, or sales tax calculations. Critical for tax compliance in multi-jurisdiction agricultural operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 12; GAAP ASC 740; EPA/USDA tax compliance

description: Monetary amount of this line item expressed in the originating transaction currency. Represents the raw posting value before currency translation. Satisfies TRANSACTION_LINE role LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category requirement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830; SOX Section 302/404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the originating transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL). Used for multi-currency agricultural commodity trading and international supply chain transactions.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The bank value date for this posting, representing when funds are available or debited at the bank. Used for cash management, bank reconciliation, and liquidity planning in agricultural treasury operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 7; GAAP ASC 230

description: Primary key for period_close
Reference to the employee or manager authorized to sign off and approve completion of this period close task. Critical for SOX segregation of duties and financial controls.
reference: SOX; GAAP; SAP SuccessFactors

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Ag period-close tasks include commodity inventory valuation (LIFO/FIFO/average cost), hedge mark-to-market postings, and grain settlement reconciliation — all commodity-specific. Linking commodity_id
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the legal entity or company code within the agricultural enterprise for which this period close task applies. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA company code configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Reference to the cost center responsible for this close task, enabling OPEX and CAPEX tracking by organizational unit within the agricultural enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP

description: Reference to another period close task that must be completed before this task can begin. Enables sequencing and dependency management within the close calendar workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX

description: Reference to the employee or user responsible for executing and completing this period close task. Supports SOX accountability and segregation of duties controls.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors; SOX; GAAP

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Period-end close tasks in agricultural enterprises are often scoped to specific profit centers (e.g., crop segment close, livestock segment close). Linking period_close.profit_center_id → finance.prof
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The actual financial value posted or recorded as a result of completing this close task (e.g., actual accrual posted, actual depreciation run amount, actual inventory valuation). Compared against estimated amount for variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Full name of the approver who signs off on this period close task. Retained for audit trail and SOX control documentation.
reference: SOX; GAAP

description: Classification of the close period indicating whether this is a month-end, quarter-end, year-end, crop-year-end (agricultural fiscal cycle), or special period close. Crop-year-end is specific to agricultural enterprises following USDA crop year conventions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS; USDA crop year conventions

description: Externally-known business identifier for this period close task record, used for cross-system tracking and audit trail. Typically sourced from SAP S/4HANA or Oracle NetSuite close management workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite; SOX

description: Human-readable name or short description of the specific period close task, providing additional context beyond the task type code (e.g., 'Q3 Grain Inventory Mark-to-Market', 'Harvest Settlement Accruals Posting').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite

description: The specific type of period-end close activity being tracked. Examples include accruals posting, depreciation run, inventory valuation at harvest, intercompany reconciliation, grain inventory mark-to-market, COGS allocation by commodity, financial statement preparation, and tax provision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: accruals_posting|depreciation_run|inventory_valuation|intercompany_reconciliation|grain_mark_to_market|cogs_allocation|financial_statement_prep|tax_provision|bank_reconciliation|fixed_asset_close — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; Oracle NetSuite; GAAP; IFRS; SOX

description: The actual date on which this period close task was completed and marked as finished. Null if the task has not yet been completed. Used to calculate close cycle time and on-time performance KPIs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP

description: SAP S/4HANA controlling area code that defines the organizational unit for cost accounting and management reporting. Groups company codes for EBITDA and COGS reporting across the agricultural enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: The date and time when this period close task record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp for SOX compliance and data lineage.
reference: SOX; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial amounts associated with this close task (e.g., USD for US Dollar). Supports multi-currency consolidation for international agricultural operations.
reference: ISO 4217; GAAP; IFRS IAS 21

description: The scheduled deadline by which this period close task must be completed, as defined in the close calendar. Drives overdue detection and escalation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP

description: Indicates whether this period close task has been escalated due to being overdue, blocked, or requiring management intervention. True when escalated; False under normal processing.
reference: SOX; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: The estimated financial value associated with this close task (e.g., estimated accrual amount, estimated depreciation charge, estimated inventory valuation adjustment). Used for pre-close planning and variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: The fiscal period number (1–12 for monthly, 13–16 for special periods) within the fiscal year to which this close task applies. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA period configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: The fiscal year to which this period close task belongs. In agriculture, the fiscal year may align with the crop year (e.g., October–September) rather than the calendar year.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS; USDA crop year conventions

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) / International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Reporting Standard
description: The accounting standard under which this period close task is executed and reported. Supports dual GAAP/IFRS reporting for consolidated financial statements and regulatory filings.
reference: GAAP (FASB ASC); IFRS (IASB); SEC reporting requirements

description: The date on which financial entries associated with this close task are posted to the General Ledger (GL) in SAP S/4HANA. Critical for period-end cutoff compliance under GAAP and IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Indicates whether this close task is specifically associated with the agricultural harvest settlement process, which involves final grain pricing, deferred payment settlements, and crop year-end accruals. True for harvest settlement tasks; False otherwise.
reference: USDA grain settlement practices; GAAP ASC 330; IFRS IAS 41 Agriculture

description: Indicates whether this close task involves intercompany reconciliation or elimination entries between legal entities within the agricultural enterprise group. True for intercompany tasks; False for single-entity tasks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 810; IFRS IFRS 10

description: The date and time when this period close task record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP

description: The last calendar date of the financial period being closed. Defines the reporting window for this close cycle and aligns with GAAP/IFRS reporting timelines.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: The first calendar date of the financial period being closed. Defines the reporting window for this close cycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Indicates whether the fiscal posting period has been locked in SAP S/4HANA to prevent further journal entries after close completion. True when the period is locked; False when still open for posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP

description: The precise date and time when the approver formally signed off on this period close task. Provides an immutable audit timestamp for SOX compliance and financial controls documentation.
reference: SOX; GAAP; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: The operational system of record from which this period close task record originates. Supports data lineage tracking across SAP S/4HANA FI/CO, Oracle NetSuite, Granular Farm Management, or manual entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite; Granular Farm Management

description: The SOX internal control identifier or reference number associated with this period close task. Links the task to the enterprise's SOX control framework for financial reporting compliance and audit evidence.
reference: Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 302 and 404; PCAOB AS 2201

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by the task owner or approver regarding the execution of this period close task, including explanations for variances, exceptions, or issues encountered during the close process.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX audit documentation

description: Full name of the employee or user responsible for executing this period close task. Retained for audit trail and SOX control documentation purposes.
reference: SOX; GAAP; SAP SuccessFactors

description: Numeric ordering of this close task within the overall period close workflow, enabling sequenced execution and progress tracking across the close calendar.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite

description: Current workflow status of the period close task indicating its progress through the close cycle. Drives close calendar dashboards and SOX control monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX; GAAP

description: The difference between the estimated amount and the actual amount for this close task. Positive variance indicates actual exceeded estimate; negative indicates under-run. Used for close quality analysis and management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Primary key for profit_center

Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Agricultural profit centers are organized around commodity lines (corn, soybeans, wheat, livestock). Linking profit_center to commodity_id enables commodity-level P&L reporting, segment reporting by c
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the legal entity company code under which this profit center operates for financial accounting purposes.
Reference to the default cost center associated with this profit center for overhead allocation and cost tracking.
Reference to the parent profit center in the organizational hierarchy for roll-up reporting and consolidated financial analysis. Null for top-level profit centers.
User identifier of the person who created this profit center record, used for audit trail and accountability.
User identifier of the person who last modified this profit center record, used for audit trail and accountability.
Method used to allocate corporate overhead and shared service costs to this profit center for budgeting and variance analysis.
Actual date when this profit center was closed or deactivated, preventing further financial postings. Null if still active.
Business justification or reason for closing this profit center, used for audit trail and organizational change tracking.
Basis used to allocate cost of goods sold to this profit center for gross margin calculation and profitability analysis.
Indicates whether this profit center is included in consolidated financial statements and regulatory filings.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of operation for this profit center, used for tax jurisdiction and regulatory compliance.
Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the local operating currency for this profit center used in financial transactions and reporting.
Geographic region or territory where this profit center operates, used for regional profitability analysis and market segmentation.
Numeric level in the profit center organizational hierarchy, with 1 being the top consolidated level and higher numbers representing more granular operational units.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this profit center record, used for change tracking and audit compliance.
Free-form text field for additional business context, special instructions, or operational notes related to this profit center.
External business identifier code for the profit center used in financial reporting and consolidation. Typically alphanumeric format aligned with chart of accounts structure.
Full business name of the profit center used for identification in financial statements and management reports.
Current lifecycle status of the profit center indicating whether it is operational and available for financial postings.
Classification of the profit center by operational function within the agriculture business (e.g., crop production, livestock operations, processing facilities, distribution centers).
Accounting method used to recognize revenue for this profit center in accordance with GAAP/IFRS standards and agriculture industry practices.
Indicates whether this profit center represents a reportable segment under ASC 280 or IFRS 8 segment reporting requirements.
Abbreviated name or acronym for the profit center used in condensed reports and dashboards.
Indicates whether this profit center is within the scope of Sarbanes-Oxley Act financial controls and audit requirements.
Method used to price inter-company or inter-profit-center transactions for internal management reporting and tax compliance.
Date from which this profit center became active and available for financial postings and reporting.
Date until which this profit center remains active. Null indicates no planned end date. Used for historical analysis and period-based reporting.
Primary key for ap_payment

Internal identifier of the company's house bank account from which the payment was disbursed. References the SAP house bank/account ID configuration. Supports bank account-level cash position monitoring and liquidity management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI House Bank Configuration; SOX Section 302/404

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: ap_payment has company_code as a STRING. Payments are made by a specific legal entity. Normalizing to a proper FK ensures referential integrity and enables entity-level payment reporting and cash flow
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the cost center to which this payment is allocated for management accounting and CAPEX/OPEX tracking. Supports COGS allocation by commodity and crop enterprise budgeting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Reference to the primary cleared accounts payable invoice that this payment settles. Supports invoice-to-payment matching and reconciliation for agricultural input purchases.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: ap_payment has gl_account_code as a STRING. Payment transactions are posted to specific GL accounts (bank clearing accounts, AP accounts). Normalizing to a FK to gl_account links the payment to the au
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: AP payment disbursements generate GL journal entries (debit AP clearing, credit bank). Linking ap_payment.journal_entry_id → finance.journal_entry.journal_entry_id provides direct sub-ledger to GL tra
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the automated payment run batch that generated this disbursement. SAP F110 payment run identifier used to group payments executed in the same batch cycle, supporting seasonal payment cycles aligned with planting and harvest.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI F110 Payment Program; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: FK to procurement.procurement_goods_receipt
FK to procurement.purchase_order
Reference to the agricultural input supplier or service vendor receiving this payment. Links to the vendor master record for supplier identity, banking details, and payment terms.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/FI Vendor Master; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: User ID or name of the financial controller or AP manager who approved this payment for disbursement. Required for SOX dual-control and segregation of duties compliance. Supports payment authorization audit trail.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Authorization; GAAP Internal Controls

description: Date and time when the payment was formally approved by the authorized approver. Provides SOX-compliant audit trail for payment authorization and segregation of duties controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Workflow; GAAP Internal Controls

description: Date on which the bank confirmed the payment as cleared in the bank statement. Used for bank reconciliation, outstanding check tracking, and cash flow actuals reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Statement Processing; GAAP ASC 230

description: Status of the payment as reported by the bank clearing system. Indicates whether the payment has been confirmed as cleared by the bank, returned (e.g., invalid account), or rejected. Drives bank reconciliation and outstanding payment follow-up.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation; NACHA ACH Return Codes; Federal Reserve

description: Physical check number assigned when payment method is CHECK. Used for check register management, positive pay file submission to the bank, and outstanding check reconciliation. Null for electronic payment methods (ACH, WIRE, EFT).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Check Management; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when this payment record was first created in the financial system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP Internal Controls

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency of this payment (e.g., USD for domestic, EUR for European suppliers, BRL for Brazilian commodity purchases). Supports multi-currency AP management for global agricultural supply chain.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration; IFRS IAS 21

description: Early payment discount amount deducted from the invoice total at time of payment. Reflects early-pay discount optimization for agricultural input purchases (e.g., seed, fertilizer, crop protection chemicals). Reduces COGS and improves ROI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP; GAAP ASC 310

description: Foreign currency exchange rate applied at the time of payment to convert the transaction currency to the company code local currency (USD). Used for foreign currency gain/loss calculation and IFRS IAS 21 compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Fiscal accounting period (1-12 or 1-16 for special periods) within the fiscal year in which this payment is posted. Used for period-level cash flow reporting, EBITDA analysis, and month-end close reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; GAAP; IFRS IAS 1

description: Fiscal year in which this payment is recorded for financial reporting purposes. Agricultural businesses may use non-calendar fiscal years aligned with growing seasons. Required for period-end close, GAAP/IFRS consolidated statements, and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; GAAP; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: Number of individual invoices cleared by this single payment transaction. Supports partial vs. full payment analysis and multi-invoice payment run reconciliation for bulk agricultural input purchases.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: Payment amount converted to the company code local currency (USD) using the exchange_rate at time of payment. Used for consolidated financial statements, EBITDA reporting, and GAAP/IFRS compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: Actual net amount remitted to the vendor after deducting any early payment discount taken. Equals payment_amount minus discount_taken_amount. Represents the true cash disbursement recorded in the bank statement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Gross payment amount disbursed to the vendor in the transaction currency before any discount deduction. Represents the total cash outflow for this payment event. Used for cash flow management and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7; SOX Section 302/404

description: Reason code indicating why a payment is blocked from processing (e.g., invoice dispute, missing COA, quality hold, vendor master incomplete). Null when payment is not blocked. Supports AP workflow management and vendor dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Block Indicators; Oracle NetSuite AP; SOX Section 302/404

description: The actual business date on which the payment was issued or disbursed to the vendor. Used for cash flow reporting, early-pay discount optimization, and seasonal payment cycle analysis aligned with planting and harvest periods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Externally-known payment document number assigned by the financial system (SAP FI or Oracle NetSuite) at the time of payment posting. Used for audit trails, bank reconciliation, and vendor remittance communication.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP; SOX Section 302/404

description: The payment instrument used to disburse funds to the vendor. ACH (Automated Clearing House) is common for domestic agricultural input suppliers; WIRE for international commodity purchases; CHECK for smaller vendors. Supports payment method analysis and bank fee optimization.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Methods; NACHA ACH Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation J

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by AP staff regarding this payment transaction. May include vendor communication details, dispute context, seasonal payment cycle notes, or special handling instructions for agricultural input suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: External payment reference number transmitted to the bank and included in the remittance advice to the vendor. For ACH payments, this is the transaction reference in the NACHA file. Enables vendor-side payment matching and dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; NACHA ACH Rules; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction. Tracks progression from initiation through bank clearing or return/void. Supports cash flow management and bank reconciliation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP; SOX Section 302/404

description: Classification of the payment transaction type. Advance payments are common for seed and input purchases ahead of planting season. Partial payments occur for large equipment or land lease transactions. Supports cash flow forecasting and working capital analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP; GAAP ASC 210

description: The accounting posting date on which the payment is recorded in the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting and GAAP/IFRS period-end close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 302/404

description: Indicates whether a remittance advice notification has been sent to the vendor detailing the invoices cleared by this payment. Supports vendor communication workflows and dispute prevention for agricultural input suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: Date and time when the remittance advice was transmitted to the vendor. Provides audit trail for vendor communication and supports SLA tracking for payment notification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; Oracle NetSuite AP

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this payment record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and multi-system reconciliation between SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle NetSuite AP.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when this payment record was last modified in the financial system. Tracks changes to payment status, clearing date, or bank confirmation. Supports data lineage and SOX audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP Internal Controls

description: The bank value date on which funds are credited to the vendor's account. May differ from payment_date due to bank processing float. Critical for accurate cash position reporting and bank statement reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the bank account record in the enterprise data platform. Primary key for the bank_account data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — House Bank Account Master

description: Reference to the legal entity (company code) that owns this bank account, aligning with SAP S/4HANA FI company code structure for financial consolidation and GAAP/IFRS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Reference to the GL reconciliation account used to post bank transactions and reconcile the bank account balance in the general ledger. Critical for period-close and SOX controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 230

description: Self-referencing FK on bank_account (parent_bank_account_id)
The official account name as registered with the financial institution, typically the legal entity name or operating division name (e.g., 'AgriCorp Operating Account — Corn Division').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI House Bank Configuration; Oracle NetSuite Bank Account Master

description: The official bank account number assigned by the financial institution. Used for payment disbursements (input purchases, payroll), crop revenue deposits, and operating line of credit draws. Classified as restricted PCI-sensitive financial data.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; SOX Section 302; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Frozen accounts may indicate regulatory holds or fraud investigations. Closed accounts are retained for historical audit trails per SOX requirements.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; GAAP ASC 230

description: Classification of the bank account by its functional purpose within agricultural cash management. Operating accounts handle day-to-day input payments and crop revenue; escrow accounts hold seasonal advance payments; money market accounts manage short-term liquidity between planting and harvest cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI House Bank Account Type; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7

description: Funds immediately available for disbursement after deducting outstanding checks, pending ACH debits, and holds. Distinct from ledger balance; used for real-time cash availability decisions during peak input purchasing seasons.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; SAP S/4HANA Cash Management; Oracle NetSuite Cash Management

description: The date as of which the daily_balance was last recorded or confirmed. Ensures temporal accuracy of cash position reporting for period-close reconciliation and treasury management.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7; SAP S/4HANA Cash Management

description: Physical street address of the bank branch. Used for correspondence, check mailing, and regulatory documentation for USDA program payment accounts and Farm Credit lending relationships.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Master; USDA Farm Service Agency Banking Requirements

description: Name of the specific bank branch where the account is held. Relevant for agricultural enterprises with regional banking relationships tied to specific farm credit districts or local agricultural lending offices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Master Data; Farm Credit Administration Regulations

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the bank branch is domiciled (e.g., USA, CAN, BRA). Determines applicable banking regulations, currency controls, and SWIFT routing conventions for international agricultural trade settlements.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SWIFT Standards; IFRS IAS 21

description: Full legal name of the financial institution holding the account (e.g., 'Farm Credit Services of America', 'AgriBank FCB', 'Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'). Used for counterparty identification in payment processing and banking relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI House Bank Master; Oracle NetSuite Bank Master

description: Frequency at which bank statements are received and processed for reconciliation. Daily statements are typical for high-volume operating accounts during planting and harvest seasons; monthly for savings and escrow accounts.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; SAP S/4HANA Bank Statement Processing (T-Code FF_5); Oracle NetSuite Bank Reconciliation

description: Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the enterprise data platform. Supports audit trail requirements for SOX financial controls and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 302/404; GAAP Internal Controls; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Current outstanding drawn balance on the operating line of credit associated with this account. Tracks seasonal borrowing against the credit facility limit for input financing and working capital management.
reference: GAAP ASC 470; IFRS IAS 39; Farm Credit Administration Regulations

description: Maximum approved credit limit on the operating line of credit linked to this account. Agricultural enterprises rely on seasonal operating lines to finance seed, fertilizer (NPK), and crop protection inputs ahead of harvest revenue. Null for non-credit accounts.
reference: GAAP ASC 470; IFRS IAS 39; Farm Credit Administration Regulations; SAP S/4HANA Credit Management

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account's operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, BRL, CAD). Determines the base currency for all transactions posted to this account and drives foreign exchange revaluation in multi-currency agricultural operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP ASC 830; IFRS IAS 21

description: Most recent end-of-day ledger balance for the account as reported by the bank or treasury management system. Critical for seasonal cash flow monitoring during planting and harvest cycles, operating line of credit management, and daily liquidity reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 7; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA Cash Management Module

description: Indicates whether this account requires dual authorization (two signatories) for payment transactions above the single-signatory threshold. Enforces segregation of duties as a SOX internal control for high-value agricultural disbursements.

Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all payments in this run (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD). All payment amounts within a single run must be in the same currency.
System-generated error or exception message if the payment run encountered processing failures. Used for troubleshooting and operational support.
The precise date and time when the payment run was executed and payments were transmitted to the banking system or payment processor.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this payment run is assigned.
The fiscal year to which this payment run is assigned for financial reporting and budgeting purposes.
Indicates whether this payment run is part of a recurring schedule (e.g., weekly payroll, monthly rent). True if recurring, False if ad-hoc.
The date and time when this payment run record was last modified.
Internal notes or comments added by treasury or accounts payable staff regarding special handling, exceptions, or other operational details.
The payment instrument or mechanism used for disbursements in this run (e.g., Automated Clearing House (ACH), wire transfer, physical check, Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT)).
Priority level assigned to this payment run for processing and cash management purposes. Urgent payments may require expedited approval and same-day execution.
Free-text description providing additional context about the purpose or scope of this payment run (e.g., 'Weekly vendor payments for harvest supplies', 'Monthly grower settlements for corn crop').
Current lifecycle status of the payment run. Tracks progression from draft through approval, processing, and completion or cancellation.
Standard payment terms applied to this run (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Immediate). Inherited from vendor master data but captured here for reporting consistency.
The accounting date on which the payment run transactions are posted to the General Ledger (GL). Used for period-end close and financial reporting.
The date on which the payment run was reconciled with bank statements.
Indicates whether the payment run has been reconciled against bank statements and whether any discrepancies were identified.
Describes the recurrence schedule if is_recurring is True (e.g., 'Weekly on Friday', 'Monthly on 15th', 'Bi-weekly'). Null for ad-hoc runs.
Business identifier for the payment run, formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.
Classification of the payment run based on payee category. Determines routing, approval workflows, and compliance requirements.
The date on which payments in this run are scheduled to be executed. Used for cash flow planning and vendor communication.
The originating system or module that generated this payment run (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI, Oracle NetSuite AP, custom grower settlement system).
The sum of all deductions applied across payments in this run, including tax withholdings, advances, and other adjustments.
The sum of all gross payment amounts before any deductions, adjustments, or fees. Represents the total obligation to payees.
The sum of all net payment amounts after deductions. This is the actual cash outflow from the corporate bank account.
The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run.

reference: DOL Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) Section 7

description: The frequency at which the employee is paid. Determines pay period boundaries and is critical for H-2A three-fourths guarantee calculations and FLSA overtime compliance.
reference: DOL Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA); H-2A Program Regulations 20 CFR 655

description: The last calendar date of the payroll processing period. Defines the boundary for wage calculations, overtime determination, and H-2A three-fourths guarantee assessment.
reference: DOL Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA); H-2A Program Regulations 20 CFR 655

reference: DOL Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA); H-2A Program Regulations 20 CFR 655

description: The compensation structure applied to this payroll record. Piece-rate is common for harvest operations; hourly applies to FLSA overtime calculations; salary for permanent staff.

description: The actual date on which wages were disbursed to the employee. Required for DOL wage payment timeliness compliance and state wage payment law adherence.

reference: DOL Wage and Hour Division; State Wage Payment Laws; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Current workflow state of the payroll record within the SAP SuccessFactors payroll processing lifecycle, from initial calculation through final payment or reversal.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Payroll Module; SOX Internal Controls

reference: State Tax Authority Regulations; DOL Wage and Hour Division

Values: Enrolled (registered, not yet attended), Completed (attended and passed), Failed (attended but did not meet passing threshold), No-Show (registered but did not attend), Waived (exempted by supervisor). Belongs to the enrollment, not the training event master.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

description: Date the employee was enrolled or registered for this training event. Belongs to the enrollment relationship, not to the employee or the training event master record.
reference: Internal - M:N Association Attribute

business_glossary_term: Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) Training Completed Flag
description: Indicates whether the employee has completed required Good Agricultural Practices (GAP) training as mandated by FSMA Produce Safety Rule. Required for all workers involved in covered produce activities including harvesting, packing, and handling. [Moved from employee: This boolean on employee is a denormalized summary flag. The authoritative per-event completion record now lives in training_enrollment.completion_status. This flag can be derived from enrollment records and should be removed from employee to avoid data inconsistency.]
reference: FSMA Produce Safety Rule 21 CFR 112; GLOBALG.A.P.; FDA

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) Training Completed Flag
description: Indicates whether the employee has completed Hazard Analysis and Critical Control Points (HACCP) training. Required for employees working in food processing facilities, packing houses, and post-harvest handling operations under FDA and USDA FSIS regulations. [Moved from employee: Same as gap_training_completed — this is a denormalized summary flag on employee that duplicates what is now tracked per-enrollment in training_enrollment.completion_status. Should be derived, not stored on the employee master.]
reference: USDA FSIS HACCP Regulations 9 CFR 417; FDA 21 CFR 120; Codex Alimentarius HACCP Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the employee holds a current OSHA safety certification (e.g., OSHA 10-hour or 30-hour General Industry or Construction card). Required for supervisory roles and equipment operators in farm and processing facility environments. [Moved from employee: Denormalized certification flag on employee that duplicates per-enrollment certificate_issued and completion_status in training_enrollment. Should be derived from enrollment records for OSHA-type training events.]
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1928 (Agriculture); OSHA 29 CFR 1910 (General Industry); OSHA Training Institute

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each training event record. Primary key for the training_event data product in the workforce domain.

description: Actual number of employees who attended this training event session. Used for compliance reporting, cost allocation, and training effectiveness analysis.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; FSMA; SAP SuccessFactors

description: Actual duration of the training session in minutes as delivered. Used for CEU (Continuing Education Unit) credit calculation, compliance reporting, and pesticide applicator license renewal tracking.
reference: EPA Pesticide Applicator CEU Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.; SQF; BRC

description: Exact date and time the training session actually concluded. Used with actual_start_timestamp to compute actual duration for compliance and audit purposes.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; EPA WPS; FSMA

description: Exact date and time the training session actually commenced. Distinct from scheduled date; used for compliance evidence and duration calculation.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; EPA WPS; FSMA

description: Numeric score achieved by the employee on the post-training assessment or exam, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used to determine pass/fail status and competency level.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; GLOBALG.A.P. Competency Assessment; SQF

description: Indicates whether a training completion certificate was issued to the employee upon successful completion of this training event. True = certificate issued; False = not issued.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; EPA WPS; FSMA; SQF; BRC

description: Unique identifier printed on the training completion certificate issued to the employee. Used for audit verification, regulatory inspections, and pesticide applicator license renewal documentation.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P.; EPA WPS; FSMA; State Pesticide Applicator Licensing

description: Number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) credits awarded upon successful completion of this training event. Applicable for pesticide applicator license renewal and professional development tracking.
reference: EPA Pesticide Applicator CEU Requirements; FIFRA; State Pesticide Applicator Licensing Programs

description: Date the employee successfully completed the training event. The SSOT completion date for compliance evidence, certificate issuance, and qualification expiration calculation.
reference: FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; EPA WPS; SQF; BRC; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Per-attendee completion status for this training event. Enrolled = registered not yet attended; In_Progress = partially completed; Completed = successfully finished; Failed = did not meet pass threshold; Withdrawn = voluntarily withdrew; No_Show = did not attend without withdrawal.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.

description: Timestamp when this training event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance record integrity.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; ISO 22000

description: Version identifier of the training curriculum or course materials used for this event (e.g., 'v2.3', '2024-Q1'). Ensures audit trail integrity when regulatory requirements change and curriculum is updated.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; FSMA; SQF; BRC; ISO 22000

description: Method by which the training was delivered to attendees. Values: classroom (instructor-led in a room), field (on-site farm/field instruction), online (e-learning/LMS), blended (combination), video (recorded/self-paced video), on_the_job (supervised practical training).
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; GLOBALG.A.P. Training Records

description: Date the employee was enrolled or registered for this training event. Used to track lead time between enrollment and completion, and for compliance deadline management.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; FSMA Worker Training Requirements

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the training event or course offering (e.g., TRN-GAP001, TRN-HACCP02). Used for scheduling, audit documentation, and regulatory reporting.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation

description: Current lifecycle status of the training event session. Scheduled = planned but not yet started; In_Progress = currently underway; Completed = session finished; Cancelled = session cancelled; Postponed = rescheduled.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System

description: Human-readable title or name of the training session (e.g., 'GAP Awareness Training', 'HACCP Food Safety Fundamentals', 'WPS Pesticide Safety', 'H-2A Worker Rights Orientation'). Used in reports, certificates, and audit documentation.
reference: FSMA Worker Training Requirements; GLOBALG.A.P.; EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS)

description: Indicates whether this training event fulfills the H-2A visa program worker rights orientation requirement under DOL regulations. True = qualifies as H-2A orientation; False = does not fulfill H-2A orientation requirement.
reference: DOL H-2A Temporary Agricultural Worker Program; 20 CFR Part 655

description: ISO 639-1 language code in which the training was delivered (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'es-MX'). Critical for H-2A and migrant worker compliance — FSMA and EPA WPS require training in a language workers understand.
reference: FSMA Worker Training Requirements; EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS); DOL H-2A Program; ISO 639-1

description: Maximum number of attendees permitted for this training event session. Used for scheduling, capacity planning, and ensuring adequate trainer-to-trainee ratios for hands-on safety training.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; OSHA Safety Training Best Practices

description: Minimum assessment score percentage required to pass this training event and receive credit or certification. Varies by training type and regulatory requirement.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; GLOBALG.A.P.; FSMA

description: Originally planned duration of the training session in minutes. Used for scheduling, workforce planning, and comparison against actual duration.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System

description: Date on which the qualification, certification, or compliance credit earned from this training event expires and retraining is required. Critical for pesticide applicator CEU renewal, OSHA certifications, and GLOBALG.A.P. annual training requirements.
reference: EPA Pesticide Applicator CEU Requirements; OSHA; GLOBALG.A.P.; FSMA; SQF; BRC

description: Date by which the employee must complete retraining for this training type. Populated when retraining_required is True. Used for compliance deadline tracking and workforce scheduling.
reference: FSMA; EPA WPS; GLOBALG.A.P.; OSHA; State Pesticide Applicator Licensing

description: Indicates whether the employee is required to retake this training (e.g., due to failed assessment, regulatory change, incident, or qualification expiration). True = retraining required; False = no retraining needed.
reference: FSMA; EPA WPS; GLOBALG.A.P.; OSHA

description: Calendar date on which the training event was originally scheduled to occur. Used for workforce planning, compliance scheduling, and audit trail.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; FSMA Worker Training Requirements

description: License or certification number of the trainer, particularly for pesticide safety and applicator training where a certified trainer is required by EPA and state regulations.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS); FIFRA (Federal Insecticide Fungicide and Rodenticide Act); State Pesticide Applicator Licensing

description: Full name of the trainer or instructor who delivered the training. Captured for audit documentation when the trainer may be external or when trainer_id is not available. Required for GLOBALG.A.P. and EPA WPS compliance records.
reference: GLOBALG.A.P. Audit Documentation; EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS); FSMA

description: Total cost incurred to deliver this training event, including trainer fees, materials, venue, and external provider charges. Used for labor cost allocation (OPEX) across farm operations and processing facilities in SAP CO module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; GAAP; IFRS

description: Classification of the training by regulatory or operational category. Examples include GAP, HACCP, WPS_PESTICIDE_SAFETY, OSHA_HAZCOM, H2A_WORKER_RIGHTS, SEXUAL_HARASSMENT_PREVENTION, EQUIPMENT_OPERATION, EMERGENCY_RESPONSE, FOOD_SAFETY, PESTICIDE_APPLICATOR_CEU. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GAP|HACCP|WPS_PESTICIDE_SAFETY|OSHA_HAZCOM|H2A_WORKER_RIGHTS|SEXUAL_HARASSMENT_PREVENTION|EQUIPMENT_OPERATION|EMERGENCY_RESPONSE|FOOD_SAFETY|PESTICIDE_APPLICATOR_CEU — promote to reference product]
reference: FSMA; GLOBALG.A.P.; EPA WPS; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR 1910.1200; H-2A Program DOL; SQF; BRC Global Standards

description: Timestamp when this training event record was most recently modified. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Learning Management System; FSMA Record-Keeping Requirements; ISO 22000

description: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) classification of the employee for pesticide safety training purposes. Agricultural Worker = works in treated areas; Pesticide Handler = mixes/loads/applies pesticides; Early Entry Worker = enters treated areas during REI; Not Applicable = WPS training not required for this role.
reference: EPA Worker Protection Standard (WPS) 40 CFR Part 170; FIFRA
